PN CN111185181-A
TI Use of heterogeneous copper-zirconium-cerium ternary non-noble metal catalyst in catalytic oxidation of acetic acid.
AU LI X
   WANG Y
   LIANG Y
   CUI G
   HAN S
AE BEIJING MACHINERY EQUIP RES INST (CAES-C)
GA 2020466050
AB    NOVELTY - Use of amorphous catalyst in catalytic oxidation of acetic acid, is claimed, where the amorphous catalyst is heterogeneous copper-zirconium-cerium ternary non-noble metal catalyst; the products of acetic acid catalytic oxidation are carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - The amorphous catalyst is useful for catalytic oxidation of acetic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst achieves acetic acid removal rate more than 93%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of the amorphous catalyst comprising (1) dissolving metal salt precursors of copper oxide, zirconia, and ceria in solvent according to specific ratio to obtain precursor mixed solution, (2) dripping precipitator to precursor mixed solution to obtain precipitate, where the volume ratio of metal salt precursor and precipitator is 1:5-10, (3) aging precipitate at room temperature for 24-48 hours, and centrifuging, and (4) calcining obtained solid in air to obtain amorphous catalyst; and
   (2) use method of amorphous catalyst comprising (1) tableting or granulating heterogeneous copper-zirconium-cerium ternary non-precious metal catalyst, and placing in reaction container, (2) filling gas and acetic acid solution to reaction container, (3) heating to pressurize reaction container to start catalytic oxidation reaction, and (4) finishing reaction, measuring acetic acid content, and calculating acetic acid conversion removal rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heterogeneous copper-zirconium-cerium ternary non-noble metal catalyst is amorphous ternary metal mixed oxide catalyst represented by structural formula of xCuO-yZrO2-zCeO2, where the ratio of x, y, and z is 1-2:1-5:1-5; the amorphous ternary metal mixed oxide catalyst is obtained by salt co-precipitation of metal salt precursor, preferably copper, zirconium, cerium nitrate, and/or chloride salt using aqueous ammonia as precipitator, and solvent, preferably water, methanol, and/or ethanol; the specific surface area of amorphous ternary metal mixed oxide catalyst is 80-300 m2/g with microporous structure. Preferred Process: In the step (3), drying is carried out at 80-120 degrees C for 12-24 hours. In the step (4), calcining is carried out at 450-750 degrees C for 3-6 hours.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC D04-A01K2; D04-B06; D04-D; E10-C04J2U; E11-Q02B; E11-W; E35-A; E35-L; J04-E04A; J04-E11; N02-D; N03-B02; N06-E01; N07-L01A; N07-L01B1; N07-L02F

PN CN111019885-A
TI Extracting exosomes preparing embryo in vitro            culture additive, comprises e.g. taking out uterus,            slaughtering, separating endometrial tissue, rinsing            shredded tissue block, discarding supernatant, and            ultra centrifuging.
AU WU Z
   HONG L
   CAI G
   HU Q
   LIU D
   LI Z
   GU T
   YANG J
   YANG H
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA AGRIC (USCG-C)
GA 2020339255
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting exosomes derived from pig                endometrium comprises (i) taking out uterus,                slaughtering, taking the two sides of the uterine                horn are fastened to ensure that the uterine cavity                is tightly closed, and separating endometrial                tissue from myometrium and cutting the endometrial                tissue into tissue pieces, (ii) rinsing the                shredded tissue block in step (i) with sterile DPBS                containing 1% (v/v) antibiotic-antimycotic, 0.002                mg/ml amphotericin B and 0.05 mg/ml gentamicin                remove impurities, centrifuging, and discarding                supernatant, (iii) placing tissue block after                centrifugation, discarding the supernatant in step                (ii) in Hanks Balanced Salt Solution, and                incubating at 37&#176; C and 5% carbon dioxide                for 20-28 hours to collect pig endometrial culture                fluid, and (iv) using culture medium of pig                endometrium collected in step and (iii) to                extracting exosomes by ultracentrifugation to                obtain exosomes derived from pig endometrium.
   USE - The porcine endometrium-derived exosomes is                useful in preparing embryo in vitro culture                additive (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   exosome derived from porcine endometrium, and                extracted by the extraction method; and
   use of porcine endometrium-derived exosomes in                preparing embryo in vitro culture additive.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  size of the tissue block is 1-5 mm3. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), the cleaning                  specifically comprises washing with 75% volume                  fraction of alcohol for 2-3 times, rinsing with                  physiological saline containing 1% (v/v)                  antibiotic-antimycotic, 0.002 mg/ml amphotericin                  B and 0.05 mg/ml gentamicin for 2-3 times,                  rinsing with sterile DPBS containing 1% (v/v)                  antibiotic-antimycotic, 0.002 mg/ml amphotericin                  B and 0.05 mg/ml gentamicin for 1-2 times. In the                  step (ii), the conditions of the centrifugation                  are 25&#176; C and 90 g for 5 minutes. In the                  step (iii), the incubation time is 24 hours. In                  the step (iv), the ultracentrifugation method                  specifically comprises centrifuging the collected                  endometrial culture fluid under centrifugal                  conditions of 2000 g at 4&#176; C for 20                  minutes, and collecting the supernatant;                  centrifuging the supernatant for 30 minutes at                  4&#176; C and 10,000g of centrifugation, and                  collecting the supernatant; centrifuging the                  supernatant for 2 hours under 120,000 g,                  discarding the supernatant and collecting                  precipitate; resuspending the precipitate with                  DPBS, centrifuging again under 120,000 g for 2                  hours, discarding the supernatant, and the                  resulting precipitate is the total exosomes                  derived from endometrium. The additive for in                  vitro culture of embryos is a product for                  promoting the proliferation of embryonic                  trophoblast cell. The embryo in vitro culture                  additive is obtained by inoculating embryonic                  trophoblast cells into the culture medium                  containing the exosomes derived from the pig                  endometrium and culturing for 24-48 hours.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN110973328-A
TI Silkworm pupa alpha-linolenic acid gel candy used            for lowering blood sugar, being prepared by drying and            crushing silkworm pupa, Perilla and flax seeds into            powder, extracting, pressing, and formulating with            alpha-linolenic acid into candy.
AU LIU J
   LI S
   SUN J
AE JILIN SILKWORM IND SCI RES INST (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 202031488P
AB    NOVELTY - Silkworm pupa alpha -linolenic acid gel candy                is prepared by (a) drying silkworm pupa, Perilla                seeds and flax seeds using JD6 series microwave                drying equipment at 70 degrees C or less, (b)                crushing dried silkworm pupa, Perilla seeds, flax                seeds and 6-10% water into powder of 20-40 meshes,                (c) performing supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction and mechanical pressing, (d) refining                silkworm pupa extract, Perilla extract, flax seed                extract by high temperature static method at                100-120 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, cooling,                resting and filtering pure pupa oil, (e)                formulating with silkworm pupa extract, Perilla                extract and flax seed extract in a ratio of 5:3:2,                78-90% unsaturated fatty acid, and 42-55% alpha                -linolenic acid, (f) formulating with water,                gelatin, glycerin, sorbitol liquid, maltitol liquid                and mulberry leaf polysaccharide by mixing and                dissolving mixture by heating, and (g) preparing                candy by capsule making process.
   USE - The silkworm pupa alpha -linolenic acid gel                candy is useful for increasing blood sugar lowering                effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, easy                implementation, low cost, and maintains                characteristics of silkworm pupa extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves                  pre-treating silkworm pupa, Perilla seeds and                  flax seeds by (a) removing oily silkworm cocoons,                  necrotic pupa, cocoon shell and dead pupa, (b)                  washing the silkworm pupa with water and drying                  the water, (c) selecting Perilla seeds by                  winnowing to remove impurities, and (d) selecting                  flaxseed by winnowing to remove impurities. The                  mechanical pressing is carried out by cold                  pressing and hot pressing. The step (d) involves                  cooling and resting for 12-24 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Hypoglycemic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC B04-A08G2; B04-A10; B04-A98; B04-B04M1; B04-C02D; B04-N02; B07-A02B; B10-A07B; B10-C04E2; B10-E04C; B14-F09; B14-S18; D03-E05; D03-E10A; D03-H01T2B

PT P
PN WO2020069711-A1; CN112839987-A; KR2021069087-A; BR112021006228-A2; EP3861062-A1; US2022033542-A1; IN202117018755-A; JP2022504369-W; EP3861062-A4
TI Producing polymer material for packaging e.g.,            bottle for storage of health and pharmaceutical            products by providing polymer material made from sugar            cane ethanol, providing biodegradable additive, and            blending.
AU BODTCHER-HANSEN M
   BOslashDTCHER-HANSEN M
AE ECO PACKAGING APS (ECOP-Non-standard)
   ECO PACKAGING APS (ECOP-Non-standard)
   ECO PACKAGING APS (ECOP-Non-standard)
GA 202028470B
AB    NOVELTY - A polymer material is produced by (a)                providing a polymer material, which is carbon                dioxide neutral in the form of polyethylene, made                from sugar cane ethanol, (b) providing a                biodegradable additive, and (c) blending the                polymer material of step (a) with biodegradable                additive of step (b). Blending ratio of polymer                material of step (a) to biodegradable additive of                step (b) is 90-98:10-2 wt.%, preferably 90-97:10-3                wt.%, and the biodegradable additive of step (b) is                organic mixture for growing of naturally occurring                organisms comprising a fungal-bacterial mixture,                preferably as a biofilm on polyethylene and which                also creates enzymes and acids for effecting                enzymatic catalysis.
   USE - Production of polymer material for packaging                e.g., bottle, pot, can, or cap for storage of                health and pharmaceutical products and for                production of articles e.g., bags, films,                membranes, pipes, fibers, toys, picnic ware,                kitchenware, insulation and fuel tanks (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The packaging material is able to comply with                food safety and health regulations as containers or                packaging made from synthetic based polymer and                reduces environmental concerns related to the use                of current packing based on synthetic                polymers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: Fungal-bacterial                  mixture is Penicillium-Bacillus.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Blending ratio is                  95-97:5-3 wt.%. Polymer material contains greater                  than or equal to 98 wt.%, preferably greater than                  or equal to 99 wt.% of the packaging. Preferred                  Process: Polymer material of step (a) and                  biodegradable additive of step (b) are provided                  in granular form. Step (c) is conducted by a                  polymer processing stage selected from extrusion,                  molding, such as injection molding and blow                  molding, calendering molding, and/or rotational                  molding. Step (c) is conducted only by extrusion.                  Preferred Parameters: Packaging has                  bio-degradability rate of greater than or equal                  to 90 wt.% loss over 180 days in aerobic                  environment as measured by ISO 14855 and greater                  than or equal to 45 wt.% loss over 27 days in                  anaerobic environment as measured by ISO                  15985.
DC A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A84 (Household and office fittings - including carpets and carbon paper.); A86 (Fancy goods, games, sports, toys.); A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.)

PN US2020105541-A1; US11049731-B2
TI Method of converting a spacer film in semiconductor wafers, involves providing a substrate with a film having a first elemental composition and exposing film to a microwave plasma formed from a reactant gas to form a converted film.
AU CHEN E
   YING C C
   CITLA B S
   TANNOS J
   MATTSON M A
AE APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
GA 2020252007
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a substrate (200) with a film (240) formed, having a first elemental composition. The film is exposed to a microwave plasma formed from a reactant gas to form a converted film (250) with a second elemental composition. The first elemental composition and the second elemental composition includes a different element. The substrate has a substrate surface with feature formed and the film fills a portion of the feature. The film includes a spacer for logic applications. The reactant gas includes hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method of converting a spacer film in semiconductor wafers.
   ADVANTAGE - Methods for modifying film compositions enables better etch rate differences between deposited materials. Modification of film properties, such as etch resistance, density and refractive index is allowed without changing other film properties, such as elemental composition and conformality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for modifying a spacer film; and
   (2) a method for converting a silicon oxide film.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of method of converting a spacer film in semiconductor wafers.
   Spacer film converting method (100)
   Substrate (200)
   Film (240)
   Converted film (250)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The plasma gas comprises argon, helium, hydrogen, oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide.

TI Pan for serving traditional dishes of Chinese people, has flow path with inner end connected to exit of dry ice accommodating unit in order to receive vaporized dry ice, and outer end made to discharge vaporized dry ice.
AU CHEN Y L
   CHEN Y
AE CHEN Y L (CHEN-Individual)
   CHEN Y L (CHEN-Individual)
   CHEN Y (CHEN-Individual)
GA 202025261N
AB    NOVELTY - The pan has a partitioned case which consists of a top opening. The liquid foodstuff is heated inside the case. A dry ice accommodating unit is provided in an annular edge of the main case in order to perform heat exchange operation. The accommodating unit has an inlet port for putting solid dry ice, and an exit for the outflow of vaporized dry ice. A flow path has an inner end connected with the exit of dry ice accommodating unit to receive vaporized dry ice, and an outer end made to protrude outward from the annular edge and discharge vaporized dry ice.
   USE - Pan for serving traditional dishes of Chinese people.
   ADVANTAGE - A surprising visual effect is provided by the carbon dioxide gas discharged in a white mist state. The sublimation of dry ice is performed by using a simple dry ice sublimation mechanism. The serving of two different foodstuffs having different flavor is possible due to the partitioning of case. The change of the shape of coupling of pan is possible to improve fun and design of the pan.

TI Chemical product supply system comprises e.g. gasification unit supplied by oxygen via electrolyzer, carbon monoxide reacts with water in chemical reactions, and syngas and hydrogen produced by water gas shift reactor and electrolyzer.
AU TAN J
   WU X
   GAO L
   ZHANG H
AE XIANGYANG ZHONGCHENG TESTING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202021279B
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical product supply system, comprises a gasification unit supplied by the oxygen produced by an electrolyzer set with ANDA gasification unit to generate syngas. The syngas used for chemical products as synthetic fuels. The system further includes product synthesis units that produce chemical products to be supplied to generators or chemical plants. The system includes a main fuel supply to the gasification unit, a primary fuel has fuel comprising coal, petroleum, petroleum gas, biomass, heavy oil, refinery residues, waste, preferably organic waste and slurry. The system further includes a water gas shift reactor, syngas is passed to a water-gas converter, carbon monoxide reacts with water in chemical reactions to form carbon dioxide and hydrogen. The syngas and hydrogen produced by the water gas shift reactor and/or the electrolyzer, which are passed to a carbon-hydrogen ratio adjustment unit to change ratio of carbon and hydrogen in the syngas, and forms modified syngas.
   USE - Used as chemical product supply system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: improves the overall efficiency of the power generation system; reduces the cost and complexity of synthetic fuels or production of chemical products.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic view of the chemical product supply system.

PT P
PN CN110835573-A
TI Method for removing organic sulfur in coal involves carrying out steam explosion reaction using crushed coal sample, feeding coal sample into extraction kettle, injecting mixed entrained medicament into extraction kettle and refluxing.
AU PENG Y
   FAN J
   LIU J
   ZHAO G
   ZHANG S
   GUI X
AE SHANXI COKING COAL GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
   JIANGSU TANQINGYUAN CLEAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY RES INSTITU (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202020208K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing organic sulfur in coal involves carrying out steam explosion reaction by adding crushed coal sample into steam explosion device and passing reaction medium under high temperature and pressure, feeding pre-treated coal sample into extraction kettle of supercritical extraction desulfurization system, sealing extraction kettle and its connecting pipes, vacuumizing extraction kettle, feeding carbon dioxide mixed entrained medicament into mixer as polar supercritical fluid and mixing uniformly, injecting medicament into extraction kettle, adjusting the temperature and pressure of supercritical extraction desulfurization system, stabilizing pressure in extraction kettle within temperature and pressure range that can maintain maximum density and maximum dissolution capacity of polar supercritical fluid, refluxing polar supercritical fluid in extraction kettle, deflating extraction kettle, cooling mixture, collecting samples for analysis and detection.
   USE - Method for removing organic sulfur in coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, strengthens the effect of extraction mass transfer, and has excellent desulfurization effect with the occurrence of micropyrolysis reaction, macromolecular pyrolysis and small molecule extraction. The supercritical fluid cooperates to form system with strong polarity, excellent reactivity and high selectivity to remove organic sulfur from coal. The method removes sulfur using safe, inexpensive and environmentally-friendly solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The steam explosion medium is water vapor, carbon dioxide or ammonia. Preferred Property: The particle size of the crushed coal sample is 3-6 mm. Preferred Conditions: The critical temperature and pressure of carbon dioxide is 31.26 degrees C and 7.38 MPa respectively. The steam explosion reaction is carried out at pressure of 3-4.5 MPa and temperature of 100-350 degrees C for 0.1-2 hours or 1-24 hours. The pressure of the supercritical extraction desulfurization system is adjusted by supercharger. When the reaction in the extraction kettle is started, the agitator is started and stirred at 50-70 rpm for every 30-40 minutes for 3-5 minutes. Preferred Process: The process involve filling the extraction kettle with carbon dioxide gas through compressor and adding an entrainer, and then discharge it, and repeating the process 5-10 times until the air in the extraction kettle is completely replaced. Through the electrical control and monitoring system, the entire reaction process is monitored and data is collected, displayed and stored.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The entraining agent is an alcoholic liquid, an aromatic liquid, an alkane gas or water. The alcoholic liquid is methanol, ethanol, isopropanol or n-butanol, and aromatic liquid is benzene or toluene. The alkane gas is ethane or propane.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

PT P
PN CN110562933-A; CN110562933-B
TI Separating calcium and sulfur from industrial            by-product gypsum involves adding atmosphere modifier            to dried industrial by-product gypsum, mixing and            decomposing in rapid decomposer to obtain sulfur            dioxide smoke and solid products.
AU TAN H
   MA X
   DONG F
   LI Y
   DENG Q
   WANG J
   HE X
   LI F
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2019A7002M
AB    NOVELTY - Separating calcium and sulfur from industrial                by-product gypsum involves adding an atmosphere                modifier to the dried industrial by-product gypsum,                mixing uniformly, and decomposing in a rapid                decomposer to obtain sulfur dioxide smoke and solid                products. The solid product undergoes magnetic                separation and re-selection to obtain an atmosphere                modifier, calcium oxide and calcium sulfide. The                water is added to calcium sulfide and hydrolyzed to                obtain calcium hydroxide precipitate and calcium                sulfide solution, and solid-liquid separation. The                carbon dioxide gas is passed into the calcium                sulfide solution to obtain calcium carbonate                precipitation and hydrogen sulfide gas. The capture                agent and pH adjuster are added in the scrubber to                absorb the hydrogen sulfide gas to get rich liquid.                The absorbed rich liquid is entered the oxidation                tank, and surfactants and foaming agents are added                and air is passed through the oxidation tank to                obtain elemental sulfur through flotation.
   USE - Method for separating calcium and sulfur from                industrial by-product gypsum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to separate calcium and                sulfur from industrial by-product gypsum is                cost-effective, high production efficiency and good                product quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The atmosphere                  modifier is one of red mud, iron alum slag,                  nickel iron slag, and manganese slag, and the                  added amount is 1-10 wt.% mass of industrial                  by-product gypsum. The combustion-supporting gas                  for the rapid decomposer is a carbon                  dioxide/oxygen mixed gas. The added amount is                  0.1-5 wt.% mass of industrial by-product gypsum.                  The pH adjusting agent is one of ammonia water,                  sodium carbonate and calcium lactate, and the                  added amount is 0.1-5 wt.% industrial by-product                  gypsum. The added amount is 0.01-0.1 wt.%                  industrial by-product gypsum. The added amount is                  0.01-0.1 wt.% industrial by-product gypsum.                  Preferred Components: The capture agent is one of                  ferric citrate, ferric hydroxide and ferric                  lactate. The surfactant is one of ethionamide,                  isothiourea salt and allyl succinate. The foaming                  agent is one of cresol glyceryl ether, methyl                  isobutyl methanol, methylpentanol, and                  polyethylene glycol ether.

PN KR2019115336-A; KR2086419-B1
TI Device used for producing polyaluminum chloride and in water treatment, comprises e.g. reactor A for reacting aluminum hydroxide with hydrochloric acid, reservoir of basicity triggers, and reactor B for mixing basicity inducing agent.
AU AHN K
AE KOREA CIVIL ENG & BUILDING TECHNOLOGY (KOCT-C)
GA 201987948D
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a reactor A for reacting aluminum hydroxide with hydrochloric acid to produce a first product comprising first polyaluminum chloride having a basicity of 25-45%; a reservoir of basicity triggers; a reactor B for producing a second product comprising a second polyaluminum chloride having a basicity of 50-65% by mixing and reacting the first product and the basicity inducing agent; a reservoir of metal aluminum powder; and a reactor C for mixing the second product and the metal aluminum powder and condensation polymerization under reduced pressure to produce a third product comprising a third polyaluminum chloride having a basicity of 70-90%.
   USE - The device is useful for producing polyaluminum chloride (claimed) and in the field of water treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has excellent storage stability of more than 70%, do not cause long-term clouding, reduces maintenance due to electricity consumption, prevents gelation due to high base with physical dispersibility of carbon dioxide generation, decreases fluidity, improves alkalinity and pH of treated water and aluminum sulfate, is economical and has cohesive effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the apparatus for producing polyaluminum chloride (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The device further comprises a heat exchanger, a recovery apparatus for recovering the unreacted metal aluminum powder from the reactor C. The unreacted metal aluminum powder recovered from the recovery device is stored in the metal aluminum powder storage tank. The content of the metal aluminum powder introduced into the reactor C from the metal aluminum powder storage tank is 5 wt.% or less based on the total amount of aluminum in the third polyaluminum chloride. The reactor A is a reaction of aluminum hydroxide and hydrochloric acid in a weight ratio of 1:1-1:3. The reactor A performs the reaction at a temperature of 120-180 degrees C and pressure conditions of 2-6 kgf/cm2. The base comprises sodium hydroxide, sodium carbonate or sodium aluminum oxide in the reactor B. The base is introduced in 10-30 wt.% based on the weight of the first derivative in the reactor B. The second polyaluminum chloride comprises 1-10 wt.% Al13 species and 15-25 wt.% Al30 species based on the total amount of aluminum species and the third poly aluminum chloride comprises 10-20 wt.% Al13 species and 25-35 wt.% Al30 species based on the total amount of aluminum species. The third polyaluminum chloride comprises 10-20 wt.% aluminum oxide. Preferred Method: The condensation polymerization in the reactor C is carried out at 30-110 degrees C for 1-6 hours under 50-200 Torr using hydrogen peroxide solution as a polymerization initiator. The third polyaluminum chloride has at least 30 days of no settling under storage conditions of 45 degrees C.

PN WO2019192079-A1; CN110339848-A; CN110339848-B; IN202037046108-A; US2021031174-A1; EP3778015-A1; GB2586404-A; US11007510-B2; JP2021526076-W; RU2760367-C1; EP3778015-A4; JP6982216-B2; ZA202006772-A; GB2586404-B
TI Preparing supported epsilon/epsilon iron carbide            catalyst, comprises e.g. impregnating catalyst carrier            in aqueous solution of iron salt, drying, calcining,            subjecting catalyst precursor to reduction, and            pretreating with carbon monoxide.
AU WANG P
   ZHAGN K
   LV Y
   LIN Q
   FENG B
   MEN Z
   ZHANG F
   SUN Q
   MIAO P
   ZHANG K
AE CHINA ENERGY INVESTMENT CORP LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY INST (CGUO-C)
   CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
   CHINA ENERGY INVESTMENT CORP LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY INST (CGUO-C)
GA 201984974M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supported epsilon / epsilon ' iron                carbide catalyst, comprises (i) impregnating a                catalyst carrier in a aqueous solution of iron                salt, drying and calcining the impregnated support                to obtain a catalyst precursor, (ii) subjecting the                catalyst precursor to precursor reduction with                hydrogen at 300-550 degrees C , (iii) pretreating                the resultant material with hydrogen and carbon                monoxide at 90-185 degrees C, where the molar ratio                of hydrogen and carbon monoxide is 1.2-2.8:1, and                (iv) reacting the resultant material in the step                (iii) with hydrogen and carbon monoxide at 200-300                degrees C to obtain carbide, where the molar ratio                of hydrogen and carbon monoxide is 1-3.2:1.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has reduced carbon dioxide and                methane selectivity; and increased product                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) supported epsilon / epsilon ' iron carbide                catalyst prepared by the above method; and
   (2) Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, comprising                contacting a synthetic feed gas with the above                catalyst under Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction                condition in a high temperature and high pressure                continuous reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying and calcining                  process include drying the impregnated carrier at                  20-30 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours, then drying at                  35-80 degrees C for 6-10 hours under vacuum of                  250-1200 Pa, drying the dried material at 110-150                  degrees C for 3-24 hours, and then calcining the                  resultant material at 300-550 degrees C for 1-10                  hours. The precursor reduction in the step (ii)                  is carried out at pressure of 0.1-15 atm,                  preferably 0.3-2.6 atm for 0.7-15 hours,                  preferably 1-12 hours, where gas velocity of the                  hydrogen in the step (ii) is 600-25000 ml/h/g,                  preferably 2800-22000 ml/h/g. The pretreatment in                  the step (iii) is carried out at pressure of                  0.05-7 atm, preferably 0.08-4.5 atm for 15-120                  minutes, preferably 20-90 minutes, where the                  total gas velocity of the hydrogen and carbon                  monoxide is 300-12000 ml/h/g, preferably                  1500-9000 ml/h/g. The carbide prepared in the                  step (iv) is carried out at pressure of 0.1-10                  atm, preferably 0.2-4.5 atm for 0.5-15 hours,                  preferably 2.5-12 hours, where the total gas                  velocity of the hydrogen and carbon monoxide is                  500-30000 ml/h/g, preferably 3000-25000 ml/h/g.                  The method further comprises raising temperature                  of the pre-treated system to 200-300 degrees C at                  heating rate of 0.2-5 degrees C/minute,                  preferably increasing the temperature of the                  pre-treated system to 210-290 degrees C at                  heating rate of 0.2-2.5 degrees C/minute. The                  process of the precursor reduction, pretreatment                  and carbide preparation is carried out in                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reactor. Preferred                  Components: The iron salt is ferric nitrate,                  ferric chloride and/or ammonium ferrous sulfate.                  The impregnation is performed such the iron                  content in the carrier after drying is 10-30                  wt.%. The catalyst carrier is silica, alumina                  and/or titania. The catalyst carrier has particle                  size of 30-200 mu m.
   EXAMPLE - Ferric nitrate nonahydrate was completely                dissolved in pure water to obtain ferric nitrate                solution having concentration of 0.12 g/ml, then                the ferric nitrate solution was saturated and                impregnated on 10 g silica carrier having particle                size of 30-200 mu m, and allowed to dried at 30                degrees C for 0.5 hours. Then the impregnated                carrier was placed in a vacuum oven at degree of                vacuum of 250 Pa, dried at 35 degrees C for 10                hours, and then dried in an oven at 110 degrees C                for 24 hours. The dried impregnated carrier was                calcined in a muffle furnace at 500 degrees C for 1                hour to obtain a first precursor. The first                precursor was placed in a tubular Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis reactor, hydrogen stream having a gas                velocity of 2800 ml/h/g and pressure of 2.6 atm was                introduced at 550 degrees C for 1 hour, temperature                in the reactor was lowered to 185 degrees C, the                hydrogen flow was switched off having gas velocity                of 9000 ml/h/g, pretreated with hydrogen-carbon                monoxide for 90 minutes having gas velocity of 9000                ml/h/g and total pressure of 0.08 atm,                hydrogen-carbon monoxide flow was switched off                having gas velocity of 25000 ml/h/g and a total                pressure of 0.2 atm, temperature was raised to 290                degrees C at heating rate of 2.5 degrees C/minute,                and insulated for 2.5 hours to obtain supported                epsilon / epsilon ' iron carbide catalyst.

PT P
PN MY171191-A
TI Activated carbon preparation involves collecting of raw precursors, drying precursors inside oven to remove water, and then grinding dried precursors by using cutting miller.
AU YUSUF S
   BORHAN A
   RASHIDI N A
AE UNIV PETRONAS TEKNOLOGI (UYPE-Non-standard)
GA 2020976590
AB    NOVELTY - Activated carbon preparation involves collecting of raw precursors (101), drying the precursors inside an oven at 110 degrees C for 12 hours to remove water (102), and then grinding the dried precursors by using a cutting miller (103). The crushed precursors (104) are sieved by using a sieve shaker for 15 minutes to segregate into various particle size groups of 0.25-1.00 mm. The precursors are exposed to an activation gas (105) for the thermal activation to produce the activated carbon. The precursors and activated carbons consist carbon yield of 26-41 wt.% with surface area of 400 m2/g with predominantly microstructures properties. The precursors include palm mesocarpfiber, palm kernel shell, rice husk, coconut shell and coconut fiber.
   USE - Method for preparing activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the activated carbon in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with smaller particle sizes to better chance for adsorption process, significantly improved surface area. The captured carbon dioxide can be stored and to be reused for activation process without using chemical solvents such as amine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of the method for preparing activated carbon.
   Raw precursors (101)
   Remove water (102)
   Cutting miller (103)
   Crushed precursors (104)
   Activation gas (105)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The selected activation gas is carbon dioxide. The thermal activation under carbon dioxide flow is a rapid process without the need for separate carbonization.

PT P
PN CN110252371-A
TI Preparation of platinum-modified cerium            oxide/three-dimensional porous carbon nitride composite            photocatalyst by mixing block graphitic carbon nitride            with ammonium bicarbonate, calcining in tubular            furnace, cooling, washing and drying.
AU WANG H
   GUAN J
   HUO P
   WU D
   YIN S
   LI J
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201996187V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of platinum-modified cerium oxide                (Pt-CeO2)/three-dimensional porous carbon nitride                (3DCN) composite photocatalyst comprises placing                melamine in a covered crucible, putting the                crucible in a muffle furnace, calcining, cooling,                collecting block graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4),                mixing ammonium bicarbonate, putting in tubular                furnace, calcining, cooling, washing and drying the                sample to obtain 3DCN, weighing cerium nitrate                hexahydrate and 3DCN, dispersing in ionic water,                stirring at room temperature, transferring to oven,                drying, putting the dried sample in a muffle                furnace, calcining to obtain CeO2/3DCN composite                photocatalyst, weighing CeO2/3DCN composite                photocatalyst, dissolving in isopropanol, removing                air in the liquid by introducing nitrogen gas,                adding chloroplatinic acid solution, stirring under                UV light, ending the reaction, filtering, washing                and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is for preparation of Pt-CeO2/3DCN                composite photocatalyst used for photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) under UV light                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, does not cause waste of                resources and formation of secondary pollution, is                green environment-friendly, and has high-efficiency                energy conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                Pt-CeO2/3DCN composite photocatalyst comprising Pt                and CeO2 nanoparticles dispersed on                three-dimensional porous carbon nitride                surface.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first muffle furnace                  calcining step is conducted at 500-700 degrees C                  for 2-4 hours. The mass ratio of block g-C3N4 and                  ammonium bicarbonate is 1:1-10. The tubular                  furnace calcining step is conducted at 200-500                  degrees C for 2-4 hours. The mass ratio of 3DCN                  and cerium nitrate hexahydrate is 1:0.38-2.28.                  The second muffle furnace calcining step is                  conducted at 300-500 degrees C for 2 hours. The                  concentration of chloroplatinic acid solution is                  10 mg/ml, and platinum is at 0.86 mass% relative                  to the mass of CeO2/3DCN. The UV light stirring                  time is 2-6 hours.

PN CN110255584-A; CN210237143-U
TI Secondary brine carbonate removing device comprises e.g. filtering brine acidification tank sealed and provided with a hydrochloric acid inlet for inputting hydrochloric acid, a filtering brine inlet, a nitrogen inlet and a gas outlet.
AU ZHOU Z
   SHI B
   LIU Z
   LEI S
   YANG T
   DU Y
AE SICHUAN YONGXIANG CO LTD (TWCO-C)
GA 201991555U
AB    NOVELTY - Secondary brine carbonate removing device comprises a filtering brine acidification tank (1) which is sealed and provided with a hydrochloric acid inlet, a filtering brine inlet, a nitrogen inlet and a gas outlet. The hydrochloric acid inlet is used for inputting hydrochloric acid, and the filtered brine inlet is used for inputting filtered brine outputted by a brine refining process. The nitrogen inlet is set at the bottom of the filtering brine acidification tank and is used for continuous input of nitrogen gas for maintaining a positive pressure above the filtered brine acidification tank. The gas outlet is opened at the top end of the filtered brine acidification tank for discharging the gas into the filtered brine acidification tank.
   USE - Used as secondary brine carbonate removing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device prevents the outside air from entering the filtered brine acidification tank to avoid secondary pollution of the filtered brine by promoting timely discharge of carbon dioxide gas generated by the addition of hydrochloric acid into the brine from the gas outlet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the secondary brine carbonate removing device (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Filtering brine acidification tank (1)
   Mixer (2)

AE FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)

GA 201980796H
AB    NOVELTY - A steam methane reformer-integrated fuel cell                system comprises greater than or equal to 1 fuel                cell comprising anode, cathode and electrolyte                matrix separating the anode and cathode; and steam                methane reformer which reacts methane with steam to                produce 1st product stream including hydrogen (H2),                carbon dioxide (CO2), and carbon monoxide                (CO).
   USE - A steam methane reformer-integrated fuel cell                system.
   ADVANTAGE - Fuel cell and steam methane reformer (SMR)                efficiency are increased while eliminating nitrogen                oxide (NOx) output from the system, and capital                costs are reduced by integrating a steam methane                reformer (SMR) with high-temperature fuel cell                (such as a molten carbonate fuel cell), such that                the fuel cell system is modified to use lower-level                waste heat to preheat air for fuel-cell, and                converting the fuel cell waste heat into high level                heat that is used by the SMR. The fuel cell-steam                methane reformer integrated system has improved                efficiency with minimal impact on standard fuel                cell system and co-produces hydrogen with lower                parasitic power loads.

Preferred Device: The system further comprises a                  flash gas apparatus which receives rich solvent                  from absorber column and produces a 1st carbon                  dioxide-rich flashed gas stream comprising                  greater than or equal to 60 mol.% carbon dioxide                  (CO2).

PN CN110229787-A; CN110229787-B
TI Primary extraction of dental pulp stem cells by            collecting removed stagnant incisors, collecting            residual pulp tissue, digesting, counting cell            suspension, culturing cell suspension and observing            culture of primary cells under microscope.
AU ZHANG Y
AE SINONEURAL SHANGHAI CELL & GENE ENG HOLD (SINO-Non-standard)
   SNC CELL ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNCC-Non-standard)
GA 201980514R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for primary extraction of dental pulp                stem cells involves (a) collecting the freshly                removed stagnant incisors from children aged 5-14                years and wiping the surface with ethanol for                disinfecting, (b) collecting the residual pulp                tissue using experimental instrument and digesting                the extracted pulp tissue at 37 degrees C for 1                hour to obtain single cell suspension using                digestive solution comprising 3 g/l type I                collagenase and 4 g/l dispase, (c) counting the                cell suspension with a counting plate, adjusting                the density of the cell suspension using the                culture solution in accordance with the experiment,                and dispensing the density-adjusted cell suspension                into the culture flask, (d) culturing the primary                cells in a carbon dioxide incubator at a constant                temperature of 37 degrees C and (e) observing the                culture of the primary cells in each culture flask                under a microscope.
   USE - The method is useful for primary extraction of                dental pulp stem cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases quality of the collected                pulp tissue, reduces the more active cells in the                external environment and mix into the pulp tissue,                hence simplifies subsequent purification and                cultivation of dental pulp stem cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Device: The experimental                  instrument comprises extraction device table,                  where the upper end of the extraction device                  table is provided with a placement groove, an                  electric sliding table is fixedly connected to                  the placement groove, an electric slider is                  slidably connected to the left and right ends of                  the electric sliding table, a support block is                  fixedly connected to an upper end of the electric                  slider and two ends of the two supporting blocks                  are fixedly connected with a clamping plate. An                  electric drill is fixedly connected to an inner                  end of the extracting device table, a drill bit                  is fixedly connected to an upper end of the                  electric drill, and an pulp tissue collecting                  mechanism is arranged at an outer end of the                  electric drill. The pulp tissue collecting                  mechanism comprises a semi-annular slider,                  vertical block and limiting block. The                  semi-annular slider is connected with the                  electric drill. The left end of the semi-annular                  slider is cut with a vertical slot, the vertical                  block is slidably connected with the vertical                  slot, and the lower end wall of the vertical                  block (803) is fixedly connected to the limiting                  block. The limiting block is slidably connected                  to the electric drill, the limiting block and the                  semi-annular slider are matched in shape, and the                  upper end of the vertical block is fixedly                  connected with several tooth blocks. Two                  auxiliary blocks are fixedly connected to the                  left end of the semi-annular slider, and a                  rotating rod is rotatably connected between the                  two auxiliary blocks. The outer end of the                  rotating rod is drilled with several slots. The                  tooth groove and the tooth block are matched with                  each other, and the connecting rodis fixedly                  connected to one end of the rotating rod away                  from the electric drill. A collecting box is                  fixedly connected to one end of the connecting                  plate away from the rotating rod. The outer end                  of the collection box is opened with a groove. A                  storage plate is fixedly connected to an upper                  end of the semi-annular slider. A cell storage                  box is cut away from one end of the storage plate                  away from the electric drill. The cell storage                  box and the collection box are matched to each                  other. A sealing ring is fixedly connected to one                  end of the cell storage box near the groove, and                  the sealing ring and the groove are matched with                  each other. The inside of the collecting box can                  be sealed without being used under the action of                  the sealing ring, the inner wall of the groove is                  kept clean, and the inner wall of the groove is                  reduced from the external environment. The card                  block is a plastic material. The card block has                  an interference fit with the limit block. The                  upper and lower moving positions of the limiting                  block can be controlled to a certain extent under                  the interference fit of the block and the                  semi-annular slider and the clamping block is a                  plastic material. The limit block can be                  vertically slid up and down along the electric                  drill through the block by causing the limit                  block to be subjected to a force in the vertical                  direction.

PT P
PN CN110117034-A
TI Centrifugally extracting and recycling            high-concentration phenol-containing wastewater by            pumping high concentration phenol-containing wastewater            into centrifugal extractors connected in series,            stripping, extracting and recovering phenol.
AU XIAO J
   CHENG H
   ZI S
   LIN W
   WANG W
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI SHENYANG INST AUTOMATIO (CAYA-C)
   SHENYANG AUTOMATION INST CAS (CAYA-C)
GA 201971577M
AB    NOVELTY - Centrifugally extracting and recycling                high-concentration phenol-containing wastewater,                involves (a) pumping high concentration                phenol-containing wastewater into a centrifugal                extractors connected in series, (b) flowing out the                wastewater from the discharge port of the terminal                centrifugal extractor, and flowing back the                extractant through a pump to centrifugal extractors                comprising the wastewater, (c) flowing an                extractant out from the discharge port of the                extractor, extracting, pumping extraction phase to                the extractor of a stripping section, and                introducing raffinate phase wastewater to the                subsequent treatment device or directly                discharging, (d) passing the stripping solution                into the extractor filled with the extracting phase                by a countercurrent flow, stripping, and recovering                the stripped extracting agent and phenol salt, (e)                reusing the stripped extractant, and extracting the                wastewater again, (f) introducing carbon dioxide                gas to the stripped phenolate, and recovering                phenol.
   USE - The method is useful for centrifugally                extracting and recycling high-concentration                phenol-containing wastewater. The recovered phenol                salt is used as raw material for phenolic resin                production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has greater than or equal to 98%                phenol extraction rate, and the phenol content in                the wastewater drops below 970 mg/l, reduces the                pressure of subsequent biochemical treatment, and                has cost effective extractant system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The high-concentration                  phenol-containing wastewater is produced by a                  phenol resin production or resin oil production                  plant, having a phenol content of 40000-88300                  mg/l, a chemical oxygen demand of 200000-250000                  mg/l, and a pH of 0.6-2. Preferred Device: The                  series of connected centrifugal extractors is                  one-three centrifugal extractors are connected in                  series. The centrifugal extractor step (a) and                  (b) is an annular centrifugal extractor with a                  rotation speed of 2500-7000 rpm, a separation                  factor of 200-1100, and a flow ratio of                  1:100-100:1. Preferred Conditions: In step (c),                  the extraction order is n which is equal to 1-3,                  the extraction time is 3-5 minutesx n, and the                  centrifugal extractor rotation speed is 3000-5000                  rpm, and phase ratio of the extractant and                  wastewater is 1:1-1:6 and has a two-phase flux of                  less than or equal to 70 ml/minute. In step (d),                  the centrifugal extractor rotation speed is                  3000-5000 rpm, and has a flux of the stripping                  extract and extract phase is less than or equal                  to 70 ml/minute, and the volume ratio of the                  stripping solution to the extract phase is                  1:10-15 and the stripping grade is 1-2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The stripping                  solution comprises sodium hydroxide aqueous                  solution, ammonia or potassium hydroxide having a                  concentration of 5-20 %mass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The extracting agent                  comprises less than or equal to 30 vol.%                  complexing agent comprising tributyl phosphate,                  less than or equal to 70 vol.% modifier                  comprising n-octanol and less than or equal to 70                  vol.% diluent comprising kerosene.

PN CN110041289-A; CN110041289-B
TI Method for synthesizing buprofezin involves providing N-chloromethyl-N-phenylcarbamoyl chloride, obtaining by-product by cyclization reaction, re-acquiring acetate to be reused as acid binding agent after adding alkali to acetic acid.
AU ZHAO J
   LIU Z
   MA H
   SUN Y
   WANG C
   TIAN Q
   LIU J
   CHEN B
   QIAO Z
AE BEIJING NUTRICHEM LAB CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU CHANGLONG AGROCHEMICALS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019660309
AB    NOVELTY - The method for synthesizing buprofezin involves providing raw material such as N-chloromethyl-N-phenylcarbamoyl chloride and N-tert-butyl-N'-isopropylthiourea, and the acid binding agent is mainly composed of acetate, and the cyclization reaction gives a buprofezin. The acid binding agent further comprises a hydroxide, and the hydroxide is preferably sodium hydroxide, obtaining the by-product by the cyclization reaction, re-acquiring the acetate to be reused as an acid binding agent after adding alkali to acetic acid.
   USE - Method for synthesizing buprofezin.
   ADVANTAGE - The synthesis method adopts sodium acetate instead of ammonium hydrogen carbonate as the base required for synthesizing the buprofezin, and solves the problem of solvent loss caused by the release of carbon dioxide generated by ammonium bicarbonate in the process of synthesizing the product. The by-product sodium chloride can be directly filtered and removed, and can be recycled to form certain economic benefits, while reducing the energy consumption generated by wastewater treatment, achieving zero discharge of wastewater, and the average yield of preparation of buprofezin is more than 86%, the finished buprofezin content of 97.0% or more.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acetate is at least one of potassium acetate and sodium acetate. The reaction solvent is dichloroethane or toluene, preferably toluene. Preferred Composition: The acid binding agent is previously formulated as a mixed aqueous solution having a pH between 7.5 and 12, preferably between pH 8.5 and 9. The mass ratio of N-chloromethyl-N-phenylcarbamoyl chloride to toluene is 1:(2-6). The mass ratio of N-chloromethyl-N-phenylcarbamoyl chloride to toluene is 1:3. Preferred Process: The preparation of mixed aqueous solution involves mixing acetic acid with a base at 20-35 degrees C, detecting the pH, and obtaining aqueous solution by stirring and filtering. The acid binding agent is added dropwise in a mixed system of N-chloromethyl-N-phenylcarbamoyl chloride, N-tert-butyl-N'-isopropylthiourea and a reaction solvent. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of cyclization reaction is between 30-50 degrees C, and the time is 2-7 hours.

GA 201963611H

A gas generator is provided outside the suction port in order to generate a gas that attracts flying insects. The fan main portion is alternately rotated and stopped. PN BR102017026857-A2
TI Obtaining a biocurative, comprises isolating mesenchymal stem cells, synthesizing poly (1-lactic acid), electrophilizing poly (1-lactic acid), and cultivating stromal mesenchymal stem cells in the biocurative.
AU MANZINI B M
   MALHEIROS LUZO A C
   MUSSI R K
   D'AVILA M A
AE UNIV CAMPINAS ESTADUAL UNICAMP (UNES-C)
GA 2019623957
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining a biocurative, comprises: (a) isolating mesenchymal stem cells; (b) synthesizing poly (1-lactic acid); (c) electrophilizing poly (1-lactic acid); and (d) cultivating stromal mesenchymal stem cells in the biocurative.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining biocurative which is useful in surgery, preferably thoracic surgery for the repair of tubular organs e.g. airways undergoing resection for the purpose of replacing a small area removed or even in minor repairs e.g. tracheoesophageal or tracheocutaneous fistulas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides the biocurative that allows the regeneration and replacement of injured tissue, thus recovering its normal morphology and functionality and improves the function of the injured organ; utilizes the electrophilic poly(lactic acid) (PLLA) blanket with cultured adipose derived stromal mesenchymal stem cells, in the human body, PLLA breaks down into lactic acid, leading to water and carbon dioxide production via the citric acid cycle and does not require removal, which makes process costs feasible and improves patient's quality of life; and provides the biocurative that provides complete tissue regeneration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a biocurative obtained according to the method, comprising electrophilic poly (1-lactic acid) and cultured stromal mesenchymal stem cells obtained from human adipose tissue from normal individuals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The mesenchymal stem cells are obtained from the human adipose tissue of normal individuals. The poly (1-lactic acid) is produced by the reaction of the cyclic diester of the L-lactic acid conducted by mass polymerization with the addition of the catalyst tin(II) 2-ethylhexanoate, at a monomer/catalyst ratio of 4400. The mixture is frozen in liquid nitrogen, followed by the production of vacuum, immersion in an oil bath (130 degrees C for 24 hours), dissolved in chloroform, chloroform, precipitated in methanol and dried in a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C for 8 hours. The fibers prepared with two equal solutions with the same concentration of poly(lactic acid) in a mixture of chloroform and acetone form a tangle of homogeneous and stable fibers of diameter of the order of 10 mu m, with a regular diameter between them and nanopores that contribute to cell adhesion. The polymer is subjected to the electrophilization process by producing a solution of 1 g of the solubilized polymer in 5 g of chloroform and 1 g of acetone.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.    ADVANTAGE - The material has fatigue resistance, creep resistance, wear resistance, impact resistance and excellent performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing 3D printed material, comprising (i) dissolving potassium dihydrogen phosphate in polymethyl acrylate to obtain mixed solution; (ii) vacuuming to pressure of 0.05-0.1 MPa, injecting mixed gas of nitrogen and carbon dioxide, adding the polysiloxane and the expanded graphite, stirring and mixing, stirring at 40-60 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, and making semi-finished products; (iii) heating the semi-finished product to 60-80 degrees C, adding the glyoxal droplets and the chloroauric acid solution into the semi-finished product, and reacting for 10-20 minutes; and (iv) cooling the mixed solution to room temperature, adding the organic titanate and adhesive, stirring the mixture and sealing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic titanate includes isopropyl titanate, tetrabutyl titanate or tetra-methyl titanate. The adhesive includes hydroxypropyl cellulose, methyl cellulose, hydroxypropyl cellulose, or sodium carboxymethyl cellulose. The volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 5-20%. Preferred Method: The mixture is stirred and mixed uniformly through a mixer and the speed of the mixer is 1600 revolutions/minute and the stirring time is 20 minutes.

PT P
PN CN109810279-A; CN109810279-B
TI Preparing a high dielectric property polymer-based            composite microporous material comprises e.g. preparing            gamma-aminopropyltriethoxysilane modified multi-walled            carbon nanotube aqueous dispersion, and ultrasonically            dispersing.
AU MA Z
   WEI E
   MA J
   SHAO L
   KANG S
   DONG D
   NIU J
   JI Z
   WEI A
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
   UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 201948803K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a high dielectric property                polymer-based composite microporous material                comprises (i) preparing gamma                -aminopropyltriethoxysilane modified multi-walled                carbon nanotube aqueous dispersion; (ii) preparing                graphene oxide (GO) using improved Hummers method,                dispersing resulting GO in deionized water,                ultrasonically dispersing to obtain a negatively                charged GO aqueous dispersion; (iii) preparing a                graphene oxide-coated multi-walled carbon nanotube                solid powder according to results obtained in steps                (i) and (ii); (iv) preparing                polyetherimide/graphene oxide-coated multi-walled                carbon nanotube composition sheet; (v) preparing a                composition/carbon dioxide (CO2) mixed sheet                according to the result obtained in the step (iv);                (vi) taking out the obtained composition                material/CO2 mixed sheet, transferring to constant                temperature silicone oil bath for foaming, the                foaming temperature is 180-220 degrees C, the                foaming time is 5-60 seconds, and placing in an ice                water bath, cooling and setting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a high                dielectric property polymer-based composite                microporous material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts a supercritical CO2 fluid                as a physical foaming agent to introduce                microporous structure into composition material,                and re-orients the carbon nanomaterial to form                large amount of microcapacitors to improve                dielectric properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) is                  specifically comprises performing surface                  modification of hydroxylated multi-walled carbon                  nanotubes with gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane,                  centrifugally washing and freeze-drying to obtain                  gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane modified                  multi-walled carbon nanotubes, dispersing the                  obtained gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane                  modified multi-walled carbon nanotubes in                  deionized water, and adding hydrochloric acid                  solution in dropwise to adjust the pH to 4 to                  obtain a positively charged gamma                  -aminopropyltriethoxysilane multi-walled carbon                  nanotube aqueous dispersion liquid. The step                  (iii) is specifically comprises dispersions                  simultaneously added in dropwise to the beaker                  under ultrasonic agitation after the addition,                  the mixture is placed in an ice bath and                  ultrasonic stirring was continued for 30 minutes                  and performing centrifugal separation, washing,                  freeze-drying to obtain a graphene-coated                  multi-walled carbon nanotube solid powder, the                  mass ratio of GO and gamma                  -aminopropyltriethoxysilane modified multi-walled                  carbon nanotubes is 1:1. The step (iv) is                  specifically comprises ultrasonically dissolving                  and dispersing the polyetherimide particles and                  graphene oxide-coated multi-walled carbon                  nanotube solid powder obtained in the step (iii)                  in N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, then mixing                  polyetherimide solution and graphene oxide-coated                  multi-walled carbon nanotube dispersion and                  ultrasonic for 10 minutes, and then the mixture                  is injected into cold water with a needle tube to                  perform anti-solvent precipitation to obtain a                  flocculent composite material, and then                  polyetherimide/graphene oxide-coated multi-walled                  carbon nanotube composition sheet is obtained by                  hot press molding. The step (v) is specifically                  comprises placing the obtained                  polyetherimide/graphene oxide-coated multi-walled                  carbon nanotube composite sheet in an autoclave,                  heating and passing supercritical CO2 fluid, the                  CO2 is diffused into the polyetherimide/graphene                  oxide-coated multi-walled carbon nanotube                  composite sheet, the saturation temperature is                  30-100 degrees C, the saturation pressure is                  10-20 MPa, the saturation time is 12 hours, and                  the composition/CO2 mixed sheet is obtained after                  complete saturation. Preferred Components: In the                  step (iv), the content of graphene oxide-coated                  multi-walled carbon nanotubes in                  polyetherimide/graphene oxide-encapsulated                  multi-walled carbon nanotube composition sheet is                  0.5-12 wt.%.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.)

PN RU2688662-C1
TI METHOD OF CATALYTIC CRACKING OF BUTANE-BUTYLENE FRACTION AND CATALYST FOR ITS IMPLEMENTATION.
AU POTAPENKO O V
   DORONIN V P
   SOROKINA T P
   ALTYNKOVICH E O
AE AS SIBE BORESKOV CATALYSIS INST (ASBO-Non-standard)
GA 201951155J
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the refining and petrochemical industries, and in particular to a method for processing industrial butane-butylene fraction and producing a catalyst for the implementation of this method. Proposed cracking catalyst of the butane-butylene fraction includes phosphorus-modified zeolite ZSM-5 with a Si/Al ratio from 40 to 150 and a matrix containing aluminum oxide, and moreover, the zeolite ZSM-5 contains from 0.5 to 2.0 wt. % of phosphorus, the matrix contains in its composition kaolin clay, in the following ratio of components in the catalyst, wt. %: phosphorus-modified zeolite ZSM-5 4050; aluminum oxide 2050; kaolin clay 2035. Proposed method of cracking the butane-butylene fraction involves feeding the butane-butylene fraction to a fixed bed reactor with a weight feed rate of 2.55.0 h-1, at a temperature of 550600C. In the claimed method, the butane-butylene fraction is diluted with an inert gas, which can be steam, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, in a volume ratio of the butane-butylene fraction:inert gas 1:(15), while the cracking of the butane-butylene fraction is carried out on the proposed catalyst.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Increasing the selectivity of the conversion of the butane-butylene fraction to ethylene and propylene.

PT P
PN CN109761366-A
TI Preparation of composite plant floating bed involves taking raw materials including cement and filler, separately mixing, stirring, pouring precursor into the mold, oven-drying, placing body in muffle furnace and calcining.
AU LEI J
AE LEI J (LEIJ-Individual)
GA 2019464510
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of composite plant floating bed comprises taking cement, filler, activated alumina, silicon powder, crushed corn granules, urea, diammonium hydrogen phosphate, polycarboxylic acid superplasticizer, retarder boric acid and deionized water, separately mixing, stirring to obtain precursor, pouring precursor into the mold, oven-drying at 120-160 degrees C for 2-4 hours to obtain green body, placing body in muffle furnace, calcining, cooling to room temperature to obtain planting basket and bundling planting baskets in grid format with polyester rope.
   USE - The method is used for preparing composite plant floating bed.
   ADVANTAGE - The product uses bentonites with strong hygroscopicity and can adsorb water and zeolites with uniform micropores of porous crystal aluminosilicate metal salt which have physical adsorption properties, adopts carbonation treatment technology to infiltrate carbon dioxide into the surface layer of concrete, reacts with alkaline material produced by hydration of cement to form carbonate and water, reduces alkalinity of concrete, uses activated alumina with large surface area, strong adsorption capacity, high catalytic activity and has significant effect on removal of organic matter in water, especially carboxylic acid, and has good adsorption properties for fluorine, arsenic and phosphorus plasma in water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The filler is prepared by mixing zeolite molecular sieve and modified bentonite at mass ratio of 1:5, kneading at 120-140 degrees C for 1-2 hours to obtain kneaded product, subjecting to heat preservation and pressure treatment, and cooling to room temperature. The mixture of raw materials is carried out by mixing cement, filler, crushed corn granules, urea, diammonium hydrogen phosphate and deionized water, stirring at speed of 120-160 revolutions/minute for 30-50 minutes to obtain mixed slurry, adding activated alumina, silicon powder, polycarboxylic acid superplasticizer and retarder boric acid to mixed slurry, and stirring for 1-2 hours. The calcination step is done at 600-700 degrees C for 30-60 minutes. The modified bentonite is prepared by mixing sodium bentonite and deionized water, magnetically stirring at rotation speed of 80-100 revolutions/minute for 20-30 minutes to obtain suspension, add cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to suspension, shaking at room temperature, cooling to room temperature, filtering to obtain filter residue, washing with deionized water for 2-3 times, drying at 90-100 degrees C to constant weight and crushing through 100-140 mesh sieve. The heat preservation and pressure holding treatment is done by placing kneaded material under pressure of 8-10 MPa at 170-180 degrees C for 10-16 minutes. The cetyltrimethylammonium bromide is added to suspension at 60-70 degrees C for 6-12 hours. Preferred Composition: The raw materials are 40-50 pts. wt. cement, 10-15 pts. wt. filler, 5-15 pts. wt. activated alumina, 1-5 pts. wt. silicon powder, 20-25 pts. wt. crushed corn granules, 1-10 pts. wt. urea, 1-10 pts. wt. diammonium hydrogen phosphate, 0.5-0.8 pt. wt. polycarboxylic acid superplasticizer, 0.4-0.6 pt. wt. retarder boric acid and 80-100 pts. wt. deionized water. The mass ratio of sodium bentonite and deionized water is 2:25. The mass ratio of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide and suspension is 1:10.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC A10-E05B; A12-R01A; A12-W11J; D04-A01F1; D04-A01F3; D04-A01P1; D04-B06; D04-B07; D05-A03
IP C02F-003/32; C02F-003/34; C02F-003/10
