PN CN109745514-A
TI Antibacterial and antiviral composition used for            preparing pharmaceutical, disinfectant, cosmetic,            health care or hygiene product, comprises ginger            extract, myrobalan extract, agrimony extract, gallnut            extract and Phyllanthus extract.
AU ZHANG B
   LI H
   ZHANG W
AE ZHUHAI BAODE RUNSHENG HEALTH TECHNOLOGY (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 2019445696
AB    NOVELTY - Antibacterial and antiviral composition                comprises ginger extract, myrobalan extract,                agrimony extract, gallnut extract, Phyllanthus                extract, malefern extract, pokeberry root extract,                clove extract, cinnamon extract, wild Chrysanthemum                extract, Eucalyptus leaf extract, Forsythia                extract, Stemona extract, Geranium extract, rhizoma                atractylodis extract, mugwort extract, lavender                extract, patchouli extract, turmeric extract and/or                peppermint extract.
   USE - Antibacterial and antiviral composition used                for preparing pharmaceutical, disinfectant,                cosmetic, health care or hygiene product                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The antibacterial and antiviral composition                has components with good synergistic effect, and                the significant elimination effect on pathogenic                bacteria and viruses existing in the skin and air,                without stimulation and side effects, and easy                obtained sources, simple preparation and suitable                for mass production and environment friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the antibacterial and antiviral                composition, which involves:
   (A) weighing the raw materials, adding singly                extracting or mixed extracting solvent with                different concentration, condensing the extracting                liquid to obtain crude extract, or further using                aqueous extracting-alcohol precipitating method,                organic solvent extraction, column chromatography,                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, or steam                distillation to obtain the refined extract as raw                material extract; and
   (B) weighing the raw material extract, and                adding or not adding auxiliary material to obtain                the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: Antibacterial and                  antiviral composition comprises ginger extract                  solution, agrimony extract, Phyllanthus extract,                  malefern extract, rhizoma cyrtomii extract                  liquid, pokeberry root extract, cinnamon extract,                  wild Chrysanthemum extract, Eucalyptus extract,                  Stemona extract, Geranium extract, rhizoma                  atractylodis oil, mugwort extract oil, cinnamon                  oil, lavender oil, wild Chrysanthemum oil,                  patchouli oil, Eucalyptus oil, turmeric oil                  and/or peppermint oil. The humectant is glycerin,                  propylene glycol, 1,3-butanediol, dipropylene                  glycol, sorbitol and sorbitol-20, amide                  humectant, glycerin polyether humectant, lactic                  acid and sodium lactate, sodium pyrrolidone                  carboxylate, hydroxyethyl urea, erythritol,                  sodium hyaluronate, saccharide isomers,                  D-panthenol, polyethylene glycol, oat                  beta-glucan, trehalose, allantoin, silk serine,                  hydrolyzed soy protein, hydrolyzed rice bran                  protein, hexanediol, xylitol, polypropylene                  glycol, chitin and/or betaine. The thickener is                  carbomer, xanthan gum, gum arabic, tragacanth,                  locust bean gum, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose,                  hydroxypropylmethyl cellulose, methyl cellulose,                  hydroxyethyl cellulose, acrylate copolymer,                  glycerol polymethacrylate polymer, polyvinyl                  alcohol, ammonium acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP                  copolymer, hydroxyethyl acrylate/sodium                  acryloyldimethyltaurate copolymer,                  polyisobutylene, magnesium sulfate, and/or                  acrylate/10-30C alkanol acrylate crosspolymer.                  The emulsifier is methyl glucose sesquistearate,                  PEG-20 methyl glucoside sesquistenate, stearyl                  ether-2, stearyl ether-21, cetearyl alcohol-2,                  cetearyl ether-21, eicosyl glycol ether-25,                  glyceryl stearate, Tween-80, Tween-60, Tween-20,                  whale wax hard lipo-glucoside/cetitol stearyl                  alcohol, glyceryl stearate, 20-22C alkyl                  alcohol/20C alkyl glucoside, sodium stearyl                  glutamate, sucrose polystearate/hydrogenated                  polyisobutylene, polyglycerol-3/cetitol oleyl                  oleate, acetylated monoglyceride, lanolin,                  hydrogenated castor oil, and/or palmitol.                  Preferred Compositions: It contains 0.01-50 pts.                  wt.rhizoma cyrtomii extract liquid 0.01-50 pts.                  wt.Eucalyptus oil, 0.01-50 pts. wt.wild                  Chrysanthemum oil, 0.001-20 pts. wt.peppermint,                  0.1-100 pts. wt. cinnamon extract, 0.0041-50 pts.                  wt. cinnamon, 0.001-50 pts. wt. Eucalyptus oil,                  0.001-20 pts. wt.pokeberry root                  extractingsolution, 0.01-50 pts. wt. commercial                  extract, 0.001-20 pts. wt. Eucalyptus oil,                  0.001-20 pts. wt. Chrysanthemum oil and 0.001-10                  pts. wt. cinnamon. Preferred Conditions: It is an                  external preparation, formulated as a solution,                  powder, lotion, oil, tincture, gel, emulsion,                  ointment, paste, plaster, liniment, paint or                  aerosol. Preferred Processes: The solution                  composition prepartion involves weighing the raw                  material, extracting by water, concentrating, and                  centrifuging to obtain the supernatant A, adding                  the supernatant A slowly with ethanol to reach                  the corresponding alcohol concentration, and                  precipitating the alcohol, centrifuging the                  supernatant B to recover the ethanol, and                  concentrating the raw material extract, weighing                  the raw material extract according to the formula                  amount, adding appropriate amount of water to                  dissolve, filtering, adding the humectant,                  thickener and purified water into the filtrate,                  heating, and obtaining A phase, weighing the raw                  material oil and emulsifier according to the                  formula amount, and stirring uniformly to obtain                  phase B, slowly adding phase A into phase B,                  stirring, homogenizing, continue stirring, and                  slowly cooling down.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Virucide.

PN CN109627015-A
TI Production method of new material ceramic plate, involves determining qualified ceramic product by placing formed ceramic product into pressure machine for pressure testing, in which semi-finished brick is formed by baking in furnace.
AU LI Z
AE LI Z (LIZZ-Individual)
GA 2019379871
AB    NOVELTY - The production method involves preparing (S1) ceramic raw material into the crusher for crushing, filtering (S2) crushed raw material into grinder to obtain grain size of 100 mesh ceramic particles, and ceramic particle and water rich in carbon dioxide are placed into processing mixing device to mix and form (S3) mud. The mud is poured into forming die and blown with blower to accelerate the forming (S4) of mold. The temperature in furnace raised to 1000 degrees centigrade and then semi-finished product brick is baked (S5). The formed ceramic product is placed in pressure machine for pressure test (S6) and to determined the qualified ceramic product.
   USE - Production method of new material ceramic plate.
   ADVANTAGE - Avoids rupture during high temperature roasting of ceramic semi-finished product plate, and ceramic plate pressure test, thus improving the qualified rate of the ceramic plate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the process flow diagram of the production method of new material ceramic plate.
   Ceramic raw material preparation step (S1)
   Crushed raw material filtering step (S2)
   Mud forming step (S3)
   Mold forming step (S4)
   Baking step (S5)
   Qualified ceramic product determination step (S6)

PT P
PN CN109569195-A
TI Method for removing methyl sulfide and methyl sulfide in gas by using ionic liquid, involves using ionic liquid as absorbent to remove methyl sulfide in gas by absorption method, where raw material gas enters from bottom of absorption tower.
AU LEI Z
   CHENG J
   DAI C
   WANG Z
   MU M
   YU G
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201933627T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using an ionic liquid as an absorbent to remove methyl sulfide in a gas by an absorption method. The raw material gas enters from the bottom of the absorption tower. The ionic liquid is added from the top of the tower, and the bottom of the tower. The ionic liquid that has absorbed methyl sulfide and methyl sulfide passes through a gas-liquid separator at normal temperature and normal pressure to remove a small amount of raw material gas entrained in the ionic liquid. The residual liquid is desorbed into the ionic liquid through the flash tank. The dimethyl sulfide, dimethyl disulfide, ionic liquid collected at the bottom of the flash tank is recycled. The raw material gas is nitrogen, air or carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for removing methyl sulfide and methyl sulfide in a gas by using an ionic liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - Ensuring to avoid environmental pollution, and also ensures low investment and low energy consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for removing dimethyl sulfide and dimethyl disulfide from a gas under high pressure by an ionic liquid.

   WU G
AE WUHAN TEMO WELDING CONSUMABLES CO LTD (CSHI-C)
GA 2019311364

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The smelting agent is prepared by smelting sodium carbonate glass powder, sodium-fluorine (NaF), feldspar and rutile to a mass ratio of 1:1:1:3-4. Preferred Process: The preparation of the smelting agent is carried out at melting temperature of 900-1000 degrees C, and the melting time is 1-3 hours. The mass fraction of the carbon element in the deposited metal of the flux-cored wire is 0.04-0.06%. The flux-cored wire has a cross-sectional diameter of 1.2-1.4mm. Preferred Compositions: The outer sheath is made of a low carbon steel strip, and the proportion of every component of the low carbon steel strip comprises less than or equal to 0.15-0.4 wt.% manganese, less than or equal to 0.025 wt.% silicon, less than or equal to 0.03 wt.% carbon, less than or equal to 0.015 wt.% sulfur, less than or equal to 0.012 wt.% phosphorus and less than or equal to 0.005 wt.% nitrogen.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a substrate (4) with two coplanar electrodes (6). A zinc oxide nanowire network (8) is formed on two electrodes by synthesizing zinc oxide nanowires with a liquid phase sequential growth process by preparing a solution of zinc chloride and hexamethylenetetramine dissolved into water, dispersing the synthetized nanowires in a solvent, drop-casting a solution containing the solvent and the zinc oxide nanowires on the electrodes and drying the solution at temperature inferior to 85 degree Celsius, where the zinc chloride and hexamethylenetetramine are equimolar.

PN CN109402054-A
TI Expanding and culturing cluster of differentiation 4 memory T lymphocytes comprises e.g. adding cluster of differentiation 4 T lymphocytes to culture flask coated with cluster of differentiation 3 antibody and resuspending in medium.
AU WEI Z
   LIN K
   HU R
   ZHANG R
AE SHENZHEN RUNKE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2019226450
AB    NOVELTY - Expanding and culturing cluster of differentiation 4 (CD4+) memory T lymphocytes comprises (i) adding CD4+ T lymphocytes to culture flask coated with cluster of differentiation 3 (CD3) antibody and CD28 antibody and resuspending in T lymphocyte medium, adding 5% inactivated autologous plasma of the total volume of liquid in the culture bottle and placing culture flask in an incubator at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) for 24 hours; (ii) adding cytokine IL-2, cytokine IL-7 and cytokine IL-21 to the culture flask and culturing culture flask in an incubator at 37 degrees C and 5% CO2; and (iii) adding inactivated autologous plasma to culture bottle every two days while culturing, adding T lymphocyte medium of cytokine IL-2, cytokine IL-7 and cytokine IL-21, where volume of inactivated autologous plasma in T lymphocyte medium is 5%.
   USE - The method is useful for expanding and culturing CD4+ memory T lymphocytes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can induce sufficient expansion of required cell volume to meet the demand for in vitro expansion culture of the T lymphocytes, can reduce the amount of peripheral blood collected, is economical, safe and reliable and simple in operation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The total culture duration of expansion culture is 14 days. In the step (ii) and step (iii) adding cytokine IL-7 and cytokine IL-21 to cytokine IL-2 in culture flask, and final concentrations of cytokine IL-2, cytokine IL-7 and cytokine IL-21 is 500-1000 IU/ml, 300-1000 IU/ml and 200-1000 IU/ml respectively. In the step (iii) adjusting cell density to 0.5-1.2x 106/ml each time T lymphocyte medium is added. The coating method of coated CD3 antibody and CD28 antibody culture flask comprises pouring coating solution containing CD3 antibody and CD28 antibody into culture flask and incubating culture flask in 37 degrees C for 3 hours or at 4 degrees C for more than 8 hours. The method further comprises sorting CD4+ T lymphocytes from peripheral blood before step (i), (a) subjecting collected peripheral blood is subjected to heat treatment, centrifuging and separately collecting upper layer of plasma and the lower layer of concentrated blood cells and diluting concentrated blood cells with physiological saline to the original volume to be a blood dilution liquid; (b) adding Ficoll separation solution to the bottom of centrifuge tube and adding blood dilution solution to the upper layer of Ficoll separation solution to form an upper layer liquid and centrifuging white membrane layer in the centrifuge tube and collecting and washing white membrane layer with physiological saline and collecting by centrifugation to obtain peripheral blood mononuclear cells, where volume ratio of Ficoll separation solution to the blood dilution solution is 1:2; and (c) sorting CD4+ T lymphocytes from peripheral blood mononuclear cells. In the step (c) sorting CD4+ T lymphocytes in peripheral blood mononuclear cells using CD4+ magnetic beads. Preferred Composition: In the step (i) cell density of CD4+ T lymphocytes inoculated in culture flask is 0.5x 106/ml. The T lymphocyte medium is AIM-V medium of GIBCO.

GA 201909305E

PN CN109043207-A
TI Preparation of fully fermented feed by crushing            soyabean, millet, wheat, sorghum, corn, wheat, flower,            and corn flower, fermenting with Lactobacillus, and            soaking bream fish viscera powder and fermentation            product into processing liquid.
AU ZHAO X
AE JIESHOU WANGJI MAOMAO FAMILY FARM (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 201902328X
AB    NOVELTY - A fully fermented feed is prepared by                providing raw materials comprising 13-17 pts. wt.                soyabean, 7-9 pts. wt. millet, 8-14 pts. wt. wheat,                12-16 pts. wt. sorghum, 13-16 pts. wt. bream fish                visceral meal, 13-19 pts. wt. corn, 14-16 pts. wt.                wheat, and 12-15 pts. wt. platycodon grandiflorum                corn flower; crushing soyabean, millet, after                wheat, sorghum, corn, wheat, flower, corn flower                for 30-40 minutes to obtain mixed powder of 20-mesh                sieve; fermenting mixed powder and Lactobacillus in                the fermentation tank for 3-4 days, and taking out                to obtain primary fermentation product; soaking                bream fish viscera powder and primary fermentation                product into processing liquid, inletting carbon                dioxide into soaking liquid, soaking for 50-60                minutes, and filtering to obtain filter residue;                putting residue and Lactobacillus into silage kiln,                compacting, sealing, fermenting for 8-14 days, and                taking out to obtain secondary fermentation                product; and drying and granulating.
   USE - Method for preparation of fully fermented feed                for pigeon.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared feed has good palatability,                effectively improves ingestion rate and                digestibility, helps to promote healthy growth, and                increases young pigeon meat quality.

AE HUIZHOU DIANMING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4281X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an explosion-proof lithium battery, comprising a lithium battery main body, positive and negative electrode column, anti-explosion shell and an explosion-proof shell cover; the lithium battery main body is arranged in the anti-explosion shell, upper part of said explosion-proof shell opening and it is equipped with an explosion-proof shell cover; the flame-proof shell connected through an insulating sealing ring with the explosion-proof shell cover; said lithium battery main body by the fastening piece and inner surface of said anti-explosion shell connecting the lithium battery main body and the gap between the flame-proof shell is filled in when the heat energy absorbing reaction and produce carbon dioxide of carbonate-proof layer; the anti-explosive shell cover is provided with a blast hole, the blast hole is a blind hole, the bottom of the blast hole is provided with a bursting membrane, the rupture disk is provided with an annular groove. The beneficial effect of the utility model is: it greatly improves safety protection performance of the lithium battery, reduces the battery using safety risk.

PN CN108914252-A
TI Viscose-based activated carbon fiber felt preparation involves taking raw material viscose fiber felt, soaking in diammonium hydrogen phosphate solution, and then putting into industrial microwave oven.
AU CHEN T
   GE Y
   LI W
   CHENG B
   ZHAO T
AE JIANGSU SUTONG CARBON FIBER CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201898361J
AB    NOVELTY - Viscose-based activated carbon fiber felt preparation involves taking raw material viscose fiber felt, soaking in diammonium hydrogen phosphate solution in a ratio of 1:100 for 24 hours, and then putting into industrial microwave oven. The nitrogen gas flow replaces air from the furnace, and then sets the carbonization temperature of the microwave irradiation. The microwave oven is automatically turned off, where the nitrogen atmosphere is maintained until the furnace temperature drops to room temperature. The product carbon fiber felt is taken out and weighed to get dry product. The carbon fiber felt of the product is soaked in 1 liter water, boiled for 2 hours, and placed in an oven at 105 degrees C for drying, and then placed in an industrial microwave oven at a certain flow rate. The carbon dioxide gas flow replaces the air in the furnace for setting activation temperature of the microwave irradiation.
   USE - Method for preparing viscose-based activated carbon fiber felt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the viscose-based activated carbon fiber felt in a simple, precise, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with high yield and low energy consumption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic scanning electron microscopic image of the prepared viscose-based activated carbon fiber felt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The diammonium hydrogen phosphate solution concentration of 3-40 wt.%. The carbonization temperature of the microwave irradiation is adjusted at 350-700 degrees C. The microwave irradiation has a holding time of 1-30 min. The activation temperature of the microwave irradiation is 500-800 degrees C.

   Cylinder (1)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The cylinder has a container valve (12) containing fusible plug which melts at a temperature higher than a predetermined temperature. The ultrasonic generator has a container (21) for accommodating the liquid medium and an ultrasonic oscillator for applying ultrasonic wave to the cylinder through the liquid medium. The liquefied gas purifier further consists of a chiller (3) for adjusting the liquid medium to predetermined temperature and an analyzer (5) connected to the piping for analyzing the gaseous-phase component. The piping has a pressure-reduction valve (71).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ultrasonic wave is applied through a liquid medium (22). The gas discharge process and the ultrasonic processing method are performed simultaneously in parallel. Purification of liquefied gas further involves stopping the discharge process and the ultrasonic processing method, when the concentration of impurity in the gaseous-phase component is reduced to less than predetermined value, and analyzing the gaseous-phase component.

PT P
PN CN108101235-A; CN108101235-B
TI Biodegrading mineral oil in water, involves formulating mixed bacterial suspension of mineral oil degrading bacteria, adding to contaminated area, and adding microbial nutritional composition and biodegradation accelerator to degrade.
AU FANG J
   WU J
   ZHANG N
   YU K
   LIU P
   GU K
AE PLA GROUND FORCE SERVICE COLLEGE (PLAG-Non-standard)
GA 201844344X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for biodegrading mineral oil in water, involves (a) culturing and formulating a mixed bacterial suspension of mineral oil degrading bacteria, where the mineral oil degrading bacteria are selected and enriched from water with serious mineral oil pollution, and (b) adding the mixed bacterial suspension to the mineral oil contaminated area, and then adding microbial nutritional composition and biodegradation accelerator to naturally degrade for 10 days, where the biodegradation accelerator is an N-acyl glycinate, and the addition amounts of the mixed bacteria suspension, microbial nutritional composition and N-acyl glycinate are respectively 0.3-0.5 %mass, 0.4-0.6 %mass and 1-2 %mass in the contaminated area.
   USE - The method is useful for biodegrading mineral oil in water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method biodegrades mineral oil in water cost-effectively in an environmentally-friendly manner by utilizing synergistic compatibility between biodegradation accelerator, mixed bacterial suspension and microbial nutritional composition, and exhibits improved biodegradation efficiency and 100% mineral oil removal rate in polluted water in 10 days. The method is performed simply using cost-effective raw materials, and produces biodegradable product of mineral oil, preferably carbon dioxide and water, without any secondary pollution to the environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The mixed bacterial suspension is prepared by inoculating the mineral oil degrading bacteria in Luria-Bertani (LB) liquid culture medium, performing shake culture at 28-32 degrees C under stirring condition at 150-200 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 24-36 hours, centrifuging the obtained culture solution, discarding supernatant, collecting thalli, resuspending in sterile physiological saline with mass fraction of 0.85% to obtain single strain bacterial suspension with mass fraction of 20%, and mixing the suspensions of several single strains to obtain product. Preferred Components: The addition amounts of the mixed bacteria suspension, microbial nutritional composition and N-acyl glycinate are respectively 0.4 %mass, 0.5 %mass and 1.5 %mass in the contaminated area. The mineral oil degrading bacteria are Pseudomonas sp., Bordetella sp., Ochrobactrum sp. or P.aeruginosa. The N-acyl glycinate is sodium cocoyl glycinate, potassium cocoyl glycinate or cocoyl glycine triethanolamine salt. The microbial nutritional composition comprises 1-2 wt.%, preferably 1.5 wt.% sodium chloride, 6-7.5 wt.%, preferably 7.35 wt.% magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 7-7.5 wt.%, preferably 7.35 wt.% anhydrous calcium chloride, 1-2 wt.%, preferably 1.5 wt.% potassium chloride, 1-2 wt.%, preferably 1.5 wt.% ammonium nitrate, 7-8 wt.%, preferably 7.35 wt.% potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1-2 wt.%, preferably 1.73 wt.% oleoyl glycine, 1-2 wt.%, preferably 1.52 wt.% lauroyl alanine and dipotassium phosphate heptahydrate (remainder).
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Adsorption device useful for pyrolyzing, liquefying and co-producing of biomass, comprises a square tower body and a multi-layer drawer adsorption layer arranged in the tower body.
AU CHEN H
   TANG C
   CHEN Y
   YANG H
   WANG X
   SHAO J
   ZHANG X
   YANG Q
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201840270W
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorption device useful for pyrolyzing, liquefying and co-producing of biomass, comprises a square tower body and a multi-layer drawer adsorption layer arranged in the tower body. The bottom of the square tower is provided with a rough gas inlet, and a clean gas outlet is arranged at the top, the crude gas to be purified enters from the crude gas inlet. The adsorption of multi-layered drawer adsorption layer in the tower body is discharged from the clean gas outlet. The replacement chamber is also arranged at the position corresponding to the outside of the square tower body and the adsorption layer, when the adsorbent in the adsorption layer reaches the saturation state. The adsorption layer slides into the corresponding displacement chamber to realize online replacement of the adsorbent.
   USE - The adsorption device is useful for pyrolyzing, liquefying and co-producing of biomass (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes real-time on-line replacement of the adsorbent; absorbs raw fuel gas impurities e.g. carbon- monoxide and carbon-dioxide, CO2; has high adsorption and strong operability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the adsorption device for pyrolyzing, liquefying and co-producing of biomass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The adsorption layer has a square structure as a whole. The front, back and left sides of the square structure are in close contact with the inner wall of the tower body. The right side of the square structure is flush with the outer wall of the tower body. The adsorption layer includes a grid type bottom plate, a left sealing plate, and a right sealing plate. The left sealing plate and the right sealing plate are respectively connected to the left and right ends of the grid type bottom plate. The left seal plate and the right seal plate are connected by a connecting rod, and the left seal plate is in close contact with the inner wall of the tower. The right sealing plate is flush with the outer wall of the tower, and a pull rod is also arranged on the right sealing plate. The replacement chamber is adapted to the shape of the adsorption layer, which is composed of four sealing plates, three of which are connected to the outer wall of the tower. The other seal plate can be rotated open to facilitate replacement of the adsorbent in the adsorbed layer that has reached saturation. A chute for sliding the adsorption layer is arranged in the tower body. A concentration sensor is provided on the tower wall above the adsorption layer. A decontaminator is also provided above the uppermost adsorbent layer in the tower body.

AU SHIMIZU G K H
   EVANS D
   GABERT E
   GLAVINOVIC M
   HUYNH R
   PADEANU D
   PERRAS J
   TAYLOR J
   ZHANG J
AE UTI LP (UTIU-C)
GA 2022982054
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of chromium(III) phosphonate                metal-organic framework involves dehydrating a                hydrogen-bonded metal-organic framework (HMOF)                comprising chromium(III) hydrogen bonded to one or                more organic polyphosphonate molecules by heating                the HMOF at a controlled rate and cooling the                dehydrated HMOF.
   USE - Manufacture of chromium phosphonate                metal-organic framework used for uptake of at least                one substance such as hydrogen, methane, oxygen,                carbon dioxide and nitrogen or lithium-ion, for                purpose of storage, absorption, separation,                controlled release, chemical reaction or as                support, and for proton conducting solid for proton                exchange membrane fuel cells, and for ion                conducting solid, preferably lithium-ion conducting                solid for battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared chromium phosphonate                metal-organic framework has high chemical, thermal                and hydrolytic stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   method to uptake at least one substance, which                involves contacting a source comprising the at                least one substance with the MOF under conditions                for uptake of the at least one substance into the                MOF;
   MOF or HMOF, which further comprises at least                one substance or at least one gas; and
   proton conducting solid, which comprises the                MOF.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The controlled rate                  for heating is 0.01-500 K/minute. The HMOF is                  heated to 25-500&#176; C. The cooling rate is                  1-300 K/minute. Preferred Components: The HMOF                  comprises hexa aquachromium(III)chloride units                  forming hydrogen bonds to one or more organic                  polyphosphonic acids. The organic polyphosphonic                  acid molecule is an arylpolyphosphonic acid, a                  heteroarylphosphonic acid and/or a salt thereof.                  The chromium(III) salt is a nitrate, halide,                  tetrafluoroborate, sulfate, perchlorate, acetate,                  hexafluorophosphate, organosulfonate,                  organocarboxylate, carbonate, bicarbonate,                  bisulfate, hydrogenophosphate, or nitrite salt.                  The organic polyphosphonic acid molecule is                  preferably chosen from 50 compounds e.g.                  compounds of formulae (A)-(E). The organic                  polyphosphonic acid molecule, or its salts is of                  formula: H4L1, H6L2or H8L3. The HMOF comprises                  repeating unit of formula: {[Cr(H2O6)][HL1]                  (C3H6O)2}, {[Cr(H2O6)][H4.5L2]2(C8H10)1.5} or                  {[Cr(H2O6)][H2.L3](H2O)2}. The guest molecule or                  template molecule is chosen from acetone,                  acetonitrile, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol,                  acetic acid, ethylene glycol, ethyl acetate,                  nitrobenzene, nitromethane, toluene, ammonium                  compound, carbon dioxide, methane, hypophosphoric                  acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, hydrogen                  bromide, formic acid, carbonic acid, nitric acid,                  o-xylene, meta-xylene and para-xylene, and salts                  therof, preferably carbon dioxide or lithium                  salt. The solution comprising one or more organic                  polyphosphonate molecules is in acetone,                  acetonitrile, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol,                  acetic acid, ethylene glycol, water, or mixture                  thereof. The solution comprising a chromium(III)                  salt is in methanol, water, or mixture                  thereof.L =4,4'-biphenyldiphosphonate;L =1                  ,3,5-tris(4-phosphonophenyl)2,4,6-trimethylbenzene;andL =durenetetraphosphonate.Preferred Process: The HMOF is prepared by                  adding a solution comprising one or more organic                  polyphosphonic acid molecules and/or salts                  thereof to a solution comprising a chromium(III)                  salt to form a mixture, adding a guest molecule                  or template molecule to the mixture under                  conditions to provide the HMOF in which the guest                  molecule or template molecule is included                  therein. The method further involves after                  preparing the HMOF, collecting and drying the                  HMOF. The dehydrating is conducted by heating the                  HMOF under gas pressure. By heating under gas                  pressure, the resulting MOF forms pores that                  selectively entrap or capture the gas.

The shaft journal of the                generator rotor of the power station is machined by                a grinding machine.
   USE - Laser cladding repair method for damage to                rotor journal of power station generator applied in                technical field of generator journal repair (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cladding layer and the combined surface of                the base material are metallurgically combined                without air hole, impurity, micro-crack defect,                cladding layers with higher surface hardness,                wear-resistant, corrosion resistance, oxidation                resistance, and oxidation resistance after cladding                the shaft neck of the power station electric                generator.

PT P
PN WO2022058517-A1; EP4052582-A1
TI Preparing pulpy mass, by swelling grain,            pseudo-grain, legume and/or oilseed, wet-milling puffed            grain into pulp, first fermenting slurry using            acid-forming microorganisms, and second fermenting            pulpy mass.
AU BIENER J
AE BIENER J (BIEN-Individual)
   BIENER J (BIEN-Individual)
GA 202244269C
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for preparing pulpy mass involves                (a) swelling grain, pseudo-grain, legume and/or                oilseed without being germinated, (b) wet-milling                the puffed grain, pseudo-grain, legume and/or                oilseed into a pulp, (c) primary fermenting the                pulp using acid-forming microorganism, and (d)                secondary fermenting the fermented pulpy mass using                carbon dioxide (CO2)-forming microorganisms, where                the primary and secondary fermentation takes place                separately from one another in time and/or space,                and the consistency of the pulpy mass is retained                between the primary and secondary                fermentation.
   USE - The method (M1) is useful for preparing pulpy                mass, which is useful for making food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces a pulpy mass with                preferably high porosity and/or elasticity using                (pseudo)cereals, legumes and oilseeds. The mushy                mass is suitable for the production of a very                porous, very fluffy, non-sticky food.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a pulpy mass being prepared by                above-mentioned method (M1), where the pulpy mass                contains legume;
   (2) a method (M2) for making a food involves                cooking the pulpy mass; and
   (3) a food being prepared by above-mentioned                method (M2).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method (M1) involves                  (c-i) filling or transferring the primary                  fermented pulp into another vessel. The method                  (M1) further involves (e) mixing the secondary                  fermented pulpy mass, and (f) adding spices,                  salt, cooking oil, sweetener, dairy product, nut                  flour, shredded vegetables and/or moisture                  enhancers. The primary fermentation is performed                  for 8-14 hours. The secondary fermentation is                  performed for 15-25 minutes. The method involves                  using at least 25% legume. The method does not                  utilize artificial leavening agent i.e. baking                  powder, during any step. Preferred Component: The                  grain is rice, wheat, spelt, oat, barley, rye,                  corn or millet. The pseudo-grain is buckwheat,                  sesame, amaranth, quinoa or chia. The legume is                  lentil, bean, pea, peanut, chickpea, soya or                  sweet lupine. The oilseed is pumpkin seed,                  linseed, hemp seed or poppy seed. The pulpy mass                  contains (i) legume and grain, (ii) legume and                  pseudo-grain, (iii) legume and oilseed, (iv)                  legume, grain and pseudo-grain, (v) legume,                  pseudo-grain and oilseed, (vi) legume, grain and                  oilseed, or (vii) legume, cereal, pseudo-grain                  and oilseed.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D11 (Baking - including bakery products, flour, doughs, bakery ovens, dough transporting and/or handling equipment, pies and pasta, but not flour milling (A21).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

Besides, it comprises valves with control system to switch adsorbers from cleaning mode to regeneration mode and back, and regeneration unit. It contains throttle with pipeline for supply of cleaned air to regenerated adsorber, compressor, high pressure receiver. PT P
PN CN114032356-A
TI Low-cost composite pre-deoxidation method for molten steel, involves adding coke powder in tapping process with oxygen in molten steel reaction, adding deoxidation alloy, and adding calcium carbide on slag surface.
AU JI W
   LI S
   WU J
   CHEN C
   LIANG Y
   SUN J
   ZHAO M
   XUE Y
   TONG S
   WANG X
   LIU J
AE SHANDONG IRON & STEEL GROUP RIZHAO CO (JGJT-C)
GA 202234006H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding coke powder when the converter taps 1/3, so that the coke powder is reacted with the oxygen in the molten steel during the tapping process, and 1-2 minutes after the coke powder is added, the deoxidized alloy is added. The 20-30kg of coke powder is added on the slag surface before the molten steel reaches the treatment position of the LF refining furnace, turn on the bottom blowing argon gas and carry out stirring to pre-deoxidize the oxygen in the slag. The 20-40kg of calcium carbide is added to the slag surface, when the molten steel reaches the treatment position of the LF refining furnace, and the slag is deoxidized by stirring. The 20-40kg calcium carbide is added again to deoxidize the oxygen in the slag again, if the slag turns black during the slagging process of the LF refining furnace.
   USE - Low-cost composite pre-deoxidation method for molten steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The deoxidation product is carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide, the cleanliness of molten steel is effectively promoted and good conditions for product quality improvement is provided.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

The ruthenium                  tetroxide-containing gas generation inhibitor and                  the ruthenium-containing waste liquid treatment                  agent further contain bromide ions, bromite ions,                  bromate ions and chlorine-containing ions. The                  oxidizing agent is selected from hypochlorite                  ions and/or ozone. The chlorine-containing ions                  are selected from chlorite ions, chlorate ions                  and/or chloride ions. The pH buffering agent is                  carbonic acid, boric acid, phosphoric acid,                  trishydroxymethyl aminomethane, ammonia,                  pyrophosphate, p-phenolsulfonic acid,                  diethanolamine, ethanolamine, triethanolamine,                  5,5-diethylbarbituric acid, glycine,                  glycylglycine, imidazole,                  N,N-bis(2-hydroxyethyl)-2-aminoethanesulfonic                  acid, 3-morpholinopropanesulfonic acid,                  N-tris(hydroxymethyl)methyl-2-aminoethanesulfonic                  acid,                  2-(4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazinyl)ethanesulfonic                  acid, 4-(2-hydroxyethyl)-1-piperazinyl                  propanesulfonic acid, tricin,                  N,N-di(2-hydroxyethyl)glycine,                  2-cyclohexylaminoethanesulfonic acid,                  hydroxyproline, phenol and/or EDTA. Preferred                  Properties: The redox potential of the oxidizing                  agent exceeds the redox potential of the                  hypobromite ions/bromide ions. The concentration                  of hypobromite ions is 0.001-0.2 mol/L. The                  concentration of pH buffering agent is 0.00001-1                  mol/L.    YANG Y
   YANG X
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2021E1097L
AB    NOVELTY - A titanium dioxide/coal-based porous film foam carbon composite material is prepared by separating coal sample to obtain sparse medium group, pressing into blocks, putting into tubular furnace, performing carbonization treatment under inert gas protection, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain foam carbon, putting into high temperature tubular activation furnace for activating with activating gas water vapor or carbon dioxide at 700-950degrees Celsius for 10-180 minutes to obtain multi-level hole film foam carbon, impregnating with titanium dioxide suspension, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing titanium dioxide/coal-based porous film foam carbon composite material for photocatalytic degradation of organic pollutants in waste water and/or waste gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and has low cost. The material has high degradation efficiency and good circulation, and exerts catalytic activity of photocatalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a titanium dioxide/coal-based porous film foam carbon composite material comprising foam carbon and titanium dioxide loaded on foam carbon. The foam carbon has large hole, middle hole and micro hole. The middle hole rate is 10-40%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves before pressing, using washing solvent to wash separated sparse medium group at room temperature, drying and grinding to 100-200 meshes. Carbonization treatment is done at 400-800degrees Celsius at heating rate of 1-10degrees Celsius/minute, keeping temperature for 1-5 hours and naturally cooling to room temperature. The titanium dioxide suspension is prepared by dissolving nano-titanium dioxide in ethanol, isopropanol, ethylene glycol or methanol solution, stirring for 10-120 minutes and ultrasonically dispersing for 10-60 minutes. The multi-level hole film foam carbon is equal volume impregnated or excess impregnation method.Preferred Components: The specific surface area of foam carbon is 200-600 m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanium dioxide is 10-60 wt.% titanium dioxide.

TI Preparing graphite aluminum composite material useful in lithium ion battery anode material, comprises mixing graphite worm or nano carbon powder and organic solvent, dispersing to obtain nano carbon slurry, and adding raw material powder.
AU SUN J
AE XIAN RONGXI TECHNOLOGY NEW MATERIAL CO (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A77159
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphite aluminum composite material comprises (i) adding graphite worm or nano-carbon powder and organic solvent into a sealed pressure hydrocinning kettle, mixing at the speed of not less than 9000 revolutions/second, and dispersing to obtain the nano-carbon slurry, (ii) adding the raw material powder in the nano-carbon slurry prepared in the step (i), graphite powder or nano-carbon powder, zinc powder chromium powder, zirconium powder, and stirring and mixing uniformly, (iii) introducing the carbon dioxide gas into the sealed pressure hydrocinning kettle, firstly heating, pressurizing, then reducing and/or reducing pressure to obtain the nano-carbon mixture, and (iv) heating the aluminum alloy raw material substrate to 400-550 degrees C, then adding the nano-carbon mixture obtained in the step (iii), stirring and mixing uniformly, raising the temperature to 560-900 degrees C, keeping for 30-600 minutes to obtain the graphite aluminum composite material.
   USE - The graphite aluminum composite material is useful in lithium ion battery anode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and suitable for industrial production and has high productivity.

AB    NOVELTY - The heating method involves circulating                cooling liquid from a stream through a                refrigeration cycle heat pump to another stream so                that the heat pump extracts heat from the former                stream to return to the latter stream. The pump uses carbon                dioxide as a refrigerant. The cooling liquid is soy                bean oil or canola oil. PN CN113318183-A
TI Composition useful for reducing uric acid, comprises rosemary extract, dandelion extract, lily extract, and peony root extract, where rosemary extract is prepared by e.g. taking rosemary leaf stem powder, and extracting with ethanol.
AU TIAN S
AE HUBEI MEITIAN AGRIC BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021A78516
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises rosemary extract, dandelion extract, lily extract, and peony root extract. The rosemary extract is prepared by (1) taking rosemary leaf stems, and freeze-drying the rosemary leaf stems in vacuum, (2) taking out the rosemary leaf stems from the freeze dryer, crushing the rosemary leaf stems, and placing them in a dry environment, (3) taking rosemary leaf stem powder, and extracting the rosemary leaf stem powder with ethanol, (4) centrifuging the extract after extraction, and collecting and concentrating the supernatant to obtain an ethanol extract of rosemary, (5) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the rosemary precipitate after centrifugation, (6) collecting the rosemary extract in the primary separation kettle, and (7) mixing the ethanol extract and the primary separated extract to obtain a rosemary extract.
   USE - The composition is useful for reducing uric acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: has scientific compatibility, synergistic effect, reasonable matching of auxiliary materials; does not cause liver and kidney damage; and is safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) health product for reducing uric acid, comprising composition, and food-acceptable auxiliary materials; and
   (2) medicine for reducing uric acid, comprising composition, and auxiliary materials.

The composition further includes 3-10 pts. wt. kudzu root extract, 1-5 pts. wt. Gardenia extract, 5-10 pts. wt. mulberry leaves extract, 2-15 pts. wt. purple potato extract, and/or 2-8 pts. wt. chicory extract. The composition further comprises 1-10 pts. wt. mango extract, 5-20 pts. wt. kelp extract, 5-20 pts. wt. carrot extract, and/or 1-10 pts. wt. tea extract.

AE HASEGAWA CO LTD T (HASE-C)
GA 202199045H
AB    NOVELTY - A flavor-imparting agent comprises at least                one hydroxyaldehyde compound (I).
   USE - Flavor-imparting agent used in fragrance                composition for improving fragrance of consumer                goods (all claimed). Uses include but are not                limited to perfume, shampoo, hair cream,                foundation, lipstick, antiperspirant spray,                deodorant cream, carbon dioxide gas system,                skin-care, enzyme system, sunscreen product, face                washing material, detergent for kitchen, bleaching                agent, toilet paper, mask, bandage, black tea, rice                cracker, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The flavor-imparting agent has excellent                diffusibility and lingering scent property, and can                create aroma with high quality such as natural                feel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A flavor-imparting agent comprises at least                one hydroxyaldehyde compound of formula (I).
   R1 = OH;
   R2,R3 = H or linear or branched 1-3C                alkyl;
   or R2+R3 = 3-membered ring at position C9 or                methylene with double bond at position C9 (when C8                position in compound (I) is single bond);
   or R1 = absent;
   R2 = H or 1-3C linear or branched                alkyl;and
   R3 = OH or CH2OH (when C8 position in compound                (I) is double bond).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) improvement of fragrance of a fragrance                composition, which involves blending the                flavor-imparting agent with the fragrance                composition; and
   (2) improvement of fragrance of consumer                goods, which involves blending the flavor-imparting                agent or fragrance composition with the consumer                goods.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Compound (I) is                  preferably of formula (I-1) or (I-2).
   EXAMPLE - 0.36 g                (6E)-2,6,10-Trimethyldodeca-6,11-diene-2,3,10-triol                was dissolved in 15 mL mixture of tetrahydrofuran                and water. To the obtained solution, 0.37 g sodium                periodate was dripped over 2 hours. The mixture was                stirred at room temperature for 4 hours. Sodium                chloride, water and ether were added to the                obtained reaction solution and the organic layer                was separated. The organic layer was washed with                saturated brine, dried over anhydrous magnesium                sulfate, and the solvent was evaporated with an                evaporator to obtain 0.27 g colorless oily                substance. The colorless oily substance was                purified by silica gel column chromatography using                n-hexane and ethyl acetate (10:1) to obtain 0.25 g                colorless oily compound of                8-hydroxy-4,8-dimethyl-4,9-decadienal. The                fragrance composition containing the                8-hydroxy-4,8-dimethyl-4,9-decadienal as a                flavor-imparting agent had distinctive fragrance                and could create an aroma with high quality.

AB    NOVELTY - Laser arc hybrid welding process for stainless                steel comprises forming a stainless steel                connection in the form of a butt joint connection                in Y-shape with a joint angle of 28-32 degrees and                a height of flattened edge of 5-8 mm, performing a                laser arc hybrid multi-layer welding process with a                flattened edge gap of less than or equal to 1 mm, a                laser power of 0-6 kW, a focus error amount of -1                to -3 mm, a laser arc distance of 1-3 mm, a welding                current of 230-290 A and a welding speed of 0.4-1.2                m/minute and using argon gas or active gas of                85-80% argon and 15-20% carbon dioxide to protect a                welding surface during hybrid laser arc                welding.

   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the melt depth to avoid                welding defects e.g. insufficient penetration and                air voids; and improves the structure, property and                the back side formation of the welded joint, and                improves the weld quality and weld production                efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The stainless steel                  has a plate thickness of 10 mm. The connection is                  in the form of a butt joint in a Y-shape with a                  joint angle of 32 degrees and a height of the                  flattened edge of 5 mm, where the welding wire                  has 1.2 mm and two-layer for laser arc hybrid                  welding, and a weld joint of the first layer is                  formed with a laser power of 5 kW, a welding                  current of 230 A and a welding speed of 1.2                  m/minute. The subsequent welds of multilayer                  welding are formed with a laser power of 2 kW, a                  welding current of 230 A and a welding speed of                  0.7 m/minute with a protective gas of 80% argon                  and 20% carbon dioxide with a gas flow rate of 20                  l/minute during welding.

PN CN113003590-A
TI Preparing lithium carbonate by thermal reduction of spodumene involves using type spodumene as raw material, under vacuum and high temperature conditions, mixing type spodumene with reducing agent and flux inhibitor, and thermally reducing.
AU ZHONG H
   GAN Y
   YANG P
AE SICHUAN WANBANG SHENGHUI NEW ENERGY TECH (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2021757766
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium carbonate by thermal reduction of spodumene involves using type spodumene as raw material, under vacuum and high temperature conditions, mixing spodumene with a reducing agent and flux inhibitor, and thermally reducing in a reduction furnace to reduce the lithium in the spodumene to metal lithium vapor is condensed to obtain metallic lithium and reduced spodumene slag containing metallic lithium and lithium oxide. The spodumene slag containing lithium metal and lithium oxide is taken out and leached with water. The obtained leaching residue and slurry are precipitated and decalcified by precipitation method, the leaching residue and slurry are filtered. The filtrate is a lithium hydroxide solution, which is evaporated, the carbon dioxide gas is introduced to precipitate lithium by carbonation, the slurry is filtered, the filter cake is dried to obtain a lithium carbonate product, and the mother liquor is returned to the concentration section for circulation.
   USE - Method for preparing lithium carbonate by thermal reduction of spodumene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has small environment load, good environmental benefit and good safety and avoids the production of high risk chemical chlorine in the existing technology and use of sulfuric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The spodumene is a-type spodumene concentrate, the grade of which is 5%, and the particle size is over 150-200 mesh sieve. The reducing agent is a composite reducing agent, which is prepared by mixing high-silicon ferrosilicon, aluminum powder, and aluminum-silicon powder in a mass ratio of 1:0.1-0.5:0.1-0.5. The flux inhibitor is lime, where the calcium oxide content is 90-98 (wt.%). Preferred Conditions: The amount of type spodumene concentrate, reducing agent and flux inhibitor is 1:0.1-0.5:0.3-2.5. The method involves using dough-making machine to press into balls or flakes with a particle size of 10-100mm. The conditions of the thermal reduction includes temperature is 850-1300 degrees C, the absolute pressure is 2-50 Pascal, and the time is 5-25 hours. The high-silicon ferrosilicon has a silicon content of 75 wt.% and a particle size of 6-8 micrometer. The aluminum powder has an aluminum content of 99 wt.%, activity of 70 wt.% and a particle size of 6-8 micrometer. The aluminum-silicon powder has a combined aluminum-silicon content of 98 wt.% and a particle size of 6-8 micrometer. The metallic lithium is crude lithium, which is protected by argon gas and refined under stirring to obtain metallic lithium with a purity of 99%. The filter cake obtained by leaching slag and slurry precipitation and decalcification and filtering is dried and used to produce cement.

PT P
PN RU2747905-C1
TI METHOD OF FORMING A MODEL OF PRESSURE PNEUMOTHORAX            OF LARGE ANIMALS ON THE EXAMPLE OF A SWINE.
AU GOLOVKO K P
   MARKEVICH V YU
   VERTII A B
   DEMCHENKO K N
   PETUKHOV K V
AE KIROV MILITARY MEDICAL ACAD (KIRO-Non-standard)
GA 202168224J
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to experimental medicine,                namely to military field surgery, damage surgery,                thoracic surgery, disaster medicine. A model of a                pressure pneumothorax is formed on a large animal -                a swine. An artificial carboxythorax is created                using an endovideosurgical rack insufflator                connected to the pleural drainage. Pleural drainage                is installed in the 4th intercostal space along the                anterior axillary line. The drainage is connected                to the endovideosurgical rack insufflator, and                carbon dioxide is injected at a speed of 3 l/min                until a pressure of 15 mm Hg is reached. At the                same time, the heart rate, respiratory rate, blood                pressure and saturation are monitored. Then a                control radiography of the breast is performed. At                the same time, the displacement of the mediastinum                in the opposite direction, the presence of free air                in the pleural cavity and the appearance of free                air in the hypodermic tissue confirms the                successful formation of a pressure pneumothorax.                Then decompression is performed, while                simultaneously monitoring the dynamics of the main                indicators of the respiratory and cardiovascular                systems. The breast radiography is performed                again.
   USE - Experimental medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Method makes it possible to form an optimal                experimental model of a pressure pneumothorax on a                large laboratory animal - a swine, followed by                testing of prototypes of medical devices developed                to eliminate this life-threatening condition.

PN CN112675858-A; CN112675858-B
TI Catalyst for synthesis of dimethyl carbonate,            comprises tubular catalyst carrier and active component            with catalytic function, where catalyst carrier is            silica and active component is copper-nickel-aluminum            hydrotalcite.
AU XU K
   ZHANG X
   YIN X
   LU J
   WU X
   SHUI Q
AE SHANDONG DEPU CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DEPU NEW MATERIAL SCI & TECHNOLOGY              CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202145749W
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises a tubular catalyst                carrier and an active component with catalytic                function, and the catalyst carrier is silica. The                active component is copper-nickel-aluminum                hydrotalcite, and the copper-nickel-aluminum                hydrotalcite is supported on a catalyst                carrier.
   USE - Catalyst for synthesis of dimethyl                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves conversion rate and                selectivity, and has long service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the catalyst, which involves (1)                cutting the wheat straw into sections with the                tubular shape intact, rinsing it with clean water,                and drying it for later use,(2) preparing                copper-nickel-aluminum hydrotalcite powder, micron                silica powder and solvent into a slurry for use,                (3) mixing the slurry with the wheat straw dried in                step (1), stirring to make the slurry fully adhere                to the inner and outer surfaces of the wheat straw,                and then separating and drying the wheat straw to                obtain a catalyst precursor for use, and (4)                calcining the catalyst precursor in a protective                atmosphere of flowing carbon dioxide and water                vapor to solidify copper-nickel-aluminum                hydrotalcite powder and micron silica powder on the                surface of wheat straw to obtain a catalyst with a                tubular structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silica is of nano                  level. Preferred Composition: The active                  components with catalytic function include 25-45                  %mass copper-nickel-aluminum hydrotalcite. In                  step (2), the mass ratio of the                  copper-nickel-aluminum hydrotalcite powder to the                  micron silica powder is 25-45:55-75. The mass                  volume ratio of the solid powder formed by the                  copper-nickel-aluminum hydrotalcite powder and                  the micron silica powder to the solvent is 1 g:                  6-12 ml. Preferred Property: In step (1), the                  length of the wheat straw obtained after cutting                  into sections is between 0.4-1 cm. Preferred                  Process: In step (2), the copper-nickel-aluminum                  hydrotalcite powder, micron silica powder and the                  solvent are mixed and then subjected to                  ultrasonic vibration. In step (3), the catalyst                  precursor is immersed in the slurry again,                  separated and dried, and the above steps are                  repeated 2 or 3 times. Preferred Conditions: In                  step (4), the calcination is carried out at                  500-750 degrees C for 30-90 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (2), the                  solvent includes any one of water, methanol, and                  ethanol.

PN CN112569963-A; TW202112443-A; TW733200-B1
TI Catalyst comprises mid-porous transition metal oxide, and single-atom noble metal anchored on mesoporous transition metal oxide.
AU LI C
   YAN S
   LAI Y
AE IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
   IND TECHNOLOGY RES INST (ITRI-C)
GA 202137124X
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a mid-porous transition metal oxide, and a single-atom noble metal anchored on the mesoporous transition metal oxide. The transition metal comprises cobalt (Co), manganese (Mn), iron (Fe), nickel (Ni) and/or cerium (Ce), and the single-atom noble metal is platinum (Pt), rhodium (Rh), palladium (Pd) or rubidium (Ru). When the single-atom noble metal is Pt, the transition metal does not include Fe. When the single-atom noble metal is Ru, the transition metal does not include Ni or Ce.
   USE - Used as catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) forming a catalyst, comprising providing a neutral aqueous solution of a salt of a precious metal and a salt of a transition metal, dispersing a mesoporous template in a non-polar solvent to form a dispersion, mixing the neutral aqueous solution and the dispersion to form a mixed liquid, heating the mixed liquid to remove the non-polar solvent and water in the mixed liquid and form a powder, sintering the powder to form a catalyst in the hole of the middle hole template, and removing the middle hole template to retain the catalyst; and
   (2) removing volatile organic compounds, comprising passing a volatile organic compound into a catalyst to oxidize the volatile organic compound into water and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: When the single-atom noble metal is Pt, the transition metal includes Co, Mn, Ni and/or Ce. When the single-atom noble metal is Rh, the transition metal includes Co, Mn, Fe, Ni and/or Ce. When the single-atom noble metal is Pd, the transition metal includes Co, Mn, Fe, Ni and/or Ce. When the single-atom noble metal is Ru, and the transition metal includes Co, Mn and/or Fe. The mass ratio of the mesoporous transition metal oxide to the monoatomic noble metal is 1:0.002-1:0.06. The specific surface area of the catalyst is 60-200 m2/g. The average pore size of the catalyst is 8-20 nm. When the single-atom noble metal is Pt, Rh, Pd or Ru, the transition metal includes Co, Mn, Fe, Ni and/or Ce. The weight ratio of the mesoporous transition metal oxide to the monoatomic noble metal is 1:0.002-1:0.06. Preferred Method: The temperature at which the mixed liquid is heated to remove the non-polar solvent and water in the mixed liquid and form a powder is 55-75 degrees C. The temperature of sintering the powder to form the catalyst in the holes of the middle hole template is between 280-350 degrees C. The step of removing the mesoporous template to retain the catalyst utilizes an aqueous solution of hydrofluoric acid or sodium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volatile organic compound comprises propane, isopropanol, acetone, toluene, propylene glycol methyl ether and/or propylene glycol methyl ether acetate.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN112569694-A
TI Method for preparing filter mesh for air purification by using santa barbara amorphous-15 (SBA-15), involves adding sodium hydroxide solution to adjust pH of solution and vacuum drying mixed liquid to obtain filter mesh for purification.
AU ZHANG W
   GUO X
AE SHAANXI SHISHENG KEBANG CONSTR ENG CO (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202138941R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing a modified mesoporous molecular sieve SBA-15. The modified mesoporous molecular sieve SBA-15, nano-titanium dioxide particles, ethyl acrylate, surfactant and deionized water are mixed uniformly and stirred at room temperature for 1.5 hours to obtain a mixed solution. Nano zinc dioxide particles and ethyl 2-methacrylate are added to the mixed solution and stirred for 1 hour. A sodium hydroxide solution is added to adjust a pH of the solution to 7.5-8.0 to obtain mixed liquid. The obtained mixed liquid is vacuum dried to obtain a filter mesh for air purification. A methyltrimethoxysilane, ethyl orthosilicate, and hydrochloric acid are added. A surfactant is fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene-polyoxypropylene ether or polyglycerol fatty acid ester.
   USE - Method for preparing filter mesh for air purification by using SBA-15.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively absorb air pollution particles or toxic and harmful gas and can absorb harmful gas carbon dioxide of three-dimensional space, hydrogen sulfide and nitrogen. The nano titanium dioxide particles and nano zinc dioxide particles can effectively absorb air pollution particles. The method improves the efficiency of air purification.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN KR2021033251-A; KR2274756-B1
TI Method for producing hydrogen from biomass for producing electricity comprises crushing mixture of biomass and acid, subjecting to mechanical catalytic depolymerization and performing hydrothermal treatment.
AU CHOI Y
   UK L D
   PARK J H
   JIN M
   LEE Y
   SONG K
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 202132938X
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing hydrogen from biomass comprises (i) crushing a mixture of biomass and acid and subjecting to mechanical catalytic depolymerization, (ii) dissolving and dispersing the crushed biomass and acid mixture, oxidizing agent and polar aprotic solvent in water to obtain a mixed aqueous solution, (iii) performing hydrothermal treatment of the mixed aqueous solution of biomass, acid, oxidizing agent and polar aprotic solvent, (iv) cooling the hydrogen gas mixture, formic acid-containing aqueous solution, and solid lignin generated after the hydrothermal treatment to room temperature and then separating, (v) obtaining an aqueous formic acid solution in which the concentration of impurities is minimized from the separated aqueous solution containing formic acid and (vi) producing a mixture of hydrogen and carbon dioxide from formic acid by adding a heterogeneous dehydrogenation catalyst to the aqueous formic acid solution in which the concentration of impurities is minimized.
   USE - The method: is stable and economical; improves the yield of formic acid and improves the thermal efficiency; and does not include the addition of basic additives.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen is useful for producing electricity (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method of producing electricity, comprising supplying hydrogen produced by above method to a fuel cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The biomass comprises woody biomass, herbal biomass, chitosan, chitin, lignin, cellulose, starch, hemycellulose and/or algae. The acid is citric acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid, malic acid, lactic acid, tartaric acid, tannic acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid and/or nitric acid. The oxidizing agent is hydrogen peroxide, heteropolyacid and/or sodium percarbonate. The polar aprotic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, ethyl acetate, acetone, acetonitrile, dichloromethane and/or propylene carbonate. The heterogeneous dehydrogenation catalyst is palladium, silver, gold, ruthenium, rhodium, iridium, copper, nickel, manganese, zinc, zirconium, titanium, vanadium, molybdenum, cobalt and/or iron. The heterogeneous dehydrogenation catalyst is supported on at least one of silica, alumina, titania, carbon, zinc oxide, zirconia and ceria. Preferred Method: The hydrothermal treatment is carried out at 25-250 degrees C under atmospheric pressure. In the step (v), the method of minimizing the concentration of impurities adopts distillation and separation membrane. The step (v) further comprises generating at least one by-product including acetic acid, levulinic acid, furfural, 5-hydroxymethylfurfural and saccharides, and performing steam reforming reaction of the by-product to additionally produce hydrogen. In the step (vi), the dehydrogenation reaction is carried out at 5-300 degrees C, preferably 5-50 degrees C for 10 minutes to 5 hours using pine as biomass, citric acid as acid, hydrogen peroxide as oxidizing agent, dimethyl sulfoxide as polar aprotic solvent and palladium/amino-functionalized silica particles as heterogeneous dehydrogenation catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - Formic acid aqueous solution was diluted with water to 12.8 g/l. 0.055 g palladium/amino-functionalized silica particles catalyst was added to the diluted aqueous formic acid solution to perform formic acid dehydrogenation reaction at 25 degrees C under atmospheric pressure. The formic acid dehydrogenation reaction was performed for 60 minutes to produce 240 ml mixed gas in which hydrogen and carbon dioxide were mixed in a molar ratio of 1:1 for 10 minutes, and the turn over frequncy of the catalyst was 279.24 mol hydrogen mol catalyst/hour to obtain hydrogen.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN CN112521717-A
TI Preparation method of silicon dioxide aerogel            composite material with high mechanical strength            comprises e.g. drying and pretreating fiber, mixing            epoxy resin, curing agent, and diluent and performing            curing reaction.
AU SONG D
   CAO Y
   YANG C
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 202134721W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of silicon dioxide aerogel                composite material with high mechanical strength                and low thermal conductivity comprises (i) drying                and pretreating the fiber at 60-100 degrees C for                1-3 hours used as the reinforcement to obtain the                fiber substrate, (ii) mixing epoxy resin, curing                agent, and diluent in proportion to prepare epoxy                resin glue and taking the quality of epoxy resin as                the reference standard 100, where the content of                curing agent is 20-50%, and the content of diluent                is 30-100%, (iii) compounding the fiber substrate                and the epoxy resin glue at a mass ratio of                100:400-600, and performing a curing reaction at                room temperature or high temperature to prepare a                fiber-reinforced epoxy resin composite substrate,                and (iv) homogeneously compositing the composite                substrate prepared in step (iii) with the silicon                dioxide aerogel glue, and subjecting to the gel,                aging and hydrophobically modifying, and                supercritical carbon dioxide drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon                dioxide aerogel composite material with high                mechanical strength.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple materials and processes,                good microstructure controllability. The composite                sheet has high compressive strength, low thermal                conductivity, and super-hydrophobicity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fibers used as                  reinforcements include glass fiber felt, carbon                  fiber felt, high silica fiber felt, pre-oxidized                  fiber felt, ultrafine rock wool fiber felt,                  alumina fiber, mullite fiber felt, and quartz                  fiber felt. The epoxy resin includes bisphenol A                  type epoxy resin, the epoxy value of which is                  0.3-0.6, the diluent includes acetone, methyl                  ethyl ketone, cyclohexanone, benzene, toluene,                  dimethyl toluene, n-butanol and styrene and the                  curing agent includes aliphatic polyamines,                  aromatic amines, phthalic anhydride and its                  adducts. The silicon dioxide aerogel glue liquid                  comprises silica sol, acid catalyst, and alkaline                  catalyst. The silica sol includes silicon dioxide                  sol, and its raw materials include methyl                  orthosilicate, ethyl orthosilicate, and                  polymethyl silicate. The acid catalyst includes                  oxalic acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid,                  sulfuric acid, and/or acetic acid. The basic                  catalyst includes ammonia, sodium hydroxide,                  ammonium fluoride, ammonium carbonate, ammonium                  bicarbonate, and/or ethylenediamine. Preferred                  Method: The aging and hydrophobic modification                  comprises placing the composite material in a                  solvent containing a hydrophobic modifier at 50                  plus minus 5 degrees C for 24-72 hours, and                  replacing the solvent every 8 hours, where the                  solvent includes ethanol, methanol, propanol,                  ether, alkylating agent and/or linear alcohol and                  the hydrophobic modifier includes                  hexamethyldisilazane, hexamethyldisilazane,                  trimethylmethoxysilane, trimethylchlorosilane,                  methyltriethoxysilane, methyltriethoxysilane,                  and/or ethyltriethoxysilane. The supercritical                  CO2 drying comprises solvent replacing in a                  drying kettle, placing the wet aerogel material,                  dissolving the solvent in the material and taking                  away by the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                  and drying at 50-60 degrees C with pressure of                  12-16 MPa, and flow rate of 2500-3500 kg/hour for                  3-8 hours.

AE CHINA TIANCHEN ENG CORP (CNCE-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon monoxide conversion condensate refining process system comprises high temperature condensed liquid stripping tower (C-01), low temperature condensed liquid stripping tower (C-02), stripping gas separating tower (C-03) and carbon dioxide stripping tower (C-04). The high temperature condensing liquid stripping tower is orderly connected with waste heat boiler (E-01) and gas-liquid separator (D-01) through pipeline. The gas-liquid separator top outlet and bottom outlet are respectively connected with low temperature condensed liquid stripping tower middle collecting inlet through pipeline. The high temperature condensed liquid stripping tower bottom is connected with condensed liquid pump (P-01) through pipeline.
