PN CN112501690-A
TI Growing sapphire single crystal, comprises e.g. adding alumina into crucible with seed crystals, passing high-purity helium gas into heat exchanger to cool seed crystal, passing carrier gas and mixed gas, heating furnace blank, and cooling.
AU GUO F
   LIU X
AE TONGLIAO PRECISION SAPPHIRE CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 202131245S
AB    NOVELTY - Growing sapphire single crystal, comprises (i) adding alumina into crucible with seed crystals, placing crucible on top of heat exchanger, closing furnace blank, and vacuumizing or injecting stable gas to form positive pressure, (ii) placing furnace blank under vacuum or positive pressure, passing high-purity helium gas into heat exchanger to cool seed crystal, heating graphite heater until alumina starts to melt, and (iii) starting melting raw material, passing carrier gas and mixed gas from inlet pipe at bottom of furnace blank, discharging gas from gas outlet pipe at top of furnace blank, where mixed gas is carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, (iv) heating furnace blank until alumina melts completely, and after temperature stabilizes, increasing flow rate of heat exchanger to start crystal growth until completes crystal growth, and (v) cooling furnace blank until all crystals solidified, closing inlet and outlet pipes at same time, cooling furnace blank, and taking out crystal ingot.
   USE - The method is useful for growing sapphire single crystal.
   ADVANTAGE - The crystal: improved crystal quality; and can grow higher-quality sapphire single crystals by maintaining negative or positive pressure in the furnace blank during crystal growth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the stable gas includes nitrogen, argon or helium. In step (iii), the carrier gas is high-purity inert gas, where the high-purity inert gas includes helium, argon, nitrogen or hydrogen, and the purity of high-purity inert gas is above 99.995%.

PN CN112504981-A
TI Method for detecting exhaust gas emission of motor vehicle by using wavelength modulation spectrum technology, involves performing harmonic detection to electric signal and reference signal by lock phase amplifying module.
AU LI Y
   ZHANG T
   LI H
   FAN H
   WANG L
AE TIANJIN TONGYANG TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021284875
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating a low-frequency saw-tooth wave signal and a high-frequency sine wave signal by a digital phase-locked amplifier signal generating module. The low-frequency saw-tooth wave signal and the high-frequency sine wave signal are input to a laser driving module. A reference signal is generated by the signal generating module to a phase-lock amplifier. An electric signal of a laser is collected by a photoelectric detector. Harmonic detection is performed by a lock phase amplifying module to obtain a signal of target gas. Concentration of the target gas is obtained.
   USE - Method for detecting exhaust gas discharge of motor vehicle by using wavelength modulation spectrum technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing simultaneous measurement of carbon monoxide (CO) and carbon dioxide (CO2). The method enables scanning to measure concentration of two kinds of gas so as to greatly reduce complexity of a system. The method enables obtaining concentration ratio of the obtained two kinds of gas in an accurate manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of method for detecting exhaust gas discharge of motor vehicle by using wavelength modulation spectrum technology.(Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN IN202011051144-A
TI Magnetic biochar used for removing pollutants e.g. ofloxacin, phenols and nitrogenous compounds, comprises iron oxide and is manufactured from industrial effluent plant sludge.
AU SRIVASTAVA V C
   SINGH V
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY ROORKEE (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 202126054V
AB    NOVELTY - A magnetic biochar comprises iron oxide and is manufactured from industrial effluent plant (ETP) sludge.
   USE - Magnetic biochar used for removing pollutants e.g. ofloxacin, phenols and nitrogenous compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The biochar is economical, efficient, sustainable and effectively removes toxins and pollutants such as ofloxacin, phenols and nitrogenous compounds from aqueous solution. The method produces the biochar without the addition of any iron salt, and is environmentally-friendly since it uses industrial solid wastes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of magnetic biochar from ETP sludge, which involves pretreating the sludge collected by segregation of unwanted materials, washing with double distilled water, drying in an oven at 110 plus minus 5 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours, grinding and sieving in a size of 0.05-1.0 mm to make sludge suitable for further modifications, physically and chemically modifying the sludge by carrying out physical activation by nitrogen and/or carbon dioxide at 300-700 degrees C for 0.5-6 hours with or without acid washing with a acid having a concentration of 0.2-5 equivalent/L, drying and grinding to obtain magnetic biocharby and using to remove pollutants from wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The characterization of the biochar is carried out by thermogravimetric analysis (TG)/differential thermal analysis (DTA) , BET, X-ray diffraction (XRD), field emission scanning electron microscope (FESEM), transmission electron microscopy (TEM), vibrating-sample magnetometer (VSM), X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), RAMAN and Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy (FTIR). The magnetic behavior of the biochar due to iron oxide for separation of the biochar after its use as adsorbent is confirmed by VSM, TEM and XRD. The regeneration of the biochar is carried out thermally and tested for five or more successive runs.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The biochar is applied at 5-45 degrees C for 10-480 minutes for removing ofloxacin, phenols and nitrogenous from aqueous solution at an initial adsorbate concentration of 1-500 mgL-1 and a pH of 2-12. The aqueous solution after each cycle of adsorption is tested for iron leaching by inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) is within the industrial discharge limits as per central pollution control board (CPCB).
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); P73 (Layered products (B32).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PN CN112062342-A
TI Continuous film filter-type electroplating wastewater treatment method by adding flocculating agent, ferrous sulfate and sodium hydroxide into the water to remove oil and suspended substance, an and perfroming aeration treatment.
AU SHI Y
AE JIANGSU ZHONGZHI XINYU ENVIRONMENTAL TEC (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C6488N
AB    NOVELTY - A continuous film filter-type electroplating wastewater treatment method by introducing an electroplating wastewater into the oil separating tank for oil separating treatment. The oil separated from the oil separating tank is recycled. The water separated from the oil separating tank is sent into the reaction tank. Flocculating agent, ferrous sulfate and sodium hydroxide are added into the water to remove the oil and suspended substance. The water filtered by the reaction tank is sent to the continuous film filtering system for filtering. The filtered water is sent to the clean water tank for recycling. The sludge discharged from the continuous film filtering system is sent into a sludge concentration tank for aeration treatment. The sludge after aeration treatment is sent to the supercritical water oxidation system for processing. The organic material in the sludge is oxidized to carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - Continuous film filter-type electroplating wastewater treatment method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the processing efficiency of the electroplating waste water by the continuous film filtering system. It also saves cost.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic flow diagram illustrating a continuous film filter-type electroplating wastewater treatment method (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AU CHU J
   YUAN Q
   GAO X
AE RES INST FORESTRY CHINESE ACAD FORESTRY (CLYK-C)
GA 2020C41040
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using an integrated eddy correlation observation system to obtain a vorticity-related observation flux data in a desert grassland ecosystem in which the flux data includes a three-dimensional wind speed data, high-frequency ultrasonic temperature data, carbon dioxide density data and water vapor density data. The acquired vorticity-related observation flux data is preprocessed to obtain a preprocessed vorticity-related observation flux data. A secondary rotation method or plane fitting method is used to obtain a coordinate rotation matrix of the respective reference planes and the coordinate rotation matrix is used to perform coordinate rotation correction on a three-dimensional wind speed data in the preprocessed vorticity-related observation flux data to obtain a corrected Eddy related observation flux data.

PN CN111838483-A
TI Preparing ultra micro extracted fruit juice            beverage comprises e.g. choosing fresh fruit, washing,            removing the core and shell, grinding, centrifuging,            treating fruit slag, mixing filtrate, freezing and            drying, and preparing juice.
AU ZHOU R
AE ANHUI KANGZHIWEI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020A9568U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ultra-micro-extracted fruit juice                beverage comprises (i) processing raw material                including choosing fresh fruit, washing, removing                the core and shell, placing in grinder to grinding                into slurry, adding slurry in water bath at 30-35                degrees C for 20-30 minutes, and centrifuging in                centrifuge to obtain supernatant as filtrate and                precipitate is fruit slag, (ii) treating fruit slag                including placing fruit slag at 50-55 degrees C to                drying, placing in ultra-micro crusher for                ultra-micro crushing, adding fruit slag powder to                deionized water in ultrasonic homogenizer for                ultrasonically homogenizing and adding yeast to                fermenting at 30-35 degrees C for 1-2 hours to                obtain fermented material, (iii) mixing filtrate                including adding filtrate to fermented material,                continuously warming and fermenting for 1-2 hours,                adding fermentation liquid in reactor, passing                supercritical carbon dioxide for 1-1.5 hours and                taking out to obtain mixed fermentation liquid,                (iv) freezing and drying, and (v) preparing                juice.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                ultra-micro-extracted fruit juice beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: overcomes the shortcomings of the                prior art; and enhances the nutritional value,                flavor and taste of the obtained juice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the                  freezing and drying is carried out by                  concentrating the above mixed fermentation liquid                  under vacuum to 1/3-1/5 of the original volume,                  freezing and drying under at low temperature                  environment, taking out, grinding and crushing to                  obtain freeze-dried powder, and adding the                  freeze-dried powder in the ultra-micro crusher                  for ultra-micro crushing to obtain ultra-micro                  powder. In the step (v), the juice is prepared by                  mixing the ultra-micro powder with de-ionized                  water, thickener, sweetener, and acidity                  regulator, uniformly mixing and stirring, placing                  in homogenizer for homogenizing, sterilizing and                  filling. In the step (i), the centrifuging is                  carried out at 8000-10000 revolutions/minute for                  5-8 minutes. In the step (iii), the temperature                  in the reactor at 35-40 degrees C under 10-12                  MPa. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  fresh fruit comprises apple, pear, banana, grape,                  kiwi fruit, orange, lemon and/or other common                  fruit. In the step (ii), the particle size of the                  fruit slag after ultra-micro crushing is 4-8 mu                  m. The mass ratio of fruit slag powder, deionized                  water, and yeast is 10:14-16:0.2-0.4. In the step                  (iii), the supercritical carbon dioxide                  introduced is 2-3 times the volume of the                  fermentation liquid. In the step (iv), the                  particle size of the ultra-micro powder is 0.2-2                  mu m. In the step (v), the mixed mass ratio of                  each substance is ultrafine powder: deionized                  water: thickener: sweetener: acidity regulator =                  20-30:75-85:0-1.6:0-2:0-2.

TI Synthesizing of compound with at least one            alkylene group and at least one thiol or thiolate group            involves reacting compound with at least one            five-membered cyclic monothiocarbonate group, shortly            referred to as thiocarbonate with starter.
AU RUDOLF P
   CHRIST E
   BRUCHMANN B
   THIEL I
   WURM T M
   WURM T
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   UNIV FREIBURG ALBERT-LUDWIGS (UYFR-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2020A6174R
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing of a compound with at least one                alkylene group and at least one thiol or thiolate                group involves reacting compound with at least one                five-membered cyclic monothiocarbonate group,                shortly referred to as thiocarbonate with a starter                selected from compounds with at least one thiol                group, from compounds with at least one                hydroxygroup or from basic inorganic compounds to                obtain a compound with at least one alkylene group                and at least one thiol or thiolate group and carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Method for synthesizing of a compound with at                least one alkylene group and at least one thiol or                thiolate group.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesized of a                compound with at least one alkylene group and at                least one thiol or thiolate group, that used                suitable solvents e.g., toluene, tetrahydrofuran                and dimethylformamide, which is an advantage oft he                process that usually no additional solvent is                required as the starting materials are usually                liquid or at least one of thestarting materials                serves as solvent for any solid starting materials,                and if the starting materials are solid at room                temperature, the reaction may be at temperatures                above the melting point of any solid starting                materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The starter is a                  compound with one to three reactive groups,                  selected from thiol groups, hydroxy groups or any                  combination, preferably she starter is an                  inorganic salt, selected from metal hydroxides,                  metal sulfides, metal hydrogen sulfides, metal                  oxides, metal phosphates and metal silicates. The                  inorganic salt is a metal hydroxide, a metal                  sulfide or a metal hydrogen sulfide. The catalyst                  is a compound with a tertiary amino group, a                  guanidino group, an amidine group or a phosphine                  group or a metal hydroxide. The obtained compound                  is a polyalkylenesulfide with at least one                  terminating thiol group or thiolate group.                  Preferred Conditions: The hydroxy or thiol groups                  of the starter are transferred into the                  respective alkoxylate or thiolate groups before                  the reaction or by a catalyst used in the                  reaction. The obtained compound is further                  reacted with a compound with at least one group                  that reacts with a thiol group thiol (SH),                  shortly referred to as SH reactive                  compound.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU LI F
   GUO Y
   ZHAO S
   WANG W
   XING J
AE HEBEI KEXING PHARM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020A5666U
AB    NOVELTY - Vitamin C and sodium chloride injection comprises 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. vitamin C, 0.9 pts. wt. sodium chloride, 0.2-0.7 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, 0.005-0.2 pts. wt. antioxidants, 0.005-0.05 pts. wt. complexing agent, make up to 100 parts by volume with water.
   USE - Vitamin C sodium chloride injection is used as auxiliary medicine for treating/alleviating animal scurvy (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The vitamin C sodium chloride injection is suitable for injection of animals suffering from scurvy, maintains stable pH value, effectively prevents vitamin C from being oxidized, solves problem of maintaining proper dosage of vitamin C and sodium chloride in injection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing vitamin C and sodium chloride injection, which involves:
   (A) taking water, passing carbon dioxide to saturation, and then adding antioxidant and complexing agent to dissolve to obtain solution A, adding vitamin C and sodium bicarbonate to solution A to dissolve in order to obtain solution B, adding sodium chloride to solution B, then adjusting the pH value with sodium bicarbonate, making the volume constant with water, and filtering; and
   (B) resulting filtrate is vitamin C sodium chloride injection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The antioxidant is combination of any two antioxidants selected from sodium bisulfite, anhydrous sodium sulfite, sodium metabisulfite, thiourea and sodium formaldehyde sulfoxylate. The complexing agent is disodium edetate or citric acid.

Preferred Conditions: The centrifugal speed of                  supernatant is 4000-4400 rpm.

PN CN111296562-A
TI Preserving and storing fruits comprises e.g.            picking fruit, soaking in salt water, cooling, drying,            irradiating dried fruits with ultraviolet rays,            spraying atomized emulsion on surface, and adding fruit            in sealed bag.
AU CHEN Y
   WANG H
   LI J
AE UNIV NANNING (UNNG-C)
GA 2020582389
AB    NOVELTY - Preserving and storing fruits comprises (i)                picking the fruit, quickly soaking it in salt water                for 15-25 minutes, where the salt water                concentration is 15-23%, completing the soak,                cooling in air and drying quickly, (ii) irradiating                dried fruits with ultraviolet rays for 10-15                minutes, and after 5 minutes of irradiation,                spraying the atomized emulsion on the surface with                an ultrasonic atomizer, where the atomized emulsion                contains 2 wt.% chitin and 2.5 wt.% saturated fatty                acids, (iii) adding the fruit in a sealed bag, and                filling the sealed bag with a mixed gas, where the                mixed gas contains 5-10 vol.% oxygen, 10-15 vol.%                carbon dioxide, and nitrogen (remaining amount),                and (iv) sealing, adding it in a cold storage and                keeping it at -1&#176; C to 2&#176; C under                humidity 85-95%.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving and                storing fruits.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: extends the storage time of the                fruit, can be kept fresh for more than 90 days; and                maintains the flavor and moisture of the fruit                without losing; and extends the shelf life.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  temperature of the brine is 6-12&#176; C                  lower than the surface temperature of the fruit.                  During the salt water immersion process, an                  ultrasonic transducer is used to intermittently                  clean the surface of the fruit, the power of the                  ultrasonic transducer is 800 W/cubic meter, the                  cleaning is carried out for 10-15 seconds at                  intervals 10-15 seconds. In the step (iii), the                  sealed bag is made of a breathable material, and                  the oxygen permeability of the sealed bag is                  14400-24000 (ml/24hours-m2-101kPa). In the step                  (i), the concentration of the brine is 19%, and                  the soaking is carried out for 18 minutes,                  polyvinyl alcohol with a mass fraction of 4.8% is                  also added to the brine. In the step (iv), the                  surface temperature of the fruit is controlled to                  decrease by 4-8&#176; C every 6 hours.

PN CN111282593-A
TI Preparation of mercury removal catalyst used for            pyrolysis and gasification of garbage, involves soaking            carbon aerogel in hydrochloric acid solution,            carbonizing, calcining, adding solution containing            potassium thiocyanate, and drying.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHOU Y
   SUN Y
   CHEN D
   LIANG P
AE UNIV QINGDAO TECHNOLOGY (UQDT-C)
GA 202056005P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of mercury removal catalyst                involves pre-treating carrageenan as raw material,                adding to urea solution, stirring, cooling to room                temperature, freezing, dehydrating, obtaining                carrageenan-urea aerogel, placing in nitrogen                atmosphere, subjecting to pyrolysis at 600 degrees                C, 700 degrees C, 800 degrees C, 900 degrees C and                1000 degrees C, obtaining carbon aerogel, soaking                in hydrochloric acid solution, injecting carbon                dioxide at 50 degrees C, heating to 1100 degrees C,                subjecting to carbonization-calcination at 1100                degrees C for 20-40 minutes, annealing, obtaining                carbon aerogel after pore formation, mixing                chlorobutylmethylimidazole and potassium                thiocyanate, obtaining dark brown liquid,                extracting with dichloromethane, obtaining ionic                liquid, dissolving ionic liquid in dichloromethane,                adding resultant solution to carbon aerogel after                pore formation, stirring, removing dichloromethane,                and drying. The usage amount of                chlorobutylmethylimidazole in the mercury removal                catalyst is 20-50 %mass.
   USE - Preparation of mercury removal catalyst used                for pyrolysis and gasification of garbage                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of mercury                removal catalyst having mercury removal efficiency                of more than 97%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for usage of                the catalyst for pyrolysis and gasification of                garbage, which involves placing the catalyst in                tube furnace of fixed bed reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  hydrochloric acid solution is 1 mol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  urea solution is 30-40%. Preferred Process: The                  stirring of urea solution and carrageenan is                  carried out at 70-90 degrees C for 1-3 hours. The                  heating rate of pyrolysis is 5 degrees C/minute.                  The soaking process is carried out at normal                  temperature for 8-12 hours. During preparation of                  the catalyst, 75 atm carbon dioxide is injected                  using a high-pressure piston pump, and annealed                  in nitrogen atmosphere. The dark brown liquid is                  dried in vacuum oven at 60 degrees C for 24                  hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The carrageenan is                  I-type carrageenan.

PN CN110479064-A
TI Method for preparing mercury removing agent, involves preparing dried strip followed by preparing alumina support and dipping solution, immersing support in dipping solution, obtaining impregnated support, purging, washing and drying.
AU TIAN X
   TIAN Y
   TIAN H
AE TIANJIN HAOCHUANG ENERGY CONSERVATION (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A05094
AB    NOVELTY - A mercury removing agent preparation method involves uniformly mixing quick-release alumina powder, pseudoboehmite dry powder and lubricant, adding acid-containing aqueous solution, mixing into uniform block, utilizing orifice plate of extruder to extrude block into strips, drying the extruded strip, obtaining dried strip, calcining the dried strip at 950-1050 degrees C under oxidizing atmosphere, until moisture content of the dried strip is calcined to less than 5%, obtaining alumina support, slowly dripping silver nitrate solution into thiosulfate solution, stirring to obtain silver thiosulfate dipping solution, placing in dark place, immersing the alumina support in the dipping solution to obtain impregnated support, placing in sealed condition, until the silver thiosulfate dipping solution is uniformly distributed in the alumina support, taking out the support, purging using carbon dioxide, washing using water and drying to less than 80 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing mercury removing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing mercury removing agent with elemental sulfur content of 7-10%, specific pore volume, surface area and active ingredient content and improved accuracy in a simple, environmentally-friendly and easy manner at low cost by not utilizing gas phase sulfide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The acid-containing aqueous solution comprises 5-7 pts. wt. pure phosphoric acid and 3-8 pts. wt. ammonium dihydrogen phosphate. The mass ratio of quick-release alumina powder, pseudoboehmite dry powder and lubricant is 90-95:5-10:2-4. The weight ratio of silver nitrate and thiosulfate is 1:3-6, where concentration of silver ions in the silver thiosulfate dipping solution is 0.05-0.5 mol/l.

PN CN110467421-A; CN110467421-B
TI Functional cellulose aerogel composite material comprises nanocellulose aerogels and inorganic aerogels, cyanuric fluoride, silicon dioxide and titanium oxide.
AU CHEN X
AE JIAXIN TECHNOLOGY SHENZHEN CO LTD (JIAX-Non-standard)
   CHEN X (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2019990297
AB    NOVELTY - Functional cellulose aerogel composite material comprises nanocellulose aerogels and inorganic aerogels that penetrate each other, the components and their mass percentages includes 10-50 wt.% cyanuric fluoride, 90-240 wt.% silicon dioxide, 20-20 wt.% titanium oxide.
   USE - Used as functional cellulose aerogel composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material keeps the high porosity of cellulose gas gel, greatly enhances the compression strength, has hydrophobicity, strong absorbing ability, low coefficient of heat conductivity, photo-catalytic performance, has high brittleness, hydrophilicity, and simple process, is easy to control and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) functional cellulose aerogel composite material obtained by performing an organic-inorganic hybrid composite modification of a nanocellulose aerogel with a sol of silicon oxide and titanium oxide; and
   (2) preparing functional cellulose gas gel composite material comprising (1) preparing nano cellulose gas gel, (2) configuring a sol of silicon oxide and titanium oxide, (3) adding propylene oxide in the sol of silicon oxide and titanium oxide, stirring, adding alkali liquid, adding nano cellulose gas gel at 40-80 degrees C to obtain the nano composite cellulose gel and carrying out nano-cellulose composite gel replacement and modification, (4) carrying out carbon-dioxide supercritical drying or normal pressure drying to obtain functional nano cellulose gas gel composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of silicon oxide and titanium oxide is 8-10:1. The modifier includes trimethylchlorosilane, hexamethyldisiloxane and hexamethyldisilazane. Preferred Method: In the step (4), aging the nanocellulose composite gel obtained in step (3) at 25-45 degrees C for 5-24 hours, and replacing it with absolute ethanol to remove the reaction waste liquid, and placing the nanocellulose composite gel in a modifying solution to modify and drying it, the volume ratio of the modifier to ethanol is 0.05-1:1. In the step (1), preparing cellulose hydrogel, cellulose, water, wet strength agent after mixing in ice water machine or ultrasonic stirring to obtain cellulose hydrogel, transferring to the freezing and drying device, freezing at -25 degrees C to -60 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours, vacuumizing for 0.5-3 hours under the state of vacuum freeze-drying for 5-20 hours to obtain nano cellulose gas gel. The cellulose is cellulose nanofiber, nano cellulose whiskers, bacterial cellulose or microfibrillated nano cellulose, controlling the degree of vacuum during the freeze-drying process to 5-200 Pa, the diameter of the nano-cellulose in the nano-cellulose aerogel is 3 to 500 nm and length is 100-50,000 nm. In the step (2), mixing silicon source, titanium source, ethanol, stirring well , mixing with deionized water to form a colorless transparent solution, a sol of silicon oxide and titanium oxide. The silicon source includes ethyl orthosilicate, methyl orthosilicate, silica sol, sodium silicate, methyltriethoxysilane, hexamethyldisilazane, trimethylchlorosilane and fluorosilanes. The titanium source includes butyl titanate, titanium nitrate, titanium oxide and titanium chloride. The carbon dioxide supercritical drying temperature is 45-70 degrees C. The pressure is 10-20 MPa. The normal pressure drying adopts a step drying method, drying at 20-60 degrees C for 2-8 hours, drying at 60-80 degrees C for 2-6 hours and drying at 80-120 degrees C for 2-6 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PT P
PN CN110344253-A
TI Method for improving hydrophilic properties of nylon fabric, involves pretreating nylon fabric, treating with amino silicone oil emulsion, treating with subcritical ethanol-water-N-vinylpyrrolidone system, steaming, and drying.
AU ZHANG H
AE HEFEI QIAOZHI TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201989119Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving hydrophilic properties of nylon fabric, involves immersing and stirring the fabric in an ethanol solution for 30-35 minutes, filtering, naturally drying, adding the fabric to a reactor, adding an amino silicone oil emulsion, stirring at 65-68 degrees C for 2 hours, ultrasonically treating for 4-6 minutes, taking the fabric out, washing with deionized water, vacuum-drying to constant weight, adding the fabric to a reactor, adding anhydrous ethanol, stirring at 300 rpm for 35-40 minutes, adding a hot alkaline solution, stirring for 20-25 minutes, adding vinylpyrrolidone, stirring for 10-12 minutes, introducing carbon dioxide to remove air, increasing temperature to 110-112 degrees C, stirring at 500 rpm for 2-3 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, leaving still for 1 hour, filtering, washing with deionized water, vacuum-drying to constant weight, steaming the fabric for 20-25 minutes at 100 degrees C, naturally cooling to room temperature, and drying.
   USE - Method for improving hydrophilic properties of nylon fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides nylon fabric having excellent hydrophilicity, which improves sweat absorption performance, thus providing a fresh and comfortable feel when worn in summer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The hot alkaline solution is 0.2 mol/L sodium hydrogen carbonate solution.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of ethanol solution is 15-20 %mass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The amino silicone oil emulsion is prepared by stir-mixing 5 pts. wt. cetyl alcohol, 20 pts. wt. sodium lauryl sulfate, 25 pts. wt. amino silicone oil, and 40 pts. wt. deionized water at 2000 rpm and 60 degrees C for 1 hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Conditions: The ethanol solution immersion temperature is -1 degrees C. The mass ratio of hot alkaline solution, nylon fabric, absolute ethanol, and vinylpyrrolidone is 35:30:68:4. The emulsion treatment step involves dyeing at less than 104 degrees C for 30 minutes. Ultrasonic processing is carried out at 35 kHz and 800 W. The temperature of hot alkaline solution is 80 degrees C. The carbon dioxide pressure is 3.5 MPa.

PN CN110218652-A
TI Promoting growth and oil/fat accumulation of microalgae in blue-green medium (BG-11) medium by preparing walnut shell extract medium and BG-11 medium, sterilizing, mixing, culturing microalgae in medium, centrifuging and extracting oil/fat.
AU HAN B
   QIAO T
   YU X
   NING D
   GENG S
   MA T
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
   YUNNAN ACAD FORESTRY (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 201979547S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for promoting growth and oil/fat accumulation of microalgae in blue-green medium (BG-11) medium, involves (i) separately preparing walnut shell extract medium and BG-11 medium, adjusting the pH of the walnut shell extract medium to 6.8-7 with sodium hydroxide solution, adjusting the pH of the BG-11 medium to 6.8-7 with hydrochloric acid solution, and separately sterilizing the two media and mixing in a reactor, where the concentration of the walnut shell extract in the mixed medium is 20-50 g/l, (ii) culturing the microalgae by inoculating the oil/fat-producing microalgae into the mixed medium, and introducing a carbon dioxide-air mixed gas to perform light culture, and (iii) culturing the microalgae to a late stage of stability, centrifuging, enriching, and extracting the oil/fat in the microalgae by an organic solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting growth and oil/fat accumulation of microalgae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to operate, effectively utilizes the nutrient components in the walnut shell to increase the biomass and oil/fat content of the microalgae, reduces the production cost and reduces the generation of greenhouse gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The walnut shell extract is prepared by adding 9.5-10.5 times water of walnut shell, and keeping at 93-95 degrees C for 240-260 minutes. In step (i), the sterilization is carried out at 121 degrees C for 20 minutes. In step (iii), the extraction of oil/fat in microalgae involves culturing the microalgae to stable phase, centrifuging at 3500 rpm for 5 minutes, washing twice with distilled water, lyophilizing at -80 degrees C, mixing with 2 times the mass of quartz sand, extracting the oil/fat in the microalgae with chloroform/methanol solution in a volume ratio of 2:1, repeating the extraction 3 times, combining the extracts and drying at 40 degrees C. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the initial inoculum of microalgae is 0.4 g/l, the light intensity is 6500-7000 lux, the carbon dioxide content in the carbon dioxide-air mixed gas is 12%, and the culture temperature is 25 plus minus 1 degrees C. Preferred Microorganisms: The microalgae is a Monoraphidium sp. QLZ-3.

A portion of the pothead body and the insulating block is gold plated inside the pothead body. Diffusion bonding the lead foil to the gold plating of the insulating block.
   USE - Method for implementing a gas seal around a power cable connection to a submersible motor.
   ADVANTAGE - The gold plating and lead foil within pothead may allow a robust seal from downhole gas such as sour gas and carbon dioxide, in spaces that conventionally have been susceptible to gas permeation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas seal apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a pothead.

   Armor (300)
   Lead foil (400)

   USE - A composition for selectively separating oxygen from air.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process which involves contacting a gas stream containing oxygen at 1st concentration with metal-organic framework material having the formula m/Cu-BTC, where BTC is benzene-1,3,5-tricarboxylic acid and m/Cu represents partial substitution of copper (Cu) with metal ion (m) selected from scandium (Sc), titanium (Ti), vanadium (V), manganese (Mn), iron (Fe) and cobalt (Co) at 1st temperature and 1st pressure to adsorb oxygen from gas stream into the metal-organic framework; and desorbing oxygen from the metal-organic framework material at 2nd temperature and 2nd pressure.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The 1st and 2nd temperatures are equal. The 2nd pressure is less than the 1st pressure. The 2nd temperature is greater than the 1st temperature. The 1st temperature is ambient temperature.

The firing step comprises placing the second molded body in an inner shell and placing an inner shell on a firing furnace, supplying oxygen containing gas and/or inert gas into the firing furnace, firing the outer shell, cooling, and pulverizing the fired second molded body.

TI Preparing chimeric antigen receptor thymocyte cells (CAR-T) cell involves collecting donor peripheral blood mononuclear cells, sorting out cluster of differentiation 3 thymocyte lymphocytes from peripheral blood mononuclear cells.
AU LI J
AE SHANGHAI HARMONY CELL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201954505Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chimeric antigen receptor T cells (CAR-T) involves collecting donor peripheral blood mononuclear cells, sorting out cluster of differentiation 3 (CD3+) T lymphocytes from peripheral blood mononuclear cells collected, activating culture of the selected CD3+ T lymphocytes at a culture temperature of 37 degrees C and a carbon dioxide (CO2 ) concentration of 5%, infecting lentiviral vector carrying the gene of interest, activating cultured T lymphocytes, where culture temperature at the time of transfection is 37 degrees C, and the CO2 concentration is 5%, and transfecting by adding dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) in the medium in a ratio of 0.1-5 ml per 100 ml of the medium. The T lymphocytes transfected are subjected to expansion culture at a culture temperature of 37 degrees C and a CO 2 concentration of 5% to obtain a CAR-T cell final product.
   USE - Method for preparing chimeric antigen receptor thymocyte cells (CAR-T) cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare chimeric antigen receptor T cells, which significantly reduces the MOI required for virus transfection, reducing amount of virus, improving transfection efficiency of CAR-T cells, and greatly reducing the preparation cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The content of CD3+ T lymphocytes after sorting needs to be 5.0-10x 106/kg or more. The infecting the lentiviral vector is carried out after T-lymphocyte activation culture for 24-36 hours. The density of T lymphocytes is controlled at 10-1 million cells/ml. The time of virus infection is 18-36 hours. The return requirement is that the ratio of T cells is not less than 60%, and the proportion of CAR-T cells is not less than 2%.

PN CN109806729-A
TI Preparation of aluminophosphate-18 molecular sieve membrane includes mixing aluminum source, phosphorus source and water, adding templating agent N,N-diisopropylethylamine, aging, performing hydrothermal reaction and coating.
AU CHEN X
   ZHAN T
   GUI T
   WU T
AE UNIV JIANGXI NORMAL (UYJX-C)
GA 201948882P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aluminophosphate (AlPO)-18 molecular sieve membrane comprises mixing aluminum source, phosphorus source and water to obtain uniform gel, adding templating agent N,N-diisopropylethylamine, mixing, aging for 6-12 hours to obtain first sol system aluminum oxide-phosphorus pentoxide-N,N-diisopropylethylamine-water (Al2O3-P2O5-DIPEA-H2O), sending to reactor containing liner, performing hydrothermal reaction at 120-210 degrees C to obtain pure phase AEI molecular sieve seed crystal, coating molecular sieve crystal of AEI structure on the surface of the support to obtain seed layer, mixing aluminum source, phosphorus source and water to obtain stable sol, adding template N, N-diisopropylethylamine, mixing, aging for 6-24 hours to obtain second sol system Al2O3-P2O5-DIPEA-H2O, placing support in the reaction vessel, adding second sol system Al2O3-P2O5-DIPEA-H2O to reaction vessel, crystallizing at 120-210 degrees C for 6-20 hours and removing templating agent by calcination.
   USE - Method of preparing aluminophosphate-18 molecular sieve membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces cost, shortens synthesis time and is convenient to operate. The ideal selectivity of AlPO-18 zeolite membrane for carbon dioxide and methane and nitrogen and methane is 88 and 13.69.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aluminum source is aluminum isopropoxide, aluminum powder, aluminum hydroxide or aluminum sec-butyl alcohol. The aluminum source is aluminum isopropoxide,aluminium powder, aluminum hydroxide or aluminum sec-butyl alcohol. The ,ol ratio of P2O5 and Al2O3 is 0.8-1.2. The mol ratio of DIPEA and Al2O3 is 2-6. The mol ratio of H2O and Al2O3 is 100-150.

PN CN109809500-A
TI Preparation of high-nickel ternary positive            electrode material based on precursor pre-oxidation and            air purification by adding hydrogen peroxide to            high-nickel ternary precursor, oxidizing, grinding with            lithium hydroxide, and heating.
AU ZHOU F
   ZHOU H
   LIU Y
AE UNIV NANJING AERONAUTICS & ASTRONAUTICS (UNUA-C)
GA 201948819M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-nickel ternary positive                electrode material based on precursor pre-oxidation                and air purification comprises taking high-nickel                ternary precursor into deionized water and                continuously stirring to obtain precursor solution;                slowly adding dropwise hydrogen peroxide solution                with concentration of 10-30% into precursor                solution and fully oxidizing to obtain high-nickel                ternary precursor; centrifuging and drying to                obtain hydrogen peroxide pre-oxidation precursor;                weighing high-nickel ternary precursor and lithium                hydroxide monohydrate, grinding in mortar and fully                mixing; pumping air into non-volatile alkaline                waste liquid, removing carbon dioxide and acidic                gas in air, and removing water vapor in air using                desiccant to obtain dried nitrogen and oxygen mixed                gas; putting materials into sintering furnace,                passing purified air, heating to 450-550 degrees C                and keeping for at least 4 hours; adequately                grinding in mortar; and heating to 750-850 degrees                C and keeping for at least 16 hours.
   USE - The method is for preparing high-nickel                ternary positive electrode material based on                precursor pre-oxidation and air purification.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good electrochemical                performance, can be used with high-pH waste liquid,                and is low-cost, green and                environment-protecting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dessicant is                  calcium oxide, anhydrous calcium chloride,                  anhydrous magnesium sulfate, solid sodium                  hydroxide, soda lime or anhydrous calcium                  sulfate.

PN CN109775794-A; CN109775794-B
TI Recycling waste recovered organic wastewater by            layered double hydroxide comprises e.g. adding layered            double hydroxide into organic wastewater containing            high concentration of organic matter, adsorbing organic            matter and pre-treating.
AU ZHANG P
   WEN X
   HE T
   XIANG M
   OUYANG S
   PENG G
AE UNIV NANCHANG (NANU-C)
GA 201949183U
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling waste after treating recovered                organic wastewater by layered double hydroxide                comprises (i) adding layered double hydroxide (LDH)                into organic wastewater containing high                concentration of organic matter, adsorbing organic                matter in wastewater using LDH, after the                adsorption is completed, taking the adsorbent                precipitate out by solid-liquid separation, and                pre-treating to obtain dried solid O-LDH waste,                (ii) introducing the solid O-LDH waste produced in                the step (i) into a high temperature resistant                porcelain container, and cracking in a protective                atmosphere in a tube furnace to obtain                graphene-supported material G-LDO product.
   USE - The graphene-supporting material is useful for                organic pollutant adsorption, photocatalytic                reaction, sterilization, lithium battery electrode                processing and supercapacitor processing in                pollution control (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: cracks the waste after adsorbs the                organic matter; converts it into a high value-added                graphene supported material; has simple and easy                process; and is environmentally friendly without                secondary pollution. The graphene-supported                materials: achieves treatment of wastes with                processes of wastes; reduces carbon dioxide                emissions; achieves environmentally-friendly and                high-recycling utilization of adsorbed                products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                graphene-supporting material obtained by above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The maximum temperature of                  the tube furnace described in the step (ii) is                  500-700 degrees C, the shielding gas is nitrogen                  gas , and the aeration rate is 100 ml/minutes. In                  step (ii), the temperature regulation program of                  the tube furnace is divided into three stages,                  the initial temperature of the first stage is                  20-30 degrees C to 200-250 degrees C at a heating                  rate of 20-30 degrees C for 20-25 minutes; the                  second stage is increased from 200-250 degrees C                  to 500-700 degrees C at a heating rate of 10                  degrees C/minutes, and at a constant temperature                  of 500-700 degrees C for 240 minutes, the solid                  O-LDH waste is cracked; and the third stage is                  reduced from 500-700 degrees C to 300-350 degrees                  C at a cooling rate of 13-15 degrees C/minutes,                  and then naturally cooling to room temperature.                  The layered double metal hydroxide is magnesium                  aluminum layered bimetal hydroxide (Mg/Al-LDH),                  calcium aluminum layered bimetal hydroxide                  (Ca/Al-LDH) or nickel aluminum layered bimetal                  hydroxides (Ni/Al-LDH).

TI Preparing pure natural plant shaving water            comprises e.g. preparing dry shavings, controlling            thickness, storing in container to avoid moisture and            mildew, crushing shaving to maintain required size,            sifting pulverized piece and distilling.
AU MA P
   LU Z
   CHEN C
   FU P
   SU Z
   HUANG W
AE ZHONGSHAN POLYTECHNIC (ZHON-Non-standard)
   ZHONGSHAN XIAOSHENDAO COMMERCE & TRADE (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2019391146
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pure natural plant shaving water                comprises (i) preparing dry shavings, controlling                thickness not greater than 1 mm, storing in                container at 2-6 degrees C to avoid moisture and                mildew, (ii) crushing the shavings to maintain the                size of 30-40 meshes, (iii) sifting pulverized                piece, adding 50-100 times mixture of ultrapure                water, ethanol and ether, extracting by                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 3-8                hours, (iv) distilling at 30-45 degrees C under                vacuum to remove ethanol and diethyl ether, and (v)                adding 0.5-5 pts. wt. natural plant preservative in                filtrate, maintaining for 160-200 days at 30-40                degrees C, Obtaining shaving water without high                activity, repeating same addition procedure,                placing solution in the sun for 50-70 days,                maintaining temperature at 20-35 degrees C to                obtain ultraviolet-resistant shaving water,                repeating at different temperature intervals of                2-40 degrees C for total duration of 160-200 days                to obtain natural safe shaving water.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pure                natural plant shaving water.
   ADVANTAGE - The natural plant shaving water: has good                activity, and long-lasting effect of hair care; and                is safe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of                  ultrapure water, ethanol, and diethyl ether is                  5-10:0-4:4-0. The natural plant preservative                  comprises bamboo leaf extract, Aloe vera gel,                  Geranium extract, licorice extract, Ginkgo leaf                  extract, Magnolia extract, clove extract and/or                  cinnamon extract in the ratio of                  2-10:0-2:0-2:0-2:0-1:0-1:0-1. Preferred Method:                  In the step (iii), the extracting temperature is                  25-40 degrees C and extraction pressure is 30-40                  MPa. In the step (iv), adding 0.5-1.5 wt.%                  activated carbon in the extract, heating to 30-40                  degrees C, stirring for 10-15 minutes, filtering                  under vacuum while hot, and collecting the                  filtrate.

TI Foamable composition for foam premix composition comprises thermoplastic foam forming material and blowing agent which comprises trans-1,1,1,3-tetrafluoropropene, and 1,1,1-trifluoro-3-chloro-propene, dimethylether, ethanol, and/or acetone.
AU BOWMAN J M
   WILLIAMS D J
AE HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
GA 2019345651
AB    NOVELTY - A foamable composition comprises thermoplastic foam forming material and blowing agent which comprises (a) trans-1,1,1,3-tetrafluoropropene (trans-HFO-1234ze); and (b) greater than or equal to 1 compound selected from 1,1,1-trifluoro-3-chloro-propene (HFCO-1233zd), dimethylether, ethanol, acetone, and/or carbon dioxide.
   USE - A foamable composition for foam premix composition (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has low flammability. The improved blowing agent is generally compatible with existing foaming methods and systems. The foams produced from the composition can achieve, preferably in connection with thermoset foam, exceptional thermal performance, such as can be measured by the K-factor or lambda, particularly and preferably under low temperature conditions. Other advantages of the foam includes thermal insulation efficiency, dimensional stability, compressive strength, aging of thermal insulation properties, all in addition to the low ozone depletion potential and low global warming potential associated with numerous preferred blowing agents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) formation of foam which involves foaming the foamable composition; and
   (2) foam comprising a set of polymeric cells and a composition contained in greater than or equal to 1 cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The greater than or equal to 1 compound comprises HFCO-1233zd. HFCO-1233zd comprises trans-1,1,1,trifluoro-3-chloro-propene (trans-HFCO-1233zd). Foamable composition comprises 1-50 wt.%, preferably 20-80 wt.% of the greater than or equal to 1 compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Foam forming agent comprises greater than or equal to 1 thermoset foam component selected from components capable of forming polyurethane foam, polyisocyanurate foam, and/or phenolic foam. Foam forming agent comprises greater than or equal to 1 thermoplastic foam component selected from monovinyl aromatic compounds, ethylene-based compounds, and/or propylene-based polymers. Preferred Properties: The foam is in the form of block, slab, laminate, rigid foam, open cell foam, closed cell foam, flexible foam, integeral skin foam, refrigerator foam, and freezer foam. The foam has a k-factor (BTU in/hour ft2 degrees F.) at 40 degrees F of not greater than 0.14.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109593024-A
TI Production of industrial-grade 1,2-propanediol by feeding dimethyl carbonate and crude propylene glycol in rectification column, concentrating, carrying out carbonization and pressure filtration, and introducing gas phase propylene glycol.
AU YU T
   FANG Y
   ZHANG S
   XU L
   YIN F
   XIE C
AE TONGLING JINTAI CHEM IND CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 201935212Q
AB    NOVELTY - Production of an industrial-grade 1,2-propanediol comprises reacting propylene carbonate, methanol, and sodium methoxide to form dimethyl carbonate and crude propylene glycol through a reaction rectification column; feeding dimethyl carbonate and crude propylene glycol in the rectification column, and discharging light components including methanol, dimethyl carbonate, propylene glycol, and propylene glycol monomethyl ether, returning the light components to an original reactive distillation column at 50-80 KPa and 150-170 degrees C; concentrating in a propylene glycol distillation column horizontal kettle for 4-5 times at 5-20 KPa and 120-140 degrees C; carrying out carbonization by adding 1-3% water and 2-4% carbon dioxide in a carbonization tank, carrying out pressure filtration, and stirring slurry at 30-50 Hz and 100-120 degrees C; and returning mother liquor to the rectification column, and introducing gas phase propylene glycol by heating at 120-135 degrees C and 5-20 KPa.
   USE - Method for producing an industrial-grade 1,2-propanediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, safe, and environmentally friendly. It has low energy consumption, has high automation degree, and produces 99.5% 1,2-propanediol.

PT P
PN CN109550499-A; CN109550499-B
TI New catalyst useful for decomposing heavy components in neopentyl glycol synthesized waste liquid.
AU WEN D
   KONG X
   LI J
   XU Y
   WANG L
   GUO X
   CHANG L
   ZHANG H
   HUA W
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 201932566V
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst, is new. The catalyst is a copper/magnesium-zirconium-aluminum-oxygen composite base catalyst. The copper element exists in the form of basic copper carbonate and loaded on magnesium-zirconium-aluminum-oxygen solid base.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for decomposing heavy components in neopentyl glycol synthesized waste liquid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst improves economic benefit of the device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing catalyst, comprising (i) adding soluble salts of magnesium, zirconium and aluminum into water to obtain solution A, and dissolving sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate in water to obtain solution B, (ii) adding solution A and solution B into the water, adjusting the pH of the feed control solution to 9-10, stirring at 40-60 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, (iii) placing the mixture in a thermostatic chamber, aging, filtering and washing to neutrality, drying the filter cake and calcining to obtain magnesium-zirconium-aluminum-oxygen solid base, and (iv) impregnating the magnesium-zirconium-aluminum-oxygen solid base in a soluble salt solution of copper, and calcining in a carbon dioxide atmosphere; and
   (2) use method of catalyst for decomposing heavy components in neopentyl glycol synthesis waste liquid, comprising adding the catalyst into the fixed bed reactor, carrying out ester decomposition reaction, separating the neopentyl glycol and recovering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises 10-30 wt.% magnesium oxide, preferably 15-25 wt.%, 210-30 wt.% zirconium oxide, preferably 10-20 wt.%, 310-30 wt.% aluminum oxide, preferably 10-20% and 30-50 wt.% copper carbonate, preferably 35-45 wt.%. The specific surface area of the catalyst is 300-500 m2/g, and the channel radius is 30-150 nm. In the step (i), the soluble salt of magnesium includes magnesium chloride, magnesium sulfate and/or magnesium nitrate, the soluble salt of zirconium includes zirconium chloride, zirconium sulfate, and/or zirconium nitrate, the soluble salt of aluminum includes aluminum chloride, aluminum sulfate, and/or aluminum nitrate. In the step (iv), the soluble salt of copper includes copper chloride, copper sulfate, and/or copper nitrate. The molar ratio of magnesium, zirconium and aluminum is 1:0.1-2.0:0.2-2.5, the molar ratio of sodium hydroxide to sodium carbonate is 0.5-1.0:1, the total mass of sodium hydroxide and sodium carbonate is 1-5 times, preferably 1-3.5 times the mass of the total mass of magnesium, zirconium and aluminum. In the step (iv), the soluble salt solution of copper has concentration of 1-10 mol/l and immersed in equal volume for 6-12 hours. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the stirring speed is 400-600 revolutions/minute. In the step (ii), the aging temperature is 60-80 degrees C, and the aging time is 16-24 hours, drying temperature is 50-70 degrees C, drying time is 16-24 hours, the calcination temperature is 600-800 degrees C, and the calcination time is 6-8 hours. In the step (iv), the calcination temperature is 60-80 degrees C, and the calcination time is 6-8 hours. The ester decomposition reaction process, the reactor bed temperature is 80-150 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 0.1-0.3 Mpa, the liquid hourly space velocity is 1-3 per hour, and the reaction time is 2-4 hours. The catalyst is 20-40 mesh particles before use.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN109485176-A
TI Treating graphene production wastewater involves            adding calcium oxide or calcium hydroxide to graphene            production wastewater, adjusting pH, stirring and            carrying out solid-liquid separation by pressure            filtration.
AU WANG S
AE BEIJING SHENGMENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201926900F
AB    NOVELTY - Treating graphene production wastewater                involves adding 200-400g calcium oxide or 400-600g                calcium hydroxide to 1000 L of graphene production                wastewater, adjusting pH to 5-6, stirring for 3-5                hours, carrying out the solid-liquid separation by                pressure filtration through a plate and frame                filter press, collecting the filtrate and the                precipitate, drying the precipitate to be reserved,                introducing 300-600g of carbon dioxide into the                above filtrate, adding a pH adjuster to adjust pH                to 8 and stirring for 1 hour, adding 100-200g                oxidant to wastewater, reacting for 1 hour under                the condition of electric stirring speed of 500                revolutions per minute, adding 300-400g flocculant,                adjusting pH to 7-8 with cesium hydroxide, stirring                for 0.5-1 hour into plate and frame filter press,                sedimenting, concentrating and dehydrating for                solid waste treatment, passing the supernatant                liquid into water supply tank, and discharging into                the municipal sewage pipeline after reaching the                discharge standard.
   USE - Method for treating graphene production                wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat graphene                production wastewater, which improves the                industrial output value, and the water treated has                low salt content and can be directly discharged                into the municipal sewage pipe network without                affecting the subsequent biochemical                treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Condittions: The oxidant is                  hydrogen peroxide or sodium hypochlorite, and the                  flocculating agent is any one of polyacrylamide,                  polyferric sulfate, or polyaluminum chloride. The                  pH adjuster is one or two of barium hydroxide and                  barium carbonate. The rate of carbon dioxide is                  100 L/minute. The temperature at which the                  precipitate is dried is 100 degrees C for 5                  hours.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN109467062-A
TI Preparation of detonation of boron nitride quantum            dot, involves collecting nano-sized boron nitride            powder on inner wall of container, washing with            deionized water and mixing boron nitride powder with            alkaline aqueous solution and heating.
AU MIAO Z
   TIAN H
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 201926542Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of detonation of boron nitride                quantum dot, involves (1) placing hexagonal boron                nitride into an explosion container, evacuating the                explosion container into a vacuum, using carbon                dioxide, argon or nitrogen as a shielding gas,                igniting and detonating the drug column, and                discharging the exhaust gas after the product is                settled, collecting nano-sized boron nitride powder                on the inner wall and bottom of the container,                washing with deionized water to neutrality, drying                and storing in a drying dish, and (2) mixing boron                nitride powder with alkaline aqueous solution into                a hydrothermal kettle, heating to obtain                edge-modified boronated boron nitride, and                centrifuging the reaction product, adding water or                organic solvent to ultrasonic treatment.
   USE - Preparation of detonation of boron nitride                quantum dot.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of detonation                of boron nitride quantum dot with high quantum dot                yield, and the modified hydroxyl group at the edge                of boron nitride improves the efficiency of                ultrasonic stripping and increases the dispersion                of quantum dots in solution, and has high                reproducibility and is easy to control and                processing steps are simple, and yield is                high.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (1), the                  vacuum pressure is less than 100 Pa. In step (2),                  the heating is carried out at 120-160 degrees C                  for 6-12 hours. In step (2), the ultrasonic                  treatment is carried out for 0.5-12 hours.                  Preferred Property: In step (1), the nano-sized                  boron nitride particles are mainly distributed at                  1-20 nm. Preferred Components: In step (2), the                  alkaline solution includes potassium hydroxide,                  sodium hydroxide, and barium hydroxide. Preferred                  Composition: In step (2), the concentration of                  boron nitride is 0.5-10 mg/mL, and the                  concentration of the alkali solution is 1-10                  mol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (2), the                  organic solvent includes N-methylpyrrolidone, an                  aldehyde, an organic acid, dimethylformamide,                  dimethylacetamide, chlorobenzene, dichlorobenzene                  and preferably N-methylpyrrolidone.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hydrogen gas involves reacting                mineral ion water containing at least an alkaline                earth metal ion and having a pH of 11-14 with a                mineral-supported high-temperature calcined                carbonaceous material obtained by impregnating a                biological-origin carbon precursor with mineral ion                water and calcining at a high temperature.
   USE - Manufacture of hydrogen gas produced in                general household, food factory and vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and                environmentally-friendly, and can produce hydrogen                with high purity at low cost, and can continuously                and stably supply hydrogen gas without emitting                carbon dioxide.

   (2) manufacture of mineral ion water, which                involves dissolving oxide of at least magnesium or                calcium, hydroxide, carbonate or hydrogen carbonate                in water to incorporate alkaline earth metal ions                to water, and adjusting pH to produce a mineral ion                water for dissolving water-soluble component of an                incineration material of a living organism                preferably protista or a plant; and
   (3) manufacture of antioxidized water derived                from the mineral ion water, which involves                optionally carrying out the quality change of the                mineral ion water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The biological-origin                  carbon precursor is impregnated with an alkaline                  aqueous solution formed by dissolving a flame                  retardant in the mineral ion water. The high                  temperature calcining is microwave excitation                  carbon plasma calcining. Preferred Components:                  The biological carbon precursor is a plant,                  seaweed, charcoal or bamboo charcoal.

TI Environmentally friendly mobile phone battery, has explosion-proof insulating sleeve which is aluminum protective cover whose inner surface is adhered to insulating sealing layer.
AU WANG C
AE ZHANGJIAJIE YUCHEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019246114
AB    NOVELTY - The battery has battery (1), explosion-proof insulating sleeve (2), alumina protective cover and battery explosion-proof diaphragm. The battery is sealed and wrapped with an explosion-proof insulating sleeve. The explosion-proof insulating sleeve is an aluminum protective cover. The inner surface of the alumina protective cover is adhered to the insulating sealing layer. The surface of the alumina protective cover is fitted with a battery explosion-proof diaphragm. The surface of the battery explosion-proof diaphragm is pressed at a high temperature to prevent the anti-static film.
   USE - Environmentally friendly mobile phone battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The battery explosion-proof diaphragm can reduce the impact damage of the explosion caused by the battery to the surrounding environment when the battery is exploding, and reduce the pressure and the ejection of the fragments. The alumina protective sleeve has good electrical conductivity and unique metal strength. The antistatic film can reduce the electrostatic effect on the peripheral surface of the battery, and the insulating sealing layer made of the carbon dioxide-based biodegradable plastic material has good degradability characteristics and environmental protection while satisfying the insulating effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of an environmentally friendly mobile phone battery.
   Battery (1)
   Explosion-proof insulating sleeve (2)

PT P
PN CN109422779-A
TI Process for hydrolysis of hair into complex amino acid used in e.g. fertilizer, involves heating water, adding complexing agent to obtain complexing solution, adding with solid strong base, adding dried hair, adding catalyst and stirring.
AU LU B
AE SHANYANG HENGRUI MEAT PROD CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019233582
AB    NOVELTY - The process for hydrolysis of hair into complex amino acid involves washing hair, drying and leaving still, taking 400-500 pts. wt. water, heating at 90-100 degrees C under normal pressure, adding 3-57 pts. wt. complexing agent, stirring until complexing agent is completely dissolved to obtain complexing solution, adding 12-50 pts. wt. solid strong base to the obtained complexing solution, maintaining the temperature of the solution at 90-100 degrees C, stirring until solid strong base is completely dissolved, adding 70-100 pts. wt. hair, maintaining the temperature of solution at 90-100 degrees C, adding 4-28 pts. wt. catalyst, stirring for 5-30 minutes until hair is completely dissolved, leaving still and cooling to room temperature, stirring obtained solution and adjusting pH of solution to 6-7 with a dilute acid.
   USE - Process for hydrolysis of hair into complex amino acid used in fertilizer, feed additive, feed, medicine, pesticide, food processing and chemical industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables hydrolysis of hair into complex amino acid with energy saving effect and low carbon dioxide emission by simple, short, economical and environmentally-friendly process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complexing agent is copper salt complexing agent. The copper salt complexing agent is copper sulfate pentahydrate or copper chloride. The catalyst is zinc salt catalyst. The zinc salt catalyst is zinc chloride or zinc sulfate or zinc sulfate and zinc chloride. The strong base is potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide. The acid is dilute hydrochloric acid or dilute sulfuric acid. The concentration of dilute hydrochloric acid or dilute sulfuric acid is 1-50 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The complex amino acid is produced by washing hair, drying and leaving still, taking 400-500 pts. wt. dilute acid solution, heating at 90-100 degrees C under normal pressure, adding 3-57 pts. wt. complexing agent, stirring until complexing agent is completely dissolved to obtain complexing solution, adding 70-100 pts. wt. hair, maintaining the temperature of solution at 90-100 degrees C, adding 4-28 pts. wt. catalyst, stirring for 5-30 minutes until hair is completely dissolved, leaving still and cooling to room temperature, stirring obtained solution with strong base to adjust pH of solution to 6-7.

   YAO X

GA 201897249V

PN CN108865893-A; CN108865893-B
TI Performing alkali flocculation harvesting and circulating cultivation of microalgae involves cultivating microalgae using initial medium containing bicarbonate, and after completion of cultivation, harvesting portion or algae liquid.
AU CHI Z
   CUI Q
   ZHU C
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2018954488
AB    NOVELTY - Performing alkali flocculation harvesting and circulating cultivation of microalgae involves cultivating the microalgae using an initial medium containing bicarbonate, and after the completion of the cultivation, harvesting portion or all of the algae liquid. The pH of the harvested algae solution is adjusting to 10-12, and adding a certain concentration of calcium ions and/or irons ions for flocculation and sedimentation, and harvesting the microalgae to obtain supernatant. The supernatant obtained by flocculation and sedimentation is supplemented with carbon dioxide to pH 8-9. The multiple nutrients are add to the supernatant obtained to a concentration in the initial medium to prepare a circulating medium. The obtained circulating medium is subjecting to physical or chemical treatment, and recycling the cultured microalgae and repeat the perform process to obtain the desired process.
   USE - Method for performing alkali flocculation harvesting and circulating cultivation of microalgae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform alkali flocculation harvesting and circulating cultivation of microalgae, which has high alkalinity to add flocculating ions with low concentration flocculation ions to flocculate sedimentation, achieves reuse of carbon source and nutrient salt, and saves the harvesting and cultivation cost of microalgae, and has important application prospects for large-scale cultivation of microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae is Dunaliella salina. The physical or chemical method is an oxidizing agent. The oxidizing agent is sodium hypochlorite, ozone, sodium dichloroisocyanurate or hydrogen peroxide at a concentration of 0.1-0.5 mmol/L. Preferred Conditions: The microalgae cultivation contains medium of pH is 9.5-11.5. The calcium ions concentration added is 4-16 mmol/L. The iron ions concentration added is 80 mu mol/L or more. The calcium ions and iron ions are added. The physical or chemical method is a filter membrane having a pore diameter of 0.001-0.5 mu m. The physical or chemical metho is activated carbon, and the concentration of the activated carbon is 0.5-5 g/L. Preferred Compositions: The formulation of the initial culture medium of Dunaliella salina comprises 1.2-1.7 mol/L sodium chloride, 0.07-0.3 mol/L sodium bicarbonate, 5.0asterisk103-1.0asterisk102mol/L potassium nitrate, 1.0asterisk104-3.5asterisk104 mol/L dipotassium phosphate, 5.0asterisk103-1.0asterisk104 mol/L calcium chloride, 5.0asterisk104-1.0asterisk103 mol/L magnesium chloride, 2.0asterisk103-2.5asterisk104 mol/L magnesium sulfate, 1.0asterisk105-3.0asterisk105 mol/L ferric chloride hexahydrate, 1.0asterisk103-3.0asterisk103 mol/L disodium disodium ethylenediaminetetraacetate dihydrate and 1 mL/L A5 element.

TI Preparation of nickel oxide powder material involves dehydrating and drying paste nickel carbonate or powder nickel carbonate by rotary flash dryer, carrying out solid-gas separation of dried powder nickel carbonate material and recovering.
AU GU Y

   GUO J
   HE Y
AE JINCHUAN GROUP CO LTD (JCJT-C)
   LANZHOU JINCHUAN SCI PARK CO LTD (LANZ-Non-standard)
GA 201886135W
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of nickel oxide powder material involves dehydrating and drying paste nickel carbonate or powder nickel carbonate by rotary flash dryer, carrying out solid-gas separation of dried powder nickel carbonate material using a cyclone separator, collecting separated solid-gas and recovering separated solid-gas, calcining collected solid-gas in a calcining furnace at high temperature to form nickel oxide and carbon dioxide, cooling to 450-480 degrees C, placing nickel oxide and water in a washing kettle at mass ratio of 1:30-1:40, heating at 90-95 degrees C, washing with water and stirring for 50-60 minutes, pumping water-washed nickel oxide slurry into a filter press, filter pressing and filtering, placing the filter cake in a rotary flash dryer and drying, carrying out solid-gas separation by a cyclone separator and collecting solid nickel oxide, sampling and analyzing the collected solid nickel oxide, and packaging qualified nickel oxide powder material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nickel oxide powder material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of nickel oxide powder material with improved recovery rate, quality and washing effect.
