AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 2018813203
AB    NOVELTY - Intensive turbidity and descaling water purification device comprises dosing mechanism for dosing raw water, where water purification device further includes a water inlet mechanism (2), an aeration stripping mechanism (3), a sedimentation mechanism (4), and a filtering mechanism (5). The raw water passes through the dosing mechanism to generate floccules and a small amount of carbon dioxide gas, the raw water is sequentially introduced into the aeration stripping mechanism, sedimentation mechanism and the filtering mechanism via the water inlet mechanism. The aeration stripping mechanism is used for aerating and stripping the raw water after the dosing to separate the generated flocs and gasses from the raw water. The sedimentation mechanism is used for precipitating the solid matter in the raw water after the aeration stripping treatment. The filtering mechanism is for filtering the remaining impurities in the raw water after the precipitation treatment.
   USE - Intensive turbidity and descaling water purification device.
   ADVANTAGE - The intensive turbidity and descaling water purification device has reduced running cost, greatly increases filtering mechanism, and reduces times of back washing.

PN CN108455668-A; CN108455668-B
TI Treating arsenic-containing waste acid using green            resource comprises adding macromolecule arsenic capture            agent or solution into arsenic-containing waste acid,            dehydrating, concentrating, drying, roasting, and            oxidizing or discharging.
AU MIN X
   XU Z
   FANG F
   LIU Y
   MA C
   LUO K
   LI L
   ZHENG P
AE JIANGSU BAOSTEEL PRECISION STEEL WIRE CO (BAOS-C)
   ZHANGJIAGANG GREEN TAIKE ENVIRONMENTAL (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201869001V
AB    NOVELTY - Treating arsenic-containing waste acid using                green resource comprises (i) adding macromolecule                arsenic capture agent or a solution into the                arsenic-containing waste acid, promoting uniform                mixing and reaction of the two by stirring to                produce a poorly soluble arsenic-containing                precipitate, (ii) separating the arsenic-containing                precipitate from the mother liquor by a filter                press or a centrifuge device, performing                dehydration, reusing the supernatant directly as a                recovered acid or concentrating into by-product                acid, and (iii) subjecting the arsenic-containing                precipitate to drying, roasting, sublimation, and                cooling to obtain high-purity arsenic trioxide, and                absorbing the generated sulfur dioxide or carbon                dioxide gas by a gas absorbent, or oxidizing by a                catalyst, or treating to discharge after reaching                the standard, where the arsenic removal agent used                is a chemically stable macromolecule arsenic                capture agent, which can directly remove arsenic in                a strong acidic environment.
   USE - The method is useful for treating                arsenic-containing waste acid using green                resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high-value recycling of                arsenic-containing waste acid, no waste water,                solid waste and toxic gas, does not need to add                alkali and inorganic arsenic removal agent and is                simple, safe and environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The macromolecule                  arsenic capture agent includes mercapto-modified                  saccharides, polymeric thiols, thiocarboxylic                  acids, thiocyanic acid, thiopolyolefin,                  thioaromatic compounds, and thioheterocyclic                  compounds. The gas absorbent is at least one of                  an alkali metal or alkaline earth metal compound                  and its solution, water, and sulfuric acid. The                  catalyst includes oxide of vanadium, manganese,                  titanium, rare earth oxide, and organic-inorganic                  compound catalyst. Preferred Method: The                  arsenic-containing precipitate has a drying                  temperature of 80-200 degrees C and a roasting                  temperature of 500-1200 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN108455635-A
TI Extracting lithium from lithium-containing raw material containing one-step separation and preparing lithium carbonate comprises crushing lithium-containing raw material to obtain powder particles, then adding auxiliary agent and stirring.
AU OUYANG X
   MAO P
   BAO R
   MAO L
AE HUNAN TRAILBLAZER ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 201869169G
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lithium from lithium-containing raw material containing one-step separation and preparing lithium carbonate comprises crushing the lithium-containing raw material to obtain powder particles, then adding the auxiliary agent, and stirring uniformly and uniformly adding the lithium-containing raw material to which the auxiliary agent is added into the heating chamber, where the heating cavity is fixed in the material rolling device, then uniformly heating the material to volatilize the gas, and spraying the volatilized gas in the sealed automatic spray recovery gas device to absorb the solution, purifying and decontaminating the solution obtained after spraying, adjusting the pH value, filtering, subjecting to ion exchange and analyzing the desorption liquid, passing the carbon dioxide gas, aqueous ammonia or carbonate into the desorption liquid to obtain solution.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting lithium from lithium-containing raw material containing one-step separation and preparing lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short preparation process, high production rate, high product purity, saves energy and emission reduction, thus solves the problem of extracting and utilizing lithium resources from low-grade lithium-containing raw materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lithium-containing raw material is lithium mica, spodumene, iron mica lithium, apatite and lithium feldspar, where the powder particles are crushed to obtain ball mill with the particle size of 20-100 meshes. The auxiliary agent is an oxide and a chloride, and the oxide is sodium oxide, potassium oxide, magnesium oxide, and calcium oxide, where the chloride is sodium chloride, calcium chloride, ammonium chloride, potassium chloride and magnesium chloride and the amount of the oxide added is 5-50 wt.% and the amount of the chloride added is 1-30 wt.%. The absorbing liquid used in the spray recovery gas device is hydrochloric acid solution, sodium chloride solution or sulfuric acid solution. The pH adjustment is performed by using sodium hydroxide solution, and the end point of the pH is adjusted to 1-5, the resin is D201, D301, D314, D113, D630 and/or D231, the ion exchange rate is 0.5-10 cubic/hour, the desorption liquid is 3-30% sodium hydroxide solution; The carbonate solution is sodium carbonate solution, potassium carbonate solution and/or magnesium carbonate solution. Preferred Method: The heating temperature in the material rolling device is 600-1300 degrees C, the residence time of the lithium-containing material to which the additive is added is 1-2 hours in the material rolling device, and the gas volatilization time is 30-90 minutes.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)
MC A12-M; A12-W11; E11-W; E33-G

TI Environment controlled multi span structured greenhouse has capture blower and release blower which function with compressor, capture manifold, two dehumidifying tanks, release manifold and earth tube heat exchanger.
AU GUPTA S P
AE GUPTA S P (GUPT-Individual)
   GUPTA S P (GUPT-Individual)
   GUPTA S P (GUPT-Individual)
   GUPTA S P (GUPT-Individual)
GA 201865362N
AB    NOVELTY - The greenhouse has a capture blower and a release blower which function with a compressor, a capture manifold (110), two dehumidifying tanks, a release manifold (115) and an earth tube heat exchanger. The release blower functions with the release manifold to release into the greenhouse at cultivation level. The oxygen rich dehumidified conditioned greenhouse air is mixed with existing humid greenhouse air to adjust relative humidity of the greenhouse air to a predetermined set point, or to adjust the temperature of the greenhouse air to a predetermined temperature set point by utilizing evaporative cooling during the dark hours. The release blower functions with the release manifold to release into the greenhouse at a cultivation level during the light hours the captured carbon dioxide rich air from first compartment of the earth tube heat exchanger, and release into greenhouse during the dark hours the captured oxygen rich air from second compartment of heat exchanger.
   USE - Environment controlled multi span structured greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The environment controlled multi span structured greenhouse is provide with a useful or commercial choice.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the environment controlled multi span structured greenhouse.

TI Sensor device for detecting permanent gas such as hydrogen gas, has adsorption filter that is connected or glued to carrier to close opening so that gas to be detected diffuses through main portion towards sensing element.
AU ROECK F
   HUNZIKER W
   ROCK F
AE SENSIRION AG (SESN-C)
   SENSIRION AG (SESN-C)
GA 201854816J
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has an adsorption filter (30) that comprises a main portion (2) consisting of a molecular sieve material. A sensing element (10) detects a gas (G). A carrier (4) carries the sensing element. The carrier comprises an opening (50) through which gas to be detected reaches the sensing element. The adsorption filter is connected or glued to the carrier to close the opening so that the gas to be detected diffuses through the main portion towards the sensing element. The adsorption filter comprises merely a single main portion of molecular sieve material. The main portion is bead, section of bead, pellet, and plate.
   USE - Sensor device for detecting permanent gas such as hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and methane gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The performance of gas sensor devices for permanent gases is improved by the use of adsorption filters.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing sensor device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view and top view of the three sensor devices.
   Sensor device (1)
   Main portion of adsorption filter (2)
   Carrier (4)
   Sensing element (10)
   Adsorption filter (30)
   Opening of carrier (50)
   Gas (G)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The molecular sieve material is or comprises zeolite, silica gel, clay, activated carbon, polymer adsorbent, aromatic polyimide, polyether ether ketone (PEEK), perfluoropolymer, poly(2,6-diphenylphenylene oxide), and cellulose acetate.

TI High density culturing of Saccharomyces cerevisiae            yeast useful in field of food, health care or microbial            fermentation comprises storing inclined strain into            first-level and second-level seed culture,            centrifuging, washing and inoculating.
AU LI X
   YU X
   LI Z
   TAN Y
   WU Y
AE ANQEL YEAST CO LTD (ANQE-Non-standard)
GA 201853340M
AB    NOVELTY - High density culturing of Saccharomyces                cerevisiae yeast comprises (i) storing inclined                strain to the first-level and second-level seed                culture, (ii) centrifuging, and washing enriched S.                cerevisiae seed, (iii) inoculating the S.                cerevisiae seed into fermenting tank to ferment and                culture, where ventilation ratio of the                fermentation tank is 1.6-2.5 VVM, dissolved oxygen                volume is 45-70%, real-time monitoring of the                physiological state of the cells, regulating the                rate of fed feed according to physiological                parameters monitored in real time, where the                physiological parameter comprises carbon dioxide                release rate, oxygen uptake rate, and respiratory                quotient, the fermentation regulation process does                not require additional addition of inorganic salts,                acids, bases, and trace elements except for the                addition of feed, and (iv) completing fermentation,                separation washing, and collecting S.                cerevisiae.
   USE - The yeast is useful in field of food, health                care or microbial fermentation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and safe. The                yeast is food grade has high protein content and                high conversion rate of fermentation raw material                without acid or alkali.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The feed fed during                  the fermentation regulation process comprises                  carbon source and nitrogen source, the carbon                  source comprising one of molasses, sucrose,                  glucose or wort, the yeast source comprises yeast                  extract or corn syrup, instruments used for                  online real-time monitoring comprises biological                  exhaust gas analyzers. The fed feed during the                  fermentation regulation process is molasses and                  yeast soaking powder. The method comprises                  precipitating the molasses by slag, and                  acidifying and hydrolyzing to obtain fermented                  molasses having a reducing sugar content of                  20-25%, and the acid is sulfuric acid,                  hydrochloric acid, nitric acid or phosphoric                  acid. The respiratory quotient value in the                  fermentation regulation process is controlled at                  0.6-1.2, when the respiratory quotient value is                  greater than 1.2, the feed rate of the feed is                  reduced, when the respiratory quotient value is                  less than 0.6, the feed rate of the feed is                  increased. The fermentation regulation process                  controls the pH at 4.2-6. In the step (i), the                  primary culture medium used in the first-stage                  seed culture comprises 1-2% yeast extract, 2-4%                  peptone and 2-4% glucose, inoculation of the                  primary seed culture amount is calculated by                  inoculating one ring strain per 200-250 ml of the                  primary medium, and the culture condition is                  28-32 degrees C, and the culture is carried out                  for 18-26 hours at 200-300 revolution/minute                  (rpm). In the step (iii), the inoculation amount                  is 10-15% of the volume of the liquid in the                  fermenter, the fermentation temperature is 26-35                  degrees C, the pressure is 0.03-0.15 MPa, and the                  time is 14-20 hours. The S. cerevisiae seed has a                  wet weight of 400-700 g/l. In the step (iv), the                  S. cerevisiae cell density is 60-80 g/l on a dry                  basis. In the step (iv), the method of collecting                  the cells to centrifuging at 15-20 degrees C at                  speed of 4500-6000 rpm for 10-20 minutes. The S.                  cerevisiae strain is S. cerevisiae FX-2, which is                  deposited with the China center for type culture                  collection under the accession number                  M2016418.

The Aspergillus mildew is selected from Aspergillus flavus, Penicillum is selected from Penicillium polonicum, Penicillium chrysogenum, Trichoderma spore mildew is dendritic branch Mortierella, Cladosporium cladosporioides, Fusarium disease is from Fusarium solani and Fusarium oxysporum, mildew of alternaria genus is selected from ginseng Alternaria panax, Nectria mildew is selected from pseudo-ginseng nectria Ilyonectria mors-panacis and robust nectria Ilyonectria robusta. The surface of object is selected from mildew growth or storage environment selected from group consisting of ginseng, American ginseng, pseudo-ginseng, Caladium, radix astragali, licorice, radix codonopsitis, Angelica, Achyranthes, balloon flower, coltsfoot flower, radix isatidis, red sage root, safflower, Ophiopogon root, rhizoma bletillae, medicinal plant rhizoma ligustici wallichii, bighead Atractylodes rhizome, lily, Rhodiola rosea, Polygonatum, almond, peach, tangerine seed, spina date seed, Polygala root. Preferred Conditions: The field crops are selected from rice, wheat, corn, rice, peanut and soybean, vegetable crops are selected from onion, potato, tomato, cucumber, sweet potato, celery, hot pepper and eggplant selected from group consisting of tobacco and other economic crops selected from mango, lichee, grape, orange, strawberry, loquat and apple and passion fruit crops.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Tsaoko cardamom essential oil comprises 50-63 wt.%, preferably, 1,8-cineole and 1-3.5 wt.% citral. Preferred Components: The solvent used in emulsion is selected from group consisting of methanol, ethanol, normal butanol, normal propyl alcohol, isopropanol, isobutyl alcohol, n-amyl alcohol, isoamyl alcohol, hexanol, acetone, dichloromethane, ethyl acetate, dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran, dimethylformamide, cyclohexanone, pyrrolidone, N-methyl pyrrolidone, or mixture of two or more, preferably, solvent is selected from ethanol, methanol, propanol, isopropanol, butanol, glycerol, acetone or mixture of two or more, preferably, solvent is selected from methanol, ethanol or mixture of both. The surface active agent is selected from Tween, Span, sodium dodecyl sulfate, alcohol, propylene glycol, polyethylene glycol and glycerol, where dispersant is selected from sodium hexametaphosphate, sodium pyrophosphate, sodium polyacrylate and polyvinyl alcohol, and carrier is selected from liposome, cyclodextrin and microcapsules, preferably, auxiliary agent is surfactant and/or a support, surface active agent is chosen from Tween (RTM: non-ionic surfactant), alkyl phenol polyoxyethylene ether, Span (RTM: non-ionic surfactant), sodium dodecyl sulfate and carrier is selected from liposome, cyclodextrin and micro-capsule. Preferred Process: The extraction using steam distillation method is performed by crushing dried tsaoko cardamom to small blocks or powder, adding water in weight ratio of 1:3-1:20, preferably, 1:7, soaking for 0-24 hours, preferably, 1 hour, putting in steam distillation extracting volatile oil extraction device for 0.5-10 hours, preferably, 7 hours, collecting fruit essential oil, calculating the oil yield, where yield of oil is in range of 1.0-2.5 wt.%. The herbal essential oil emulsion is obtained by mixing and stirring 1 pts. wt. herbal essential oil, 3.5 pts. wt. ethanol, 1.5 pts. wt. Tween and 4 pts. wt. water. It is also prepared by taking 1 pts. wt. essential oil of grass fruit, 2 pts. wt. ethanol, 1.5 pts. wt. alkyl phenol polyoxyethylene ether surfactant and 5.5 pts. wt. water, mixing and stirring uniformly. It is further obtained by mixing 0.5 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene ether surfactant, 4 pts. wt. water, 1 pts. wt. herbal essential oil, 3.5 pts. wt. ethanol, 1.5 pts. wt. alkylphenol ethoxylate surfactant and 4 pts. wt. water and stirring uniformly. Preferred Conditions: The dosage form of tsaoko cardamom essential oil formulation is selected from solid, liquid, emulsion, spray and aerosol, where fluoro-chloro-alkane propellant used in spray is selected from hydrocarbon and compressed gas, preferably emulsion is in spray form. Tsaoko cardamom essential oil is suitable for mildew prevention of subject selected from group consisting of dried fruits, feed, medicine, cosmetics, tobacco, wood, bamboo, wall, clothing, shoes , electronics, bathroom, cabinets, sinks, plumbing, faucets, carpets, furniture, leather, textiles, interiors and interior trims.

PN CN107913714-A; CN107913714-B
TI Fischer-Tropsch synthesis iron-based catalyst comprises iron, copper and/or manganese, potassium, silica, calcium, strontium and/or barium, lanthanum, cerium, yttrium and/or neodymium.
AU CHENG M
   WANG P
   ZHU J
   ZHANG K
   LV Y
AE SHENHUA GROUP CORP LTD (SHGR-C)
   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
   SHENHUA GROUP CORP LTD (SHGR-C)
GA 201831520S
AB    NOVELTY - A Fischer-Tropsch synthesis iron-based catalyst comprises iron, copper and/or manganese, potassium, silica, first auxiliary agent, and second auxiliary agent at weight ratio of 100:2.5-14.5:0.5-6:7-30:1-8.5:1-6. The first auxiliary agent is calcium, strontium and/or barium. The second auxiliary agent is lanthanum, cerium, yttrium and/or neodymium.
   USE - Fischer-Tropsch synthesis iron-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The Fischer-Tropsch synthesis iron-based catalyst can reduce the selectivity of carbon dioxide and methane simultaneously in the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction, improves conversion rate of carbon monoxide, and has improved stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the catalyst comprising adding water with water-soluble iron salt, water-soluble cupric salt and/or water-soluble manganese salt, water-soluble first auxiliary agent salt, water-soluble second auxiliary agent salt, precipitating agent and most portion of silicon source, co-precipitating, collecting solid phase, reacting with potassium-containing silicon source in water in the presence of inorganic acid, aging, pulping, filtering, drying, and calcining; and
   (2) Fischer-Tropsch synthesis comprising contacting syngas with reduced Fischer-Tropsch synthesis iron-based catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst has total specific surface area of 90-220 m2/g, preferably 120-180 m2/g, total pore volume of 0.3-0.65 cm3/g, preferably 0.4-0.65 cm3/g, and average pore diameter of 9-16 nm, preferably 11-16 nm. The water-soluble iron salt is iron nitrate, iron sulfate and/or iron chloride. The water-soluble copper salt is copper nitrate, copper chloride and/or copper sulfate. The water-soluble manganese salt is manganese nitrate, manganese sulfate, and/or manganese chloride. The water-soluble first auxiliary agent salt is calcium nitrate, strontium nitrate, barium nitrate, calcium chloride, strontium chloride and/or barium chloride. The water-soluble second auxiliary agent salt is lanthanum nitrate, yttrium nitrate, cerium nitrate, neodymium nitrate, lanthanum chloride, yttrium chloride, cerium chloride, neodymium chloride, lanthanum sulfate, yttrium sulfate, cerium sulfate, neodymium sulfate and/or mixed rare earth salts. The potassium-containing silicon source is potassium silicate and/or silica sol. The iron and silica have weight ratio of 100:7-30, preferably 100:14-26. The precipitating agent is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and/or ammonia water. Preferred Method: The co-precipitation is done at pH of 5-8.5 and at temperature of 25-85 degrees C for 20-60 minutes. The reaction with potassium-containing silicon source is done at pH of 5-7 and at temperature of 25-85 degrees C for 15-60 minutes. The aging is done at 25-85 degrees C for 20-150 minutes. The calcining is done at 10-35 degrees C and at heating rate of 5-10 degrees C/hour, and increasing temperature to 400-600 degrees C for 4-8 hours. The method further comprises subjecting catalyst to reductive activation to obtain reduced Fischer-Tropsch synthesis iron-based catalyst.

PT P
PN CN107751498-A
TI Complex healthcare tea comprises kudzu vine, Ampelopsis grossedentata, licorice, lotus leaf, medlar and Pueraria flower extract.
AU XIANG H
AE CHANGSHA XIANGZI BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018201593
AB    NOVELTY - Complex healthcare tea comprises 20-35 pts. wt. flower of kudzu vine, 10-20 pts. wt. Ampelopsis grossedentata, 3-10 pts. wt. licorice, 5-10 pts. wt. lotus leaf, 5-10 pts. wt. medlar and 5-10 pts. wt. Pueraria flower extract.
   USE - As complex healthcare tea comprising flower of kudzu vine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing complex healthcare tea, which involves (a) drying and crushing flower of kudzu vine at 40-50 degrees C, (b) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of Pueraria flower and drying extract, (c) crushing lotus leaf and medlar, and spraying water, until moisture content reaches 25-40%, steaming sprayed product at 90-110 degrees C for 3-25 minutes, and heating at 25-30 degrees C for 10-30 minutes for fermentation, (d) placing fermented product in a sealed container at 50-60 degrees C for 60-120 minutes, until the relative air humidity reaches 70-80%, adding crushed A.grossedentata, and standing at room temperature for 2-4 hours, (e) adding product obtained in step (a), A.grossedentata and licorice, drying, until the moisture content reaches below 8%, spraying Pueraria flower extract, performing ultrasonic treatment for 20-50 minutes, drying, until the water content reaches below 12% and packaging dried product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The complex healthcare tea comprises 25-30 pts. wt. kudzu vine, 10-20 pts. wt. A.grossedentata, 3-5 pts. wt. licorice, 5-10 pts. wt. lotus leaf, 5-10 pts. wt. medlar and 52-10 pts. wt. Pueraria flower extract. Preferred Method: The method involves spraying Angel Baker's yeast powder during steaming process, and heat-preserving at 25-30 degrees C. The drying process is performed at 40-60 degrees C. The method involves packing 4-6 g of product.

TI New amidate compound used for catalyst in production of polyurethane resin used for forming cured product e.g. motor vehicle component.
AU MIYAGI M
   NITTA S
   TSUBOI H
   TAKAHASHI S
AE KOEI CHEM CO LTD (KOEI-C)
   KOEI CHEM CO LTD (KOEI-C)
   KOEI CHEM CO LTD (KOEI-C)
   KOEI CHEM CO LTD (KOEI-C)
GA 201811137X
AB    NOVELTY - An amidate compound (I), is new. The compound (I) excludes 1,3-dimethylimidazolium-2-N-(p-chlorophenyl)amidate and 1,3-dimethylimidazolium-2-N-(3',5'-dichlorophenyl)amidate.
   USE - New amidate compound is used for catalyst in production of polyurethane resin (all claimed) used for forming cured product. Uses include but are not limited to motor vehicle component, construction material, household appliance component, heavy anticorrosion material, plastic coating, adhesive agent, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The amidate compound enables easy formation of catalyst having excellent handleability. The catalyst does not generate carbon dioxide gas during polyurethane resin production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An amidate compound of formula (I), is new. The compound (I) excludes 1,3-dimethylimidazolium-2-N-(p-chlorophenyl)amidate and 1,3-dimethylimidazolium-2-N-(3',5'-dichlorophenyl)amidate.
   A=optionally substituted hydrocarbyl;
   n=1 or more;
   D'=nitrogen-containing organic group of formula (i);
   R1-R3=hydrocarbyl optionally containing heteroatom;
   X=nitrogen atom, oxygen atom, or sulfur atom;and
   a=0 or 1.
   (i) Two or more R1-R3 combine to form ring. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) catalyst, which comprises amidate compound (I);
   (2) production of polyurethane resin, which involves reacting a polyol and a polyisocyanate in the presence of the catalyst; and
   (3) preparation of amidate compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The preparation of amidate compound (I) involves reacting a nitrogen-containing organic compound of formula (I-i) with dimethyl carbonate to obtain a carboxylate compound of formula (I-ii), and reacting carboxylate compound (I-ii) with an isocyanate compound of formula (I-iii). Preferred Process: The preparation of amidate compound (I) is performed in the presence of a hydrocarbon solvent. Preferred Composition: The compound (I) is chosen from compounds of formulae (I-1)-(I-3). The hydrocarbon solvent is an aromatic hydrocarbon solvent or a halogenated-aromatic hydrocarbon solvent. The aromatic hydrocarbon solvent or halogenated-aromatic- hydrocarbon solvent is chosen from toluene, xylene and chlorobenzene. The compound (I) is preferably chosen from 18 compounds e.g. compounds of formulae (Ia)-(Ie).R4,R5=optionally substituted hydrocarbyl;andm=0-4.
   DEFINITION - Preferred Definitions:
   group (i)=group chosen from groups of formulae (i-1)-(i-3);and
   R6-R13=H or optionally substituted 1-6C hydrocarbyl optionally containing heteroatom.
   EXAMPLE - In a three-necked flask purged with nitrogen, 3 g 1,3-dimethylimidazolium-2-carboxylate, 100 ml toluene and 2.5 g phenyl isocyanate were charged, and the resulting mixture was stirred at an internal temperature of 110 degrees C for 3 hours. The obtained reaction mixture was cooled to 25 degrees C, and concentrated under reduced pressure to obtain 5.3 g (yield 97%) 1,3-dimethyl-N-phenyl-1H-imidazol-3-ium-2-carboximidate.

PN WO2022174030-A1
TI Adjusting pH of cell culture medium useful for            culturing cell for producing recombinant polypeptide,            by adding carbonate salt or bicarbonate salt to cell            culture medium, and adjusting pH of cell culture medium            using charge balance model.
AU BALCARCEL R R
   DUONG T
AE BAYER HEALTHCARE LLC (FARB-C)
GA 2022A6629X
AB    NOVELTY - Method (A) for adjusting pH of a cell culture                medium involves (a) obtaining a functional                relationship between a concentration of dissolved                carbon dioxide in the cell culture medium and a                mole fraction of gaseous carbon dioxide applied to                the cell culture medium, and a concentration of net                medium acids in the cell culture medium, for the                cell culture medium, (b) adding carbonate salt or                bicarbonate salt to the cell culture medium to                obtain a desired carbonate salt or bicarbonate salt                concentration in the cell culture medium, and (c)                determining an amount of strong acid or strong base                to-be-added to the cell culture medium to adjust                the pH of the cell culture medium to a desired pH                using a charge balance model.
   USE - The method (A) is useful for adjusting pH of                cell culture medium. The pH-adjusted cell culture                medium is useful for culturing cell, which is                useful for producing recombinant polypeptide. The                cells are mammalian cells, preferably Chinese                hamster ovary (CHO) cells. The recombinant                polypeptide is an antibody or its fragment (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves a desired pH of cell                culture medium without aid of titration                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method (A) for adjusting pH of a cell culture                medium involves (a) obtaining a functional                relationship between a concentration of dissolved                carbon dioxide in the cell culture medium and a                mole fraction of gaseous carbon dioxide applied to                the cell culture medium, and a concentration of net                medium acids in the cell culture medium, for the                cell culture medium, (b) adding carbonate salt or                bicarbonate salt to the cell culture medium to                obtain a desired carbonate salt or bicarbonate salt                concentration in the cell culture medium, and (c)                determining an amount of strong acid or strong base                to-be-added to the cell culture medium to adjust                the pH of the cell culture medium to a desired pH                using a charge balance model, where the charge                balance model is based on at least the functional                relationship between the concentration of dissolved                carbon dioxide in the cell culture medium and the                mole fraction of gaseous carbon dioxide applied to                the cell culture medium, the concentration of net                medium acids in the cell culture medium, the                desired carbonate salt or bicarbonate salt                concentration in the cell culture medium, and the                desired pH. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method (B) for culturing cell involves (a)                adjusting the pH of a cell culture medium by                above-mentioned method (A), and (b) culturing cells                in the pH-adjusted cell culture medium;
   a method (C) for producing a recombinant                polypeptide involves (a) culturing cells by                above-mentioned method (B), and (b) producing the                recombinant polypeptide in the pH-adjusted cell                culture medium;
   a system comprising (a) one or more                processors, and (b) a memory communicatively                coupled to the one or more processors and                configured to store instructions that, when                executed by the one or more processors, cause the                system to receive one or more parameters indicating                a functional relationship between a concentration                of dissolved carbon dioxide in the cell culture                medium and a mole fraction of gaseous carbon                dioxide applied to the cell culture medium, and a                net medium acids parameter indicating a                concentration of net medium acids in the cell                culture medium at the one or more processors, for a                cell culture medium, receive a carbonate salt or                bicarbonate salt parameter indicating a desired                carbonate salt or bicarbonate salt concentration in                the cell culture medium at the one or more                processors, receive a pH parameter indicating a                desired pH of the cell culture medium at the one or                more processors, and determine an amount of strong                acid or strong base to be added to the cell culture                medium to adjust the pH of the cell culture medium                to the desired pH using a charge balance model;                and
   a method (D) for producing a polypeptide in a                host cell expressing the polypeptide involves (a)                (i) culturing the host cell in a cell culture                medium by preparing a cell culture medium with                sodium bicarbonate to tightly control the pH of the                medium, by determining the excipients and relative                amounts to-be-added to a cell culture medium to                define a recipe, preparing a solution using the                recipe and determining the pH of the solution to                define a first data set, placing the solution in a                carbon dioxide (CO2) gassed and agitated bioreactor                and allowing it to equilibrate to determine the                resulting pH and pCO2 values to define a second                data set, placing the solution in an incubator at a                defined temperature and molar percent CO2 and                determining the pH and pCO2 measurements to define                a third data set, using the first, second, and                third data sets and a pH model to solve for the                parameter values of m, s, and net medium acids                simultaneously by defining a target pH for the cell                culture medium and adding an appropriate                concentration of base to the cell culture medium as                determined from the pH model to achieve the pH                equivalence where the cell culture medium pH is                tightly controlled, and (ii) producing the                polypeptide, or (b) (i) culturing the host cell in                a production phase of the culture in a                glutamine-free production culture medium, by adding                7.5-15 mM asparagine to the cell culture medium,                adding 1-10 mM aspartic acid to the cell culture                medium, adding a salt to the cell culture medium,                determining the excipients and relative amounts to                be added to a cell culture medium to define a                recipe, preparing a solution using the recipe and                determining the pH of the solution to define a                first data set, placing the solution in a CO2                gassed and agitated bioreactor and allowing it to                equilibrate to determine the resulting pH and pCO2                values to define a second data set, placing the                solution in an incubator at a defined temperature                and molar percent CO2 and determining the pH and                pCO2 measurements to define a third data set, using                the first, second, and third data sets and the pH                model to solve for the parameter values of m, s,                and net medium acids simultaneously by defining a                target pH for the cell culture medium and adding an                appropriate concentration of base to the cell                culture medium as determined from the pH model to                achieve the pH equivalence, where the cell culture                medium pH is tightly controlled, and (ii) producing                the polypeptide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method (A) further                  involves adding the determined amount of strong                  acid or strong base to the cell culture medium,                  for making a pH-adjusted cell culture medium, and                  supplementing the cell culture medium with one or                  more ionic compounds, where the charge balance                  model is further based on the concentration of                  the one or more ionic compounds. The charge                  balance model is defined by using specific                  equation involving predetermined parameters such                  as [Na+], [H+], [OH-], [NMA], [A], [B+], [HCO3-]                  and [CO32-], where (i) [Na+] is a concentration                  of sodium ions added as sodium hydroxide, sodium                  bicarbonate or sodium carbonate to the cell                  culture medium, (ii) [H+] is a concentration of                  protons in the cell culture medium needed to                  obtain the desired pH, (iii) [OH-] is a                  concentration of hydroxide anions in the cell                  culture medium, (iv) [NMA] is the concentration                  of net medium acid ions in the cell culture                  medium, (v) [A] is a concentration of negatively                  charged ions added to the cell culture medium,                  multiplied by the absolute value of their charge,                  excluding any OH- or negatively charged ions                  included in [NMA-], (vi) [B+] is a concentration                  of positively charged ions added to the cell                  culture medium, multiplied by the absolute value                  of their charge, excluding any H+, sodium ions                  included in [Na+], or positively charged ions                  included in [NMA-], and (vii) [HCO3-] and [CO32-]                  are calculated by using specific equation                  involving predetermined parameters such as K0,                  K1, K2, P, yCO2, m and KH in which K0, K1and                  K2are dissociation constants for bicarbonate and                  carbonate anions, P is a gas pressure applied to                  the cell culture medium, yCO2is a molar                  percentage of CO2 gas phase applied to the cell                  culture medium, and m and KH are each empirically                  determined parameters for the cell culture                  medium. The concentration of net medium acids in                  the cell culture medium is modeled in charge                  balance model as a function of pH of the cell                  culture medium, or in a linear relationship with                  pH of the cell culture medium. The concentration                  of net medium acids in the cell culture medium is                  modeled as: [NMA-] = [C0p+C1p*(pH-7)], where                  [NMA] is the concentration of net medium acid                  ions in the cell culture medium, and C0pand                  C1pare each empirically determined constants for                  the cell culture medium. The concentration of net                  medium acids in the cell culture medium is                  modeled in the charge balance model as a function                  of temperature, or as a function of pH and                  temperature. The functional relationship and the                  concentration of net medium acids for the charge                  balance model are obtained by empirically                  determining the functional relationship and the                  concentration of net medium acids of the cell                  culture medium. The functional relationship and                  the concentration of net medium acids of the cell                  culture medium are determined by measuring pH                  data for a several conditions of the cell culture                  medium equilibrated at different gaseous carbon                  dioxide levels and containing different amounts                  of added strong acid or strong base, and fitting                  the charge balance model using the measured pH                  data. The method (A) involves equilibrating the                  cell culture medium at the several conditions at                  a desired culturing temperature i.e.                  35-40&#176; C prior to measuring the pH data.                  The cell culture medium is prepared at room                  temperature. The desired sodium carbonate or                  sodium bicarbonate concentration is 1.5-2 g/l.                  The cells are cultured in the cell culture medium                  at 35-40&#176; C, under 0.1-20 mol.% carbon                  dioxide (CO2), where the cells comprise a nucleic                  acid molecule encoding a recombinant polypeptide.                  In method (D), a salt is added to the solution to                  maintain the osmolality. The medium is placed in                  an incubator at 36.5&#176; C and 5% CO2. The                  cell culture medium is prepared at room                  temperature, where the pH of the cell culture                  medium is within 0.005 standard deviations of an                  expected pH value, and the pH of the cell culture                  medium is 7.272+0.005. The method (D) is                  automated, is performed in a batch fed process,                  is applicable at manufacturing scales and ensures                  robustness across scales, is applicable to ensure                  high quality comparison of multiple solutions                  with different amino acid additives during medium                  development or research, and is applicable at                  small scale systems such as shake flasks. The                  method (D) further involves isolating                  polypeptide. In method (D), the production phase                  is a batch or fed batch culture phase, and the                  production medium is serum-free. Preferred                  Component: The carbonate salt or the bicarbonate                  salt is sodium carbonate or sodium bicarbonate.                  The ionic compounds comprise one or more amino                  acids or ammonium chloride. The amino acids                  comprise glutamine, asparagine, or glutamic acid.                  The strong base is sodium hydroxide. The strong                  acid is hydrochloric acid. The excipients are                  glutamine, glutamate, asparagine, ammonium                  chloride, sodium chloride, and sodium                  hydroxide.

TI Hydrogen supply system has formic-acid decomposition apparatus that decomposes formic acid into hydrogen and carbon dioxide according to catalytic reaction in deoxidization environment of normal temperature and normal pressure.
AU MORI K
   NISHIKAWA Y
   WATANABE A
   SAKABE Y
   AMAO Y
AE IIDA GROUP HOLDINGS CO LTD (IIDA-Non-standard)
   UNIV OSAKA CITY (OSAU-C)
   IIDA GROUP HOLDINGS CO LTD (IIDA-Non-standard)
   UNIV OSAKA PUBLIC CORP (OSAU-C)
GA 201860611B
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a formic-acid production apparatus (10) which produces formic acid by an artificial photosynthesis from the inside of water and air, or an exhaust gas carbon dioxide. The formic-acid storage tank (55) stores the formic acid produced by the formic-acid production apparatus. The formic-acid decomposition apparatus (60,70) decomposes formic acid supplied from the formic-acid storage tank into hydrogen and carbon dioxide according to catalytic reaction in the deoxidization environment of normal temperature and normal pressure. The hydrogen obtained by the formic acid decomposition apparatus decomposing the formic acid supplied from the formic-acid production apparatus is supplied to an external device. The formic-acid production apparatus is a hydrogen-ion production device (20) which obtains hydrogen ion and electron while decomposing water and generates oxygen.
   USE - Hydrogen supply system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is effectively utilized without releasing it to the outside. The hydrogen obtained by decomposing formic acid is safely and efficiently supplied as an energy source to the external device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The formic-acid production device has a porous layer formed of aluminum oxide micro particles formed on the surface of the substrate supporting the pigment, methyl viologen and formate dehydrogenase. The thickness of the porous layer is 1-10 micrometers. The formic acid decomposition apparatus uses a catalyst in which platinum fine particles are dispersed with a water-soluble polymer polyvinylpyrrolidone. The particle diameters of the platinum micro particles are 1-50 nm. The amount of the water-soluble polymer polyvinylpyrrolidone to be added is 1-20 %mass based on the platinum fine particles. The formic-acid decomposition apparatus has a storage portion which stores formic acid. The reaction portion decomposes formic acid supplied from the storage portion into hydrogen and carbon dioxide. The isolation portion isolates the hydrogen and carbon dioxide produced in the reaction portion. The control portion controls the reaction portion under deoxidization of normal temperature and normal pressure. The formic acid is decomposed and hydrogen is supplied to external device, while the formic-acid decomposition apparatus circulates carbon dioxide by supplying the carbon dioxide obtained by decomposing the formic acid supplied from the formic-acid storage tank to the formic-acid production apparatus. The external device is a hydrogen electric power generating apparatus for residences. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a hydrogen supply method which involves utilizing hydrogen ion and electron obtained by carrying out the photolysis of the water to oxygen. The formic-acid production process produces formic acid to the porous layer by the aluminum oxide micro particles formed in the surface of solid substrate by an artificial photosynthesis.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the hydrogen supply system.

AE JINAN DAZHENG DONGZHI AUTOMOTIVE PIPELIN (JINA-Non-standard)
GA 202146358S

PT P
PN AR116073-A1
TI In situ production of natural gas in nanoreactor involves the use of propan 1,2,3-triol and carbon dioxide as raw materials.
AU ZULIANI P A
AE ZULIANI P A (ZULI-Individual)
   CADENAS VEGA GLEDYS STAILER (CADE-Non-standard)
GA 2021408107
AB    NOVELTY - In situ production of natural gas in nanoreactor comprises using propan 1,2,3-triol and carbon dioxide as raw materials. The propan 1,2,3-triol is used as a source of hydrogen and carbon dioxide as a source of carbon source. The method includes entering propan 1,2,3-triol in a nanoreactor, gasifying the propan 1,2,3-triol, heating to produce methane gas, producing carbon dioxide collected by the same nanoreactor, and sending to the nickel pipe thus achieving the increase in methane production and then reaching the carburetor or injection, systems that feed the methane engine. Once the engine exceeds an average temperature of 250 degrees C, the first resistance is turned off, since this temperature is sufficient to gasify the propan 1,2,3-triol and generating natural gas. The start of the reaction in the pyrolytic nanoreactor can be induced by an external fuel as in the case of the current compressed natural gas equipment that uses gasoline to heat the engine and then enters the natural gas.
   USE - The method is used for in situ production of natural gas in nanoreactor.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

   XIA S

PN CN112316652-A; WO2021023197-A1; CN112316652-B
TI Gas adsorbing member for lithium ion battery, has covering material for forming gas permeable coating, where adsorbent comprises hydroxide and strong base weak acid salt as carbon dioxide absorbing agent, and oxide as water-absorbing agent.
AU JIANG L
   GE X
   ZHONG W
   LIN D
AE NINGDE CONTEMPORARY AMPEREX TECHNOLOGY (NDCT-C)
   CONTEMPORARY AMPEREX TECHNOLOGY LTD (NDCT-C)
GA 202115388Y
AB    NOVELTY - A gas adsorbing member comprises an adsorbent and a covering material for forming a gas permeable coating to the adsorbent. The adsorbent comprises a hydroxide and/or a strong base weak acid salt as a carbon dioxide absorbing agent, an oxide as a water-absorbing agent and porous carrier supported halide catalyst.
   USE - The gas adsorbing component is useful in lithium ion battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gas adsorption component can realize double adsorption of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. The air pressure in the lithium ion battery is reduced. The degree of the electric core expansion and the interface impedance increasing are reduced. The service life of the lithium ion battery is prolonged. The water generated in the process of adsorbing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is absorbed, thus preventing the water from adversely affecting the performance of the lithium ion battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a lithium ion battery, which comprises the gas adsorption component and the electrode assembly, electrolyte are sealed in the battery shell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydroxide is selected from alkali metal hydroxides and/or alkaline earth metal hydroxides, preferably, from lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, magnesium hydroxide or barium hydroxide. The strong alkali weak acid salt is selected from sodium metaaluminate, potassium metaaluminate, magnesium metaaluminate, calcium metaaluminate, sodium acetate or potassium acetate. The oxide is selected from alkali metal oxide and/or alkaline earth metal oxide, preferably, the oxide is selected from lithium oxide, sodium oxide, calcium oxide, potassium oxide, magnesium oxide or barium oxide. The halide is selected from copper chloride, cuprous chloride, copper bromide, cuprous bromide, copper iodide, cuprous iodide, silver chloride, ferric chloride, ferrous chloride, nickel chloride, palladium chloride or zinc chloride. The porous carrier is selected from A type molecular sieve, Y type molecular sieve, X type molecular sieve, zeolite socony mobil (ZSM) type molecular sieve, aluminum phosphate molecular sieve, active carbon and silica gel.

PT P
PN CN112316887-A
TI Regulating microstructure of calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent by dissolving calcium nitrate tetrahydrate and citric acid monohydrate in water, heating mixed solution, aging, drying, igniting dry gel and calcining.
AU LUO C
   LUO T
   ZHANG L
   LI X
   WU F
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 202116840C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for regulating microstructure of calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves (i) dissolving calcium nitrate tetrahydrate and citric acid monohydrate in deionized water to obtain mixed solution and heating the mixed solution in a constant temperature water bath to obtain a gel, aging and drying the gel to obtain a dry gel and (ii) igniting the dry gel at a constant temperature and calcining calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbents with different microstructures after being burned out, under controlling the constant temperature ignition conditions and the calcination conditions, where the constant temperature ignition involves heating from normal temperature to 480-520 degrees C at a rate of 2-10 degrees C/minute and calcining by heating from 480-520 degrees C to 840-860 degrees C at a rate of 9-11 degrees C/minutes and calcining for 15-25 minutes.
   USE - Method for regulating microstructure of calcium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls the microscopic morphology of the synthesized adsorbent by changing the heating rate to reach the ignition temperature and changing the carbon capture performance of the corresponding adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the mixed solution, the molar ratio of the calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, the citric acid monohydrate and the deionized water is 1:1:(33-37). Preferred Conditions: The temperature in the constant temperature water bath is 75-85 degrees C, and the time is 3-5 hours. The temperature during aging process is 23-27 degrees C and the time is 18-22 hours. The drying process is carried out at 75-85 degrees C for 3-6 hours and then at 100-120 degrees C for 10-14 hours. The ignition temperature is 500 degrees C in the constant temperature ignition. In the constant temperature ignition, the ignition rate is 2 degrees C/minute or 5 degrees C/minute or 10 degrees C/minute. The calcination process is carried out at 850 degrees C for 20 minutes.

PT P
PN CN111875562-A
TI Purifying crude product and commercial 2-mercaptobenzothiazole by carbon dioxide process, comprises e.g. adding 2-mercaptobenzothiazole (M) to be purified into aqueous ammonia in batches, mixing and dissolving, heating and stirring.
AU LIU Y
   LI Z
   WANG Q
   JING X
   MU X
   QIN H
   ZHANG Y
AE SHANDONG FINE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B4601R
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying crude product and commercial 2-mercaptobenzothiazole by carbon dioxide process, comprises (1) adding 2-mercaptobenzothiazole (M) to be purified into the aqueous ammonia in batches, mixing and dissolving, heating and stirring, gradually clarifying the solution until there is obvious precipitation or brown suspended particles appear, and removing precipitated or suspended particulate matter by filtration to obtain colored clear liquid containing M ammonium salt; (2) diluting the above clear liquid with water, acidifying it with carbon dioxide under certain pressure and continuously stirring to obtain light yellow suspension, after a period of time, unchanging pressure, and detecting the pH value of the suspension to determine whether the reaction reaches the end point; and (3) filtering the above suspension for solid-liquid separation, washing the obtained solid with water, dehydrating, and drying to obtain refined M.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying crude product and commercial 2-mercaptobenzothiazole by carbon dioxide process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can not only obtains refined M, but also ammonium bicarbonate solution produced is applied to agricultural fertilizer and biochemical treatment ponds, which is environmentally friendly and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the M to be purified comprises product M purified by solvent extraction, product M purified by acid-base shift process, product M purified by solvent acid-base process, product M purified by hydrogen peroxide process, and crude product M not purified. In the step (1), the concentration of aqueous ammonia is 5-30 wt.%, the molar ratio of M to aqueous ammonia is 1:1-4, and the number of batch additions is 1-6 times. Preferred Method: In the step (1), if there is undissolved M in the precipitated or suspended particles removed by filtration, obtaining refined M by collecting and repeating steps (1)-(3) for many times for purification. In the step (1), evaporating the obtained colored clear liquid of the M ammonium salt to recover the escaped ammonia gas. In the step (1), heating and stirring are carried out in two separate steps, temperature of the first heating is 30-70 degrees C, and the temperature is kept at this temperature for 0.1-3 hours, and the temperature of the second heating is 50-90 degrees C. In the step (2), the pressure of introducing carbon dioxide is 0.1-3 MPa, and the temperature is less than or equal to 30 degrees C. In the step (1) and the step (2), the stirring rate is both 60-1000 revolutions/minute. In the step (2), the volume of water added during dilution is 50-500% of the volume of the entire suspension. In the step (3), the mass of water added in washing is 50-500% of the mass of the solid, returning the filtrate obtained by filtration to continue the acidification reaction with carbon dioxide, and reusing the washing water for many times.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC E06-F01; E11-Q01B; E31-N05C; E32-A04

PN CN109316900-A; CN109316900-B
TI Method for comprehensively utilizing converter tail gas, involves cooling and dedusting converter tail gas, performing purification on first mixed gas followed by separating carbon dioxide and water, mixing fourth mixed gas with first gas.
AU ZHANG W
   WANG D
   GUO Q
   WANG H
   LIU J
   ZENG S
AE SHANDONG ACID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHIHENG SPECIAL STEEL GROUP CO LTD (SHIH-Non-standard)
GA 201917422N
AB    NOVELTY - A converter tail gas comprehensively utilizing method involves cooling and dedusting the converter tail gas to obtain a first mixed gas. Deep purification is performed on the first mixed gas to remove gases other than carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide and nitrogen in the converter tail gas to obtain a second mixed gas followed by separating carbon dioxide and water in the second mixed gas by a pressure swing adsorption method for preparing a carbon dioxide product, in which the remaining gas is a third mixed gas, separating the carbon monoxide in the third mixed gas by a pressure swing adsorption method, directly utilizing for preparing a carbonyl compound, in which the remaining gas is a fourth mixed gas, and mixing the fourth mixed gas with the first mixed gas, and performing again from step two.
   USE - Method for comprehensively utilizing converter tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables fully utilizing the heat and exhaust gas composition of the converter tail gas, increasing economic benefits, realizing recycling economic utilization of resources, in simple, energy saving, and environmentally friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The gas composition in the converter tail gas is 50-70% carbon monoxide, 10-20% carbon dioxide, 10-20% nitrogen, 0.5-2.0% oxygen, 1.0-1.5% hydrogen sulfide, and 0.5-0.9% carbonyl sulfide, 0.2-0.6% carbon sulfide, 0.8-1.5% phosphine, 1.5-2.0% arsine, 0.3-0.5% hydrogen fluoride, 0.4-0.78% hydrogen cyanide, 0.3-0.75% hydrochloride, and 0.3-0.5% metal carbonyl.

AE GUANGZHOU SHIPYARD INT CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 201879737T
AB    NOVELTY - Automatic welding method of duplex stainless steel full penetration fillet weld comprises (1) providing workpiece to be welded, the workpiece includes duplex stainless steel panel and duplex stainless steel web disposed above the duplex stainless steel panel, the duplex stainless steel web is provided with a single-face groove welded to the duplex stainless steel panel, the angle of the groove is 40-50 degrees , and the gap of the groove is 0-3 mm, and (2) mounting oscillator on the fillet welding trolley, fixing the carbon dioxide welding gun on the swinger, driving the fillet welding trolley to move in the horizontal direction during automatic welding, the oscillator drives the carbon dioxide welding torch to perform front welding and reverse welding on the groove in turn, and the purity of the carbon dioxide is set to be higher than 99.5%.
   USE - The method is useful for automatic welding of duplex stainless steel full penetration fillet weld.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves double phase stainless steel welding speed of angle welding seam; and ensures welding quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Composition: The groove has a thickness of 16 mm for the front side welding. The thickness of the reverse weld to the groove was 8 mm. The duplex stainless steel panel and/or the duplex stainless steel web has thickness of 8-32 mm. Preferred Method: In the step (2), the welding process parameters used for the front side welding of the groove are, welding current is 225-240A, welding voltage is 28-30V, and the walking speed of the welding carriage is 20-25 cm/minute. In the step (2), the welding process parameters used for the reverse welding of the groove are, welding current is 210-230A, welding voltage is 30-32V, and the walking speed of the welding carriage is 20-25 cm/minute. In the step (2), the method further includes performing rooting treatment on the back surface of the groove before performing the reverse welding on the groove. The root cleaning process is performed using a plasma root cleaner. In the front side welding and the back side welding of the groove, a multi-layer multi-bead welding process is employed. In the method, after the front welding and the back welding are completed at the groove, the welding slag at the periphery of the groove is cleaned and the surface of the weld is smoothed.

TI Preparation of titania/graphene composite material for lithium ion battery, involves reacting titanium-containing compound and graphene oxide, under carbon dioxide gas, treating reaction liquid, carbonizing treated product, and cooling.
AU HUANG H
   YU J
   LIANG C
   XIA Y
   ZHANG W
   GAN Y
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 201855185D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of titania/graphene composite material using super critical carbon dioxide involves (1) preparing graphene oxide by Hummer's method using crystalline flake graphite, freeze-drying, leaving still, (2) mixing the titanium-containing ester or inorganic salt solution with the graphene oxide, vacuuming in a high-pressure ball mill tank, pumping the carbon dioxide into the tank for grinding, (3) cooling the mixture to room temperature, discharging the carbon dioxide gas and taking out the reaction liquid, from the tank, placing in a PTFE hydrothermal kettle, and reacting at 100-200 degrees C for 6-60 hours, (4) taking out the product and soaking in 0.1 mol/L dilute hydrochloric acid, suction filtering, drying, performing carbonization by heating to 400-1000 degrees C at 20 degrees C/minute under protection of nitrogen or argon gas, for 0.5-12 hours, and cooling and grinding.
   USE - Preparation of titania/graphene composite material used as negative electrode material for lithium ion battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has small particles with uniform distribution.

Preferred Components: The titanium-containing ester is tetrabutyl titanate, having purity of not lower than the chemical purity, and the titanium-containing inorganic salt is titanium oxysulfate, titanium trichloride and/or titanium tetrachloride. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the titanium-containing ester or inorganic salt solution, to graphene oxide, is 0.1-5:1.

   USE - The method is useful for sequestering CO2 and                creating precipitated calcium carbonates                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The removal of the carbohydrates removes the                metabolic by-products that would otherwise acidify                the media.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Providing a liquid                  calcification medium involves adjusting the                  nutrient broth to a pH of from pH of 6 or more to                  pH of 9 or less and sterilizing the nutrient                  broth, both prior to providing the bacteria. Adding the                  carbon source involves suspending the carbon                  source in water to form a suspended carbon source                  and filter-sterilizing the suspended carbon                  source. Introducing CO2involves agitating the                  liquid calcification medium to expose it to CO2                  within the surrounding atmosphere; and bubbling                  CO2through the liquid calcification medium.                  Allowing precipitation of calcium carbonate                  involves maintaining the liquid calcification                  medium at a temperature providing aeration for                  growth of the bacteria. The liquid calcification                  medium includes 2-7.5 g/L yeast extract, and                  2.5-30 g/L carbon source.Preferred Components: The carbon source is a                  metabolizable carbon source. It is selected from                  calcium formate, calcium acetate, calcium                  propionate, calcium butyrate, calcium succinate                  (preferred), and calcium citrate. The nutrient                  broth is devoid of urea. It is devoid of                  acidifying carbohydrate sources that drive one or                  more of: metabolic overflow, acetogenesis, and                  mixed acid fermentation. The bacteria are                  selected from the genera Bacillus,                  Microbacterium, and Escherichia. The bacteria are                  selected from Escherichia coli, Escherichia coli                  K12, Bacillus sp., and Microbacterium sp.

TI Stabilizing natural saponin comprises taking subcritical carbon dioxide and resin solid acid catalyst and stabilizing structure of natural saponin.
AU ZHU L
   MA S
   XU W
   CHEN D
   JIANG J
AE UNIV BEIJING FORESTRY (UYBF-C)
   GUANGZHOU DEGU PERSONAL-CARE SUPPLY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D30003
AB    NOVELTY - Stabilizing natural saponin comprises taking subcritical carbon dioxide and resin solid acid catalyst and stabilizing structure of natural saponin.
   USE - The method is useful for stabilizing natural saponin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has a mild reaction and provides sulfonic acid groups for the system, breaks the weak bond between the sugar group and the sapogenin, and couple the two for stabilization treatment, reduces carbon dioxide pressure and solid acid dosage can effectively control the degree of reaction, realizes the high-efficiency stabilization of the saponin structure and the high-value utilization of saponin, after the stabilization treatment, can be recovered and reused, and since there are few side reactions during the stabilization treatment process, and is beneficial to subsequent separation and purification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components:The resin-based solid acid catalyst is Amberlyst 15 resin. The amount ratio of the saponin ethanol aqueous solution to the resin-based solid acid catalyst is 20 ml: 0.1 g.Preferred Method: The structural stabilization treatment comprises: adding resin solid acid catalyst into the reaction kettle filled with saponin ethanol water solution, introducing high-purity carbon dioxide gas, controlling the pressure in the reaction kettle is 1plus minus0.1 MPa, and reacting at 70plus minus2degreesC for 1-3 hours. The high-purity carbon dioxide gas is introduced, the gas in the reactor is replaced first, and the number of replacements is preferably 2 times. The saponin ethanol aqueous solution is obtained by dissolving natural crude saponin in an 80% ethanol aqueous solution and removing impurities, and the usage ratio of the natural crude saponin to the 80% ethanol aqueous solution is 4-6 g: 100 ml. The structure stabilization treatment is performed, the resin-based solid acid catalyst is filtered and recovered, and the filtrate is post-treated to obtain the finished natural saponin. The post-treatment comprises centrifuging at high speed and ultra-filtering to remove the impurity, condensing and drying to obtain the natural saponin, where the rotating speed of the high speed centrifuging is 3500-4500 revolutions/minute. The method comprises (i) dissolving 4-6 g natural crude saponin in 100 ml of 80% ethanol water solution, sealing and stirring for 2 hours at 50degrees Celsius, centrifuging and filtering for 10 minutes at 2000 revolutions/minute, removing insoluble substance to obtain saponin ethanol water solution, (ii) adding 20 ml saponin ethanol aqueous solution into the reaction kettle, adding 0.1 g resin solid acid catalyst, introducing high-purity carbon dioxide gas, replacing for two times, controlling the pressure at 1 MPa, reacting for 1-3 hours at 70degrees Celsius, and (iii) filtering the reaction liquid, recycling the resin solid acid catalyst, filtering the filtrate by high speed centrifuging and ultrafiltration to remove impurities, concentrating and drying.

GA 202188524J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methane-carbon dioxide reforming                catalyst comprises loading active metal cobalt and                auxiliary agent using nitrogen-modified carbon                material as carrier and performing high temperature                carbonization of carbon-containing gas.
   USE - The method is useful in methane-carbon dioxide                reforming catalytic technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has better catalytic activity and                thermal stability; utilizes active metal precursor                cobalt and auxiliary agent, which can precisely                control the microstructure of the active phase of                cobalt carbide and the exposure of specific crystal                faces.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                catalyst prepared by the above mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  carbon material and nitrogen-containing precursor                  is 0.5:1-1:1. The                  nitrogen-containing precursor is melamine,                  dicyandiamide, aniline, and/or pyrrole. The                  auxiliary agent is manganese salt, sodium salt                  and lithium salt. The metal cobalt is cobalt(II)                  nitrate, cobalt(II) acetate or cobalt(II)                  chloride. Preferred Method: The nitrogen-modified                  carbon material is obtained by mixing carbon                  material with nitrogen-containing precursor and                  modifying at a high temperature in an inert gas                  atmosphere. The method specifically comprises (i)                  mixing carbon material with the                  nitrogen-containing precursor, placing in a                  high-temperature roasting furnace at 650-850                  degrees C to obtain nitrogen-modified carbon                  material, (ii) immersing the nitrogen-modified                  carbon material in a cobalt salt solution, and                  adding auxiliary agent during the dipping                  process, and (iii) performing high-temperature                  carbonization of cobalt-containing carbon                  material, introducing carbon-containing gas                  during the carbonization process, and carbonizing                  for 3-8 hours, and naturally cooling.

The weight ratio of cement and slag is 1:0.05-1: 1.5.

GA 201875334P
AB    NOVELTY - Increasing the bromine elution rate in an air blowing method comprises introducing carbon dioxide gas into a bromine-containing solution during air blowing of the oxidizing completion liquid or before air blowing.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing the bromine elution rate in an air blowing method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for bromine extraction apparatus comprising an oxidation tower, a blowing tower, and other equipment necessary for the air extraction method for extracting bromine, the oxidation tower and/or the blowing tower are provided in the tower for bubbling carbon dioxide gas into a bromine-containing solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the oxidation completion liquid before the air is blown, or is simultaneously introduced into the chlorine gas or when the air is blown out, or when the air is blown, the blown gas is changed to carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide gas is preferably introduced simultaneously with chlorine gas for oxidation. The raw material liquid of the bromine-containing solution is natural bromine-containing brine or concentrated seawater. The bromine-containing solution is subjected to an acidification and chlorine oxidation step in sequence before air blowing. The oxidizing completion liquid has a pH of 2.5 to 4, preferably 2.5 to 3, when air is blown. The oxidation completion liquid has a pH of more preferably 3 when air is blown. The air blowing method is an air blowing acid absorption method or an air blowing alkali absorption method.
