PN CN114653378-A
TI Catalyst for synthesizing dimethyl carbonate,            comprises modified carrier and noble metal loaded on            modified carrier, where noble metal is palladium,            modified carrier is combination of copper, lithium,            lanthanum or cerium on carrier, and carrier is carbon            aerogel.
AU NI J
   WANG J
   XIAO Z
   SHAN W
   LIU Y
   TANG D
AE SHANGHAI HUIQIAN CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202293568L
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises modified carrier and                noble metal loaded on modified carrier. The noble                metal is palladium. The modified carrier is                combination of copper, lithium, lanthanum or cerium                on carrier, and the carrier is carbon aerogel. The                catalyst is prepared by grinding organic gel and                roasting in high-temperature tubular furnace,                introducing nitrogen and carbon dioxide, taking out                roasted sample, neutralizing with hydrochloric acid                and distilled water, washing and drying, dissolving                copper nitrate, lithium nitrate, lanthanum nitrate                or cerium nitrate, in deionized water, placing                obtained first solution in rotary evaporator,                weighing obtained activated carbon aerogel,                evaporating water and calcining, mixing                polyvinylpyrrolidone, absolute ethanol, deionized                water and palladium salt solution, adding reducing                agent to obtained fourth mixed solution, mixing                obtained third modified carrier and obtained fifth                mixed solution and filtering, and calcining                obtained catalyst precursor.
   USE - Catalyst used for synthesizing dimethyl                carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is applied to reaction of carbon                monoxide and methyl nitrite to synthesize dimethyl                carbonate at low temperature and low pressure, the                reaction conversion rate is as high as 99%, and the                selectivity of the product dimethyl carbonate is                also as high as 97%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst comprises modified carrier and                noble metal loaded on the modified carrier as                active component. The noble metal is palladium. The                modified carrier is a combination of copper,                lithium, lanthanum or cerium on the carrier, and                the carrier is a carbon aerogel. In the catalyst,                the weight ratio of the active components is                0.05-3%, the total weight ratio of copper, lithium,                lanthanum or cerium is 0.1-5%, and the balance is                carrier. The catalyst is prepared by grinding                organic gel after normal pressure drying in a                grinding machine and placing in high-temperature                tubular furnace for roasting, introducing nitrogen                for roasting for t1 time, introducing carbon                dioxide for roasting for t2 time, lowering the                temperature to room temperature, taking out roasted                sample, neutralizing in a centrifuge tube with                hydrochloric acid and distilled water, washing to                neutrality and drying overnight to obtain activated                carbon aerogel, weighing copper nitrate, lithium                nitrate, lanthanum nitrate or cerium nitrate                according to preset mass ratio, dissolving in                preset amount of deionized water to obtain first                aqueous solution, placing the first aqueous                solution in rotary evaporator, weighing preset                amount of activated carbon aerogel and putting into                rotary evaporator and evaporating water to obtain                second mixture, calcining second mixture in                high-temperature tubular furnace nitrogen for t3                time reduction to obtain a third modified carrier,                mixing preset amount of polyvinylpyrrolidone,                absolute ethanol, deionized water and preset                concentration of palladium salt aqueous solution to                obtain fourth mixed solution, adding preset amount                of reducing agent to the fourth mixed solution and                carrying out reaction at preset temperature for t4                time to obtain fifth mixed solution, mixing third                modified carrier and fifth mixed solution for                period of time and performing process of                filtration, washing and drying to obtain sixth                catalyst precursor, and calcining the catalyst                precursor in nitrogen atmosphere for time of                t5.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for synthesizing dimethyl carbonate using the                catalyst, which involves: (a) using fixed bed                reaction, using raw material monoxide and nitrous                acid ester to perform contact reaction, using                nitrogen or dioxide as diluent, contacting the raw                material and catalyst at 90-150&#8451; for                0.1-6 seconds under pressure of 1 MPaG and gas                hourly space velocity of 2000-8000/hour, where the                volume content of carbon monoxide in raw material                gas is 5-30%, the volume content of nitrite is                5-20% and the gas volume content of the generated                hydrogen chloride is 10-100 ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon aerogel                  specifically adopts resorcinol and formaldehyde                  as precursors, in the molar ratio of 1:2 to                  prepare an activated carbon aerogel by gel-sol                  method. The mass ratio of copper nitrate, lithium                  nitrate and lanthanum nitrate or cerium nitrate                  is 10-30%.

PT P
PN KR2022057678-A
TI Manufacturing porous foam insulation board comprises heating porous foam insulation board to make creamy state and applying it to certain thickness to produce porous foam insulation board by applying it to predetermined thickness.
AU JEON C T
   SEOK K C
AE JEON C T (JEON-Individual)
   SEOK K C (SEOK-Individual)
GA 2022651131
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing porous foam insulation board comprises manufacturing paint having heat insulation and heat shielding, waterproof, flame retardant and non-flammable functions, dissolving emulsifier and citric acid in deionised water while mixing and stirring, mixing sodium silicate and emulsifier and stirring to modify the sodium silicate with a material containing an emulsifier to prepare an adhesive binder, preparing ceramic powder mixture by mixing fly ash cement, sodium peroxodicarbonate, slaked lime, and zinc oxide in a fine powder state, respectively, mixing deionized water, dispersant, and water repellent, adding ceramic powder mixture, and reforming ceramic powder mixture to form a ceramic sol, mixing adhesive binder, adding ceramic sol in sol state, mixing sodium peroxide, generating carbon dioxide gas from sodium peroxide, adding hydrogen peroxide solution and preparing creamy foam board mixture and molding manufactured cream-form foam board mixture into plate shape.
   USE - The method is useful for manufacturing porous foam insulation board.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prevents transmission through flow and maximizes insulation, heat shielding, waterproofing, flame-retardant and non-combustible functions when applied for construction or industrial purposes, and improves durability of surface portion of porous foam insulation board in which the paint is applied to the surface of the porous foam board.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacturing porous foam insulation board comprises (i) manufacturing a paint having heat insulation and heat shielding, waterproof, flame retardant and non-flammable functions; (ii) dissolving 29 pts. wt. emulsifier and 1 pts. wt. citric acid as a preservative in 70 pts. wt. deionised water while mixing and stirring at a constant temperature condition; (iii) mixing 95 pts. wt. sodium silicate and 5 pts. wt. emulsifier and stirring to modify the sodium silicate with a material containing an emulsifier to prepare an adhesive binder; (iv) preparing ceramic powder mixture by mixing 87 pts. wt. fly ash cement, 1 pts. wt. sodium peroxodicarbonate, 10 pts. wt. slaked lime, and 2 pts. wt. zinc oxide in a fine powder state, respectively; (v) mixing 30 pts. wt. deionized water, 1 pts. wt. dispersant, and 2 pts. wt. water repellent, adding 67 pts. wt. ceramic powder mixture, and reforming ceramic powder mixture to form a ceramic sol; (vi) mixing 48 pts. wt. adhesive binder manufactured through bonding binder manufacturing step (v), adding 49 pts. wt. ceramic sol in the sol state manufactured through ceramic sol manufacturing step (v), mixing 2 pts. wt. sodium peroxide and raising the temperature to 20-30 degrees C, generating carbon dioxide gas from sodium peroxide, adding 2 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide solution (5%) and mixing while increasing the generation of bubbles, adding 1 pts. wt. thickener and maintaining temperature at 30-40 degrees C, preparing a creamy foam board mixture by maintaining the bubbles while the ceramic sol does not precipitate; (vii) molding manufactured cream-form foam board mixture into a plate shape having a thickness of 10-100 mm, molding mold to form a porous, raising the temperature to 60-80 degrees C with an infrared wavelength so that the particles are uniformly maintained at the same time as bubbles are formed and the preceding curing proceeds at the same time, advancing post-curing through irradiation of micro-wavelengths to form a porous foam board as a cured product; and (viii) heating coating material having heat insulation, heat shielding, waterproofing, flame retardant and non-flammable functions manufactured through step (i) to a constant temperature condition to create a cream on the surface of the porous foam board made through the forming step (vii) of the porous foam board for manufacturing porous foam insulation board by applying it to a predetermined thickness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), mixing 29 pts. wt. emulsifier and 1 pts. wt. citric acid as a preservative in 70 pts. wt. deionized water while mixing and stirring under a constant temperature condition, where constant temperature condition for dissolving modified emulsifier is applied as a temperature condition of 40-70 degrees C. In the step (viii), heating the paint at 20-4 degrees C, while a certain thickness for applying paint is applied in a thickness ranging from 1-100 mu m. The step (i) is performed at 40-70 degrees C by adding 28 pts. wt. emulsifier, 1 pts. wt. flowability improving agent, and 1 pts. wt. citric acid as a preservative in 70 pts. wt. deionized water, preparing an emulsion mixture by dissolving it while mixing and stirring, adding 35 pts. wt. hydrous aluminum silicate and 10 pts. wt. silver foil paste as an inorganic insulating material, 3 pts. wt. zinc oxide and 3 pts. wt. titanium oxide as an antibacterial agent, preparing ceramic mixture by mixing 3 pts. wt. sugar with a dye, preparing 90 pts. wt. ceramic mixture, preparing an emulsified ceramic mixture in a cream state by mixing 10 pts. wt. emulsified mixture prepared through the process of preparing emulsified mixture and adding 59 pts. wt. emulsified ceramic mixture prepared through the process of preparing the emulsified ceramic mixture, 34 pts. wt. polyurethane water-dispersed with a urethane binder, 1 pts. wt. antifoaming agent, 1 pts. wt. ethylene glycol as a curing retardant, 2 pts. wt. silane, 0.5 pts. wt. ammonia water (25%) as a pH adjusting agent, mixing and stirring 2 pts. wt. texanol as an additive for film formation flotation, and 0.5 pts. wt. thickener to prepare the final paint product. In the step (vi), mixing 48 pts. wt. adhesive binder manufactured through the adhesive binder manufacturing step (iii), 7 pts. wt. quicklime with 42 pts. wt. ceramic sol in the sol state manufactured through the ceramic sol manufacturing step, raising temperature by the reaction heat through the hydration reaction, and generating carbon dioxide gas, mixing 2 pts. wt. sodium peroxide and raising the temperature to 20-30 degrees C, generating carbon dioxide gas from sodium peroxide, adding 2 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide solution (5%) and mixing while increasing the generation of bubbles, adding 1 pts. wt. thickener to maintain the temperature at 30-40 degrees C and the ceramic sol is not precipitated and air bubbles are maintained to prepare a creamy foam board mixture. In the step (iv), applying ceramic powder mixture as an alternative to 10 pts. wt. hemihydrate gypsum with respect to 10 pts. wt. slaked lime. The process of manufacturing final paint product comprises mixing 59 pts. wt. emulsified ceramic mixture prepared through the process of preparing the emulsified ceramic mixture, 34 pts. wt. water-dispersed polyurethane as a urethane binder, 1 pts. wt. antifoaming agent, 1 pts. wt. ethylene glycol as a curing retardant, 0.5 pts. wt. potasium diphenyl sulfone sulfonate, 1.5 pts. wt. silane, 0.5 pts. wt. ammonia water (25%) as pH adjusting agent, mixing and stirring 2 pts. wt. texanol as an additive for film formation flotation, and 0.5 pts. wt. thickener to prepare the final paint product. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), emulsifier is provided as polyoxyethylene nonylphenyl ether. In the process of manufacturing final paint product, thickener is provided as sodium polyacrylate at a concentration of 20%. In the process of manufacturing final coating product, silane is provided as potassium methylsilantriolate. In the process of preparing the ceramic mixture, zinc oxide and titanium oxide provided as antibacterial agents are applied to have an average particle size of 30 nm.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Preparing carbon material coated nickel nano-particle catalyst used for catalytic hydrogenation synthesis of halogenated aniline, involves dissolving nickel salt, organic ligand in alcohol solvent, dropping alkali liquor in mixed solution.
AU FENG F
   ZHANG Q
   LI X
   YANG Y
   LU C
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 202210439T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon material coated nickel nano particle catalyst involves weighing certain mass of nickel salt and organic ligand into alcohol solvent to dissolve and stirring for 3-24 hours at 0-50degrees Celsius to obtain mixed solution; dropping alkali liquor in mixed solution, adjusting pH value of solution in three stages specifically as followed by firstly using 0.1 to 5 mol/L sodium hydroxide aqueous solution to adjust pH value to 4.0-5.5, keeping for 0.5 to 3 hours, using 80-99 % of triethanolamine water solution to adjust pH value to 6.5-7.5, keeping for 1-5 hours, adjusting pH value to 8.5-9.5 with 25-28 % of ammonia water, keeping for 1-3 hours in adjusting process, where mixed solution is always in stirring state; sealing slurry, placing on vibration platform for crystallization, depositing, aging, then filtering and washing, vacuum drying to obtain metal organic frame precursor where vibration program is set as 1.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon material coated nickel nano-particle catalyst used in halogenated aniline of formula (I) for catalytic hydrogenation synthesis of halogenated aniline of formula (II) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to effectively control precursor shape on basis of improving metal utilization rate to obtain catalyst with specific micro-structure, effectively improves selectivity of product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon material coated nickel nano particle catalyst involves weighing certain mass of nickel salt and organic ligand into alcohol solvent to dissolve and stirring for 3-24 hours at 0-50degrees Celsius to obtain mixed solution; dropping alkali liquor in mixed solution, adjusting pH value of solution in three stages specifically as followed by firstly using 0.1 to 5 mol/L sodium hydroxide aqueous solution to adjust pH value to 4.0-5.5, keeping for 0.5 to 3 hours, using 80-99 % of triethanolamine water solution to adjust pH value to 6.5-7.5, keeping for 1-5 hours, adjusting pH value to 8.5-9.5 with 25-28 % of ammonia water, keeping for 1-3 hours in adjusting process, where mixed solution is always in stirring state; sealing slurry, placing on vibration platform for crystallization, depositing, aging, then filtering and washing, vacuum drying to obtain metal organic frame precursor where vibration program is set as 1, vibration frequency is 10-20 hertz (Hz), transverse amplitude vibration is 1-5 minutes, vibration frequency is 20-40 Hz and transverse amplitude is vibrated for 1-5 minutes, vibration frequency is 40-55 Hz, transverse amplitude is vibrated for 1-5 minutes; running program 1-3 is vibration period, where whole crystallization precipitation aging process is 10-20 hours, interval 1-2 hours, running vibration period; placing precursor in agate mortar for crushing and grinding, roasting for 5-24 hours at 300-800degrees Celsius, cooling to room temperature and grinding to obtain carbon material coated nickel nano particle catalyst, where roasting atmosphere is inert atmosphere or carbon dioxide atmosphere contains carbon dioxide.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a carbon material coated nickel nano particle catalyst, which is prepared by above preparation method;
   (2) a application of carbon material coated nickel nano-particle catalyst, which is provided in halogenated aniline of formula (I) for catalytic hydrogenation synthesis of halogenated aniline of formula (II), where application is not needed to add dehalogenation inhibitor, where Rn represents n substituents on benzene ring where n= 0,1,2,3 or 4; each substituent R is 1-3C alkyl, Xm represents m halogen substituents on benzene ring, where m is 1-5, each halogen substituent is independent m + n is not more than 5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nickel salt is nickel chloride, nickel carbonate, nickel nitrate, nickel acetate. The inert atmosphere is nitrogen or argon gas. The catalyst is composed of nickel nano particles and graphene layer completely coated with nickel nano particles, where size distribution range of carbon material coated nickel nano particles is 2-20 nanometer (nm); thickness of graphene layer is corresponding carbon layer number is 1 to 6 layers; carbon layer has pore; catalyst is fully washed by 0.1-5 mol/L of sulfuric acid (H2SO4) or phosphoric acid (H3PO4) after drying, metal loss is less than 1 wt .%, carbon material is coated with nickel nano particle catalyst, where catalyst is fully washed by 0.1-5 mol/L hydrochloric acid (HCl) or hydroflouride (HF), after drying, mass percentage of metal amount retained in catalyst is less than 20 wt % of metal feeding amount when preparing catalyst.Preferred Conditions: The mol ratio of nickel and organic ligand in nickel salt is 1:1 to 1:8, preferably 1:2 to 1:6; the mass ratio of nickel and alcohol solvent in nickel salt is 1:50 to 1:1000, preferably 1:100 to 1:500. The stirring temperature is 0-50degrees Celsius, preferably 20-50degrees Celsius; the stirring time is 3-24 hours, preferably 6-15 hours. The roasting temperature is 300-800degrees Celsius, preferably 400-600degrees Celsius. The roasting time is 5-24 hours, preferably 5-10 hours. The volume fraction of carbon dioxide in roasting atmosphere is not less than 10 %, most preferably roasting atmosphere is carbon dioxide atmosphere, where total flow rate of gas is 5- 50 mL/minute.Preferred Method: The method for preparing involves precipitating and aging, filtering and washing by ethanol, placing in vacuum oven at 50-120degrees Celsius and drying for 2-15 hours; and taking out.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solvent is methanol or absolute ethyl alcohol, where volume concentration is more than 95 %. The organic ligand is at least one of benzoic acid, terephthalic acid, urea, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, 4-pyridine formic acid, 2,2'-bipyridyl, triphenylphosphine, oxalic acid and glycine.

PN WO2021118741-A1
TI Conversion of hydrocarbon-containing feed in production of e.g. olefins, involves pyrolyzing feed with heated particles to produce effluent comprising olefins and particles in which coke is formed on surface, and gasifying particles.
AU HARANDI M N
   RATERMAN M F
   KEUSENKOTHEN P F
AE EXXONMOBIL CHEM PATENTS INC (ESSO-C)
GA 202165601E
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of hydrocarbon-containing feed by pyrolysis and gasification involves contacting hydrocarbon-containing feed with heated particles to perform pyrolysis to produce a pyrolysis zone effluent comprising olefins and particles in which coke is formed on the surface, separating the effluent into a gaseous stream (s1) rich in olefins and a particle stream (s2) rich in particles, contacting the stream (s2), the oxidant stream and the steam stream to perform gasification to produce a gasification zone effluent comprising heated and regenerated particles and a synthesis gas comprising molecular hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, separating the effluent into a gaseous stream (s3) rich in synthesis gas and a particle stream (s4) rich in heated and regenerated particles, and feeding the stream (s4) into the pyrolysis zone. The velocity of gaseous components within the pyrolysis zone is at least 20% more than a velocity of the particles within the pyrolysis zone.
   USE - Conversion of hydrocarbon-containing feed by pyrolysis and gasification in production of olefins, such as such as ethylene, propylene and butene, and synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and simple conversion of hydrocarbon-containing feed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Conversion of hydrocarbon-containing feed by pyrolysis and gasification involves feeding (i) a hydrocarbon-containing feed and heated particles into a pyrolysis zone, contacting (ii) the hydrocarbon-containing feed with the heated particles in the pyrolysis zone to perform pyrolysis of at least a portion of the hydrocarbon-containing feed to produce a pyrolysis zone effluent comprising olefins and particles in which coke is formed on the surface, separating (iii) the pyrolysis zone effluent into a gaseous stream (s1) rich in olefins and a particle stream (s2) rich in particles, feeding (iv) at least a portion of the stream (s2), an oxidant stream and a steam stream into a gasification zone, contacting (v) the stream (s2), the oxidant stream and the steam stream within the gasification zone to perform gasification of at least a portion of the coke arranged on the surface of the particles to produce a gasification zone effluent comprising heated and regenerated particles and a synthesis gas comprising molecular hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, separating (vi) the gasification zone effluent into a gaseous stream (s3) rich in synthesis gas and a particle stream (s4) rich in heated and regenerated particles, and feeding (vii) at least a portion of the stream (s4) into the pyrolysis zone as at least a portion of the heated particles fed into the pyrolysis zone. The velocity of gaseous components within the pyrolysis zone is at least 20% more than a velocity of the particles within the pyrolysis zone. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system (101), which comprises a pyrolysis reactor (1007) comprising the pyrolysis zone, a separation device (1015) adapted for receiving the pyrolysis zone effluent, optionally receiving a stripping steam stream, separating the pyrolysis zone effluent to obtain stream (s1) and stream (s2), discharging the stream (s1), and discharging the stream (s2), a channel (c1) adapted for feeding at least a portion of the stream (s2) to the gasification zone, a gasifier (1027) comprising the gasification zone, a channel (c2) adapted for feeding at least a portion of the heated and regenerated particles to the pyrolysis reactor, such that the regenerated and heated particles make up at least a portion of the heated particles contacted with the hydrocarbon-containing feed in the pyrolysis zone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system.
   System (101)
   Pyrolysis reactor (1007)
   Separation devices (1015,1047)
   Gasifier (1027)
   Urea reactor (1083)
   Ammonia reactor (1091)
   Methanol reactor (1099)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The process further involves converting (viii) at least a portion of the carbon monoxide in the stream (s3) and steam into carbon dioxide to produce a shifted synthesis gas stream, separating (ix) the shifted synthesis gas stream into a carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream and a carbon dioxide rich stream, separating (x) the oxidant stream comprising 40 vol.% or more molecular oxygen and a nitrogen stream comprising 60 vol.% or more molecular nitrogen from an air stream, exposing (xi) at least a portion of the carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream to an ammonia synthesis catalyst under ammonia synthesis conditions to produce ammonia, exposing (xii) at least a portion of the ammonia and at least a portion of the carbon dioxide rich stream to reaction conditions appropriate to produce urea, exposing (xiii) at least a portion of the carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream to a methanol synthesis catalyst under methanol synthesis conditions to produce methanol, and separating (xiv) the carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream to obtain a hydrogen rich stream comprising 20 vol.% or more molecular hydrogen and a nitrogen rich stream. The process involves indirectly transferring heat from the stream (s3) to a cooling medium to produce a cooled gaseous stream comprising water, separating a portion of water, if present, a portion of any regenerated particles and, if present, a portion of any hydrogen sulfide from the cooled gaseous stream to produce a purified second gaseous stream, and compressing at least a portion of the purified gaseous stream to produce a compressed gaseous stream, between the process (vi) and the process (viii). In the process (viii), at least a portion of the carbon monoxide in the compressed gaseous stream is converted to carbon dioxide into produce the shifted synthesis gas stream. A portion (p1) of the coke on the surface of the particles is combusted within the gasification zone, and a portion (p2) of the coke on the surface of the particles and a portion of the steam are converted into molecular hydrogen and carbon monoxide within the gasification zone. The process (iv) involves feeding a hydrocarbon fuel stream into the gasification zone, combusting a portion (p1) of the hydrocarbon fuel stream within the gasification zone, and converting a portion (p2) of the hydrocarbon fuel stream into molecular hydrogen and carbon monoxide, or feeding a diluent stream comprising a portion of the stream (s3) or comprising a portion of the compressed gaseous stream into the gasification zone. When the steam stream is fed into the pyrolysis zone, the weight ratio of the steam stream with respect to hydrocarbon-containing feed within the pyrolysis zone is 0.01:1 to 6:1, the pyrolysis zone is operated at a temperature of 800-1100 degrees C, a pressure within the pyrolysis zone is 200-1000 kPa-absolute, a velocity of the gaseous components within the pyrolysis zone is 9-155 m/second, a velocity of the particles within the pyrolysis zone is up to 15.5 m/second, a weight ratio of the particles with respect to hydrocarbon-containing feed stream fed into the pyrolysis zone is 10:1 to 50:1, and the hydrocarbon-containing feed is contacted with the heated particles within the pyrolysis zone for a gas residence time of 10-700 milliseconds. The stream (s2) is at a temperature of 1000-1500 degrees C, and the cooled gaseous stream is at a temperature of 10-100 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The synthesis gas further comprises molecular nitrogen, and on a volume basis, the synthesis gas comprises a greater amount of molecular hydrogen than molecular nitrogen. The stream (s3) comprises 10-25 wt.% molecular hydrogen, 15-30 wt.% carbon monoxide, and 5 wt.% or more carbon dioxide. The oxidant stream comprises air or a fraction of air, and on a volume basis, ratio of a total amount of molecular oxygen with respect to total amount of molecular nitrogen within the gasification zone is 0.26:1 or more. The hydrocarbon-containing feed has a total carbon content of 10-45 wt.%, preferably 20-40 wt.%, and the total carbon content is converted into carbon dioxide and utilized in an enhanced oil recovery process, sequestered and/or converted into another compound.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The gasification zone is operated at a temperature of 1000 degrees C or more, preferably 1200-1500 degrees C, and a pressure of 100-1000 kPa-gauge. Preferred Composition: The system further comprises a compression stage adapted for compressing at least a portion of the stream (s3) to produce a compressed gaseous stream, a converter comprising a conversion zone containing a catalyst adapted for converting at least a portion of the carbon monoxide in the compressed stream to carbon dioxide to produce shifted synthesis gas stream, a separation device (1047) adapted for receiving the shifted synthesis gas, separating at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the shifted synthesis gas to obtain carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream and carbon dioxide rich gas stream, discharging the carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream, and discharging the carbon dioxide rich gas stream, and a methanol reactor (1099) containing a methanol synthesis catalyst adapted for converting at least a portion of the carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream to methanol, an ammonia reactor (1091) adapted for receiving at least a portion of the carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream, the ammonia reactor containing an ammonia synthesis catalyst adapted for converting at least a portion of the carbon dioxide lean synthesis gas stream to ammonia and/or a urea reactor (1083) adapted for receiving at least a portion of the ammonia and at least a portion of the carbon dioxide rich gas stream. The urea reactor is adapted for converting at least a portion of the carbon dioxide rich gas stream and at least a portion of the ammonia into urea.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The heated particles comprise an oxide of a transition metal element capable of oxidizing molecular hydrogen within the pyrolysis zone, and a portion of the transition metal element in the particles in the stream (s2) is at a reduced state compared to the transition metal element in the particles fed into the pyrolysis zone. The oxide of the transition metal element favors the oxidation of hydrogen over the oxidation of hydrocarbons in the pyrolysis zone.

PN CN112662706-A
TI Preparing exosomes encapsulated adeno-associated virus vector comprises e.g. cultivating Expi293F cells in dulbecco's modified eagle medium, and replacing old medium with dulbecco's modified eagle medium basal medium before transfection.
AU DUAN L
   LIANG Y
   XU X
   XU L
AE SHENZHEN FUTIAN SECOND PEOPLE HOSPITAL (SPHS-C)
GA 202148702N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing exosomes encapsulated adeno-associated virus (AAV) vector comprises (1) cultivating Expi293F cells in dulbecco's modified eagle medium, and using them for transfection when the cells grow to a confluency of 3-5x 106 viable cells/ml, replacing old medium with DMEM basal medium for 1-4 hours before transfection, (2) co-transfecting pAAV-target gene plasmid, pAAV-RC2 plasmid and pHelper plasmid into obtained Expi293F cell culture medium, after co-transfection at 37 degrees C for 12-16 hours, adding exosome-free fetal bovine serum, culturing for 48-72 hours, and collecting supernatant, and (3) centrifugal separation of exosomes encapsulated AAV to obtain exosomes encapsulated AAV vectors.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing exosomes encapsulated adeno-associated virus (AAV) vector.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enhances transport of AAV in retinal tissues, and improves efficiency of target gene transfection.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for purifying exosomes-encapsulated AAV vectors comprising (a) preparing materials, Expi293F cells, plasmids, PEI reagents, Opti-MEM medium, SMM-293TII medium, preheating to 37 degrees C before use, shaking and mixing cells, taking out 100 mu l cells and placing in EP tube, adding 100 mu l 4% trypan blue solution to EP tube, mixing, using 10 mu l for cell counting, the cell volume needs to reach 2.5-3x 106 cells/ml before proceeding, for transfection, the cell viability should be above 95%, if the cell density is higher than 3x 106 cells/ml, diluting with pre-warmed SMM-293TII medium to make the final cell concentration 2.5-3x 106 cells/ml, (b) preparing 50 mu g plasmid for every 50 ml cells, diluting plasmid with 3 ml Opti-MEM medium, mixing and allowing to stand for 5 minutes, increasing or decreasing the amount of plasmid and Opti-MEM medium in equal proportions according to the specific culture volume, (c) preparing 160 mu l polyethylenimine reagent for every 50 ml cells, diluting with 2.8 ml Opti-minimum essential media medium, mixing and allowing to stand for 5 minutes, increasing or decreasing amount of polyethylenimine and Opti-minimum essential media medium in equal proportions according to the specific culture volume, (d) mixing reagents and allowing to stand for 15 minutes, (e) adding reagents to cells, mixing, and placing in incubator for culture, (f) collecting exosomes after 48-72 hours, (g) conventional culture, culturing Expi293FTM cells to 3-5x 106 viable cells/ml, preheating SMM293TII medium to 37 degrees C, using polycarbonate or PETG material for culturing suspended cells, without baffle, disposable sterile Ventilated shake flasks, e.g. Nalgene PETGErlenmeyer disposable flat-bottomed sterile Erlenmeyer flasks, reagents and equipment needed to determining density and viability of living cells, e.g. trypan blue, hemocytometer or automatic cell counter, placing orbital shaker less than or equal to 37 degrees C incubator with 80% relative humidity and 8% carbon dioxide, (h) set the seeding density and culture volume, and use the density of viable cells to calculating volume of cell suspension requiring for seeding into fresh shake flask, transferring calculated volume of cells to a culture flask containing freshly pre-warmed Expi293TM expression medium, placing culture flask on an orbital shaker, and set the incubator to 37 degrees C, relative humidity less than or equal to 80% and 8% carbon dioxide, until density of the culture reaches 3-5x 106 viable cells/ml.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (g), the cell growth density is controlled not to exceed 5x 106 viable cells/ml.

PN CN112593727-A
TI Building wall crack repairing device has repairing device main body with movable working plate, movable motor, turning cavity, turning working rotating shaft, turning working plate, rotating connecting cavity, and meshing rotating cylinder.
AU CUI Y
AE YIWU HEBANG DECORATION ENG CO LTD (YIWU-Non-standard)
GA 2021370474
AB    NOVELTY - A building wall crack repairing device comprises repairing device main body (11) provided with movable working plate (30) and movable motor connected with translation thread shaft. The translation thread shaft is connected with the lower end of the movable working plate by screw thread. The movable working plate is provided with turning cavity (37) fixed with turning working rotating shaft (55) and turning working plate (45). The working plate inner right end is provided with rotating connecting cavity. The outer circle surface of the transmission supporting shaft is connected with meshing rotating cylinder. The working plate left end is provided with connecting opening cavity. The right side between the front and back walls of the connecting opening cavity is provided with rotating shaft. The outer circular surface of the rotating shaft is connected with working turning plate provided with supporting rotating cavity.
   USE - The building wall crack repairing device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device changes output speed of carbon dioxide when repairing a wall surface, promotes solidification of lime solution, improves efficiency of repairing work, and reduces waste of lime solution caused by improper repair work in the wall repairing process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A building wall crack repairing device comprises repairing device main body (11) provided with movable working plate (30) and movable motor connected with translation thread shaft. The translation thread shaft is connected with the lower end of the movable working plate by screw thread. The movable working plate is provided with turning cavity (37) fixed with turning working rotating shaft (55) and turning working plate (45). The working plate inner right end is provided with rotating connecting cavity. The outer circle surface of the transmission supporting shaft is connected with meshing rotating cylinder. The working plate left end is provided with connecting opening cavity. The right side between the front and back walls of the connecting opening cavity is provided with rotating shaft. The outer circular surface of the rotating shaft is connected with working turning plate provided with supporting rotating cavity. The supporting rotating cavity is provided with rotating supporting shaft connected with working brush cylinder. The left side of the lower wall of the turning cavity is connected with solution storage cavity for storing the lime liquid. The right wall of the solution storage cavity is provided with storage working box with storage cavity for storing high pressure carbon dioxide gas. The right side of the storage working box is provided with fan cavity and rotating fan. Between the left wall of the fan cavity and the right wall of the storage cavity is connected with an air inlet pipeline provided with heating plate. The right wall of the fan cavity is provided with switching cavity. Between the left and right walls of the switching cavity is provided with supporting rotating shaft connected with switching rotating cylinder, arc-shaped switching sliding groove and switching cavity upper wall inner gear cavity. Between the gear cavity lower wall and switching cavity upper wall is connected with connecting sliding groove. The gear cavity is provided with mobile switching plate fixedly connected with switching clamping plate clamped with the switching sliding groove. The upper end of the rotating motor is dynamically connected with driving bevel gear. The left side of the front and back walls of the turning cavity is provided with wind energy output port.

   Movable working plate (30)
   Turning cavity (37)
   Turning working plate (45)
   Turning working rotating shaft (55)

PN CN111659437-A
TI Preparing nitrogen-doped graphene-supported core-shell copper-carbon composite catalyst for carbon dioxide based electrocatalytic production of formic acid, involves adding nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide to mixed solution.
AU FENG Y
   LI D
   TIAN Y
   WU J
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 202092240A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nitrogen-doped graphene-supported core-shell copper-carbon composite catalyst involves adding nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide to the mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol to fully disperse, then adding Copper(II) Nitrate Trihydrate (Cu(NO3)2.3H2O) and continue stirring until it is completely dissolved to get mixture. The benzimidazole is dissolved in a mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol and stirring to dissolve to obtain benzimidazole solution, and dissolving trimesic acid in a mixture of N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol and mixing under stirring in the mixed solution until it dissolves to obtain trimellitic acid solution. The concentration of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O in the mixed solution is 2.5mg/mL-3.0mg/mL. The mass ratio of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O to nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide in the mixed solution is 1:(0.1-0.3).
   USE - Method for preparing nitrogen-doped graphene-supported core-shell copper-carbon composite catalyst for carbon dioxide based electrocatalytic production of formic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nitrogen-doped graphene-supported core-shell copper-carbon composite catalyst, which solve the problems of poor product selectivity and low stability of the existing porous carbon-supported metal catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nitrogen-doped graphene-supported core-shell copper-carbon composite catalyst involves adding nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide to the mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol to fully disperse, then adding Copper(II) Nitrate Trihydrate (Cu(NO3)2.3H2O) and continue stirring until it is completely dissolved to get mixture. The benzimidazole is dissolved in a mixed solution of N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol and stirring to dissolve to obtain benzimidazole solution, and dissolving trimesic acid in a mixture of N,N-dimethylformamide and ethanol and mixing under stirring in the mixed solution until it dissolves to obtain trimellitic acid solution. The concentration of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O in the mixed solution is 2.5mg/mL-3.0mg/mL. The mass ratio of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O to nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide in the mixed solution is 1:(0.1-0.3). The concentration of the benzimidazole in the benzimidazole solution is 10 mg/mL to 15 mg/mL; and concentration of trimesic acid in the trimesic acid solution is 1.0 mg/mL to 1.5 mg/mL. The mixed solution is mixed with the benzimidazole solution and stir until it appears bright blue, then add the trimellitic acid solution to it and continue mixing and stirring to obtain the precursor solution, and transferring the precursor solution to polytetrafluoroethylene in a stainless steel reaction kettle lined with ethylene, and hydrothermal reaction is carried out for 5-8 hours at a constant temperature of 80-100 degrees C to obtain a CuMOF precursor supported by nitrogen-doped graphene. The mass ratio of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O to benzimidazole in the precursor solution is 1:(3-5), and weight ratio of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O and trimellitic acid in the precursor solution is 1:(0.7-1.0). The cleaning and drying are carried out after centrifuging the CuMOF precursor loaded on nitrogen-doped graphene, for multiple times with absolute ethanol and then drying in a vacuum drying oven to obtain dried product. The dried product os placed in a tube furnace and passed in high-purity argon (Ar) gas, and increasing the temperature to 700 degrees C at a heating rate of 1-5 degrees C/minute and calcined at 700-1000 degrees C for 3-8 hours to obtain desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The graphene oxide is specifically prepared by adding graphene oxide powder to deionized water, then adding urea and transferring it to the reactor, and reacting for 3-8 hours at a temperature of 150-200 degrees C to obtain nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide. The volume/weight of the graphene oxide powder to the deionized water is 1 mg:(1-5) mL, and weight ratio of the graphene oxide powder to the urea is 1:(20-40). The purity of the Ar gas is 99.99%. The volume ratio of N,N-dimethylformamide to ethanol in the mixed solution of dimethylformamide and ethanol is 1:(0.5-2.0). The vacuum drying oven for drying to drying in a vacuum drying oven at a temperature of 60 degrees C. The copper (Cu) is present in the mixed solution, and concentration of (NO3)2.3H2O is 2.7mg/mL-3.0mg/mL. The mass ratio of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O to nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide in the mixed solution described in step one is 1:(0.2-0.3). The concentration of benzimidazole in the benzimidazole solution described in step one is 11.2mg/mL-15mg/mL, and concentration of trimesic acid in the trimesic acid solution described in step one is 1.2 mg/mL to 1.5 mg/mL. The mixed solution is mixed with benzimidazole under stirring until it appeared bright blue, and then adding trimellitic acid solution to it to continue mixing and stirring to obtain the precursor solution. The precursor solution was transferred to a polytetrafluoroethylene-lined stainless steel reactor at a temperature of 90-100 degrees C. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out for 6-8 hours to obtain CuMOF precursor supported by nitrogen-doped graphene. The mass ratio of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O to benzimidazole is 1:(4.2-5), and mass ratio of Cu(NO3)2.3H2O to trimesic acid in the precursor solution is 1:(0.88-1.0). The heating rate is 2-5 degrees C/minutes, and increasing the temperature to 800-1000 degrees C. The calcination temperature is maintained at 800-1000 degrees C for 5-8 hours.

PN CN111054297-A
TI Preparation of manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride for treating low-concentration uranium-containing wastewater, by using porous graphitic carbon nitride, iron(III) chloride hexahydrate, and manganese(II) sulfate monohydrate.
AU LIU J
   GE Y
   XIE S
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA (USOA-C)
GA 202036700G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride involves: (1) preparing a porous graphitic carbon nitride by dissolving melamine and calcium carbonate in deionized water and carrying out ultrasonic treatment, heating the treated solution, stirring to form a gel, vacuum drying, fully grinding and sieving, and putting the powder into a muffle furnace, calcining, grinding the product, sieving and removing a template of calcium carbonate with dilute hydrochloric acid until no carbon dioxide bubbles are generated in water, washing the removed calcium carbonate template with deionized water and drying; and (2) weighing the porous graphitic carbon nitride into an ethylene glycol solution, ultrasonically dispersing, then adding iron(III) chloride hexahydrate, manganese(II) sulfate monohydrate, sodium acetate and polyvinylpyrrolidone, stirring, placing the stirred solution in a stainless steel reactor, heating, cooling, washing the obtained black solid, and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing manganese ferrite/porous graphite carbon nitride, which is useful for treating low-concentration uranium-containing wastewater (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride involves: (1) preparing a porous graphitic carbon nitride by dissolving melamine and calcium carbonate in deionized water and carrying out ultrasonic treatment for 30 minutes, heating the treated solution at 60-90 degrees C, stirring to form a gel, vacuum drying, fully grinding the dried powder and sieving through a 200-mesh sieve, and putting the powder into a muffle furnace, calcining at 500-550 degrees C for 2-5 hours, grinding the product, sieving through the 200-mesh sieve, and removing a template of calcium carbonate with dilute hydrochloric acid until no carbon dioxide bubbles are generated in water, where the mass ratio of melamine to calcium carbonate is 5:1, washing the removed calcium carbonate template with deionized water for several times until the solution is neutral, and placing in an oven to dry; and (2) preparing manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride by adding the weighed porous graphitic carbon nitride into an ethylene glycol solution and ultrasonically dispersing for 30-60 minutes, then sequentially adding iron(III) chloride hexahydrate, manganese(II) sulfate monohydrate, sodium acetate and polyvinylpyrrolidone, stirring for 30-90 minutes, where the mass ratio of porous graphitic carbon nitride, iron(III) chloride hexahydrate, manganese(II) sulfate monohydrate, sodium acetate and polyvinylpyrrolidone is 1:(0.5-2):(0.2-0.5):(1.5-2.5):(0.1-0.5), the molar mass ratio of iron(III) chloride hexahydrate and manganese(II) sulfate monohydrate is 2:1, and the weight ratio of porous graphitic carbon nitride to ethylene glycol solution is 20-50 g/l, placing the stirred solution in a stainless steel reactor containing PTFE liner, heating at 110-180 degrees C for 9-12 hours, then cooling, washing the black solid obtained several times with absolute ethanol and deionized water until the pH value of the supernatant after washing is determined to be neutral, then putting the washed black solid into a vacuum drying oven and drying at 60-80 degrees C for 5-7 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for treating a low-concentration uranium-containing wastewater by using the manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride, which involves adjusting the pH value of uranium-containing wastewater to 4-6, then adding 150-600 mg/l manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride for adsorption, and adopting a water bath oscillator for oscillation adsorption, where the adsorption oscillation time is 10-140 minutes, the concentration of uranium in the low-concentration uranium-containing wastewater is 10 mg/l, and the pH value is 2-8.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method for treating low-concentration uranium-containing wastewater further involves performing desorption of the saturated manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride, where the desorption involves using a magnet to separate the saturated manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride from the uranium-containing wastewater, then putting the manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride into hydrochloric acid for desorption, separating the desorbed manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride from hydrochloric acid by the magnet and washing several times with deionized water until the supernatant after washing is neutral, drying and reusing the desorbed manganese ferrite/porous graphitic carbon nitride.

PT P
PN CN110902710-A; CN110902710-B
TI Preparation of high purity optical glass additive ytterbium fluoride includes mixing ytterbium oxide of rare earth and deionized water, adding nitric acid, taking samples, detecting contents of metal elements, concentrating and cooling.
AU LIU M
   WEI N
   LIN Y
   ZHU J
   YANG Y
   ZHANG F
AE CHENGDE YINGKE FINE CHEM CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202024154F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high purity optical glass additive ytterbium fluoride comprises mixing ytterbium oxide of rare earth and deionized water at weight ratio of 3-4:8-10, adding nitric acid, reacting at 70-80 degrees C, adjusting pH to 2.5-3 to obtain ytterbium nitrate solution, adding deionized water to ytterbium nitrate solution, heating at 100-105 degrees C for 2-3 hours, filtering, adding ytterbium hydroxide to filtrate of ytterbium nitrate, adjusting pH of solution to 3-3.5, adding 5-7 wt.% metastannic acid solution, mixing, heating at 100-105 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, removing head, filtering to obtain filtrate of ytterbium nitrate, taking samples from filtrate of ytterbium nitrate, detecting contents of metal elements iron, copper, nickel, chromium, cobalt, manganese, titanium, vanadium and/or lead in solution by inductively coupled plasma (ICP), heating ytterbium nitrate filtrate, concentrating, cooling to room temperature to obtain ytterbium nitrate crystal.
   USE - The method is for preparation of high purity optical glass additive ytterbium fluoride.
   ADVANTAGE - The mehtod has 0.07-0.2% loss on ignition of ytterbium fluoride and produces 99.9-99.95% purity of ytterbium fluoride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of high purity optical glass additive ytterbium fluoride further comprises placing crystal of ytterbium nitrate in centrifuge for liquid-solid separation, washing nitrate ions on crystal surface of ytterbium nitrate with deionized water to below 30 ppm, centrifuging for 30 minutes, placing ytterbium nitrate crystal in container, adding deionized water, dissolving into ytterbium nitrate solution with specific gravity of 1.05-1.10, placing industrial sodium carbonate into another container, adding deionized water, dissolving to specific gravity of 1.1-1.15 to obtain sodium carbonate solution, cooling sodium carbonate solution to below 30 degrees C, adding trace hydrogen peroxide, mixing, standing for 30 minutes, adding 1.5-3 wt.% sodium sulfide solution to solution, mixing at 90-95 degrees C for 2 hours, removing head, filtering in another container to obtain purified sodium carbonate filtrate, taking samples from sodium carbonate filtrate, detecting content of metal elements iron, copper, nickel, chromium, cobalt, manganese, titanium, vanadium and/or lead in solution by ICP, synthesizing ytterbium carbonate by ytterbium nitrate solution and qualified sodium carbonate filtrate to obtain ytterbium carbonate filter cake, adding 45-65 wt.% high purity hydrofluoric acid to polytetrafluoroethylene reactor, adding deionized water, mixing to obtain 30-35 wt.% high purity hydrofluoric acid, mixing ytterbium carbonate filter cake with water to form paste with 25-35 wt.% solid content, heating at 45-50 degrees C, adding ytterbium carbonate paste to high-purity hydrofluoric acid, adjusting pH to 1-2 to obtain ytterbium fluoride precipitate, adding synthesized ytterbium fluoride precipitate to deionized water, mixing into paste with solid content of 35-40%, heating at 90-110 degrees C for 2-3 hours, removing carbon dioxide, testing pH of reactant, sending ytterbium fluoride after carbon dioxide removal to plate and frame filter press for filtration, washing until chloride ion and sulfate ion in ytterbium fluoride are less than 20 ppm, pressing to dry into ytterbium fluoride filter cake, placing filter cake of ytterbium fluoride in ceramic crucible, calcining at 110-120 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours to separate surface water of ytterbium fluoride, calcining at 450-500 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours to remove crystal water of ytterbium fluoride, calcining at 600-700 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours to remove structural water of ytterbium fluoride, calcining at 750-800 degrees C for 2-3 hours and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of sodium carbonate to hydrogen peroxide is 1000-1500:1-1.5. The concentration of meta stannic acid is 4% or 6%. The concentration of sodium sulfide solution is 2%.

PN CN110564683-A
TI Co-culturing and inducing the expansion of            gamma-delta-T cells and NK cells comprises e.g.            separating monocytes, and resuspending cells in a mixed            medium containing RMPI 1640 containing fetal bovine            serum or autologous serum.
AU XING Y
   DENG M
   CHENG X
   LIU D
   WU J
   WANG B
AE ANHUI RUIDA HEALTH IND CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6640R
AB    NOVELTY - Co-culturing and inducing the expansion of                gamma - delta -T cells and NK cells comprises e.g.                separating monocytes, resuspending cells in a mixed                medium containing RMPI 1640 containing fetal bovine                serum (FBS) or autologous serum and X-vivo (RTM:                Hematopoietic cell medium)-15, inoculating in flask                or bag, adding a mixed medium containing zoledronic                acid for 4 days in vitro stimulation, adding                anti-human CD3Ab, antihuman CD28Ab, IL-15, IL-21,                500-3000 U/ml IL-2 mixed medium for half volume                exchange, culturing in 5% carbon dioxide incubator,                after culturing, supplementing serum-free mixed                medium and IL2 cytokine in a culture bottle or                culture bag, so that the final concentration is the                same as the original concentration, and then                culturing in an incubator, after incubation,                centrifuging the cells, discarding the supernatant                after centrifugation, and transferring the cells in                the culture flask or bag to the T25 cell culture                flask.
   USE - The method is useful for co-culturing and                inducing the expansion of gamma-delta-T cells and                NK cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: cultures the gamma-delta-T cells                and NK cells, which has high amplification factor,                and cytotoxicity of gamma-delta-T cells and NK                cells compound to form a co-culture cell                composition, which adopts the co-culture induction                expansion method to improve cell culture                efficiency, and drastically reduces training costs,                difficulty of culturing process, simplifies the                treatment process in clinical treatment process;                and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-culturing and inducing the expansion of                gamma - delta -T cells and NK cells comprises (i)                separating monocytes, resuspending cells in a mixed                medium containing RMPI 1640 containing 10 wt.%                fetal bovine serum (FBS) or autologous serum and                50-100% X-vivo (RTM: Hematopoietic cell medium)-15,                inoculating in a flask or bag; (ii) adding a mixed                medium containing zoledronic acid at a                concentration of 1-100 mu M and 500-3000 U/ml IL-2                for 4 days in vitro stimulation, adding 10-500                ng/ml anti-human CD3Ab, 10-500 ng/ml antihuman                CD28Ab, 10-200 ng/ml IL-15, 10-200 ng/ml IL-21,                500-3000 U/ml IL-2 mixed medium for half volume                exchange, placing at 37 degrees C, culturing in 5%                carbon dioxide incubator; (iii) after culturing for                2-3 days, supplementing 2 ml serum-free mixed                medium and IL2 cytokine in a culture bottle or                culture bag, so that the final concentration is the                same as the original concentration, and then                culturing in an incubator for 4 days; (iv) after 4                days of incubation, centrifuging the cells at 500                g, discarding the supernatant after centrifugation                for 5 minutes, then transferring the cells in the                culture flask or bag to the T25 cell culture flask,                and then replacing 5 ml EX medium for expansion                culture, adding serum-free EX medium, IL2                cytokines, IL-15, IL-21; (v) after 3-4 days of                culture, supplementing 5 ml serum-free EX medium,                IL2 cytokine, IL-15, IL-21, and 1-100 mu M                zoledronic acid in a T25 cell culture flask, so                that the final concentration and the original                concentration is the same, the total volume is 20                ml; and (vi) after 3-5 days of culture, measuring                the content and total number of NK and gamma -                delta -T cells. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also                included for cell composition, comprising gamma -                delta -T-NK cell composition, obtained by the                method of co-culture and induction expansion of                gamma - delta -T cells and NK cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The other phosphoantigens                  are used instead of zoledronic acid including                  isopentene pyrophosphate (IPP),                  (E)-4-hydroxy-3-methyl-but-2-enyl pyrophosphate                  (HMB-PP), ethyl pyrophosphate (EPP), farnesyl                  pyrophosphate (FPP), dimethylallyl phosphate                  (DMAP), dimethylallyl pyrophosphate (DMAPP),                  ethyl-adenosine triphosphate (EPPPA), geranyl                  pyrophosphate (GPP), geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate                  (GGPP), isopentenyl adenosine-pyrophosphate                  (IPPPA), monoethyl phosphate (MEP), and                  nitrogen-containing diphosphates, namely                  monoethyl pyrophosphate (MEPP). The concentration                  of interleukin-15 used in the cytokine                  composition is preferably 120-350 ng/ml, the                  concentration of interleukin-21 used in the                  cytokine combination is preferably 100-380 ng/ml,                  and the concentration of interleukin-2 used in                  the cytokine combination is preferably 1000-1800                  U/ml. The zoledronic acid concentration is 5 mu                  M, antihuman CD3Ab concentration is 50 ng/ml,                  anti-human CD28Ab concentration is 50 ng/ml,                  IL-15 concentration is 50 ng/ml, IL-21                  concentration is 50 ng/ml, and the IL-2                  concentration is 1000 U/ml.

PT P
PN CN109381393-A; CN209809072-U
TI Preparation device used for preparing infant emollient oil for e.g. relieving dry skin, includes two-way feed split zone, air flow channel, hard impact member, buffer zone, fluid impact chamber, expansion tube area, and steady flow zone.
AU ZOU L
   LIU W
   CHENG C
AE UNIV NANCHANG (NANU-C)
GA 2019208558
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation device comprises two-way feed split zone (1), air flow channel (2), hard impact member (4), buffer zone (5), fluid impact chamber (6), expansion tube area, and steady flow zone. The two-way feed split zone is connected with the fluid impact chamber through buffer zone having symmetric parallel binary structure. The branch ends of the buffer zone are connected to two-way feed split zone and fluid impact chamber having Y-shaped structure. The air flow channel is connected to two-way feed split zone.The hard impact member is disposed inside of the two-way feed split zone through buffer zone. A hard layer is disposed on the fluid impact chamber inner wall. The inner wall of the buffer zone is provided with hard protrusion. The hard protrusion and hard layer are enclosed in a reaction chamber. The inner diameter of the reaction chamber is smaller than of the buffer zone. The reaction chamber is connected to expansion tube area through a discharge valve.
   USE - Preparation device used for preparing infant emollient oil for relieving dry skin, skin allergy, redness and blister, nourishing and moisturizing infant skin, regulating sebaceous glands, and removing red blood clot strands.
   ADVANTAGE - The emollient oil is rich in organic plant extracts and has good therapeutic effect on children and resistance capability to the external environment and has synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the preparation of infant emollient oil comprising (1) drying Camellia, Calendula, honeysuckle and Arnica flower, crushing, sieving through a 40 meshes sieve, uniformly mixing, adding to distilled water, heating to 60-65 degrees C, stirring in pot for 30 minutes, uniformly mixing, and ultrasonically processing at 50-55 degrees C for 30-45 minutes using ultrasonic-microwave combined reactor at ultrasonic power of 1200-1500 W and ultrasonic frequency of 25-40 kHz and microwave power of 1000-1200 W and microwave frequency of 2450 MHz; and cooling to 40-45 degrees C, adjusting pH to 5-5.5, adding cellulase and pectinase, enzymatically hydrolyzing for 3 hours, centrifuging at 2500 revolutions/minute for 10-15 minutes, collecting hydrolysate, adding sodium hyaluronate and tea polyphenol, and uniformly stirring to obtain microcapsule core material; (2) mixing maltodextrin, beta -cyclodextrin and/or fish skin gelatin at a mass ratio of 2% mixed solution, adding Angel dry yeast and papain, fermenting, and enzymatically hydrolyzing to obtain microcapsule wall material; (3) adding microcapsule core material and wall material into two-way feed split zone, thoroughly mixing with carbon dioxide fluid through air flow channel, controlling flow of the fluid using check valve, passing between hard impact chamber at a pressure of 200 MPa, entering buffer zone, buffering, passing fluid impact chamber, entering expansion tube region through discharge valve, gasifying carbon dioxide fluid, releasing core material, and crushing wall material; (4) placing in microcapsule granulating device where nozzle diameter is 2 mm, nitrogen flow rate at 50 mL/second, vibration frequency of 1200 Hz, electrode tension of 800 V and nozzle temperature of 45 degrees C, adding dropwise 0.2 wt.% genipin solution, granulating at 4 degrees C and stirring speed of 500 revolutions/minute, curing for 30 minutes, washing with distilled water, draining water, pouring Camellia oil for 20 minute, standing, layering, uniformly suspending, and draining suspension to obtain microcapsule suspension; and (5) filling functional oil composition with microcapsule suspension into a squeeze dropper bottle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the preparation device.
   Two-way feed split zone (1)
   Air flow channel (2)
   Hard impact member (4)
   Buffer zone (5)
   Fluid impact chamber (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The functional oil composition comprises Camellia seed oil, caprylic/capric triglyceride, natural squalane, jojoba oil, evening primrose oil, white mango flower oil, phytosterol ester, red myrrh, vitamin E acetate, vitamin A acetate, and vitamin B6 triisopalmitate.
   ACTIVITY - Antiallergic; Dermatological; Antiinflammatory; Antiseborrheic.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

PT P
PN CN109223704-A
TI Preparing mesenchymal stem cell nano-essence comprises e.g. cutting the tissue of the decidua surface of placenta, adding serum-free culture medium, heating phase C, cooling, adding phase A and phase B, stirring, filtering and discharging.
AU WANG X
   YUAN W
   WAN Q
AE SOYOUNGLIFE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SOYO-Non-standard)
GA 201912908N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mesenchymal stem cell nano-essence comprises e.g. (i) cutting the tissue of the decidua surface of the placenta, cutting, adding serum-free culture medium, and statically placing; (ii) culturing, changing the solution, sub-culturing, and freezing; (iii) resuscitating P3, P4, P5, P6 cells in mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, culturing, collecting T175 adherent cells and adding buffer; (iv) ultrasonically crushing and sonicating the sample, centrifuging, and mixing the P3,P4, P5, and P6 cell disrupting liquid, i.e. stem cell essence liquid; (v) slowly injecting into a keratin microsphere, ultrasonically emulsifying to obtain an extract defined as A phase; (vi) mixing the culture medium of P3, P4, P5, and P6 cells for stem cell extract dilution liquid defined as phase B; (vii) mixing glycerol, sorbitol, hydrolyzed oat protein, and water defined as phase C; and (viii) heating phase C and stirring, cooling, adding phase A and phase B, stirring, filtering and discharging.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing mesenchymal stem cell nano-essence.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for large-scale production; and encapsulates the active ingredient of mesenchymal stem cells in the microcapsule core, and solves the problem of skin aging.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing mesenchymal stem cell nano-essence comprises (i) cutting the tissue of the decidua surface of the placenta, selecting bright color of the tissue, placing the tissue in 10 cm dish, cutting the tissue using a cell scraper and paving in a T75 cm2 cell culture flask, slowly adding 6 ml serum-free culture medium, placing the culture flask in a culture box at 37 degrees C in the presence of 5% carbon dioxide, and statically placing; (ii) culturing for 7 days, changing the solution twice a day, sub-culturing when the cell confluence reaches to 80%, and freezing P2-P6 cells; (iii) resuscitating P3, P4, P5, P6 cells in mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, culturing in the presence of 5% carbon dioxide in the culture box at 37 degrees C, collecting adherent living cells when the cells grow to a degree of fusion of 80%, collecting the supernatant, subjecting to long-term storage at -20 degrees C, collecting 5 bottles of T175 adherent cells and adding 2-3 ml buffer; (iv) ultrasonically crushing and sonicating the sample in an ice bath, centrifuging the sample in a high-speed centrifuge at 4 degrees C for 15 minutes with a rotating speed of 12000 revolutions/minute, taking the supernatant, dispersing in 3 ml test tube, and mixing the P3,P4, P5, and P6 cell disrupting liquid, i.e. stem cell essence liquid; (v) slowly injecting the stem cell essence obtained in step (iv) into a keratin microsphere at a rate of 10 drops/minute using a disposable syringe, adding essence while stirring with a stirrer, ultrasonically emulsifying the prepared mixed solution in ice bath by a phacoemulsification apparatus to obtain an extract defined as A phase, where the mass ratio of essence and keratin microsphere is 5:1; (vi) mixing the culture medium of P3, P4, P5, and P6 cells for stem cell extract dilution liquid defined as phase B; (vii) mixing glycerol, sorbitol, hydrolyzed oat protein, and water at a mass ratio of 5:3:5:50 to obtain humectant defined as phase C; and (viii) heating phase C to 80 degrees C and stirring uniformly, maintaining the temperature for 10 minutes, cooling, adding phase A and phase B when the temperature reduces to 45 degrees C, stirring uniformly, filtering and discharging, where the mass ratio of phase A, phase B and phase C is 1:50:50.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The serum-free culture medium is mesenchymal stem cell basal culture medium and auxiliary material with a product number of 10151A and 10151B at a mass ratio of 10:1, and adding L-glutamine for making the final concentration to 2 mM.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)
