PT P
PN CN109053615-A; CN109053615-B
TI System useful for producing melamine comprises            e.g. fluidized bed reactor to generate the mixed gas            which orderly enters gas heat cooler, hot gas filter,            crystallizer, catcher, urea washing tower, gas-liquid            separator, and demister.
AU XI Y
AE XI Y (XIYY-Individual)
GA 201900908S
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises fluidized bed reactor to                generate the mixed gas which orderly enters gas                heat cooler, hot gas filter, crystallizer, catcher,                urea washing tower, gas-liquid separator, and                demister. The demister and the crystallizer                connected. The molten salt groove is sequentially                connected with molten salt furnace and fluidized                bed reactor to provide a heat exchange medium. The                carrier gas pre-heater is respectively connected to                the fluidized bed reactor and the molten salt                groove. The molten salt is sequentially transferred                from the molten salt groove into the molten salt                furnace, the fluidized bed reactor, the carrier gas                pre-heater and returned to the molten salt groove.                The granular urea outside the boundary zone enters                the lower part of the washing tower, the molten                urea flowing out from the bottom of the urea                washing tower enters the upper and middle portions                of the urea washing tower through a portion of the                recycle pump, and the other part enters the                fluidized bed reactor.
   USE - The system is useful for producing melamine                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system: reduces the energy consumption of                molten urea and environmental pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing the melamine comprises (i) heating the                molten salt of the molten salt groove into the                molten salt furnace, where the fuel and air from                outside the boundary zone enter the molten salt                furnace, introducing the molten salt into the                fluidized bed reactor as a heating medium,                returning the molten salt after heat exchange to                the molten salt tank through the heat transfer                pre-heater, and melting the granular urea in the                washing tower and then introducing into the                fluidized bed reactor to react under the action of                the catalyst and the fluidizing gas to obtain a                reaction product gas, (ii) cooling the reaction gas                in the hot gas cooler, precipitating high-boiling                by-products, and exchanging the heat with the                channel condenser using the heat exchange medium of                the hot gas cooler, introducing the high-boiling                by-product and the remaining reaction-generating                gas to the hot gas filter for impurity removal,                discharging the filter residue obtained by the hot                gas filter, removing the impurity into the                crystallizer to obtain the precipitated melamine                and process gas in the crystallizer, (iii)                introducing the melamine and process gas into the                collector for separation, introducing the melamine                to the finished storage tank, and introducing the                process gas the urea washing tower, and (iv)                exchanging heat with the particulate molten urea                using process gas in the urea washing tower,                introducing the heated molten urea into the                fluidized bed reactor, passing the cooled process                gas through the gas-liquid separator and a part of                the demister and discharging outside the boundary,                and introducing part of the crystal to the                crystallizer as a cooling medium. In the step (i),                the temperature in the fluidized bed reactor is                380-400 degrees C, the pressure in the fluidized                bed reactor is 0.05-2.2 MPa, and the resulting                reaction gas is a mixture of melamine, ammonia and                carbon dioxide gas. In the step (ii), the reaction                gas is cooled in a hot gas cooler to 320-345                degrees C, the process gas in the crystallizer is                mainly a mixture of ammonia and carbon dioxide gas,                and the outlet temperature after cooling of the                crystallizer is 190-220 degrees C, In the step                (iii), the temperature inside the trap is 190-220                degrees C, and In the step (iv) the pressure of the                process gas separated by the gas-liquid separator                is 0.01-1.9 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - IMAGING AND COMMUNICATION - Preferred Components: The fluidized bed                  reactor is provided with a catalyst feeding                  opening, and a fluidized gas pipeline is set on                  the pipeline of the molten urea into the                  fluidized bed reactor. The top of the hot gas                  cooler is connected to the channel condenser and                  a steam buffer tank in turn, where the heated                  medium enters the channel condenser and is                  returned to the hot gas cooler after being                  heated, then the heated medium in the channel                  condenser enters the steam buffer tank,                  preferably the desalted water outside the                  boundary zone enters the steam buffer tank to                  exchange heat with the heat medium in the steam                  buffer tank, where the steam buffer tank                  discharges steam. The upper part of the hot gas                  filter is connected to the blowback gas, and the                  bottom of the hot gas filter is provided with a                  residue discharge port. The bottom of the                  collector is connected to a stirrer, and provided                  with a material outlet which is sequentially                  connected to a screw discharge machine, a                  discharge buffer tank, a finished product storage                  tank, and a packaging machine. The process gas                  discharged from the demister enters the                  crystallizer, the outer boundary, and the carrier                  gas pre-heater respectively, a carrier gas                  compressor is set on the pipe connecting the                  demister and the carrier gas pre-heater, a                  cooling air blower is arranged on the pipeline                  connected to the collector and the urea washing                  tower, or the process gas discharged from the                  demister enters the crystallizer and the outside,                  respectively, and the cooling air blower is                  arranged on the pipeline connected to the                  collector and the demister, or the process gas                  discharged from the demister enters the                  crystallizer and the outside, respectively, a                  cooling air blower is arranged on the pipe                  connecting the demister and the crystallizer, and                  the mixed gas discharged from the collector                  enters the urea washing tower and the carrier gas                  compressor respectively, where the carrier gas                  compressor is connected to the carrier gas                  pre-heater, or the demister discharges the                  process gas into the crystallizer and the                  outside, respectively, and the mixed gas                  discharged from the collector enters the carrier                  gas compressor and the cooling air blower                  respectively, where the cooling air blower is                  connected to the urea washing tower, and the                  carrier gas compressor is connected to the                  carrier gas pre-heater. Preferred method: In the                  step (iv), the process gas passes through a part                  of the demister and exits the boundary, part of                  which enters the crystallizer as a cooling                  medium, and a part enters the carrier gas                  compressor and enters the pre-heater of the                  carrier gas pre-heater to enter the fluidized bed                  reactor to participate in the reaction. In the                  step (iii), the process gas exiting the collector                  enters the urea washing tower after entering the                  cold air blower. A part of the process gas from                  the trap enters the urea washing tower, and a                  part enters the carrier gas compressor and enters                  the pre-heater of the carrier gas pre-heater to                  enter the fluidized bed reactor to participate in                  the reaction, or a part of the process gas                  exiting the collector enters the urea washing                  tower after entering the cold air blower, and a                  part enters the carrier gas compressor and enters                  the pre-heater of the carrier gas pre-heater to                  enter the fluidized bed reactor to participate in                  the reaction.

PN CN108539309-A; CN108539309-B
TI Recovering waste lithium nickel cobalt manganese            oxide cathode material comprises e.g. dismantling used            nickel-cobalt-manganese acid battery and placing anode            in a liquid nitrogen freezing crusher, sieving and            returning to continue freezing.
AU FANG J
AE FANG J (FANG-Individual)
GA 201874394V
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering waste lithium nickel cobalt                manganese oxide cathode material comprises e.g.                dismantling the used nickel-cobalt-manganese acid                battery and placing the anode in a liquid nitrogen                freezing crusher for crushing, then sieving and                returning the sieve to continue freezing and                crushing to obtain sieved material, placing the                sieved material in a reduction furnace and passing                the hydrogen for reduction and discharging the                furnace to obtain a raw material after the cooling                section in the reduction furnace, storing the raw                material in a storage box filled with nitrogen,                then washing by adding hot pure water and obtaining                washing liquid and slag after three stages of                countercurrent washing, passing carbon dioxide into                the washing liquid, then filtering to obtain                lithium hydrogencarbonate solution and aluminum                hydroxide precipitate, subjecting the aluminum                hydroxide precipitate to a three-stage                countercurrent washing and calcining to obtain                ultrafine alumina.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering waste                lithium nickel cobalt manganese oxide cathode                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, realizes the                separation and recovery of the complete component,                has high recovery rate and high product additional                value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering waste lithium nickel cobalt                manganese oxide cathode material comprises (i)                dismantling the used nickel-cobalt-manganese acid                battery and placing the anode in a liquid nitrogen                freezing crusher for crushing, then sieving,                passing through a 100-200 meshes sieve, and                returning the sieve to continue freezing and                crushing to obtain sieved material; (ii) placing                the sieved material obtained in the step (i) in a                reduction furnace and passing the hydrogen for                reduction, where the residence time of the sieved                material in the reduction furnace is 8-12 hours and                packing the sieved material in the crucible, where                the thickness of the material is 3-5cm, the flow                rate of hydrogen in the reduction furnace is 3-5                m/second, the hydrogen content in the atmosphere of                the reduction furnace is greater than 99.5%, the                reduction temperature is 400-450 degrees C, and                discharging the furnace to obtain a raw material                after the cooling section in the reduction furnace                to a temperature of less than 40 degrees C; (iii)                storing the raw material obtained in the step (ii)                in a storage box filled with nitrogen, then washing                by adding hot pure water, where the solid-liquid                ratio is 1:10-15, and obtaining washing liquid and                slag after three stages of countercurrent washing,                passing carbon dioxide into the washing liquid,                then filtering to obtain lithium hydrogencarbonate                solution and aluminum hydroxide precipitate,                subjecting the aluminum hydroxide precipitate to a                three-stage countercurrent washing, then calcining                to obtain ultrafine alumina, and subjecting the                obtained lithium hydrogencarbonate to pyrolysis to                obtain battery grade lithium carbonate; and (iv)                mixing the washing slag obtained in the step (iii)                with the hydrazine hydrate solution, then adding                sodium hydroxide to adjust the pH of the solution                to 13-14, then pouring into the stirring mill tank,                heating to a temperature of 75-85 degrees C,                stirring after reacting for 1-2 hours, pouring the                material and filtering to obtain second filtrate                and second filter residue, adding the second filter                residue into the hydrazine hydrate solution for                3-stage countercurrent washing, then placing in a                vacuum drying oven for vacuum drying after                screening and magnetic separation, then mixing the                material with the commercially available cobalt                powder, nickel powder and manganese powder to                obtain nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary alloy powder                or directly added with acid to obtain                nickel-cobalt-manganese ternary mixed                solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (ii) uses                  corundum, mullite or stainless steel crucible. In                  step (iii) the hot pure water is used for                  washing, and the temperature of the hot pure                  water in the raw material process is 75-90                  degrees C, when the carbon dioxide passed to                  precipitate the aluminum, the pH meter is                  inserted into the washing liquid to start the                  carbon dioxide process, where the pH is gradually                  decreases, the process produces precipitates of                  aluminum hydroxide and lithium carbonate, and                  then the carbon dioxide is continuously added,                  where the pH of the washing liquid gradually                  rises and then the carbon dioxide is stopped                  until the pH meter is no longer changed and then                  filtered. The washing liquid obtained by the                  third-stage countercurrent washing of the                  aluminum hydroxide precipitate is mixed with the                  lithium hydrogencarbonate solution, and the mass                  ratio of the aluminum hydroxide precipitation to                  the washing water in the washing process is 1:4                  and the temperature of the washing water is 35-45                  degrees C, where the washed aluminum hydroxide                  precipitation calcination temperature is 750-850                  degrees C, the calcination time is 2-4 hours,                  then the lithium hydrogencarbonate solution is                  pyrolyzed at a temperature of 95-99 degrees C and                  the stirring speed is 150-200 rotations/minute,                  the decomposition time is 2.5 hours, and the                  decomposed filtrate is returned to the washing                  raw material and the obtained lithium carbonate                  is dried and sieved to obtain battery grade                  lithium carbonate. In step (iv) the concentration                  of the hydrazine hydrate solution is 0.5 mol/l,                  the mass ratio of the washing slag to the                  hydrazine hydrate solution is 1:3, the stirring                  speed of the stirring mill is 30                  rotations/minute, the grinding ball added is                  zirconia material and the diameter of the                  grinding ball is 0.2-1 cm, where the carbon                  dioxide gas is added to precipitate aluminum into                  aluminum hydroxide, and the aluminum hydroxide is                  washed and mixed with the aluminum hydroxide                  precipitate in the step (iii) to be calcined and                  the concentration of the hydrazine hydrate                  solution used for the second filter washing is                  0.06 mol/l. The drying temperature of the second                  filter residue in vacuum drying process is 60-70                  degrees C and the vacuum degree is -0.09 to -0.08                  Mpa. In step (iv) the nickel-cobalt-manganese                  ternary mixed solution is returned to prepare a                  nickel-cobalt-manganese oxide anode                  material.

PN CN111945185-A
TI Graphene aerogel working electrode material useful for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises nano cuprous oxide powder supported on graphene aerogel, porous doped CNT graphene aerogel material.
AU CUI X
   SUN H
   JING T
   PANG X
   YU S
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2020B7444X
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene aerogel working electrode material comprises graphene aerogel and nano cuprous oxide powder supported on graphene aerogel, where the graphene aerogel is a three-dimensional porous doped CNT graphene aerogel material, and prepared by using graphene oxide and carbon nanotubes as raw materials.
   USE - The working electrode material/working electrode is useful for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the stability of the catalyst; is stable and simple; is easy to operate and has better application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for :
   (1) preparing graphene aerogel working electrode material comprising loading nano cuprous oxide powder on graphene aerogel;
   (2) graphene aerogel working electrode comprising electrode substrate and the graphene aerogel working electrode material, where the graphene aerogel working electrode material is attached to the surface of the electrode substrate, preferably, the electrode substrate is carbon paper or carbon nanotube sponge, the specifications of the carbon paper and the carbon nanotube sponge are any one of 1x 1 cm, 1x 1.5 cm, 1.5x 1.5 cm, 1.5x 2 cm and 2x 2 cm, where the carbon nanotube sponge is a sponge-type carbon nanotube macroscopic body formed by self-assembly of multi-wall carbon nanotubes, and has a thickness of 1-4 mm;
   (3) preparing a graphene aerogel working electrode comprising coating the dispersed suspension of the nano cuprous oxide powder and the graphene aerogel on the surface of the electrode substrate, preferably, coating an electrode substrate with the dispersion suspension, preferably by an infrared lamp; and
   (4) electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprising using the graphene aerogel working electrode as a cathode electrode for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, using a double-chamber electrolytic cell is used, an external voltage, using aqueous potassium bicarbonate solution as the electrolyte solution, using an H-type double-chamber electrolytic cell, separating by a Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer proton exchange membrane in the middle, allowing to pass hydrogen to pass through, the counter electrode is a platinum mesh, the reference electrode is silver/silver chloride, providing the voltage by the AUTOLAB electrochemical workstation, the electrolyte solution is 0.1-0.5 mol/l potassium bicarbonate solution, during the electrocatalysis process, continuously feeding H-type double-chamber electrolytic cell with carbon dioxide gas, feeding carbon dioxide gas into the electrolyte before the reaction proceeds 30-60 minutes, preferably, in the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide system, the electrode holder adopts a glassy carbon substrate electrode holder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The loading amount of the nano cuprous oxide powder is 0.1-5 mg/cm2, preferably 1-2 mg/cm2. The mass ratio of the carbon nanotubes in the raw material is 9-11 wt.%, preferably 10 wt.%, preferably, the average particle size of the nano cuprous oxide powder is 20-60 nm. Preferred Method: The preparation of graphene aerogel working electrode comprises dispersing nano cuprous oxide powder and the graphene aerogel, mixing in a solution system, obtaining after drying, preferably, the graphene aerogel is in powder form, preferably, the solvent of the solution system includes alcohol, water and Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, preferably, the alcohol includes ethanol and isopropanol, preferably, the volume ratio of the alcohol to water is 1:5-5:1, volume ratio of the ethanol and the isopropanol is 1:5-5:1, the volume ratio of the Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution to water is 1:5-1:2, the mass concentration of the Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution is 4-6 wt.%, preferably, dispersing and mixing the nano cuprous oxide powder and the graphene aerogel in a solution system includes mixing the powdered graphene aerogel and nano cuprous oxide powder, adding ethanol , isopropanol, water and Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, ultrasonically dispersing until uniform, where the water is preferably deionized water, preferably, the mass ratio of the graphene aerogel to the nano cuprous oxide powder is 1:1-30.

PN CN111218022-A; CN111218022-B
TI Preparing negative Poisson's ratio bio-based rigid            foam material involves placing the bio-based material            in the high-pressure reactor, closing the high-pressure            reactor and passing carbon dioxide until the air in the            high-pressure reactor.
AU HUANG J
   HE Y
AE CHONGQING NAYAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (CHON-Non-standard)
   CHONGQING NAYAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 202051449S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing negative Poisson's ratio bio-based                rigid foam material involves placing the bio-based                material in the high-pressure reactor, closing the                high-pressure reactor and passing carbon dioxide                until the air in the high-pressure reactor is                exhausted, and continue to passing carbon dioxide                until the pressure in the high-pressure reactor is                greater than 7.5MPa, stopping the carbon dioxide                flow, heating up to above the melting point of the                bio-based material and holding for 2-3 hours,                cooling down to below the melting point of the                bio-based material and holding for 10-15 minutes                within 10-20 degrees C. The pressure is immediately                released and taken out product is cooled to room                temperature to produce bio-based rigid foam                material, heated the bio-based rigid foam material                prepared to between the softening temperature and                the melting point of the bio-based material, and                maintained for 0.5-1 hours from the bio-based rigid                foam material.
   USE - Method for preparing negative Poisson's ratio                bio-based rigid foam material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare negative                Poisson's ratio bio-based rigid foam material has                good application prospectin aerospace, automobile                and ship, encapsulated field, and excellent                mechanical properties such ascompression, in simple                and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing negative Poisson's ratio bio-based                rigid foam material involves placing the bio-based                material in the high-pressure reactor, closing the                high-pressure reactor and passing carbon dioxide                until the air in the high-pressure reactor is                exhausted, and continue to passing carbon dioxide                until the pressure in the high-pressure reactor is                greater than 7.5MPa, stopping the carbon dioxide                flow, heating up to above the melting point of the                bio-based material and holding for 2-3 hours,                cooling down to below the melting point of the                bio-based material and holding for 10-15 minutes                within 10-20 degrees C. The pressure is immediately                released and taken out product is cooled to room                temperature to produce bio-based rigid foam                material, heated the bio-based rigid foam material                prepared to between the softening temperature and                the melting point of the bio-based material, and                maintained for 0.5-1 hours from the bio-based rigid                foam material. The axial and circumferential                compression is performed to obtain a negative                Poisson's ratio bio-based rigid foam                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The bio-based material                  is polybutylene succinate, polylactic acid,                  polyhydroxy fatty acid ester or bio-based                  polyurethane. The inner cavity of the                  high-pressure reaction kettle is cylindrical. The                  instantaneous pressure relief is carried out by                  reducing the pressure in the high-pressure                  reaction kettle to the atmospheric pressure level                  within 3 seconds. The compression is performed,                  both the axial and circumferential compression                  ratios are 10-30%.

PN CN110171848-A; CN110171848-B
TI Recycling additive materials in chromium salt production process involves using chromium-containing material with calcium-containing material, oxidative roasting, leaching and decomposition, and obtaining ammonia gas and/or carbon dioxide.
AU QI T
   LI X
   PENG Z
   LIU G
   ZHOU Q
   CHEN H
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201975276M
AB    NOVELTY - Method of producing recyclable chromium salt of additive material involves (1) mixing chromium-containing material with calcium-containing material, grinding, formulating into raw meal, (2) subjecting raw material to oxidative roasting, obtaining clinker containing chromium calcium compound and producing carbon dioxide at same time (3) grinding clinker compound and placing in ammonium salt leaching agent, subjecting to liquid-solid separation to obtain crude ammonium chromate solution and leaching residue, (4) purifying ammonium chromate salt and crystallization mother liquor, producing ammonia and carbon dioxide, (5) thermally decomposing the ammonium chromate salt obtained to form chromic anhydride, producing ammonia gas, or calcining ammonium chromate salt obtained to form chromium oxide, producing nitrogen and water, (6) absorbing the ammonia gas and/or carbon dioxide obtained, recycling resulting solution as ammonium salt solution leaching agent of step (3).
   USE - Method of producing recyclable chromium salt of additive material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses low-cost and easily cyclable calcium carbonate as additive materials for chromium salt production, avoiding consumption of large amounts of alkali in the production of chromium salts and emission of chromium-containing waste slag such as chromium mirabilite, cost-effectively recycling the additive materials in the chromium salt production process, reduce the material consumption of the chromium salt production process, environmentally-friendly, and obtained high quality chromium salt.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of producing recyclable chromium salt of additive material involves (1) mixing chromium-containing material with the calcium-containing material, grinding, formulating into raw meal, (2) subjecting raw material obtained in step (1) to oxidative roasting under an oxidizing atmosphere, obtaining clinker containing chromium calcium compound and producing carbon dioxide at the same time (3) grinding clinker compound and placing in an ammonium salt leaching agent to be leached, subjecting leaching slurry to liquid-solid separation to obtain a crude ammonium chromate solution and a leaching residue, (4) purifying and removing crude ammonium chromate solution obtained after the step (3), evaporating and obtaining ammonium chromate salt and crystallization mother liquor, simultaneously producing ammonia and carbon dioxide, (5) thermally decomposing the ammonium chromate salt obtained after step (4) to form chromic anhydride, simultaneously producing ammonia gas, or calcining the ammonium chromate salt obtained after the step (4) to form chromium oxide, simultaneously producing nitrogen and water, (6) absorbing the ammonia gas and/or carbon dioxide obtained after step (2), the step (4) and/or step (5), recycling the resulting solution as the ammonium salt solution leaching agent of step (3).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The chromium-containing material chosen from one or more of chromite, chromium-containing sludge and chromium-containing waste. The calcium-containing material chosen from one or more of calcium carbonate, calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide. The ammonium salt leaching agent is chosen from one or more of ammonium carbonate solution, ammonium bicarbonate solution, and aqueous ammonia solution, and ammonium chromate. In step (3) adding an oxidizing agent during leaching process, the oxidizing agent chosen from one or more of hydrogen peroxide, ammonium persulfate and sodium peroxide. Preferred Property: The raw meal in step (1) has a particle size of not less than 150 mu m. The concentration of the ammonium salt leaching agent is not greater than 30 g/L. In step (3), the size of the clinker after grinding is not less than 200 mu m. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of calcium-containing compound to chromium-containing compound in the raw meal is greater than or equal to 1. The total ammonium amount in the leaching system is 1.0 times or more theoretical amount required to form chromium in the clinker into ammonium chromate. The total amount of carbonate in the and/or leaching system is 1.0 times or more theoretical amount required to convert calcium in the clinker to calcium carbonate. Preferred Condition: In the step (2), The temperature of the oxidative baking is 600-1100 degrees C, the oxidizing roasting time is 0.5-6.0 hours, and the oxidizing atmosphere is air or an oxygen-rich atmosphere. n the step (3), the leaching temperature is 5-150 degrees C and the leaching time is 0.5-6 hours. In step (5), the ammonium chromate salt is thermally decomposed to form chromic anhydride at 120-250 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours. The calcination of ammonium chromate to form chromium oxide at 350-1200 degrees C for 0.1-5 hours. Preferred Process: The calcium-rich material obtained by subjecting leaching slag or the leaching slag obtained after the step (3) to the stepwise enrichment is recycled as the calcium-containing material of the step (1), mixing crystallization mother liquid obtained in the step (4) with the crude ammonium chromate solution, performing the evaporation crystallization, or introducing obtained crystallization mother liquid into the step (6) as a solution for absorbing ammonia gas and/or carbon dioxide. In step (6), the solution for absorbing ammonia gas and/or carbon dioxide is one or more of an aqueous solution, a crystallization mother liquid obtained in the step (4), and a washing liquid in the step (3) leaching residue.
DC E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.)

PT P
PN CN109652655-A; CN109652655-B
TI Recovering lithium during recycling of lithium battery comprises mechanically crushing waste lithium battery cells, soaking, filtering, performing pyrolysis, stirring, screening, heating, adding crude product heating, filtering and drying.
AU ZHOU M
   GUO Z
   HAN Y
   XU K
   JIA Z
   ZHANG L
   WEI H
   ZHANG X
   YUAN B
AE SHENYANG RES INST CHEM IND CO LTD (SNCM-C)
   SHENYANG ZHONGHUA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201939348L
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering lithium during recycling of lithium battery comprises (i) mechanically crushing waste lithium battery cells, soaking in water, performing water leaching, filtering to obtain filter cake fragments under protection of carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas under anaerobic conditions, performing pyrolysis to obtain pyrolysis material, soaking, stirring, screening to obtain copper and aluminum foil mixed chips, anode and n cathode mixed filter cake and lithium rich solution, (ii) subjecting the lithium-rich solution to stirring, soaking, heating to obtain crude lithium carbonate, (iii) adding crude product to water to prepare lithium carbonate slurry, pumping into a liquid jet loop reactor, performing reaction by passing carbon dioxide and filtering out the precipitate to obtain a refined lithium solution and (iv) heating, insulating, filtering, washing the filter cake, drying under carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering lithium during recycling of lithium battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: recovers lithium, which has a purity of more than 99.5%, and reaches the impurity index of the industry standard for battery-grade lithium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering lithium during recycling of lithium battery comprises (i) mechanically crushing waste lithium battery cells, soaking in water for 0.1-1 hours, performing water leaching, filtering to obtain filter cake fragments under protection of carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas under anaerobic conditions, performing pyrolysis to obtain pyrolysis material, soaking, stirring for 1-5 hours, performing hot and cold filtration and screening to obtain copper and aluminum foil mixed chips, anode and cathode mixed filter cake and lithium rich solution, where the amount of water is adding during the water leaching process for 2-20 times, (ii) subjecting the lithium-rich solution to stirring, soaking the filtrate repeatedly in water until the lithium carbonate has a mass concentration of 1.1-1.3 wt% in the lithium-rich solution, heating at 80-100 degrees C, precipitating the lithium carbonate by the difference in solubility and filtering to obtain crude lithium carbonate, (iii) adding crude product to water to prepare 2-8 wt.% lithium carbonate slurry, pumping into a liquid jet loop reactor, performing reaction by passing CO2 under normal temperature and normal pressure, at the pH of 7-9 and filtering out the precipitate to obtain a refined lithium solution and (iv) heating at 80-100 degrees C, insulating for 0.5-2 hours, filtering, washing the filter cake with 90-100 degrees C water, drying at 100-200 degrees C in a carbon dioxide atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprise crushing waste lithium battery cells, subjecting to water leaching, filtering at 5-15 degrees C/minute under a condition of carbon dioxide or a carbon dioxide-containing mixed gas under anaerobic conditions, heating to 400-800 degrees C for 0.5-4 hours. The step (ii) comprises taking the filtrate of the step (i) of water leaching process of material after pyrolysis. The step (iv) comprises taking filtrate of the step (iii) of preparing the crude lithium carbonate slurry Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide containing mixed gas in the step (i) comprises nitrogen, carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide in a volume ratio 1:10-10:1.

The modified dolomite is first calcined and                  then acid-modified dolomite. AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202253543G
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling rare earth molten salt from rare earth waste graphite involves crushing waste graphite, grinding, grinding to obtain graphite powder with a particle size of 200 mesh. The flotation auxiliary agent is added to the graphite solution, uniformly mixed to obtain a mixed powder, the mixed powder is added into a flotation tank, water is added and stirred uniformly, then the flotation agent is added under the condition of continuously stirring for flotation treatment, and processing is done to obtain flotation overflow and flotation underflow. The flation overflow obtained is dried, and dried to obtain Graphite powder, and sold as a product. The obtained flation underflow is roasted, oxidized, and combusted during the roasting process to generate carbon dioxide. After roasting, the pure rare-earth molten salt is obtained, and after sufficient reaction, washed, and dr to obtain the desired composite product.
   USE - Method for recycling rare earth molten salt from rare earth waste graphite used for preparing rare earth metal common material such as anode and electrolytic cell.

   (1) raw extract of sandalwood, comprising                10-70%, preferably 16-19% volatile compounds, and                30-90%, preferably 81-84% semi to non-volatile                compounds;
   (2) solubilized extract of sandalwood,                comprising semi-to non-volatile compounds including                0.0035-0.035 wt.% syringaldehyde and octyldodecanol                as solubilization solvent; and
   (3) composition comprising 0.001-1 wt.%,                preferably 0.1-1 wt.% extract of sandalwood and a                medium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The supercritical                  fluid is carbon dioxide. The co-solvent is 80-100                  vol.%, preferably 96 vol.% ethanol. The mass                  ratio of the supercritical fluid to the                  co-solvent is 0.050-0.080, preferably                  0.060-0.070. The mass ratio of supercritical                  fluid to spent sandalwood is 10-50, preferably                  25-35. The crude extract is dissolved in a                  solvent of 8-30C saturated or unsaturated, linear                  or branched fatty alcohol including                  octyldodecanol, 2-hexyl decanol, oleic alcohol                  and/or triglycerides. Preferred Method: The                  extraction is carried out at 35-85 degrees C,                  preferably 55-65 degrees C with a pressure of                  90-1000 bar, preferably 250-400 bar.
   ACTIVITY - Endocrine-Gen; Nootropic; Dermatological. Test                details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Sandalwood chips were moistened with 40 wt.%                water, then mixed with 10 wt.% powdered cellulose                and then placed in a stainless steel cartridge. The                cartridge was inserted into a supercritical fluid                extractor. Supercritical carbon dioxide was passed                at a flow rate of 15 kg/hour supplemented with 96                vol.% ethanol as polar co-solvent at a flow rate of                20 ml/minute, extracted at 60 degrees C with a                pressure of 300 bar. The extract was evaporated                under vacuum to obtain sandalwood extract.

TI Glucose monitoring system for detecting and            monitoring analyte levels, has processor that outputs            first and second notifications if determined frequency            of interaction is below and above predetermined target            level of interaction.
AU KUMAR P A
AE ABBOTT DIABETES CARE INC (ABBO-C)
   ABBOTT DIABETES CARE INC (ABBO-C)
GA 2021D4905N
AB    NOVELTY - A glucose monitoring system comprises a sensor                control device 102 comprising an analyte sensor 104                coupled with sensor electronics, and a reader                device 120 has a wireless communication circuitry                configured to receive the data indicative of the                analyte level, and processors coupled with a memory                configured to store instructions. The processors is                used to determine a frequency of interaction over a                first time period based on instances of user                operation of the reader device. A first                notification is outputted if the determined                frequency is below or above a predetermined target                level of interaction.
   USE - System for monitoring glucose level of a                patient. Uses include but are not limited to                glucose, ketones, lactate, oxygen, hemoglobin A1C,                albumin, alcohol, alkaline phosphatase, alanine                transaminase, aspartate aminotransferase,                bilirubin, blood urea nitrogen, calcium, carbon                dioxide, chloride, creatinine and hematocrit.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides improved digital                interfaces and/or features for analyte monitoring                systems that improve upon: the accuracy and                integrity of the analyte data being collected by                the system by allowing for data backfilling;                flexibility of the system, by allowing users to                transition between different reader devices; and                alarming functionality of the monitoring system by                providing for more robust inter-device                communications during certain adverse                conditions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                system.
   ensor control device (102)
   analyte sensor (104)
   reader device (120)
   Input (121)
   Display (122)

PN US2021364004-A1
TI Method for assisting in regulating airflow in connection with product displayed on display for sale in space, involves regulating fan in space adjacent to product based on condition associated with product display.
AU OLSEN J W W
   TABER C R
   LESSER T J
   ROBINSON M S
   STIVERS C M
   COMBS J D
AE DELTA T LLC (DLET-C)
GA 2021D8742X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves regulating a fan (12) in a space adjacent to a product based on a condition associated with a product display. The regulating step is performed based on the condition selected from a group consisting of a type of product, a time of day, a concentration of carbon dioxide, a relative humidity, or any combination of the foregoing. A temperature within a pile of product is predicted based on sensed surface temperature of the pile. The fan is operated at a speed based on detected difference in a temperature associated with the product at the condition. A heating, ventilation and air conditioning (HVAC) system (20) is regulated.
   USE - Method for assisting in regulating airflow in connection with product displayed on display for sale in space.
   ADVANTAGE - The useful life of the produce is prolonged by retarding spoilage by regulating the operation of one or more fans for circulating air in a space including the produce. The discomfort to consumers and/or disrupting air curtains associated with open air refrigeration cases in the space is avoided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for regulating airflow in a space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of system for regulating airflow in a space.
   Airflow regulating system (10)
   Fan (12)
   Central control (18)
   HVAC system (20)
   Sensor (16a to 16d)

PT P
PN RU2759663-C1
TI Infection unit for animals has body, formed inside whereof is at least one box for accommodating the animal, made in the form of a cell.
AU VOLF E V
AE VOLF E V (VOLF-Individual)
GA 2021D4810H
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the field of veterinary medicine, in particular, to an infection unit for animals. The unit consists of a body, formed inside whereof is at least one box for accommodating the animal, made in the form of a cell with side walls and a back wall, placed whereon is a locking valve for deactivating forced ventilation, a heated floor, ceiling and door with a hole for passing the infusion system and with an adjustable vent for hot air discharge. The body is made with a hollow stiffening bar for placing an electric shaft for electrical equipment. Holes are made on the body for connecting the inlet and outlet ventilation shafts, wherein the inlet ventilation shaft is connected with an exhaust turbine for exhaust air removal and is directed to the external ventilation system, and is connected by the opposite end thereof with the body in the back part thereof, and the outlet ventilation shaft is connected to the vent for clean air intake. One block with ozone-free bactericidal lamps is placed inside each ventilation shaft. TI Enhancing mesenchymal stem cell external secretion body mucous membrane repairing function useful in biological medicine comprises co-culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell and beneficial bacteria gel ball, replacing serum-free culture medium and filtering supernatant.
AU WANG Q
   MING L
   WANG Z
AE SHAANXI KEMEI ZHISHANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021D53676
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing mesenchymal stem cell external secretion body mucous membrane repairing function comprises (a) preparing mesenchymal stem cells, (b) using beneficial bacteria, sodium alginate solution and calcium chloride solution as material, preparing beneficial bacteria gel ball, (c) co-culturing the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell and beneficial bacteria gel ball, placing the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell in 37degrees Celsius, 5% carbon dioxide, saturated humidity culture box to culture for 24-48 oursh, replacing serum-free culture medium, adding beneficial bacteria gel ball, continuously culturing and (d) preparing the exosome after the co-culture, collecting the culture supernatant for ultrafiltration concentration and obtaining the exosome complex with enhanced mucosal remediation efficacy.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing mesenchymal stem cell external secretion body mucous membrane repairing function in biological medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: allows to grow mesenchymal stem cells adherently on the bottom surface of the culture container, and simultaneously wraps the beneficial bacteria inside the gel ball, thus the stem cells and beneficial bacteria can regulate each other through the pores of the gel through their respective secretions; strengthens the synergistic effect of stem cells and beneficial bacteria, and enhances the mucosal repair effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an exosomal complex with enhanced mucosal repair efficacy prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mesenchymal stem cell is from the animal umbilical cord of the isolated body. The animal is cattle, sheep or pig. The beneficial bacteria is at least one of Bifidobacterium, Lactobacillus and microzyme. The mass concentration of the sodium alginate solution is 2-6% and the concentration of the calcium chloride solution is 1-4g/100 ml. The mesenchymal stem cell is inoculated in the culture container at a density of 3-8x104 cells/ml.Preferred Method: The preparation method of the beneficial bacteria gel ball comprises (1) preparing 2-6% sodium alginate solution and calcium chloride solution, sterilizing, cooling to room temperature and (2) weighing the beneficial bacteria, adding proper sterile water to mix uniformly, uniformly mixing with sodium alginate solution, absorbing the mixed solution, uniformly dropping to calcium chloride solution, calcifying into ball, crosslinking for 1-12 hours in 4degrees Celsius refrigerator, using sterile water to wash the ball and storing at 4degrees Celsius. The time of co-culture is 1-4 days.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The RTX is                  administered in a formulation comprising RTX and                  a carrier comprising water and saline. The                  composition comprises 1-100 mu g/ml, preferably                  50-100 mu g/ml RTX.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Respiratory-Gen;                Hypotensive; Vasotropic; Antimicrobial;                Immunosuppressive; Antiasthmatic; CNS-Gen;                Antibacterial; Fungicide; Antiparasitic; Virucide;                Muscular-Gen; Metabolic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Interleukin-6 inhibitor; Interleukin 1 beta                inhibitor; Interferon gamma inhibitor; Transient                receptor potential vanilloid 1 (TRPV-1)                agonist.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the composition is 0.1-100                mu g, preferably 50-100 mu g by epidural,                peri-ganglionic or intra-ganglionic route                (claimed).

PN JP2021152191-A
TI Molten steel de-carbonization method in RH vacuum-degassing apparatus, involves calculating carbon monoxide gas production rate by regression created using molten steel information and waste gas information at time of decarbonization process.
AU IKESUE K
   OKAYAMA A
AE NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 2021B2794X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves circulating molten steel (5) between a ladle (2) and a vacuum chamber (1) by supply of recirculation gas. The waste gas containing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is discharged from the vacuum chamber, and the molten steel is decarbonized. The de-carbonization reaction rate in decarbonization process is defined as a differential equation of a first order reaction represented by linear expression of carbon concentration in molten steel, and product of decarbonization rate constant. Decarbonization volumetric-coefficient is calculated using carbon concentration, oxygen concentration, vacuum-chamber internal pressure, and carbon gas production speed. The carbon concentration is estimated using a mathematical model represented by the differential equation. The carbon monoxide gas production rate is calculated by the regression created using the molten steel information and waste gas information at the time of the decarbonization process.
   USE - Molten steel de-carbonization method in RH vacuum-degassing apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The de-carbonization advancing is estimated precisely and shortening of processing time is attained by heightening the decarbonization concentration to aim for, in the melting methods of ultra-low carbon steel, when using the molten steel de carbonization method in RH vacuum-degassing apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of RH vacuum-degassing apparatus for implementing molten steel de-carbonization.
   Vacuum chamber (1)
   Ladle (2)
   Evacuation installation (3)
   Exhaust hole (4)
   Molten steel (5)
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); T01 (Digital Computers)

TI Acidic gas absorbent useful for acidic gas removal apparatus to remove acidic gas, comprises amine compound, water and superfine bubble containing inert gas.
AU UDATSU M
   SANO K
   KUBOKI T
   IMADA T
   YOSHIMURA R
   NAKANO Y
   SUZUKI A
   UTATSU M
AE TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 2021B0144G
AB    NOVELTY - Acidic gas absorbent comprises amine compound, water and superfine bubble containing inert gas, where average diameter of the superfine bubble is less than 150 nm.
   USE - The absorbent is useful for acidic gas removal apparatus to remove acidic gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent reduces emission of acidic gases e.g. carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) acidic gas removal method in which gas containing acidic gas is brought into contact with the acidic gas absorbent to remove the acidic gas from the acidic gas containing gas; and
   (2) acidic gas removal apparatus (1) comprising absorption unit (2) in which a gas containing an acidic gas is brought into contact with acidic gas absorbent containing amine compound and water, so that the absorbent absorbs the acidic gas to remove it from the acidic gas containing gas, a superfine bubble-introducing unit in which superfine bubble containing inert gas, where average diameter of the superfine bubble is less than 150 nm are introduced into the acidic gas absorbent, and a regeneration unit (3) in which the absorbent holding the acidic gas is made to release the acidic gas to be regenerated, so that the absorbent regenerated in the regeneration unit is reused in the absorption unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the acidic gas removal apparatus.
   Acidic gas removal apparatus (1)
   Absorption unit (2)
   Regeneration unit (3)
   Gas inlet (4)
   Absorbent inlet (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The acidic gas absorbent further comprises at least one additive agent comprising oxidation inhibitors, pH adjusters, defoaming agents and anticorrosive agents.Preferred Components: In the acidic gas absorbent: the inert gas comprises nitrogen, helium, neon and/or argon; the content of superfine bubbles are 1x107 to 1x1012 bubbles per 1 ml; the amine compound comprises primary amines, secondary amines, tertiary amines and quaternary ammoniums; and the content of amine compound is 3-80 wt.% based on the total mass of the acidic gas absorbent.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN US2021275618-A1
TI Obtaining an extract involves reducing size of            plant biomass, plant biomass comprises psilocybin            mushroom and cannabis, ultrasonicating plant biomass,            contacting plant biomass with supercritical fluid            solvent system.
AU BERNARDO J
   GIGI V
   DAVIDSON R
AE CURE PHARM HOLDING CORP (CURE-Non-standard)
GA 2021A3612J
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining an extract involves reducing the                size of a plant biomass, the plant biomass                comprising psilocybin mushroom and cannabis,                ultrasonicating the plant biomass, contacting the                plant biomass with a supercritical fluid solvent                system above the critical pressure and above the                critical temperature and removing the supercritical                fluid solvent system from the plant biomass to                obtain an extract of psilocybin mushroom and                cannabis. The plant biomass comprises the                psilocybin mushroom and the cannabis in a weight                ratio of 1:99-25:75, respectively.
   USE - Method for obtaining an extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method used to provide an opportunity for                a faster-acting and better absorption                profile.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for obtaining an extract, which involves: reducing                the size of a plant biomass, which comprises                psilocybin mushroom and cannabis, ultrasonicating                the plant biomass, contacting for period of time of                60 seconds the plant biomass with a supercritical                fluid solvent system that comprises carbon dioxide                at a pressure of above 74 bar, and at a temperature                of above 31?oC and removing the supercritical fluid                solvent system from the plant biomass to obtain an                extract of psilocybin mushroom and cannabis, where                extract is enriched with cannabinoids relative to                the plant biomass, the extract is enriched with one                of psilocybin, psilocin, and baeocystin, and                extract is enriched with terpenes and relative to                the plant biomass, the extract is enriched with                flavonoids.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The psilocybin                  mushroom comprises one of Copelandia, Gymnopilus,                  Inocybe, Panaeolus, Pholiotina, Pluteus, and                  Psilocybe. The Cannabis comprises one of Cannabis                  sativa, Cannabis indica, and Cannabis ruderalis.                  The supercritical fluid solvent system comprises                  carbon dioxide (CO2). The supercritical fluid                  solvent system comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) and                  at least one of hydrogen (H2), neon (Ne),                  nitrogen (N2), argon (Ar), methane (CH4), ethane                  (CH3CH3), propane (CH3CH2CH3), ammonia (NH3),                  xenon (Xe), methanol, ethanol, 1-propanol,                  2-propanol, 1-hexanol, 2-methoxy ethanol,                  tetrahydrofuran (THF), 1,4-dioxane, acetonitrile,                  acetic acid, methylene chloride, dichloroethane,                  chloroform, ethyl acetate, propylene carbonate,                  N,N-dimethylaceamide, dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO),                  formic acid, carbon disulfide, acetone, toluene,                  hexanes, pentanes, trifluoromethane, nitrous                  oxide (N2O), sulfur hexafluroide (SF6), butane                  (n-C4H isobutane (i-C4H10), ethyl ether                  ((C2H5)20), benzotrifluoride (C6H5CF3),                  (p-chlorophenyl)trifluoromethane (ClC6H4CF3),                  chlorofluorocarbon (CFC), hydrofluorocarbon                  (HFA), and HFA-134a (1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane).                  The supercritical fluid solvent system comprises                  carbon dioxide (CO2) as the sole solvent.                  Preferred Conditons: The psilocybin mushroom has                  a moisture content of less than 15 wt.%. The                  cannabis has a moisture content of less than 15                  wt.%. The psilocybin mushroom is reduced to a                  size such 90 wt.% psilocybin mushroom present in                  the plant biomass is less than 10 mm. The                  cannabis is reduced to a size such that 90 wt.%                  cannabis present in the plant biomass is less                  than 50 mm. The reducing the size of the plant                  biomass includes one of blending, grinding,                  pulverizing, mincing, liquefying, cutting,                  macerating, and chopping. The ultrasonicating                  employs high-power ultrasound waves effective to                  achieve ultrasound-assisted cell lysis. The                  contacting of the plant biomass with the                  supercritical fluid solvent system is a                  supercritical fluid extraction (SFE), carried out                  once. The contacting of the plant biomass with                  the supercritical fluid solvent system is a                  fractional supercritical fluid extraction (FSFE),                  carried out multiple times. The contacting of the                  plant biomass with the supercritical fluid                  solvent system is carried out at a pressure of                  above 74 bar. The supercritical fluid solvent                  system comprises carbon dioxide (CO2). Preferred                  Process: The method for contacting the plant                  biomass with the supercritical fluid solvent                  system is carried out for period of time of at                  least 60 seconds. The method further includes                  purifying the extract, purifying the extract                  employing at least one of chromatography,                  adsorption, crystallization, distillation,                  liquid-liquid extraction, filtration, fractional                  distillation, precipitation, recrystallization,                  lyophilization (freeze drying), and sublimation.                  The extract is enriched with cannabinoids, or one                  of psilocybin, psilocin, and baeocystin.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Culture medium useful for activating T lymphocytes, comprises peripheral blood mononuclear cell culture solution and AT culture solution containing Lymactin-T anti-cluster of differentiation-3 monoclonal antibody, ALyS505N-0, interleukin (IL)-2, IL-6, isoflavones, vitamin B6 and autologous serum.
AU YANG H
AE LANLIAN HANGZHOU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LANL-Non-standard)
GA 2021A44467
AB    NOVELTY - Culture medium of T lymphocytes comprises peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) culture solution and AT culture solution. The PBMC culture solution comprises 10-20 ml ALyS505N-0. The AT culture solution comprises 10-15 ml 5 mu g/ml Lymactin-T anti-cluster of differentiation (CD)3 monoclonal antibody, 4-6 ml ALyS505N-0, 60-80 mu l 5x 105 international unit (IU)/ml interleukin (IL)-2, 10-20 mu l 5x 105 IU/ml IL-6, 300-450 mg isoflavones, 10-15 mg vitamin B6 and 3-5 ml autologous serum.
   USE - The culture medium is useful for activating T lymphocytes (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The culture medium promotes rapid proliferation of T lymphocytes during the induction period, improves efficiency of activating T lymphocytes and activates T lymphocytes with high production efficiency and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for activating T lymphocytes, which involves (a) processing plasma and collecting white blood cells, (b) culturing the white blood cells with PBMC culture solution for 2 hours, and then washing to obtain cell suspension and (c) suspending the cells, culturing in AT culture solution at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide for 7 days, harvesting AT cells and freezing for storage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The PBMC culture solution further comprises 30-60 mu l 200 mM amino acids chosen from L-glutamine, L-arginine, L-aspartic acid, L-phenylalanine and their combination, preferably L-glutamine. The PBMC culture solution further comprises 1 ml penicillin streptomycin mixture. The AT culture solution comprises 10 ml 5 mu g/ml Lymactin-T anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody, 5 ml ALyS505N-0, 70 mu l 5x 105 IU/ml IL-2, 15 mu l 5x 105 IU/ml IL-6, 380 mg isoflavones, 13 mg vitamin B6 and 4 ml autologous serum. Preferred Method: The step (c) involves placing 10-15 ml 5 mu g/ml Lymactin-T anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody in AT culture solution, incubating for 2 hours, then adding the remaining raw materials in the AT culture solution, mixing for 7 days, harvesting the AT cells at 37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide and freezing to store the cells. The 5 mu g/ml Lymactin-T is made by adding 50 mu l 1 mg/ml Lymactin-T to 10 ml Hank's buffer.

AB    NOVELTY - The sensor has a early detection of potential                induced degradation (PID) comprises a housing. A                power source is located within the housing and an                electrical connection presented by the housing for                electrically communicating the power source with a                photovoltaic (PV) module (14a), where current                supplied by the source to the module is less than                10 mA. The electrical connection includes leads or                contacts, plug-in connectors, or electrical lines                for connecting to the PV module. Electrical lines                in electrical communication with an electrical                detector are placed in communication with the                module to measure shunt resistance within the                module.
   USE - Photovoltaic sensor for photovoltaic assembly                for early detection of potential induced                degradation in photovoltaic system (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides early recovery from PID,                saves replacement costs, increases PV reliability,                and increases the lifetime of PV modules. The                method enhances green energy and reduces carbon                dioxide emissions.

TI Production of support for polyurethane-based            imitation leather free of dimethylformamide or other            solvents or water, by spraying polyurethane layer on            base by combining isocyanate and polyurethane, and            spraying uniformly on base.
AU FIDANZA V A
   VENTURA E
AE CONDOR TRADE SRL (COND-Non-standard)
   CONDOR TRADE SRL (COND-Non-standard)
GA 2021990066
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a support (5) involves preparing                a base (1,1'), spraying a polyurethane layer (11)                on the base, applying a backing (12) on the                polyurethane layer to form a support, and drying                the support. The polyurethane layer is formed by                combining two prepared components (A) and (B) of                polyurethane to obtain a mixture, and spraying the                mixture uniformly on the base.
   USE - Production of support for producing                polyurethane-based imitation leather                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The invention allows to replace the coagulate                in a simple and less polluting manner, given the                absence of any type of solvent. It allows                continuous production without the need for machine                stops since the deposition of the polyurethane does                not require any kind of mechanical leveler and                related maintenance. It also allows the direct                line-production of all the elements forming the                imitation leather, with consequent reduction of                production costs and saving of industrial surface,                since coagulation line is completely eliminated. It                provides huge savings in energy and raw material                costs compared to the current methods which include                high oven temperatures or the use of adhesives, as                well as a considerable saving in carbon dioxide                emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of imitation leather free of                dimethylformamide (DMFa), other solvents or                water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawings show schematic view of the                support production method.

   Backing (12)

PN CN113231070-A
TI Reverse catalyst of composite metal oxide solid            solution supported copper useful in catalyzing            reaction, comprises small particle of zirconium            dioxide-ceric dioxide composite metal oxide solid            solution dispersed around copper nano-particles.
AU LI F
   WANG H
   FAN G
   YANG L
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 202194749E
AB    NOVELTY - Reverse catalyst of composite metal oxide                solid solution supported copper, comprises small                particles of zirconium dioxide-ceric dioxide                composite metal oxide solid solution are uniformly                dispersed around copper active nano-particles with                large particle diameter. The average particle                diameter of the copper active nano-particle is 7-10                nm. The average particle diameter of zirconium                dioxide-ceric dioxide composite metal oxide solid                solution is 5-7 nm. The mass fraction of copper is                20-40 wt.%. The molar ratio of cerium and zirconium                is 0.33-3. The Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET)                specific surface area of the catalyst is 80-200                m2/g.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in catalyzing reaction                of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to methanol                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst exhibits good stability in the                reaction up to 72 hours, and has wide application                prospect in industrial catalysis, energy chemical                industry and other fields.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing reverse catalyst of composite metal oxide                solid solution supported copper, comprising using                micro-liquid membrane reactor auxiliary                co-precipitation method, using sodium borohydride                as precipitant and reducing agent, simultaneously                adding copper nitrate, cerium nitrate, zirconium                nitrate and surfactant solution into micro-liquid                membrane reactor explosion-type fast nucleation,                transferring obtained suspension into high pressure                reaction kettle for hydro-thermal crystallization,                washing, drying, roasting, and cooling to obtain                composite metal oxide solid solution supported                copper reverse catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (1)                  taking 0.5-2 g copper nitrate, 0.5-2 g zirconium                  nitrate, 0.5-2 g cerium nitrate and 0.3-1.8 g                  surfactant in 30-60 ml of de-ionized water, where                  the total concentration of the metal ion is                  0.2-0.3 mol/l, the molar ratio of cerium ion and                  zirconium ion is 0.33-3, and the mass of the                  surfactant is 1-5 times of the mass of copper;                  (2) preparing 30-60 ml sodium borohydride                  solution with concentration of 2-10 mol/l; (3)                  adding the solution prepared in the step (1) and                  (2) into the micro-liquid film reactor, stirring                  for 2-5 minutes at a rotating speed of 3000-4000                  revolutions/minute, transferring the obtained                  suspension to the polytetrafluoroethylene liner                  of the high pressure water heating kettle,                  hydrothermally reacting at 120-180 degrees C for                  12-36 hours, cooling to room temperature,                  filtering and washing to neutral by de-ionized                  water, drying and grinding precipitate; (4)                  roasting the product in a muffle furnace for 2-6                  hours under 300-500 degrees C to obtain catalyst                  precursor zirconium dioxide-ceric dioxide/copper                  oxide; and (5) performing reduction reaction on                  the catalyst precursor in a tubular furnace under                  a mixed atmosphere of hydrogen and nitrogen at                  200-500 degrees C for 1-4 hours to obtain reverse                  catalyst of composite metal oxide solid solution                  supported copper. The specific conditions of the                  catalytic carbon dioxide hydrogenation to                  methanol synthesis reaction comprises using a                  high pressure fixed bed micro-reactor, taking                  0.1-0.5 g composite metal oxide solid solution                  supported copper reverse catalyst and 0.1-0.5 g                  quartz sand, mixing uniformly and filling into                  stainless steel reaction tube, raising the                  temperature to 300-500 degrees C at a speed of                  2-5 degrees C/minute under the mixed atmosphere                  of hydrogen and nitrogen, performing the                  reduction reaction for 1-3 hours, cooling to room                  temperature, switching to carbon dioxide,                  hydrogen and argon mixed reaction gas, and                  raising the temperature to 220-260 degrees C for                  reaction. Preferred Components: The surfactant is                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, hexadecyl trimethyl                  ammonium bromide and/or sodium dodecyl benzene                  sulfonate.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

GA 202164346R

PT P
PN CN112763508-A
TI Method for judging generation reason of crack on            edge portion of medium-thick plate, involves observing            shape of crack fracture by acid washing after cutting            angle of continuous casting blank.
AU LI W
   MENG X
   YU F
   XU H
   QIAO Z
AE TIANJIN XINTIANGANG STEEL GROUP CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202156494W
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves observing the macroscopic                appearance of the original crack defect of medium                plate. The metallographic observation of crack                sample is performed. The shape of the crack                fracture is observed by acid washing after cutting                the angle of the continuous casting blank. The                metallographic sample is observed at the original                crack defect. The carbon monoxide or carbon dioxide                gas with oxygen is formed in air to run off while                resulting in decarburization around the crack. The                manganese is combined to form enriched silicon,                manganese oxide particles through diffusion into                the steel of oxygen and strong oxidizing element                silicon. The crack fracture is analyzed to find                that no carbon nitride is present in fracture. The                fracture with large amount of carbon nitride is                formed. The carbon nitride is separated out in the                grain boundary. The grain boundary is weakened                while expanding the third fragile region, to crack                in the straightening process.
   USE - Method for judging generation reason of crack                on edge portion of medium-thick plate.
   ADVANTAGE - By eliminating the continuous casting blank                corner transverse crack, the side portion of the                thick plate cracking crack is obviously improved.                The detection result is analyzed and summarized                while determining the generation reason of the                thick plate edge portion explosion crack, and                improving the product quality.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                medium-thick plate with crack.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC S03-E04F; S03-E04X

The grown cannabis plant is subjected to a flowering phase by exposing to a light having wavelength of 350-800 nm, to a gas mixture having a carbon dioxide to oxygen volume ratio of at least 0.0025, and to a flowering medium comprising at least one vitamin to obtain a flowering cannabis plant. The cannabis material is harvested from the flowering plant to obtain a harvested cannabis material. The ratio of red and blue light during the flowering phase is higher than during the growth phase. The amount of at least one vitamin in the flowering medium is higher than in the growth medium.
   USE - Indoor method for producing cannabis material (claimed) e.g. cannabis resin, cannabis oil, cannabis flower and cannabis tincture for medical applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces cannabis material with increased yield, improved taste, and high quality. The cannabis material has low level of heavy metals and pathogens. The material has excellent product uniformity and reproducibility of active ingredients.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for cannabis material, which has an upper limit for total aerobic microbial count (TAMC) of at most 950000 CFU/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The flowering medium comprises phosphate in a weight concentration of at least 1.05 times the weight concentration of phosphate in the growth medium. The weight concentration of potassium in the flowering medium exceeds the weight concentration of potassium in the growth medium by a factor of at least 1.05. The flowering medium comprises a higher weight amount of magnesium than calcium. The volumetric carbon dioxide concentration of the gas mixture during the flowering phase is higher than during the growth phase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The flowering medium comprises myo-inositol.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves starting the engine when                either the vehicles brake pedal is released or the                transmission has been idling for a predetermined                amount of time. The vehicle clutch pedal is                operated at the same time as the transmission is                shifted from neutral to a gear position. A timer is                started when the transmission is shifted to                neutral. The length of time that the transmission                is in neutral is determined by the timer. The                amount of time the transmission is in neutral is                compared to the predetermined time threshold and                start the engine when the time exceeds the                threshold and the vehicle clutch pedal is depressed                at the same time as the transmission is shifted                from neutral to a gear position.
   USE - Method for operating an engine of a                vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - Ensures to reduce the vehicles fuel                consumption and carbon dioxide emissions by                automatically switching off the engine when it is                determined that it is not necessary to run the                engine allow.
