AU YANG H
   CHEN Q
   HUANG K
   ZHENG B
   XU C
   DENG X
   XIE Z
AE RUYUAN HEC FLUORORESIN CO LTD (RUYU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4153U
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride block copolymer comprises an AB-type block copolymer comprising polyvinylidene fluoride segments and segments containing hydrophilic groups, where the amount of the polyvinylidene fluoride segment 50-90 wt.%, the hydrophilic group comprises a carboxyl group, a hydroxyl group and/or an amide group, the weight average molecular weight of the hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride block copolymer is greater than or equal to 700000.
   USE - The hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride block copolymer is used for water treatment membrane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride copolymer and the water treatment membrane have excellent mechanical strength and hydrophilic properties. The method is simple and controllable in process and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride block copolymer by (i) preparing polyvinylidene fluoride by polymerizing vinylidene fluoride monomer and bifunctional peroxide initiator which are polymerized under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions to obtain polyvinylidene fluoride with peroxide groups on the end groups, and (ii) preparing hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride block copolymer under inert atmosphere by adding hydrophilic monomers and organic solvents to the reaction system in step (i) for copolymerization, after the reaction is completed, and performing post-treatment to obtain the hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride block copolymer; and
   (2) a water treatment membrane obtained by spinning the hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride block copolymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bifunctional peroxide initiator used in step (i) comprises 3-(tert-butylperoxy)-3-ethylbutane, 3-(tert-butylperoxy)-3-ethylbutaneperoxymethyl ester and/or 1,4-bis-(tert-butylperoxy)benzene. Preferred Composition: The amount of the bifunctional peroxide initiator used in step (i) is 0.1-2 wt.% of the amount of vinylidene fluoride monomer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The weight average molecular weight of the hydrophilic modified polyvinylidene fluoride block copolymer is 850000-1200000. The amount of the polyvinylidene fluoride segment is 60-85 wt.%. Preferred Components: The hydrophilic group-containing segment is poly(meth)acrylic acid segment, polyvinyl alcohol segment or polyacrylamide segment. Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), the polymerization reaction is carried out at 35-40 degrees C and pressure of 7.5-9.5 MPa. In step (ii), the copolymerization reaction is carried out at 70-90 degrees C for 4-9 hours.

   WANG J
AE WUHAN CHUANTAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2020B7863Y
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a printing ink waste gas processing device, comprising a base, the top of the base is fixedly connected with a printing device, and the right side of the printing device is connected with a first connecting pipe, the utility model relates to printing ink waste gas processing technology field. The printing ink waste gas processing device is provided with an active carbon layer; the residual waste gas passing through the active carbon layer adsorption part passes through the combustion box; under the action of the combustion catalyst, the organic waste gas (VOCs) in the waste gas is burned and cracked into non-toxic carbon, carbon dioxide, steam and so on, improving the purifying rate of the waste gas, then carrying the heat of the gas into the spiral pipe through the air outlet pipe, the first inclined heat-conducting plate and the second inclined heat-conducting plate in the waste gas processing box to conduct the heat to the active carbon layer, improving the adsorption capacity of the active carbon by improving the temperature of the active carbon layer, further improves the purifying rate of the waste gas, and there is no need of additional heating device, which reaches the effect of energy saving.

PT P
PN CN111909594-A
TI Molecular sieve coated aluminum foil with anti-corrosion function comprises aluminum foil and aqueous epoxy resin coating, where aqueous epoxy resin coating is coated on both surfaces of aluminum foil.
AU ZHANG J
   QIAN B
AE JIANGSU WANYUAN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B6031H
AB    NOVELTY - Molecular sieve coated aluminum foil with anti-corrosion function comprises aluminum foil and aqueous epoxy resin coating, where the aqueous epoxy resin coating is coated on both surfaces of the aluminum foil, the aqueous epoxy resin coating includes aqueous epoxy resin, polyethyleneimine, nano copper powder and molecular sieve, the nano copper powder is distributed at the interface between the aqueous epoxy resin and the aluminum foil, and the nano copper powder is adsorbed by the carboxyl group in the molecular structure of the aqueous epoxy resin.
   USE - As molecular sieve coated aluminum foil with anti-corrosion function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process for preparing the molecular sieve coated aluminum foil with anti-corrosion function, comprising (a) taking 80-100 pts. wt. water, 40-60 pts. wt. aqueous epoxy resin, 80-100 pts. wt. cyclohexane and 4-10 pts. wt. polyethylene imine, stirring and mixing uniformly, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas during the stirring process to obtain a aqueous epoxy resin emulsion, (b) adding a copper salt solution of 10-20% of the emulsion mass to the aqueous epoxy resin emulsion, and adding a reducing agent, heating and stirring to react, and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain a concentrated solution, and (c) coating the concentrated liquid on the surface of the aluminum foil, and controlling the thickness of the coating film to 100-200 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The aqueous epoxy resin is an amphiphilic epoxy resin, and the molecular structure of the amphiphilic epoxy resin contains both carboxyl and epoxy functional groups. The epoxy resin is any one of epoxy resin E-42, epoxy resin E-41, and epoxy resin E-51. The nano silica is adsorbed on the surface of the molecular sieve, and the nano silica is nano silica treated with a silane coupling agent. Preferred Method: The specific preparation steps also include preparation of aqueous epoxy resin comprising taking 40-50 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 80-120 pts. wt. solvent, 2-4 pts. wt. catalyst, and 10-15 pts. wt. adipic acid, heating and stirring the reaction, distilling the solvent under reduced pressure to obtain an aqueous epoxy resin. The modification of molecular sieve comprises taking 10-15 pts. wt. nano-silica, 3-5 pts. wt. silane coupling agent and 100-120 pts. wt. ethanol solution, mixing, heating and stirring to react to obtain nano-silica dispersions, mixing the obtained nano-silica dispersion and the molecular sieve and reacting in a mass ratio of 5:1-10:1, filtering, washing and drying to obtain a modified molecular sieve. The preparation of aqueous epoxy resin emulsion comprises taking 80-100 pts. wt. water, 40-60 pts. wt. aqueous epoxy resin, 80-100 pts. wt. cyclohexane, 4-10 pts. wt. polyethyleneimine, 4-10 pts. wt. modified molecular sieve, stirring and mixing uniformly, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas during the stirring process to obtain aqueous epoxy resin emulsion. The reduction of copper ions comprises adding a copper salt solution of 10-20% of the emulsion mass to the aqueous epoxy resin emulsion, and adding the reducing agent, heating and stirring the reaction, and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain a concentrated solution. The coating comprises coating the concentrated liquid on the surface of the aluminum foil, and controlling the thickness of the coating film to 100-200 mu m.

TI Producing malt from cereals via steeping followed by germination and kilning, steeping process involves e.g. only one immersion step in which cereals immersed in water, during which immersion step steeping water continuously purified.
AU DEKKERS F
   BOLAT I
   DUMONT K
AE WATER IQ INT BV (WATE-Non-standard)
   WATER IQ INT BV (WATE-Non-standard)
   WATER IQ INT BV (WATE-Non-standard)
   WATER IQ INT BV (WATE-Non-standard)
GA 2020A7291W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing malt from cereals via steeping followed by germination and kilning, the steeping process comprises only one immersion step in which the cereals are immersed in water, during which immersion step the steeping water is continuously purified, where the purification comprises an adsorption process followed by an oxidation process, which adsorption process purifies the steeping water using a carrier material having a ceramic open structure to enable binding of non-charged polymeric organic molecules including a fraction of polyphenolic compounds and molecular compounds leaking from the grain in the water, and which oxidation process comprises injection of hydrogen peroxide and high concentrated oxygen into the water after the adsorption process and using a ceramic filter bed as carrier material with an immobilized catalyst on it for removing microorganisms, which catalyst comprises a complex of metal ions.
   USE - The method is useful for producing malt from cereals via steeping followed by germination and kilning, the steeping process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the quality of the final malt; reduces water consumption; reduces carbon footprint due to the limited carbon dioxide produced during steeping as a result of the long flooded period; and reduces effluent to be treated.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of a system.
   Steeping vessel (101)
   Adsorption columns (103)
   Oxidation columns (105)
   Oxygen (gas) is injected (107)
   Injection tube (109)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complex of metal ions comprises at least one of copper, iron, cobalt, or palladium ions. Preferred Method: The oxidation process the water enters at least one oxidation column (105) containing the ceramic filter bed with the immobilized catalyst via a perforated injection tube (109) extending vertically upwards in the oxidation column.

PN CN111872633-A
TI Strengthening valve and shaft surface regeneration comprises turning damaged position on workpiece to-be processed to remove damage and corrosion layer, selecting metal welding material, performing non-destructive inspection, and polishing.
AU CAI X
   YUAN J
   TANG L
AE SUZHOU XINWEI VALVE CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2100Y
AB    NOVELTY - Strengthening valve and shaft surface regeneration comprises (i) turning damaged position on workpiece to-be processed to remove damage and corrosion layer, (ii) using ultrasonic detector to detect flaws on workpiece to-be processed, (iii) polishing and smoothing surface of turning position of substitute workpiece to form the surface to-be processed, (iv) selecting corresponding metal welding material to cover layer of alloy material on flexible alloy transition layer, (v) performing non-destructive inspection on surfacing position of workpiece to prevent problems e.g. weld seams and bubbles in surfacing position, (vi) integrally heating workpiece to 630~650 degrees C, then keeping it for 1.5~2.5h, then cooling it to room temperature in air, and (vii) polishing workpiece to reach the factory standard thickness, where the material of flexible alloy transition layer is a high nickel alloy material, and surfacing method is manual arc welding, carbon dioxide shielded welding or argon arc welding.
   USE - The technique is useful for strengthening valve and shaft surface regeneration.
   ADVANTAGE - The technique: repairs the damaged surfaces of waste metal valves and shafts; is reusable; saves the resources; and reduces production and operation costs.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the regeneration position of the valve and shaft surface regeneration strengthening technique (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   ADVANTAGE - The epoxy resin coating decomposes the formaldehyde released by the material into the air, decomposes it into carbon dioxide and water, and discharge it with the airflow, thus achieving the effect of purifying the air and protecting the environment. AB    NOVELTY - The transformer fault diagnosis method                involves extracting the concentration of target                dissolved gas in transformer oil, and the relative                concentration of the target dissolved gas is                calculated, and the relative concentration                constructs the matrix to be measured as the                parameters, and the characteristic parameters of                the matrix to be measured is extracted for using                principal component analysis, and the                characteristic parameters are used to construct a                characteristic sample matrix. The characteristic                sample matrix is inputted into the trained binary                tree fuzzy twin support vector machine, which                analyze in the fault diagnosis model to get the                fault condition of the transformer. The target                dissolved gas includes hydrogen, methane,                acetylene, ethene, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide.

It mainly takes oily soil through a drying unit to dry the soil and then transport it to the thermal desorption unit, where the thermal desorption unit heats up at high temperatures to volatilize the oil contained in the soil. After the gaseous state is separated from the soil, the effect of purifying the soil is achieved. The dust, moisture and oil gas caused by the drying and thermal desorption of the oily soil in the drying unit and thermal desorption unit will be removed by the dust removal unit. After the water vapor and oil and gas are cooled into a liquid state by the heat exchange unit, they are transported to the oil-water separation unit for oil-water separation. The organic gas contained in the oil and gas is heated and cracked into water and carbon dioxide by the exhaust gas treatment unit and then discharged into the environment. The waste heat generated by the operation of the desorption unit can be used by the drying unit and the sewage pretreatment unit to save the energy consumed in the operation of the overall system and achieve the benefits such as effective cost reduction.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); H03 (Transportation and storage - only large scale systems are included. Road tankers and retail petrol station-type applications are excluded. Treatment of pollution from marine oil tankers is included.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC D04-A01; H03-G; H03-X; P43-J
IP B09C-001/06

PN CN210372354-U
TI Swinging corrugated hose, has vacuum double-layer corrugated pipe whose left and right ends are fixedly connected with port flange, and adapter group installed with limiting bracket, where outer circumferential surface of pipe is mounted with seat.
AU WU B
   CHENG L
AE ANHUI WEIMAI OME SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202035793A
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a swing corrugated flexible tube, comprising: a vacuum double layer corrugated pipe port flange, a gas charging valve, a switching seat, the switching seat II, a limiting bracket, the vacuum double layer corrugated pipe of the left and right two ends are fixedly connected with a port flange; the inflating valve is fixedly installed outside the vacuum double-layer middle circumferential surface of the corrugated pipe, the vacuum double-layer corrugated outer circumferential surface is uniformly mounted with a connecting seat I, position the vacuum double-layer bellows outer peripheral left and right ends corresponding to the switching seat of fixedly connecting seat two; and on the same length direction of the two adapter and a switching seat forms a switching seat groups, each adapter group are installed with a limiting bracket. The invention is the vacuum double-layer corrugated outer peripheral face is provided with a limiting bracket, which can prevent the whole device has relatively large torsion to deformation; at the same time, filling carbon dioxide into the vacuum double layer corrugated pipe through pneumatic valve, can effectively prevent insulation, the device outer surface frosting occurs, both of them are combined, it can improve the use lifetime of swinging a corrugated hose.

PT P
PN CN111019831-A; CN211689099-U
TI Cell tissue workstation hydrogen peroxide discharge mechanism comprises through hole formed in the body of the cell tissue workstation, and hydrogen peroxide concentration detection sensor for measuring the hydrogen peroxide concentration.
AU LIU F
   BUSUJIMA H
   XU R
   ZHAN L
   ZHANG C
   QIU C
AE BINGSHAN SONGYANG BIOTECHNOLOGY DALIAN (BING-Non-standard)
GA 2020339268
AB    NOVELTY - Cell tissue workstation hydrogen peroxide discharge mechanism comprises a through hole formed in the body of the cell tissue workstation, a hydrogen peroxide shunt discharge device connected to the through hole, a hydrogen peroxide split discharge device used to split certain amount of used hydrogen peroxide and discharge it to the atmosphere, where the cell tissue workstation includes a cell tissue workstation body, a carbon dioxide incubator, and a transfer window. The body of the cell tissue workstation includes catalyst for absorbing used hydrogen peroxide provided in the body. A hydrogen peroxide concentration detection sensor for measuring the hydrogen peroxide concentration, the hydrogen peroxide concentration detection sensor is arranged near the exhaust fan side. The range of the hydrogen peroxide concentration detection sensor is 0-200 ppm, and the resolution is at least 1 ppm.
   USE - Used as cell tissue workstation hydrogen peroxide discharge mechanism.
   ADVANTAGE - The discharge mechanism: realizes part of the hydrogen peroxide atomized gas is directly discharged into the atmosphere without decomposition of the catalyst; contains hydrogen peroxide concentration detection sensor, which monitor in real time to ensure that the hydrogen peroxide gas concentration in the effluent is kept below 1 ppm; decomposes remaining hydrogen peroxide atomized gas by catalyst, so as to shorten the decomposition time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of cell tissue workstation hydrogen peroxide discharge mechanism.

PT P
PN WO2020070622-A1; EP3860749-A1; US2021394126-A1
TI Fabricating zeolite-like metal-organic framework with ana-topology (ana-ZMOF) thin film membrane for separating chemical species by modifying substrate with ana-ZMOF crystal precursors and intergrowing in presence of polyvinylpyrrolidone.
AU EDDAOUDI M
   CHERNIKOVA V
   SHEKHAH O
   BELMABKHOUT Y
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 202027638F
AB    NOVELTY - A zeolite-like metal-organic framework with ana-topology (ana-ZMOF) thin film membrane is fabricated by (1) modifying substrate with ana-ZMOF crystal precursors in the presence of polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP); and (2) intergrowing the ana-ZMOF crystal precursors in the presence of PVP to form a continuous defect-free thin film of an ana-ZMOF intergrown on the substrate.
   USE - Fabrication of zeolite-like metal-organic framework with ana-topology (ana-ZMOF) thin film membrane for separating chemical species (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fabrication of thin film membranes not only overcomes the challenges associated with scale-up and pure crystal intergrowth, but has also produced continuous, defect-free thin films of ana-ZMOFs, with intrinsic properties suitable for achieving highly challenging separations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) membrane comprising thin film of continuous ana-ZMOF intergrown on a substrate, where ana-ZMOF thin film membrane has a pore window size of approximately 4.1 Angstrom and exhibits a selectivity for carbon dioxide (CO2) over hydrogen (H2) and n-butane over isobutane;
   (2) separation of chemical species which involves contacting an ana-ZMOF thin film membrane with a fluid composition containing CO2 and H2 and separating CO2 from H2; and
   (3) separation of chemical species which involves contacting an ana-ZMOF thin film membrane with a fluid composition containing n-butane and isobutane and separating n-butane from isobutane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ana-ZMOF precursors include a rare earth metal precursor, a ligand precursor, and an alkali metal precursor or an alkaline metal precursor. Rare-earth metal precursors include rare earth element selected from cerium, dysprosium, erbium, europium, gadolinium, holmium, lanthanum, lutetium, neodymium, praseodymium, promethium, samarium, scandium, terbium, thulium, ytterbium, and/or yttrium. Alkali metal precursor includes alkali metal selected from lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium, and/or francium. Alkaline metal precursor includes alkaline metal selected from beryllium, magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium, and/or radium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The 1st precursor solution is sonicated prior to immersing the substrate in the 1st precursor solution. The 2nd precursor solution is sonicated and heated prior to immersing the dried modified substrate in the 2nd precursor solution. H2 is substantially excluded from the ana-ZMOF thin film membrane. Isobutane is substantially excluded from the ana-ZMOF thin film membrane. Preferred Components: Ligand precursor is selected from 4,5-imidazole dicarboxylic acid (ImDC); 1H-imidazole-2-carboxylic acid; 2,7-diaza-antracene-1,8-dicarboxylic acid; pyrimidine-4,6-dicarboxcylic acid; pyridine-2,5-dicarboxylic acid; 2,7-diaza-anthracene-3,6-dicarboxylic acid; benzene-1,2,4,5-tetracarboxylic acid; naphthalene-2,3,6,7-tetracarboxylic acid; and/or anthracene-2,3,6,7-tetracarboxylic acid. Solvents are selected from water; N,N-dimethyl formamide (DMF); ethanol; 4,4'-trimethylene-dipiperidine; and/or 1,2-diaminocyclohexane. The thin film membrane comprises a continuous thin film of ana-ZMOF inter-grown on the modified substrate.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN US2020107428-A1; US10624196-B1
TI Extreme UV (EUV) lithography device used during semiconductor fabrication process, has sensor that senses optical emission generated in amplifier while gain medium is discharging and spectrometer which measures spectrum of optical emission.
AU TONG H Y
   WANG W
   CHEN H
   CHANG L C
   CHEN C
   CHEN L
   CHENG P
   TSAY J
AE TAIWAN SEMICONDUCTOR MFG CO LTD (TSMC-C)
GA 202025635B
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a laser source (110) which is configured to produce a seed laser beam. An amplifier comprises a gain medium, and a discharging unit to pump the gain medium to amplify the power of the seed laser beam. An optical sensor (130) is coupled to the amplifier and is configured to sense an optical emission generated in the amplifier while the gain medium is discharging. A spectrometer (140) is coupled with the optical sensor to measure a spectrum of the optical emission. A processor (150) is coupled with the spectrometer, to determine an operational status of the amplifier according to the spectrum of the optical emission. The gain medium is a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
   USE - Extreme UV (EUV) lithography device used during semiconductor fabrication process, of generating and utilizing EUV light (EUVL) to form pattern onto wafer.
   ADVANTAGE - The optical sensor is adjusted to be implement at different openings to sense the optical emission from different locations of the amplifier component, and the optical emission signal can reflect more information about different conditions of the amplifier component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating EUV lithography device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the EUV lithography device.
   EUV lithography device (100)
   Laser source (110)
   Optical sensor (130)
   Spectrometer (140)
   Processor (150)

PN US2020064783-A1; US11079128-B2
TI System for characterize gas in a building, such as hydrogen, comprises a processor, a gas sensor is coupled to the processor and gas valve is coupled to the processor and encryption module is executed by the processor to secure gas data.
AU TRAN B
   NYHOF D
AE TRAN B (TRAN-Individual)
   NYHOF D (NYHO-Individual)
   BLOCKCHAIN GENERATION VENTURES (BLOC-Non-standard)
GA 2020174415
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a processor (22), a gas sensor is coupled to the processor. A gas valve is coupled to the processor. An encryption module is executed by the processor to secure gas data. A transceiver communicate secured gas data over a network to a remote processor. A blockchain (30) is coupled to the processor through the transceiver (24). The sensor data is stored in the blockchain to avoid tampering. The data transactions sent by the processor are signed with elliptic curve signature. An application program interface (API) allow a mobile device (32) to query the processor for gas data. The processor receives commands from a phone app to the devices and communicate readings from the devices to the phone app using the API.
   USE - System for characterize gas in a building, such as hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, fluorine, argon, oxygen, methane, helium, propane, natural gas control and usage analysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The communications between network devices and the blockchain enhanced security features that protect the connections and the privacy of the users.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method to manage gas in a building.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a gas monitoring system.
   Base station (20)
   Processor (22)
   Transceiver (24)
   Blockchain (30)
   Mobile device (32)

The refrigerator system significantly increases capacity of the chiller system, and allows a low amount of refrigerant while maintaining a high capacity, and the refrigerant circuit is self-circulating due to natural flow, which rovides a reliable operation. The porous support structure comprises a system of continuous pores connecting an inlet (202) of the separation membrane to an outlet (204) of the separation membrane. The alkali metal hydroxide is mounted within pores of the porous support structure. A porous conductive coating is coupled to opposite ends of the separation membrane. The porous conductive coating comprises activated carbon and/or a graphitic material. A first profile in which larger of the pores are located near a first end of the porous support structure and smaller of the pores are located near a second end of the porous support structure.
   USE - Separation membrane for post-combustion carbon capture and separation.

The dissolved gases is quickly and efficiently migrated from high to low concentration sides of the membrane, thus improving the efficiency of the separation process. The carbon dioxide is energetic to drive the separation process without addition of energy from outside sources, thus eliminating the need to cannibalize energy from the power plant or other emission source.

GA 202004127Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing in-situ growth metal sulfide Uio-66/In2S3 composite catalyst based on metal-organic framework comprises using Uio-66 as a carrier, and directly growing indium(III) sulfide on the surface of Uio-66 by a solvent thermal method.
   USE - The composite catalyst is useful for water photolysis reaction (claimed), photo-degradation, photolysis of water, photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, and photocatalytic water decomposition fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple and feasible preparation, and In2S3 can be uniformly grown on the surface of Uio-66, and there is a closer contact with Uio-66.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of the in-situ growth metal sulfide Uio-66/In2S3 composite catalyst based on metal-organic framework comprising ultrasonically dispersing Uio-66 in the mixed solution, then adding indium(III) chloride (InCl3) and thioacetamide (TAA), and stirring and transferring to the reaction kettle for hydrothermal reaction, after completion of the hydrothermal reaction, naturally cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, washing the obtained precipitate by centrifugation and drying to obtain a Uio-66/In2S3 composite catalyst based on metal-organic framework for in-situ growth of metal sulfide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of Uio-66 comprises dissolving 84 mg zirconium(IV) chloride and 59 mg terephthalic acid are in 40 ml dimethylformamide solution, adding 3.8-4.8 ml glacial acetic acid in dropwise manner, and stirring the mixture for 60 minutes, preferably selecting 3.8 ml, and transferring the above solution to Erlenmeyer flask, and placing in an oven at 120 degrees C for 6-12 hours. The temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is 150 degrees C, and the time of the hydrothermal reaction is 12-48 hours. Preferred Components: The mixed solution is a mixed solution composed of dimethylformamide and ethylene glycol, where the volume ratio of dimethylformamide and ethylene glycol is 3:1-1:1. The ratio of the amount of the substance of Uio-66 and InCl3 in the mixed solution is 1:2-1:8, and the ratio of the amount of the substance of InCl3 and TAA is 1:2.

   ADVANTAGE - The inner surface of the sample cell is plated with gold or mirror-surface-processed in order to reduce the reflective loss of light. The flow volume of sample gas is easily controllable by adjustment of the height of the baffle. The sample gas flows in into the interior space through the inflow space and the gas inlet rapidly by the increase in gas flow rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the gas densitometer. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

GA 201984130Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of steel slag carbide                artificial porous aggregate comprises preparing                steel slag powder by crushing steel slag block, and                grinding to powder of specific surface area of                100-280 m2/g; preparing steel slag ball by weighing                30-100 pts. wt. steel slag powder, 5-20 pts. wt.                silicate cement, and 0-60 pts. wt. mixture of brick                powder and fly ash, adding water to ball with                water-cement ratio of 0.19 to obtain spherical                steel slag aggregate; pre-curing the spherical                steel slag aggregate, keeping environment relative                humidity to 40-98% and temperature to 20-60 degrees                C, and curing for 0-7 days, and putting into                carbonization kettle, carbonizing at 5-100 vt. %                carbon dioxide and pressure of 0.052-1 MPa, and                controlling the carbonizing time to 0.5-72 hours to                obtain carbonized steel slag artificial porous bone                material.
   USE - Method is for preparing carbide steel slag                artificial porous aggregate.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared aggregate has high aggregate                strength, safety and stability, simple preparation                process and equipment, easy operation, low                production cost, high efficiency, low carbon and                environment-friendly.    UNIV KYOTO (KYOU-C)
GA 201963276J
AB    NOVELTY - Polyimide (I) and its salt and being a condensate of an aromatic polyamine including a spiro ring or a fused ring and an aromatic carboxylate anhydride including many phthalate anhydride groups, are new.
   USE - The polyimide (I) is useful for producing a separation film i.e. a gas separation film, where at least one of hydrogen and carbon dioxide selectively permeates the gas separation film (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyimide (I) can be produced at low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyimide of formula (I) and its salt and being a condensate of an aromatic polyamine including a spiro ring or a fused ring and an aromatic carboxylate anhydride including many phthalate anhydride groups, are new.
   Ar11, Ar12 and Ar20 = atomic group including at least one of aromatic ring, heteroaromatic ring, non-aromatic ring and non-ring structure, and optionally further including a substituent;
   Sp = spiro ring or fused ring (optionally further including substituent); and
   n = positive integer denoting degree of polymerization.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing the polyimide or its salt (I);
   (2) producing a separation film, comprising preparing the polyimide or its salt and forming a film of the polyimide or its salt; and
   (3) separating gas, comprising pressurizing a gas separation film with containing many kinds of gases to cause at least one of the kinds of gases to selectively permeate the gas separation film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): Preparation of (I) comprises subjecting an aromatic polyamine including a spiro ring or a fused ring and a carboxylate anhydride including many anhydride groups to a condensation reaction. In the pressurizing step, pressurizing the gas separation film with a single gas selectively permeate the gas separation film. Preferred Components: (I) is preferably of formula (Ia), (Ib) or (Ic). The aromatic polyamine includes two amino groups in one molecule. The carboxylate anhydride includes two anhydride groups in one molecule. The carboxylate anhydride is an aromatic carboxylate anhydride including many phthalate anhydride groups in one molecule. The aromatic carboxylate anhydride includes two phthalate anhydride groups in one molecule. The aromatic polyamine is of formula (A1) and the carboxylate anhydride is of formula (B1).
   EXAMPLE - 1.03 g 3,3,3',3'-Tetramethyl-2,2',3,3'-tetrahydro-1,1'-spirobi(cyclopenta(b)dibenzo(b,e)(1,4)dioxine)-diamine and 0.88 g 5,5'-(perfluoropropane-2,2-diyl)diisobenzofuran-1,3-dione were added to 10 ml m-cresol. The solution was stirred at room temperature for 3 hours before 0.2 ml isoquinoline was added. The solution was then heated gradually to 180 degrees C and kept for 5 hours. The resulting polymer was then precipitated in 200 ml methanol, and the remaining m-cresol in the polymer was removed by Soxhlet extraction. The polymer was dried and then dissolved in chloroform and reprecipitated in methanol two times. Then the polymer was dried in a vacuum oven at 150 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain light yellow filament polyimide.

TI Beverage useful for strengthening spleen and digestion, enhancing human immunity and preventing and treating colds comprises water, citric acid, malic acid, sugar, edible spice, caffeine and roxburgh rose juice.
AU ZHU B
   LI K
   LI G
   LIAO G
   ZOU L
AE GUIZHOU HONGCAI JUNONG INVESTMENT CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2019501551
AB    NOVELTY - Beverage comprises 93.5-94.3 wt.% water, 0.21-0.25 wt.% citric acid, 0.16-0.19 wt.% malic acid, 2.2-2.5 wt.% sugar, 0.22-0.24 wt.% edible spice, 0.11-0.15 wt.% caffeine and 2.8-3.2 wt.% roxburgh rose juice.
   USE - The beverage is useful for strengthening spleen and digestion, enhancing human immunity and preventing and treating colds.
   ADVANTAGE - The beverage provides different taste than existing products, while increasing the nutritional content of carbonated beverages and has good gastrointestinal health effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the beverage comprising (i) filtering total volume of raw material water to obtain purified water, pressurizing the purified water, cooling to room temperature to obtain deoxidized pure water without bacteria after sterilization and sorting the sterile pure water without bacteria; (ii) mixing one part of sterile pure water mixed with sugar, heating, stirring, filtering to remove impurity to obtain syrup and storing the sterile syrup; (iii) orderly adding edible spice, caffeine, citric acid and malic acid in roxburgh rose juice, stirring, filtering them to remove impurities, sterilizing to obtain flavoring agent and sterile refrigerating the flavoring agent; and (iv) uniformly mixing remaining sterile pure water, syrup and flavoring agent to obtain mixture, degassing the mixture, cooling to obtain degassed mixture and blending carbon dioxide into the degassed mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The beverage comprises 93.5 wt.% water, 0.25 wt.% citric acid, 0.19 wt.% malic acid, 2.5 wt.% sugar, 0.24 wt.% edible spice, 0.12 wt.% caffeine and 3.2 wt.% roxburgh rose juice. Preferred Components: In step (ii) the volume ratio of the white sugar to the partially sterile purified water is 2:1. Preferred Method: In step (i) the filtration is carried out by using permeable membrane, the permeable membrane TDS less than or equal to 1000 ppm, and the pure water conductivity less than or equal to 10 ms/cm. The pure water pressurization process is to maintain the purified water at a pressure of -0.09 to -0.095 MPa for 20 minutes and the sterilizing process is heated to 100 degrees C for 30 minutes. In step (ii) the heating temperature is 100 degrees C, the heating time is 30 minutes, stirring rate is 65 rotations/minute, the stirring time is 30 minutes/minute and the filtration is carried out using 100-mesh diatomaceous earth. In step (iii) the stirring time is 20 minutes, the stirring speed is 110 rotations/minute and the filtering is carried out using 120-150 mesh filter membrane. The sterilization is instantaneous sterilization by heating to 100 degrees C and the refrigerating temperature is 4-6 degrees C. In step (iv) the degassing process is pressurized to -0.09 to -0.095 MPa and maintained for 60 minutes and the temperature lowering process is reduced to 2-5 degrees C. The process of incorporating carbon dioxide comprises pressurizing degassing mixture and the carbon dioxide through carbon dioxide mixing machine to 0.3-0.54 Kpa for 30 minutes and the carbon dioxide volume being 2-3 times the volume of the mixture.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen; Immunostimulant; Virucide.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN US2019099927-A1
TI Method for facilitating microcellular foam molding of article, involves conveying effused plastic granules in mixer to mold of injection molding machine to perform foam molding on effused plastic granules to produce foamed article.
AU LUO Z
   ZHANG W
   YIK H
AE LUO Z (LUOZ-Individual)
   ZHANG W (ZHAN-Individual)
   YIK H (YIKH-Individual)
GA 201930203L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves feeding plastic granules to a hopper (1). Ultra-critical fluid (SCF) is supplied (2) to the hopper to effuse through the plastic granules. Effused plastic granules are conveyed (3) to a mixer. The effused plastic granules are conveyed (4) in the mixer to a mold of an injection molding machine to perform foam molding on the effused plastic granules to produce a foamed article, where the SCF is selected from a group consisting of carbon dioxide, water, methane, ethane, methanol, ethanol, ethylene, propylene, acetone and nitrogen and the plastic granules are formed of polyolefin compound.
   USE - Method for facilitating microcellular foam molding of an article.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables producing the formed article in process with better reduction of manufacturing cost. The method enables utilizing foaming materials with low specific gravity, no pollution to the environment, excellent resilience, and smooth surface. The method enables utilizing a filler to improve performance or reduce production cost.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for facilitating microcellular foam molding of an article.
   Step for feeding plastic granules to hopper (1)
   Step for supplying supercritical fluid to hopper to effuse through plastic granules (2)
   Step for conveying effused plastic granules to mixer (3)
   Step for conveying effused plastic granules in mixer to mold of injection molding machine to perform foam molding on effused plastic granules to produce foamed article (4)

AB    NOVELTY - The computer program product comprises a computer readable storage medium, which have program instructions. The program instructions are executable by a processing unit (71). A ticket is provided to an individual associated with a vehicle parked in the parking area. The parking area includes multiple zones and each zone includes multiple parking spaces. The ticket is associated with the vehicle, which is associated with the individual. A first zone is identified in which the vehicle is parked. The individual is responsible to validate the ticket. The vehicle associated with the ticket will leave the identified first zone. An indication is provided that a parking space of the identified first zone is to become available.
   USE - Computer program product for managing parking spaces in a parking area.
   ADVANTAGE - Accurately identify where the vehicle is parked. Provides a probability of a departure time of a vehicle from the parking area. Enable better use of time for parking area users and reduce fuel consumption and carbon dioxide emissions associated with vehicles searching for a parking space. PN EP3447025-A1; DE102017008088-A1; EP3447025-B1; ES2842948-T3
TI Method for recovering internal energy from hot exhaust gases involves burning synthesis gas with oxidant in furnace, thermally contacting obtained exhaust gas with heat storage of reactor, and supplying oxygen-containing gas to reactor.
AU DEMUTH D M
   GABER C E
   HOCHENAUER P C
   SPOLJARIC D
   DEMUTH M
   HOCHENAUER C
   HOCHENAUER P D C
AE MESSER GROUP GMBH (MESG-C)
   MESSER AUSTRIA GMBH (MESG-C)
   MESSER AUSTRIA GMBH (MESG-C)
   MESSER GROUP GMBH (MESG-C)
GA 201918884H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering internal energy from hot exhaust gases, involves burning a synthesis gas comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen with an oxygen-containing oxidant in a furnace (2) to form a hot exhaust gas comprising carbon dioxide and water, thermally contacting the obtained exhaust gas with a heat storage of a reactor (3) which warms up and cools the exhaust gas, recirculating a partial flow of exhaust gas and feeding together with a hydrocarbon-containing fuel to a reactor (4) and reacting the fuel and the recirculated exhaust gas in the reactor (4) in an endothermic reaction to obtain a carbon monoxide and hydrogen-containing synthesis gas, cooling down the heat storage of the reactor (4), feeding the synthesis gas from the reactor (4) to the furnace, and supplying an oxygen-containing gas as an educt in addition to the fuel and the recirculated partial flow of exhaust gas to the reactor (4).
   USE - Method for recovering internal energy from hot exhaust gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of reliably preventing formation of carbon deposits in the reformer, and removing already formed carbon deposits with little effort.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device for recovering internal energy from hot exhaust gases.
   Device (1)
   Furnace (2)
   Reactors (3,4)
   Reformer (4)
   Exhaust pipe (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The educts fed to reactor (4) comprises 1-10 vol.%, preferably 2-7 vol.% oxygen.

PN CN109365522-A
TI Method for anaerobic bioremediation of contaminated soil using bioreactor, involves injecting nutrients through intermittent small dose of sieve, so that concentration of nutrients in bioreactor water is maintained.
AU CHEN B
   MU C
AE BEIJING BESEN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201920613G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a bioreactor comprising a cell portion, and a sieve tube with a microporous tube portion is arranged on the bottom of the cell portion. The contaminated soil is put into a tank portion (3), and inject the clean water through the screen to make a contaminated soil (1) in a liquid state. The nutrients are injected through the screen. An anaerobic biological agent is injected that is domesticated for the target pollutant through a sieve. The nutrients are injected through the intermittent small dose of the sieve, so that the concentration of nutrients in the bioreactor water is maintained, so that the operation continues until the contaminated soil reaches the standard. The sulfate or nitric acid is injected under pressure. The gas is compressed nitrogen, argon or carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for anaerobic bioremediation of contaminated soil using bioreactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The bioremediation method has a short repair cycle and uniform repair effect, and can efficiently degrade carbon monoxide, chloroform, tetrachloroethylene, trichloroethylene, hexachlorobenzene and polychlorinated biphenyls, which are difficult to be aerobic biodegradable pollutants. The technology does not require pretreatment of the contaminated soil by sieving, crushing and stirring, and the amount of pretreatment work is greatly reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front cross-sectional view of a bioreactor in the method.
   Contaminated soil (1)
   Blind tube (2)
   Tank portion (3)
   Screen tube (4)
   Filter material (5)

PN CN109336809-A
TI Metal organic frame material useful in gas separation, prepared by heat-treating L and copper salts in an organic solvent, subjecting reaction body to cooling, crystallizing and then solid-liquid separation.
AU YUN R
AE UNIV ANHUI NORMAL (UYAI-C)
GA 201917747P
AB    NOVELTY - Metal organic frame material is claimed. The metal organic frame material containing the complex i.e. copper complex (I) contains a plurality of binuclear copper secondary building units, in each dual-core copper secondary building unit the copper ions are coordinating to the carboxylic acid in the two L and the nitrogen in the two L to form a turbo mode.
   USE - The metal organic frame material is useful in gas separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has excellent thermal stability and excellent adsorption to carbon dioxide; and method: has simple preparation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal organic frame material is claimed. The metal organic frame material containing the complex i.e. copper complex of formula (Cu2(L)2)infinity (I) contains a plurality of binuclear copper secondary building units (Cu2(C(=O)O)4N2), in each dual-core copper secondary building unit the copper ions are coordinating to the carboxylic acid in the two L and the nitrogen in the two L to form a turbo mode.
   L = N,N'-bis(3-carboxyphenyl)-3,5-pyridinedicarboxamide; and
   infinity = continuation of the three-dimensional structure.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing metal organic frame material comprising (i) heat-treating L and copper salts in an organic solvent, and (ii) subjecting reaction body to cooling, crystallizing and solid-liquid separation to obtain metal organic framework material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complex belongs to the tetragonal system, I41/and space group. The unit cell size of the complex is: crystal axis angles alpha , beta , gamma are both 90 degrees , unit cell volume. The metal organic framework material further contains an organic solvent, where specific gravity of the organic solvent in the metal organic framework material is 5-8 wt.%, the specific surface area is 1800-1900 cm3/g, and porosity is 64-65%. The organic solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, methanol or ethanol. The molar ratio between copper salt and L is 4 mmol:0.8-2 mmol; ratio between copper salt and organic solvent is 1 mmol:1-20 ml; and copper salt is copper chloride, copper nitrate or copper acetate. Preferred Method: The heat treatment condition includes: temperature is 65-90 degrees C, and time is 24-48 hours. The cooling rate is 4-6 degrees C/minute.

PT P
PN RU2678627-C1
TI Method of processing spent catalysts containing noble metals and rhenium for complex processing not only of spent catalysts containing platinum group metals and rhenium.
AU POLYAKOV P V
   POPOV YU N
   POLYAKOV A A
   BERNGARDT M G
   PAVLOV E A
   MALTSEV E V
AE LIGHT METALS CO LTD (LIGH-Non-standard)
GA 2019218834
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the metallurgy of noble metals and can be used in the processing of spent catalysts based on aluminum, silicon, magnesium oxides containing noble metals and rhenium. Spent catalysts are poured into an electrolyzer containing an anode, a cathode and one bipolar porous electrode-collector (BPE-C) for accumulation of noble metals and rhenium in the electrolyte. BPE-C is placed in the space between the anode and the cathode. Current is passed through the anode and cathode. Oxide metal is deposited on the cathode, and the noble metals and rhenium are on the BPE-C. BPE-C is made in the form of a cellular matrix, inert with respect to the metal and electrolyte obtained at the cathode, with an open porous structure formed by internal pores or capillaries, or channels, or cavities filled with metal produced at the cathode, and the release of oxygen or carbon dioxide at the anode. Spent catalysts before grounding in the electrolyzer are crushed to 40-100 microns, followed by heating to 100-150 degrees C, after which it is fed to the electrolyte in the anode space of the electrolyzer between the anode and BPE-C.
   USE - Metallurgy.
   ADVANTAGE - Method allows complex processing not only of spent catalysts containing platinum group metals and rhenium with their extraction, as well as catalyst matrix into aluminum or silumin with an increase in yield.
13 cl, 6 dwg

TI Compound essential oil comprises e.g. dodder, medlar, raspberry, Plantago asiatica, Schisandra berry and extracting essential oil in raw material using supercritical fluid, extracting residue with ethanol aqueous solution and mixing extract.
AU LAO Y
   HUANG Y
   ZHI X
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201887359Y
AB    NOVELTY - Compound essential oil comprises 500-700 pts. wt. dodder, 300-500 pts. wt. medlar, 100-200 pts. wt. raspberry, 60-100 pts. wt. Plantago asiatica, 50-80 pts. wt. Schisandra berry, extracting essential oil in raw material using supercritical fluid, extracting residue after extracting essential oil using aqueous solution of ethanol and mixing extract extracted by reflux with the essential oil.
   USE - The compound essential oil is useful for preparing skin care product and/or traditional Chinese medicine preparation (claimed), treating impotence, spermatorrhea, enuresis and frequent urination.
   ADVANTAGE - The compound essential oil has good transdermal absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing essential oil, comprising crushing seed of Chinese dodder, medlar, raspberry, Plantago asiatica and Schisandra chinensis, sieving to obtain Chinese dodder seed powder, medlar powder, raspberry powder, Plantago asiatica seed powder and Schisandra chinensis powder, mixing raw material powder evenly to obtain mixture, crushing, sieving and mixing raw material powders; (ii) placing mixture in supercritical fluid extraction kettle, extracting with carbon dioxide supercritical fluid under the pressure of 18-30 MPa and temperature of 40-150 degrees C to obtain essential oils and residues; (iii) extracting residue by refluxing with aqueous solution of ethanol to obtain extract; and (iv) mixing essential oil of step (ii) and extract of step (iii) by ultrasonic spray.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (iii) concentration of aqueous ethanol solution is 30-80%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The compound essential oil further comprises at least one of Phyllanthus emblica, glossy privet fruit and Rosa laevigata. The dodder seed powder and raspberry powder are medium sized powder with sieve No. 4, medlar powder, Plantago asiatica powder and Schisandra powder are coarse powder, and sieve No. 3. Preferred Method: In the step (ii) extraction time is 90-180 minutes and flow rate of carbon dioxide is controlled at 20-30 kg/hours. In the step (iii) liquid-to-feed ratio during reflux extraction is 8-50 ml: 1 g, and number of times of reflux extraction is 1-3 times, time of each reflux extraction is 0.5-2.5 hours and temperature of reflux extraction is 60-90 degrees C. In the step (iv) placing essential oil of step (ii) and extract of step (iii) in two liquid spray devices, ultrasonically spraying, simultaneously opening two spray devices and ultrasonic transducer and mixing essential oil and extract evenly. The ultrasonic frequency is 40-80 Hz.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic; Antiinfertility; Uropathic; Uropathid.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. TI Large-depth deep-welding method of longitudinal joints of bridge shells comprises e.g. cleaning the embroidery, oil and dirt in the welding area of the two half-bridge shells, aligning, adjusting the electric welder and flat welding.
AU YAN L
   ZHOU W
   PENG Z
   ZHANG L
   SHI H
AE DONGFENG COMML VEHICLES CO LTD (DMOT-C)
GA 2018861657
AB    NOVELTY - Large-depth deep-welding method of longitudinal joints of bridge shells comprises (i) cleaning the embroidery, oil and dirt in the welding area of the two half-bridge shells and the surrounding area of 20-30 mm until the metallic luster is exposed, preparing the two half-bridge shells of 16 Mnl material and have a thickness of 14 mm, (ii) aligning the welding faces of the longitudinal welds of the two halves of the bridge shell, (iii) adjusting the electric welder to the high-speed arc function and setting the welding parameters, single-pass welding of the longitudinal joint of the axle housing by means of flat welding, where the welding current is 400-500 A, and the welding voltage is 20-40 V, the welding speed is 400-500 mm/minutes, and the gas is a mixed gas of argon and carbon dioxide, and the gas flow rate is 20-30 ml/minutes.
   USE - The method large-depth deep-welding of longitudinal joints of bridge shells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: achieves the requirement of 14 mm thick longitudinal weld penetration rate greater than or equal to 70% and penetration depth less than or equal to 5 mm; has low voltage, energy saving, simple steps and easy to operate, excellent welding results and outstanding practicality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (iii), the welding current is 440 A, the welding voltage is 23.6 V, welding speed is 420 mm/minutes, the gas is a mixture of 80% argon and 20% carbon dioxide, and the gas flow rate is 20 ml/minutes. In step (iii), the welding wire used is a cable welding wire. The cable wire has a diameter of 2.4 mm.

AU LI G

   DAN C

AE UNIV SHANDONG JIAOTONG (SDJT-C)
GA 2018796420
AB    NOVELTY - The environmental control system has a control box electrically connected to oxygen pressure regulation system. The cabin system includes a front cabin, a rear cabin, a passenger cabin and a bottom cabin. The total pressure and oxygen pressure regulation system is provided with a main nitrogen storage tank, a main oxygen storage tank, a total pressure sensor, and an oxygen pressure sensor. The total pressure sensor and the oxygen pressure sensor are respectively installed in the passenger cabin. The main nitrogen storage tank is connected to the air intake end of the passenger cabin through the decompression proportional valve and fan. The main oxygen storage tank is connected to the air inlet end of the passenger cabin through decompression proportional valve and fan. The outlet end of the cabin is connected to the inlet end of the carbon dioxide adsorption bed through the fan.
   USE - Environmental control system for closed environment of high-speed vacuum train.
   ADVANTAGE - The engineering cost is reduced, the operability is improved, the manufacturing difficulty is reduced, and the adaptability is improved, flexible combination, to satisfy actual demand of vacuum high speed train transportation.

PT P
PN WO2018154488-A1; US2019379009-A1; EP3586385-A1; US11024910-B2
TI Rechargeable battery for the hybrid electric vehicles for the wind and solar energy sources, comprises a solid-state device that performs the oxygen storage, where the oxygen storage material is provided with a sulfide redox.
AU YANG T
   M MIKHALEV S
   SHAULA A
   P FAGG D
   MIKHALEV S M
   FAGG D P
AE UNIV AVEIRO (UYAV-Non-standard)
   UNIV AVEIRO (UYAV-Non-standard)
   UNIV AVEIRO (UYAV-Non-standard)
GA 201868474B
AB    NOVELTY - The rechargeable battery comprises a solid-state device that performs the oxygen storage. A gas-tight shell has the oxygen-ion conducting electrolyte, and defines the contained volume. An oxygen storage material is provided with a sulfide redox. A first electrode is set in contact with the electrolyte, where the second electrode is set in contact with the other side of the electrolyte. An electrical contact is made with of the electrodes are selected from an electronically conducting material. The oxygen storage material is selected from a list of the elements, such as the gadolinium, lanthanum, cerium, neodymium, samarium, dysprosium, erbium, europium, holmium, terbium, ytterbium, yttrium, scandium, lutetium, magnesium, calcium, strontium, barium, lithium, sodium, and potassium. The shuttle gas has a mixture of steam/hydrogen gas mixture, and carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide gas mixture.
   USE - Rechargeable battery, particularly an electrochemical cell battery for the hybrid electric vehicles, smart grid, and back-up power for the wind and solar energy sources.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas-tight shell has the oxygen-ion conducting electrolyte, and defines the contained volume, and hence enhances the battery lifetime, and capacity, and prevents the short-circuits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an oxygen pump for the rechargeable battery; and
   (2) an operating method for the rechargeable battery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the rechargeable battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The electrodes are provided with the ceramic oxides of the perovskite or perovskite related structural families offering the mixed oxygen ion and electronic conductivity. The gas-tight shell is a zirconium dioxide ceramic doped with yttrium trioxide cerium (IV) oxide. The electrodes are provided with the platinum, nickel, gold, silver, and copper. The sulfide redox couple is an alkaline earth metal sulfate MS04 /MS based oxygen storage material.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage); X21 (Electric Vehicles)

PT P
PN CN108342362-A
TI New stable cell line i.e. Madin-Darby canine            kidney cell useful for expressing canine adenovirus E1A            gene and E1B gene and preparing canine adenovirus            CAV2.
AU PAN X
   LI L
   GAO J
   YANG T
   HUANG X
AE WUHAN BRAINVTA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201862114G
AB    NOVELTY - A stable cell line i.e. Madin-Darby canine                kidney (MDCK) cell capable of expressing canine                adenovirus E1A gene and E1B gene, is new. The                stable cell line is obtained by randomly inserting                the E1A gene and the E1B gene using lentivirus as a                carrier into the genome of the MDCK cell. The E1A                gene and the E1B gene are both derived from canine                adenovirus CAV2.
   USE - The stable cell line is useful expressing                canine adenovirus E1A gene and E1B gene and                preparing canine adenovirus CAV2. The method (M2)                is useful for increasing the titer value of canine                adenovirus CAV2 (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The stable cell line ensures that the E1 gene                is constructed into a short segment, E1A and E1B                genes are easy to construct and stability of stable                cells in real time is convenient to monitor, since                the E1A and E1B genes carry fluorescent proteins of                different colors, respectively and increases the                success rate of constructing stable cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method (M1) for constructing the stable                cell line;
   (2) use of a stable cell line for preparing                canine adenovirus CAV2; and
   (3) method (M2) for increasing the titer of                canine adenovirus CAV2, involves inoculating the                canine adenovirus CAV2 strain in the stable cell                line, culturing the virus strain at 35-37 degrees C                under 3-5% carbon dioxide, harvesting cell pellets                after 48-72 hours and purifying the virus                strain.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation (claimed): The stable cell line                  is constructed by constructing canine E1A gene                  and the canine E1B gene separately into a                  lentiviral vector, obtaining lentivirus                  expressing the E1A gene and a lentivirus                  expressing the E1B gene respectively, infecting                  MDCK cells with the lentivirus expressing the E1A                  gene to obtain a MDCK-E1A cell line, infecting                  MDCK-E1A cell line with the lentivirus expressing                  the E1B gene to obtain stable cell line                  MDCK-E1A-E1B. Preferred Method: The method                  involves (a) amplifying the E1A and E1B genes                  from the vector pCAV2, cloning the E1A fragment                  into the lentiviral vector pFUGW containing the                  reporter gene mCherry by homologous recombination                  to construct the recombinant plasmid                  pFUGW-his-mCherry-2a-E1A and cloning E1B fragment                  into the lentiviral vector pCDH containing the                  reporter gene green fluorescent protein (GFP) and                  screening gene puro to construct the recombinant                  plasmid pCDH-his-GFP-2A-E1Bpuro, (b) inoculating                  human embryonic kidney cells 293 (HEK293) T cells                  in a culture dish, adding the recombinant plasmid                  pFUGW-hismCherry-2a-E1A obtained in step (a), the                  lentiviral packaging plasmids g/p, PMD2G and                  RSV-REV in a mass ratio of 8:3:2:1 using the                  lentiviral transfection system, mixing with                  sterile water, transfecting the cells with                  calcium phosphate transfection, and placing the                  cells in an incubator for culturing, harvesting                  the cell culture medium containing LV-mCherry-E1A                  virus 48-72 hours after transfection, performing                  sterile filtration and collecting filtrate                  containing LV-mCherry-E1A virus, (c) inoculating                  HEK293T cells in a culture dish, adding the                  recombinant plasmid pCDH-his-GFP-2AE1B-puro                  obtained in step (a), the lentiviral packaging                  plasmids g/p, PMD2G and RSV-REV in a mass ratio                  of 8:3:2:1 using the lentiviral transfection                  system, mixing with sterile water, transfecting                  the cells with calcium phosphate transfection,                  and placing the cells in an incubator for                  culturing, harvesting the cell culture medium                  containing LV-GFP-puro-E1B virus 48-72 hours                  after transfection, performing sterile filtration                  and collecting filtrate containing                  LV-GFP-puro-E1B virus, (d) measuring the titer of                  the recombinant lentivirus, adding the                  LV-mCherry-E1A virus filtrate to the culture dish                  pre-inoculated with MDCK cells at a viral                  multiplicity (MOI) equal to 10-20, observing the                  expression of fluorescence and continuously                  subculturing until more than 90% of the cells in                  the final field of view are red fluorescence                  which indicates that the MDCKE1A cell line stably                  expressing the E1A gene is screened and (e)                  adding the LV-GFP-puro-E1B virus filtrate to a                  culture dish pre-inoculated with the MDCKE1A cell                  line at a MOI equal to 10-20, adding puromycin                  antibiotics to screen positive cell lines and                  obtaining MDCK-E1A-E1B cell line in which MDCK                  stably expressed E1A and E1B genes. The                  pFUGW-his-mCherry-2a-E1A recombinant plasmid is                  constructed by (a) amplifying the E1A fragment                  using pCAV2 as a template by PCR, then adding the                  fusion sequence and the restriction site to E1A,                  amplifying the his-mCherry-2A fragment containing                  the fusion sequence using the                  H2BmCherry-P2A-N2c(G) plasmid as a template by                  PCR and fusing the E1A and his-mCherry-2A                  fragments by homologous recombination PCR to                  obtain a PCR fragment, (b) performing                  electrophoresis detection and purification of the                  PCR fragment obtained in the step (a) and                  measuring the OD value, (c) double-digesting the                  lentiviral vector pFUGW with Xanthomonas badrii                  Xba1 and Escherichia coli EcoR1 enzyme and                  recovering the linear vector by gel-cutting                  process, (d) ligating the PCR fragment obtained                  in step (b) with the linear vector obtained in                  step (c) using the T4 DNA ligase at 16 degrees C                  overnight and (e) transforming the ligated                  product into DH10B competent cells using the                  thermal transformation method, culturing at 37                  degrees C for more than 16 hours, picking single                  colonies, extracting the plasmid,                  double-digesting the plasmid with Xba1 and EcoR1                  enzyme to verify the correctness and sequencing                  the plasmid. The pCDH-his-GFP-2A-E1B-puro                  recombinant plasmid is constructed by (a)                  amplifying the E1B fragment using pCAV2 as a                  template by PCR, then adding the fusion sequence                  and the restriction site to E1B, amplifying the                  his-mCherry-2A fragment containing the fusion                  sequence using the H2B-GFPP2A-N2c(G) plasmid as a                  template by PCR and fusing the E1A and his-GFP-2A                  fragments by homologous recombination PCR to                  obtain a PCR fragment, (b) performing                  electrophoresis detection and purification of the                  PCR fragment obtained in the step (a) and                  measuring the OD value, (c) double-digesting the                  lentiviral vector pFUGW with Xba1 and Not1 enzyme                  and recovering the linear vector by gel-cutting                  process, (d) ligating the PCR fragment obtained                  in step (b) with the linear vector obtained in                  step (c) using the T4 DNA ligase at 16 degrees C                  overnight and (e) transforming the ligated                  product into DH10B competent cells using the                  thermal transformation method, culturing at 37                  degrees C for more than 16 hours, picking single                  colonies, extracting the plasmid,                  double-digesting the plasmid with Xba1 and EcoR1                  enzyme to verify the correctness and sequencing                  the plasmid.

PN CN107936247-A; CN107936247-B
TI Preparation of halogen group-containing polyimide aerogel for preparing composite photocatalyst, involves dissolving halogen group-containing monomer in organic solvent, adding e.g. diamine and pyridine, imidizing, aging, and drying.
AU ZHAO X
   YI X
   TIAN S
   ZHANG J
   LIU B
   FAN H
   LIU X
AE NEW MATERIALS INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
   NEW MATERIALS INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
GA 201832760S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of halogen group (X)-containing polyimide aerogel involves subjecting an intermediate compound to an organic substitution reaction under a catalyst condition, obtaining a monomer containing a halogen group (X), dissolving the monomer containing halogen group (X) in an organic solvent, adding diamine or dianhydride to the mixed solution, stirring, dissolving, rapidly adding crosslinking agent, stirring, adding acetic anhydride and pyridine, performing chemical imidization reaction, obtaining polyimide wet aerogel containing halogen group (X), aging the polyimide wet aerogel in an ethanol solution, performing ethanol supercritical drying, and obtaining sheet of halogen group (X)-containing polyimide aerogel.
   USE - Preparation of halogen group-containing polyimide aerogel used for preparing composite photocatalyst for degrading organic pollutants, photolysis of water to ammonia, reducing carbon dioxide, selective organic synthesis (all claimed), and adsorbing heavy metal.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst prepared using the halogen group-containing polyimide aerogel has high specific surface area and stability, and excellent recyclability and adsorption property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of insoluble salt and polyimide aerogel composite photocatalyst, which involves dipping the halogen group (X)-containing polyimide aerogel in a soluble salt ethanol solution, such that the halogen group (X) in the polyimide aerogel reacts with the metal cation in the soluble salt, performing ethanol supercritical drying of obtained polyimide composite aerogel containing insoluble salt, and forming heterojunction between the polyimide and the precipitated salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal cation of inorganic salt is lead, silver or mercury.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The intermediate compound includes o-nitrochlorobenzene, o-nitrobromobenzene or o-nitroiodobenzene. The catalyst is sodium hydroxide/formaldehyde, hydrazine or hydrochloric acid/hydrogen bromide/hydrogen iodide. The mass ratio of intermediate compound and catalyst (g/mL) is 1-3:5. The halogen group (X) is chlorine, bromine or iodine element. The crosslinking agent is 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene. The organic solvent is N-methylpyrrolidone. The diamine is 2,2-diaminobenzidine, p-phenylenediamine or 4,4-diaminodiphenyl ether. The dianhydride is biphenyl tetracarboxylic dianhydride, pyromellitic dianhydride or diphenyl ether tetracarboxylic dianhydride. The mass ratio of diamine or dianhydride and organic solvent is 1-3:10-15. The volume ratio of crosslinking agent and organic solvent is 1-3:5. The volume ratio of acetic anhydride, pyridine and organic solvent is 1:1:5-10. The mass ratio of halogen group (X)-containing polyimide aerogel and soluble salt ethanol solution (g/mL) is 1-3:1. The soluble salt is lead(II) nitrate, silver nitrate or mercury(II) nitrate. The concentration of soluble salt in soluble salt ethanol solution is 1-2 mol/L. Preferred Process: The aging treatment of wet aerogel is performed after soaking the aerogel with 50-75% N-methylpyrrolidone in ethanol solution for 18-24 hours.

TI Producing dicarboxylic acid involves reacting composition comprising one or more aliphatic dinitriles where aliphatic dinitrile contains two-twenty carbon atoms or one or more aromatic dinitriles and aromatic dinitrile.
AU RANDALL P D
   MITFORD R R
   RANDALL D
   BLACK G
   PERRY J J B
   BLACK G W
AE CHEMOXY INT LTD (CHEM-Non-standard)
   CHEMOXY INT LTD (CHEM-Non-standard)
GA 2018277362
AB    NOVELTY - Producing dicarboxylic acid involves reacting composition comprising one or more aliphatic dinitriles where aliphatic dinitrile contains 2-20 carbon atoms or one or more aromatic dinitriles and aromatic dinitrile contains 6-20 carbon atoms with nitrilase to produce a dicarboxylic acid. The nitrilase comprises CWE motif, when tested using CPA Nitrilase Assay with 2-methylglutaronitrile as substrate has hydrolysis of 45% or using CPA Nitrilase Assay with 4-CPA as substrate has hydrolysis of 15%.
   USE - Method for producing dicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce dicarboxylic acid has low cost, saves energy, and avoids generation of carbon dioxide in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for producing diesters, which involves producing a dicarboxylic acid by producing dicarboxylic acid; converting dicarboxylic acid to diester by reaction with one or more alcohols, where alcohol contains from 1-20 carbon atoms; and
   (2) a isolated polypeptide having nitrilase activity, which comprises amino acid sequence 35 which when aligned to (SEQ ID NO: 1) has a modification at one or more of Val 90, Leu 131 and/or Thr 243 and where amino acid sequence has 90% sequence identity to (SEQ ID NO: 1) or amino acid sequence binds under stringent conditions to complement of (SEQ ID NO: 1); and
   (3) a nucleic acid encoding amino acid in accordance with producing dicarboxylic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The nitrilase is variant of (SEQ ID NO: 1), given in the specification, and which has modification at Val 90, Leu 131 and/or Thr243. The modification at Val 90 is V9oA, 15 modification at Leu 131 is L131 I, L131V, L131Y or L131M, and/or modification at Thr 243 is T243A. The nitrilase has 90% sequence identity to (SEQ ID NOs: 1, 2, 3, and 5), given in the specification. The reaction is carried out at 20-40 degrees C. The nitrilase has 90% sequence identity with wild-type nitrilase from a Bradyrhizobium or Nitratireductor species. The nitrilase is immobilized. The amidase is added simultaneously or subsequent to reaction. The amidase is capable of reacting with 4-30 CPAmide. The conversion yield to dicarboxylic acid is 60%. The reaction step is stopped when there is 75% conversion of aliphatic dinitrile to dicarboxylic acid. The reacting step is continued until there is 80%. The reacting step is carried out at pH of 6-8. The dicarboxylic acid is chiral and reaction yields an enantiomeric excess of 75% or more of one isomer of dicarboxylic acid. The enantiomeric excess of is 80% or more. The reaction yields an enantiomeric excess of one isomer of mononitrile. The enantiomeric excess is 70% or more. The reacting step is carried out over 15 period of 6 days or less, and more preferably less than 4 days. Preferred Components: The amidase is an aliphatic amidase (EC. 3.5.1.4) which is member of Pfam PFo1425. The source of dinitrile is a waste stream, preferably waste stream is a by product of manufacture of adiponitrile (1,4-dicyanobutane). The dinitrile is an aliphatic dinitrile. The dinitrile is 2-methylglutaronitrile and dicarboxylic acid produced is 2-methylglutaric acid and/or dinitrile is 2-ethylsuccinonitrile and dicarboxylic acid produced is 2-ethylsuccinic acid. The dinitrile is an aromatic dinitrile. The aromatic dinitrile contains from 6-10 carbon atoms, and preferably contains 6, 7, or 8 carbon atoms. Preferred Compositions: The amidase comprises sequence (SEQ ID NO: 7). The source of dinitrile contains more than one dinitrile compound. The source of dinitrile contains one dinitrile compound. The aliphatic dinitrile contains 5-10 carbon atoms, and preferably contains 5 or 6 carbon atoms.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable examples given.
