TI Method for manufacturing concrete structure,            involves inserting hose into hole of second lattice            portion on upper surface of side horizontal extension            portion in shell portion, and fresh concrete is fed            into shell portion using hose.
AU HANZAWA S
   HANZAWA K
AE SMRC INC (SMRC-Non-standard)
GA 202273515B
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of concrete structure (1) involves                (i) forming a shell portion (20) in a state in                which a concrete forming a structural main portion                (10) is not filled, (ii) filling a fresh concrete                into a shell portion in a state where a reinforcing                component is not arranged, (iii) discharging excess                water from the fresh concrete to the outside                through holes (21) of the shell portion, (iv)                integrally hardening the concrete from which the                excess water is discharged with the shell portion                to form the main portion, and (v) exposing the                concrete structure to the atmosphere without                blowing carbon dioxide onto the concrete structure.                The hole in the shell portion contains an inscribed                circle having a diameter of 20-30 mm (excluding                20), and is not covered with other components. In                the step (ii), the hose is inserted into hole of                lattice portion (B) on the upper surface of upper                horizontal extension portion of the shell portion,                and the fresh concrete is sent into shell portion                using the hose.
   USE - Manufacture of concrete structure for pier and                buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete structure has excellent                durability and strength, and is easy to maintain                and manage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of concrete structure (1) involves                (i) forming a shell portion (20) in a state in                which a concrete forming a structural main portion                (10) is not filled, (ii) filling a fresh concrete                into a shell portion in a state where a reinforcing                component is not arranged, (iii) discharging excess                water from the fresh concrete to the outside                through holes (21) of the shell portion by                compacting the weight of the fresh concrete, (iv)                integrally hardening the fresh concrete from which                the excess water is discharged with the shell                portion to form the main portion of the structure,                and (v) exposing the concrete structure to the                atmosphere without blowing carbon dioxide onto the                concrete structure to modify at least the surface                side of the structural main portion to a petrified                portion. The concrete structure comprises a                concrete-made structural main portion in which the                reinforcing component extending in a preset                direction is not arranged and surrounded by a                preset cross section, the shell portion provided                integrally with the main portion, an upper                horizontal extension portion, a lower horizontal                extension portion, and a vertical extension portion                connecting the upper horizontal extension portion                and the lower horizontal extension portion. The                shell portion has a tensile stress degree greater                than that of the structural main portion and                contains holes for supplying carbon dioxide in the                outside air to the main portion. The main portion                contains the petrified portion in which concrete is                carbonated and transformed into a stone material at                least on the surface side. The hole in the shell                portion contains an inscribed circle having a                diameter of 20-30 mm (excluding 20), and is not                covered with other components. In the step (iii),                the state of the fresh concrete was confirmed                through the holes in the shell, and a cement paste                of the fresh concrete flows out from the hole of                the shell portion. In the step (iv), the cement                paste is hardened inside and outside the holes. The                shell portion is provided with plate-shaped divided                units having a lattice portion forming the outer                edge of the holes. In the step (i), the shells are                assembled by combining the divided units. Each                divided unit comprises reinforcing ribs arranged in                vertical and horizontal directions at equal                intervals in the vertical and horizontal                directions. The reinforcing ribs located on the                outermost side in the vertical and horizontal                directions formed an outer frame for each divided                unit. The shell portion is provided by connecting                the reinforcing ribs forming the outer frame of                each divided unit to each other, and surrounds the                upper horizontal extension portion, the lower                horizontal extension portion and the vertical                extension portion. As the shell portion, two types                of lattice portions (A) and (B) having different                pore sizes are used. The size of the hole in the                lattice portion (A) is set so as to suppress the                outflow of coarse aggregate (11) of the fresh                concrete. The size of the hole in the lattice                portion (B) is set so that the hose for delivering                the fresh concrete is inserted. The upper surface                of the upper horizontal extension portion is formed                horizontally. The lattice portion (B) is used for a                portion of the shell portion that covers the                horizontal portion on the upper surface of the                upper horizontal extension portion and the lower                horizontal extension portion, and the lattice                portion (A) is used for the other portion. In the                step (ii), the hose is inserted into the hole of                the lattice portion (B) on the upper surface of the                upper horizontal extension portion of the shell                portion, and the fresh concrete is sent into the                shell portion using the hose.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic cross-sectional                view of concrete structure. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

PN WO2022060588-A2; WO2022060588-A3
TI Making carbonaceous perimorphic framework by deriving precursor material and mother liquor, heating precursor material, adsorbing carbonaceous perimorphic material to template material and reacting template material with extractant solution.
AU BISHOP M
   BRILL D
   CARSTENS C
   THOMAS A
   WESTLE A
AE DICKINSON CORP (DICK-Non-standard)
GA 202242954K
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (M1) a carbonaceous perimorphic framework, comprises: (a) deriving a precursor material and a mother liquor from a first stock solution via solventless precipitation; (b) heating the precursor material to form a template material; (c) adsorbing a carbonaceous perimorphic material to the template material; and (d) reacting the template material with an extractant solution comprising the mother liquor to form a carbonaceous perimorphic framework and a second stock solution, the second stock solution and the first stock solution sharing a similar chemical composition.
   USE - The methods are useful for: producing a carbonaceous perimorphic framework; and producing a hollow carbonaceous perimorphic framework.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is: scalable synthesis method and economic; and low-cost, waste-reducing method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (M2) carbonaceous perimorphic framework, comprising the step (a) and (b) as per se, adsorbing a carbonaceous perimorphic material to the template material, reacting the template material with an extractant solution comprising the mother liquor to form a carbonaceous perimorphic framework and a reconstituted stock solution, the first stock solution and the reconstituted stock solution sharing a similar chemical composition, and forming additional perimorphic frameworks by cyclical repetition of above steps, where the reconstituted stock solution formed in each cycle comprises the first stock solution used in a subsequent cycle;
   (2) producing (M3) carbonaceous perimorphic framework, comprising (i) from a first stock solution comprising ions dissolved in a process liquid, forming via solventless precipitation i.e. a precursor material comprising a solid precipitate, and a mother liquor comprising an unprecipitated portion of the ions dissolved in the process liquid, (ii) treating the precursor material to form a template material, the treating comprising at least one of decomposing a portion of the precursor material, changing a morphology of the precursor material, activating the precursor material, and heating the precursor material, (iii) adsorbing carbonaceous perimorphic material to the template material to form a perimorphic composite material, (iv) exposing the perimorphic composite material to an extractant solution, where the extractant solution comprises an extractant and the mother liquor, (v) extracting the template material from the perimorphic composite material to form a second stock solution having a composition similar to the first stock solution, and a carbonaceous perimorphic framework comprising a carbonaceous perimorphic wall and pores, where the pores are formed by the extracting of the template material, and (vi) separating the carbonaceous perimorphic framework from the second stock solution;
   (3) producing (M4) a hollow carbonaceous perimorphic framework, comprising (a1) atomizing and evaporating a first stock solution comprising ions dissolved in a process liquid to form a precursor material comprising hollow precursor particles, and a vapor comprising evaporated process liquid, (a2) condensing the evaporated process liquid in the vapor to form a conserved process liquid, (a3) treating the precursor material to form a template material comprising hollow template particles and the treating comprising at least one of decomposing a portion of the precursor material, changing a morphology of the precursor material, activating the precursor material, and heating the precursor material, (a4) adsorbing a carbonaceous perimorphic material to the template material to form a perimorphic composite material comprising hollow perimorphic composite particles, (a5) forming an extractant solution by adding an extractant to the conserved process liquid, (a6) exposing the perimorphic composite material to the extractant solution, (a7) extracting the template material from the perimorphic composite material to form a second stock solution having a composition similar to the first stock solution, and a hollow carbonaceous perimorphic framework comprising a carbonaceous perimorphic wall and pores formed by the extracting of the template material, and (a8) separating the hollow carbonaceous perimorphic framework from the second stock solution;
   (4) producing (M5) carbonaceous perimorphic framework, comprising (b1) from a first stock solution comprising aqueous magnesium bicarbonate, forming via solventless precipitation i.e. a precursor material comprising a magnesium carbonate precipitate, a mother liquor comprising an unprecipitated portion of the first stock solution with a reduced concentration of magnesium bicarbonate, and a vapor comprising at least one of a first portion of process water vapor and a first portion of carbon dioxide process gas, (b2) treating the precursor material to form a template material comprising porous magnesia particles, and a vapor comprising at least one of a second portion of process water vapor and a second portion of carbon dioxide process gas, (b3) adsorbing a carbonaceous perimorphic material to the template material to form a perimorphic composite material, (b4) optionally, condensing at least one of the first and second portions of process water vapor to form a conserved process water, (b5) exposing the perimorphic composite material to an extractant solution comprising aqueous carbonic acid, where the extractant solution optionally comprises at least one of the first and second portions of carbon dioxide process gas and the conserved process water, (b6) extracting the template material from the perimorphic composite material to form a second stock solution comprising magnesium bicarbonate and having a composition similar to the first stock solution, and a carbonaceous perimorphic framework, where the hollow carbonaceous perimorphic framework comprises a carbonaceous perimorphic wall and pores formed by the extracting of the template material, and (b7) separating the carbonaceous perimorphic framework from the second stock solution;
   (5) producing (M6) a hollow carbonaceous perimorphic framework; and
   (6) producing (M7) the carbonaceous perimorphic framework.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M6) comprises (c1) atomizing and evaporating a first stock solution comprising magnesium bicarbonate dissolved in a process water to form a precursor material comprising hollow magnesium carbonate precursor particles, and a vapor comprising a first portion of process water vapor and a first portion of carbon dioxide process gas, (c2) condensing the first portion of process water vapor to form a first portion of conserved process water, (c3) treating the precursor material to form a template material comprising hollow magnesia particles, and a vapor comprising at least one of a second portion of process water vapor and a second portion of carbon dioxide process gas, (c4) optionally, condensing the second portion of process water vapor to form a second portion of conserved process water, (c5) adsorbing a carbonaceous perimorphic material to the template material to form a perimorphic composite material, where the perimorphic composite material comprises hollow perimorphic composite particles, (c6) in at least one of the first and second portions of conserved process water, dissolving at least one of the first and second portions of carbon dioxide process gas to form an extractant solution comprising carbonic acid, (c7) exposing the perimorphic composite material to the extractant solution, (c8) extracting the template material from the perimorphic composite material to form a second stock solution comprising magnesium bicarbonate and having a composition similar to the first stock solution, and a hollow carbonaceous perimorphic framework comprising a carbonaceous perimorphic wall and pores formed by the extracting of the template material, and (c9) separating the hollow carbonaceous perimorphic framework from the second stock solution. The method (M7) comprises (d1) from a first stock solution comprising aqueous magnesium bicarbonate, forming via solventless precipitation i.e. precursor material comprising a magnesium carbonate precipitate, a mother liquor comprising an unprecipitated portion of the first stock solution with a reduced concentration of magnesium bicarbonate, and a vapor comprising at least one of a first portion of process water vapor and a first portion of carbon dioxide process gas, (d2) treating the precursor material to form a template material comprising porous, dehydrated magnesium carbonate particles, and a vapor comprising at least one of a second portion of process water vapor and a second portion of carbon dioxide process gas, (d3) adsorbing a carbonaceous perimorphic material to the template material to form a first perimorphic composite material, (d4) optionally, condensing at least one of the first and second portions of process water vapor to form a conserved process water, (d5) decomposing the template material to form a second perimorphic composite material comprising the carbonaceous perimorphic material and endomorphic magnesia, (d6) exposing the second perimorphic composite material to an extractant solution comprising aqueous carbonic acid, the extractant solution optionally comprising at least one of the first and second portions of carbon dioxide process gas and the conserved process water, (d7) extracting the endomorphic magnesia from the perimorphic composite material to form a second stock solution comprising magnesium bicarbonate and having a composition similar to the first stock solution, and carbonaceous perimorphic framework comprising a carbonaceous perimorphic wall and pores formed by the extracting of the template material, and (d8) separating the carbonaceous perimorphic framework from the second stock solution. The methods (M1)-(M7) further comprise annealing at least one of the perimorphic composite material and the carbonaceous perimorphic framework. The extractant solution is formed by dissolving carbon dioxide in water at a carbon dioxide pressure of greater than 1 atm. Preferred Components: The carbonaceous perimorphic material comprises: a synthetic anthracitic network; a graphenic monolayer; and a maximum thickness of 100 nm. The template material comprises: a porous network of conjoined subunits; a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of 10-100 m2/g; BET surface area of 100-400 m2/g; and at least one of Group I metal cations, Group II metal cations, oxyanions, and oxygen anions. The precursor material comprises: a carbonate of a Group I or II metal; magnesium salt of a weak acid; a hydrous magnesium carbonate; and a hierarchical-equiaxed morphology.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of precursor Stage procedure, an elongated nesquehonite template precursor material that do not support the claims.

PN CN109593817-A
TI Detecting effect of heating non-combusted cigarette total particle phase on cell cycle, involves pretreating sample, inoculating human nasal epithelial cell suspension, exposing test substance, fixing cells, staining and analyzing cells.
AU GUAN Y
   LI X
   ZHANG X
   HAN J
   HE D
   ZHANG W
   GAO Q
   XU Y
   TANG J
   ZHU D
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 201934986S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for detecting effect of heating a non-combusted cigarette total particle phase on a cell cycle, involves pretreating sample by using a linear smoking machine, continuously pumping, capturing, weighing flue gas trap before and after pumping, placing the captured filter in a 50 ml flask, adding dimethyl sulfoxide, treating, collecting the dimethyl sulfoxide extract, dispensing into a 1 ml cryotube, and storing at -80 degrees C, preparing single cell suspension of human nasal epithelial cells, calculating cell concentration of human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension, inoculating human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension, exposing the test substance by diluting the sample, adding different doses of the test solution to the cell culture plate, and placing in incubator, collecting cells, performing cell fixation, adding 200 mu l of dye solution prepared with PBS to each centrifuge tube, staining for 30 minutes in dark condition, and performing flow detection and analysis.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting effect of heating a non-combusted cigarette total particle phase on a cell cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method detects effect of heating a non-combusted cigarette total particle phase on a cell cycle accurately.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for detecting effect of heating a non-combusted cigarette total particle phase on a cell cycle, involves (a) pretreating sample by using a linear smoking machine with a suction capacity of 55 ml, a duration of 3 seconds and a suction frequency of 30 seconds, continuously pumping 10 ports, capturing with a Cambridge filter with a diameter of 44 mm, weighing flue gas trap before and after pumping, respectively, calculating the mass mTPM of the total particulate matter of the non-burning cigarette using formula: m1-m0 (I), where m0 is mass of the flue gas trap before pumping (in mg) and m1 is the mass of the flue gas trap after pumping (in mg), after the capture is completed, placing the captured filter in a 50 ml flask, adding dimethyl sulfoxide until the concentration of the total non-combusted cigarette phase in dimethyl sulfoxide reaches 40 mg/ml, placing the flask on the ultrasound system for 30 minutes, collecting the dimethyl sulfoxide extract by filtration through a sterile filter paper, dispensing into a 1 ml cryotube, and storing at -80 degrees C, (b) preparing single cell suspension of human nasal epithelial cells by after resuscitation of human nasal epithelial cells RPMI2650, inoculating into a 25 cm2 cell culture flask, adding 10 ml RPMI1640 cell culture medium, incubating in 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide incubator, observing confluence and morphology of the cultured cells under an inverted microscope, when the cells are grown to a confluence of 70-90%, removing the RPMI1640 cell culture medium in the culture flask, washing twice with phosphate buffer, discarding washing solution, adding 1 ml 0.25 weight/volume% trypsin solution to a single layer for 1-2 minutes, and suspending with RPMI1640 cell culture medium to form a single cell suspension, (c) calculating cell concentration of human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension by calculating the cell concentration of the human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension by using a hemocytometer counting method to obtain the living cell number per ml of the single cell suspension, (d) inoculating human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension by diluting the human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension to 2x 105 cells/mL with RPMI1640 cell culture medium, inoculating into a 6-well cell culture plate in an inoculum amount of 2 ml/well, and then culturing the 6-well cell culture plate in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, (e) exposing the test substance by diluting the sample prepared in the step (a) with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to a working solution of 400 mu g/ml, removing the 6-well cell culture plate, removing the cell culture medium, adding different doses of the test solution to the cell culture plate, setting 3 replicate wells for each dose, and placing at 37 degrees C in an 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 24 hours, (f) collecting cells by taking out the cell culture plate in step (e), collecting the cell culture solution, adding together with digested cells in the same culture well into 15 ml centrifuge tube, collecting different culture wells separately, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, and discarding supernatant, (g) performing cell fixation by adding 1 ml 4 degrees C pre-cooled 70% ethanol into each centrifuge tube, gently blowing evenly, fixing at 4 degrees C for 2 hours, centrifuging at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes, discarding supernatant, adding 500 mu l 4 degrees C pre-cooled PBS buffer, resuspending the cells, centrifuging at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes, discarding supernatant, and obtaining cells, (h) staining by adding 200 mu l of dye solution prepared with PBS to each centrifuge tube in step (g), and staining for 30 minutes in dark condition, where the dye solution comprises 1 mg/ml RNase and 50 mu g/ml propidium iodide, and (i) performing flow detection and analysis by performing DNA content analysis out by flow cytometry at an excitation wavelength of 488 nm.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. AB    NOVELTY - Detecting the effect of heating non-burning (HNB) cigarette total particle phase on early apoptosis rate of cell comprises e.g. (i) using a linear smoking machine with a suction capacity of 55 ml, a duration of 3 seconds, a suction frequency of 30 seconds, continuous suction of 10 ports, a diameter of 44 mm, Cambridge filter for collection, where the mass of the flue gas trap is weighed before and after the suction, and the mass mTPM of the total HNB cigarette phase is calculated according to formula (1) mTPM is m1-m0, where m0 is the mass of the flue gas (in mg) trap before pumping, m1 is the mass of the flue gas (in mg) trap after pumping as given in the specification, after the capture is completed, collecting in Cambridge filter, then placing in 50 ml flask, adding dimethyl sulfoxide to make the concentration of the total HNB cigarette phase in dimethyl sulfoxide 40 mg/ml, ultrasonicating by using an ultrasound machine, and filtering dimethyl sulfoxide extract.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting the effect of heating non-burning cigarette total particle phase on early apoptosis rate of cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has effective-sample treatment, and has optimal setting of the detection dose and the correct selection of the target cells that enable the method to accurately detect the effect of the total particle phase of the heated non-burning cigarette on the early apoptosis rate of the cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Detecting the effect of heating non-burning (HNB) cigarette total particle phase on early apoptosis rate of cell comprises (i) using a linear smoking machine with a suction capacity of 55 ml, a duration of 3 seconds, a suction frequency of 30 seconds, continuous suction of 10 ports, a diameter of 44 mm, Cambridge filter for collection, where the mass of the flue gas trap is weighed before and after the suction, and the mass mTPM of the total HNB cigarette phase is calculated according to formula mTPM is m1-m0, where m0 is the mass of the flue gas (in mg) trap before pumping, m1 is the mass of the flue gas (in mg) trap after pumping as given in the specification, after the capture is completed, collecting in the Cambridge filter, then placing in 50 ml flask, adding dimethyl sulfoxide to make the concentration of the total HNB cigarette phase in dimethyl sulfoxide 40 mg/ml, ultrasonicating by using an ultrasound machine for 30 minutes, filtering the dimethyl sulfoxide extract by using sterile filter paper, dispensing into 1 ml cryotube, and storing at - 80 degrees C, (ii) performing resuscitation of human nasal epithelial cells Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI) 2650 medium, inoculating into 25 cm2 cell culture flask, adding 10 ml Roswell Park Memorial Institute (RPMI) 1640 medium, incubating at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator, observing the inverted confluence and morphology of the cultured cells under a microscope, when the cells were grown to a confluence of 70-90%, removing the cell culture medium in the culture flask, washing twice with phosphate buffer, discarding the washing solution, adding 1 ml 0.25 wt.% trypsin solution, incubating for 1-2 minutes, and suspending in RPMI1640 cell culture medium to form a single cell suspension, (iii) calculating the cell concentration of the human nasal epithelial cell single cell suspension by using hemocytometer counting method to obtain a viable cell number of each ml of single cell suspension, (iv) diluting the counted single cell suspension of human nasal epithelial cells to 2x 105 cells/ml with RPMI 1640 cell culture medium, inoculating with 2 ml inoculum into a six-well cell culture plate, and incubating the culture plate at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in an incubator for 24 hours, (v) diluting the sample with phosphate-buffered saline into a working solution of 600 mu g/ml, taking out the culture plate, removing the cell culture solution, adding different doses of test solution to the cells in the culture plate, setting each well with 3 duplicate wells, and incubating at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide in a incubator for 24 hours, (vi) taking out the cell culture plate, collecting the cell culture solution and the digested cells from the same culture well into the same 15 ml centrifuge tube, separately collecting the different culture wells, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, and collecting the cells, and (vii) washing the cells for 2 times with pre-cooled phosphate-buffered saline, centrifuging each time at 1000 rpm at 4 degrees C for 5 minutes, collecting the cells, adding 100 mu l dye, resuspending the cells in a binding solution, adding 5 mu l annexin-V conjugated fluorescein isothiocyanate (Annexin V-FITC) and 5 mu l propidium iodide (PI) dye, mixing, reacting in a dark condition at room temperature for 10 minutes, adding 400 mu l dye binding solution, uniformly mixing to obtain the test sample, and detecting the sample within 1 hour by using flow cytometry.

PN EP4029843-A1
TI Obtaining sustainable goods with improved            eco-balance by blending inorganic pre-polymer mixture            comprising water glass, alkali hydroxide, calcium            aluminate and water with solid additive, and optionally            subjecting composite to curing.
AU EHSAEI M H
   SPANGENBERG B
   FUTTERKNECHT S
AE AGEMOS AG (AGEM-Non-standard)
GA 202295409Q
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining sustainable goods with improved                eco-balance comprises providing an inorganic                pre-polymer mixture comprising 2.5-12.5 wt.% water                glass, 0.7-7 wt.% alkali hydroxide, 10-70 wt.%                calcium aluminate and 6.8-20 wt.% water; providing                solid additive(s); blending the inorganic mixture                with the solid additive to form a composite;                optionally subjecting the composite to a curing                operation; and shaping the composite or the cured                composite into form to obtain the final good. The                solid additive comprises: sand, coarse gravel,                quartz powder, rubber, organic polymers, wood,                fibers, fabrics, salts and mixtures; and Iron                phosphate, calcium phosphate, magnesium phosphate,                iron oxides, lead oxides, barium sulfate, magnesium                sulfate, calcium sulfate, aluminum oxide,                meta-kaolin, kaolin, inorganic pigments,                wollastonite and/or rock wool.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining sustainable                goods with improved eco-balance which is useful in                construction and manufacturing everyday items e.g.                metal, plastics, concrete, ceramics, wood, or wood                products made from wood, as a renewable resource                from nature.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite is eco-friendly, particularly                with respect to their carbon dioxide footprint,                lower water utilization, and more sustainable                characteristics. It is easy to prepare, resistant                against environmental influences such as wind,                climate, pollution, acids fire and rain. It                represents low-cost products in terms of starting                materials, by also using residues, waste materials                and so far technically unusable raw materials. It                can be easily produced at low energy levels, with                low water consumption and with almost no carbon                dioxide emissions. AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing ethanol involves milling maize                feedstock in a first ethanol production line,                subjecting to saccharification. Starches of the                milled maize are converted into maize sourced                fermentable sugars. The first ethanol production                line is separated in a second ethanol production                line, subjected to saccharification and the maize                stover feedstock is converted into fermentable                stover sourced sugars. The maize sourced sugars and                the stover sourced sugars are fed to a common                fermentation tank and subjected to fermentation to                produce ethanol and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for manufacturing ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing the agricultural                crop residue and transforming this residual to                ethanol it eliminates the need to burn off the                excess residue in the field causing air quality                concerns, and reduces production costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                facility constructed to manufacture ethanol, which                comprises a first ethanol production line                constructed and arranged to process a maize                feedstock comprising maize starch, including a                maize mill and a saccharification tank, the maize                mill adapted to reduce the particle size of the                maize and provide the reduced size maize to the                saccharification tank, which is adapted to convert                the maize starch to a maize sourced sugar and                provide the maize sourced sugar to a fermentation                tank, a second ethanol production line, separate                from the first ethanol production line, constructed                and arranged to process maize stover feedstock,                comprising a mill and a saccharification tank, the                mill adapted to reduce the size of the maize                stover, and provide the reduced size maize stover                to the saccharification tank, which is adapted to                convert the reduced size maize stover into stover                sourced sugar and provide the stover sourced sugar                to the fermentation tank, the fermentation tank                including an effective amount of a yeast component                to ferment at least some of the maize sourced sugar                and the stover sourced sugar, the fermentation tank                adapted to ferment sugar into ethanol and carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INDUSTRIAL STANDARDS - Preferred Condition: The ethanol is                  distilled to at least about 95% ethanol. The                  ethanol is purified to about 98%, ethanol, by use                  of molecular sieve. The method of manufacturing                  ethanol requires the production of steam, the                  hydrolysate feed comprises lignin and after the                  hydrolysis step, at least some lignin is removed                  from the hydrolysate feed and at least some                  removed lignin is burned on-site to satisfy some                  steam requirements for the method of                  manufacturing ethanol. The ethanol produced by                  fermentation is distilled to increase the ethanol                  percentage and the removed lignin is burned to                  supply at least some energy for the distillation                  of the ethanol. The first ethanol production line                  operates within an existing ethanol production                  facility designed to produce ethanol from corn                  and the second ethanol production line is added                  to the existing ethanol production facility. The                  facility comprises a lignin boiler, coupled to                  and located downstream from the lignin separator,                  adapted to burn the lignin separated from the                  hydrolysate and produce steam for use in the                  facility.Preferred Method: The step of prior                  saccharification involves, subjecting the milled                  maize stover to a pretreatment process step                  including heat to disrupt the fiber structure of                  the corn stover feedstock to make it more                  accessible to saccharification enzymes.Preferred Components: The yeast component is                  selected to ferment both C5 and C6 sugars in the                  same fermentation tank.

PN CN112538775-A
TI Papermaking biological pulping processing method            involves sterilizing mixtures comprising wood fibers,            mixing biological enzymes, fermenting, filtering,            decomposing lignin and pectin fructose, grinding,            adding biocatalyst, and bleaching.
AU LAI Z
   YOU S
   LAI Q
AE LIANJIANG MINGJIE INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (LIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021311375
AB    NOVELTY - Papermaking biological pulping processing                method involves adding pulping mixtures comprising                wood fibers to ozone water, crushing or slicing,                immersing in saturated ozone water solution,                draining the raw materials, adding to a puffing                machine, mixing fungi and carbohydrases, stirring,                drying at low temperature, mixing the sterilized                raw materials and biological enzymes, stirring,                fermenting, adding a portion of immersion agent                after stopping fermentation, filtering, squeezing,                dividing wood fiber-containing raw materials into                filaments by mechanical processing, decomposing                lignin and pectin fructose into carbon dioxide and                water, decomposing and thinning fibers, grinding                the refined fiber into coarse pulp, performing                high-speed centrifugation, re-fermenting, adding                the crude slurry into water, adding a biocatalyst,                reacting in a catalytic tank, grinding, bleaching                and treating for whiteness stabilization.
   USE - Papermaking biological pulping processing                method.
   ADVANTAGE - The pulping processing can be carried out                economically without affecting the                environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Papermaking biological pulping processing                method involves adding pulping mixtures comprising                wood fibers to ozone water for cleaning and                disinfection, placing in a grinder or slicer for                crushing or slicing, immersing in saturated ozone                water solution of predetermined concentration for                predetermined time, draining the raw materials,                adding to a puffing machine for cooking and                sterilization, maintaining at predetermined                temperature for predetermined time, mixing fungi                and carbohydrases, stirring, drying at low                temperature, mixing the sterilized raw materials                and biological enzymes, stirring, fermenting,                adding a portion of immersion agent after stopping                fermentation, filtering, squeezing, recovering                filtered water to fermentation broth storage tank                for reuse, dividing wood fiber-containing raw                materials into filaments by mechanical processing,                decomposing lignin and pectin fructose into carbon                dioxide and water, decomposing and thinning fibers,                grinding the refined fiber into coarse pulp,                performing high-speed centrifugation to separate                out portion of the substandard fibers,                re-fermenting, adding the crude slurry into water,                adding a biocatalyst, reacting in a catalytic tank,                grinding, bleaching the pulping mixture in a                bleaching tank for 0.4 hours to kill the                biocatalyst bacteria in the fine pulp, and treating                for whiteness stabilization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The raw material with                  wood fiber is rice straw, wheat straw, reed,                  bagasse, corn stover, asparagus or bamboo. The                  concentration of saturated ozone water solution                  is 15-25m g/L. The reacting fungi include white                  rot fungi, Phanerochaete chrysosporium and yeasts                  in ratio of 15-20:10-25:20-35. The carbohydrase                  includes 10-20% xylanase and 10-25% glucoamylase.                  The ratio of crude slurry mixture to water is                  1:3. The addition amount of biocatalyst is 0.75                  wt.%. The biocatalyst comprises 30% white rot                  fungus, 10% soft rot fungus, 60% yeast, 50%                  ligninase and 40% pectinase.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)

TI Air purifier for vehicle with function of removing fine dust, has electronic control unit which is mounted on fine dust filter structure and including power supply device for electronic control, microcontroller, sensor and blower fan.
AU JUN K M
   BYUN H
   KIM H J
   PARK B W
   KIM Y
   JUNYONG L
AE AJOU MOTOR COLLEGE RES & BUSINESS FOUND (AJOU-Non-standard)
GA 2020835270
AB    NOVELTY - The air purifier (500) has a filter housing (100) which is installed at a bottom to discharge filtered air. A fine dust filter structure (200) is detachably coupled to an upper portion of the filter housing to contain a filter that filters dust. An electronic control unit (300) is mounted on the fine dust filter structure and includes a power supply device for electronic control, a microcontroller, a sensor, and a blower fan. The filter housing is directly coupled to the electronic control unit, and is used to filter fine dust through the fine dust filter structure and then discharge filter structure to the outside.
   USE - Air purifier for vehicle with function of removing fine dust.
   ADVANTAGE - The air purifier effectively improves the quality of air in the vehicle and the atmosphere by effectively responding to deteriorating fine dust. The purifier induces ventilation to the driver of the vehicle in a timely manner by accurately detecting the amount of carbon dioxide in the air in real time and detects the carbon dioxide concentration inside the vehicle. The atmospheric sensor accurately senses temperature, air pressure, and humidity, displays them to indicate the presence of fine dust inside the vehicle, and replaces the fine dust (PM) filter. The air purifier has a function which safely and protect driver's hygiene by promptly processing timing notifications. The power supply device separately provides for fine dust removal, an atmosphere detection sensor, and a blower fan, thus reducing the installation capacity and performing the installation and washing safely and conveniently.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a control method for vehicle air purifier; and
   (2) a self-diagnosis control method for vehicle air purifier in initial driving stage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of an air purifier for vehicle.
   Filter housing (100)
   Fine dust filter structure (200)
   Electronic control unit (300)
   Air purifier (500)

TI Biogas collection and purification system, has            central controller that receives input data about gas            availability at remote sources, and controls biogas            compressors at each remote facility feed biogas and            processed gas compressors.
AU BUCKENHAM N R
   BUCKENHAM R N
   WAINEO D
AE CALIFORNIA BIOENERGY LLC (CALC-Non-standard)
   CALIFORNIA BIOENERGY LLC (CALC-Non-standard)
GA 202077663G
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a network of conduits                (11,13,15,17,18,21,30,32,34) that are configured to                convey biogas from multiple remote sources (24) of                the biogas based on a detected availability of                biogas at the respective remote source. A central                processing facility (40) receives the biogas                conveyed in the network of conduits from multiple                sources of biogas through a feed biogas compressor                of the central processing facility. The central                processing facility processes the biogas into                biomethane, and comprises a biogas hydrogen sulfide                removal stage, an activated carbon scrubber, a                carbon dioxide removal stage. A processed gas                compressor (36) is configured to compress the                biomethane, such that a central controller (70)                receives input data, including data about gas                availability at the remote sources, and the central                controller controls the biogas compressors at each                remote facility the feed biogas compressor of the                treatment facility, and the processed gas                compressor.
   USE - Biogas collection and purification                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The remote location can include a biogas                conditioning plant that not only removes hydrogen                sulfide and water but also removes carbon dioxide                thus reducing the quantity of gas that is needed to                be collected and transported to the generators and                to the centralized upgrading plant. The conduits                can be of inexpensive polymer materials that are                impervious to attack by chemicals found in biogas,                such as water and hydrogen sulfide, which has a                highly offensive odor and is readily detected. The                detector shuts down the cutoff valve when the                detected level in the atmosphere increases above a                preset threshold, thus, preventing further flow of                the gas through any leak that might have                arise.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a simplified schematic view                of the system for aggregating and processing of                biogas to electrical generation and to                methane.
   Conduits (11,13,15,17,18,21,30,32,34)
   Remote sources (24)
   Processed gas compressor (36)
   Central processing facility (40)
   Central controller (70)
   Biogas collection and purification system                (100)

PN WO2020154799-A1; CA3127671-A1; AU2020213637-A1; CN113382957-A; KR2021131346-A; EP3917878-A1; EP3917878-A4; US2022089442-A1; JP2022523703-W
TI Manufacture of carbon nanofibers for forming carbon nanofiber reinforced polymers, involves reacting light hydrocarbon stream with oxidizing agent, and converting produced hydrogen and carbon monoxide to nanofibers.
AU ZARABIAN M
   PEREIRA ALMAO P
   PEREIRA A P
   FERREIRA A P
AE CARBONOVA CORP (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBONOVA CORP (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBONOVA CORP (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBONOVA CORP (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202075178V
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of carbon nanofibers (106) involves reacting light hydrocarbon (101) stream of reactor (111) with an oxidizing agent to perform reforming reaction to produce an intermediate gas stream (103) comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, and converting the produced hydrogen and carbon monoxide selectively to carbon nanofibers which build up inside the reactor (112) and steam which exits the reactor (112).
   USE - Manufacture of carbon nanofibers used for forming carbon nanofiber reinforced polymer for forming energy storage and reinforced plastics. Uses include but are not limited to transportation vehicles, aerospace, sport equipment, wind turbine, and pressure vessel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of carbon nanofibers having high selectivity and purity and provides the lightweight vehicles having high fuel efficiency, and reduced carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) carbon nanofiber production apparatus, which comprises reactor (111) which is configured to receive light hydrocarbon stream and carbon dioxide stream and convert to produce the intermediate gas stream, and reactor (112) which is configured to convert the produced hydrogen and carbon monoxide to carbon nanofibers;
   (2) catalyst for conversion of hydrogen and carbon monoxide into carbon nanofibers and water (109), which comprises nanoparticles comprising at least one of iron, nickel, copper, zinc, cobalt, magnesium, manganese and molybdenum, which are mounted on a support;
   (3) preparation of the catalyst, which involves heat treating an iron-aluminum alloy to enable formation of aluminum oxide whiskers on surface, thereby forming support surface, impregnating the support surface with nanoparticles of transition metal oxides comprising iron, nickel, copper, zinc, cobalt, magnesium, manganese or molybdenum, and reducing the nanoparticles of transition metal oxides; and
   (4) conversion of hydrogen carbon monoxide into carbon nanofibers and water using the catalyst, which involves passing hydrogen and carbon monoxide over the catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of system for converting light hydrocarbons and oxidizing agent into water and carbon nanofibers.
   Light hydrocarbon (101)
   Oxidizing stream (102)
   Intermediate stream (103)
   Carbon nanofibers (106)
   Water (109)
   Reactors (111, 112)
   Separator (113)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The reactor (111) is configured to enable dry catalytic reforming of the hydrocarbon. The reactor (112) comprises magnetic field generator which is configured to orientate the carbon nanofibers, and catalyst, and having corrugated support surfaces, in which at least a portion of support which is positioned on the inner surface of reactor (112). The apparatus comprises separator (113) which is configured to receive and separate intermediate gas streams, into hydrogen and carbon monoxide intermediates, carbon dioxide and hydrocarbon reactants, and transmit the separated intermediates to the reactor (112) and recycle the reactants to the reactor (111).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The oxidizing agent comprises at least one of carbon dioxide, water and oxygen. The support in the reactor (112) comprises barriers of filamentous alumina whiskers which are configured to restrict motion of the nanoparticles across surface of the support. The support comprises an iron-aluminum alloy, 5% aluminum, and iron-chromium-aluminum alloy. Preferred Process: The preparation of the catalyst involves depositing a catalyst precursor on the support, heat treating and reducing the catalyst precursor with carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen diluted with an inert gas, argon, helium, and nitrogen at a temperature of 500-800 degrees C for 2-48 hours. Preferred Conditions: The reduction of nanoparticles is performed at temperature of 70-1000 degrees C for 5-48 hours. Preferred Property: The nanoparticles have diameter of 30-150 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The manufacture of carbon nanofibers further involves separating unreacted portions of carbon dioxide and light hydrocarbon from the converted intermediate gas stream, by using membrane separator, recycling the separated unreacted portions of carbon dioxide and light hydrocarbon into reactor (111), harvesting heat from reactor (112) and supplying the harvested heat to reactor (111), and passing the unreacted portions of oxidizing agent and light hydrocarbon through the reactor (112) along with the produced hydrogen and the carbon monoxide. The reaction in reactor (111) is carried out to provide hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the ratio of 0.5-1.2. Preferred Component: The hydrocarbon is methane. Preferred Conditions: The conversion process is performed in the reactor (111) at temperature of 480-850 degrees C and pressure of 5 MPa or less.

PN CN111374934-A
TI Preparing liposome-encapsulated human stem cell factor includes preparing mesenchymal stem cell culture supernatant and blank liposome suspension, adding mesenchymal stem cell culture medium, and determining liposome suspensions performance.
AU JIE Z
   CHANG L
AE HOPE BIO-TECH SUZHOU CO LTD (HOPE-Non-standard)
GA 202067543N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing liposome-encapsulated human stem cell factor comprises preparing mesenchymal stem cell culture supernatant, then preparing blank liposome suspension, adding 10 wt.% mesenchymal stem cell culture medium to the blank liposome suspension under aseptic conditions at 2-8 degrees C to prepare a liposome suspension containing mesenchymal liposome stem cells, and determining the performance of liposome suspensions.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing liposome-encapsulated human stem cell factor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the stem cells stability; has convenient storage and transportation; and can be used in the field of beauty.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) detecting the effect of the liposome suspension on the proliferation of human skin fibroblasts, or proliferation activity of cells after ultraviolet A irradiation comprising preparing the human skin fibroblasts cell suspension with a cell density of 5x 103 cells/ml with Dulbecco's Modified Eagle's /F12 medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum by volume, and injecting them into two 96-well cell culture plates, each with 100 mu l, and placing in a constant temperature incubator containing 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C for 40 hours, discarding the original culture medium, adding 100 mu l medium containing 1%, 2%, 5%, and 10% liposome suspension respectively, placing in a constant temperature incubator containing 5% carbon dioxide at 37 degrees C for 24-48 hours, then adding 10 mu l CCK-8 reagent at the end of culturing, incubating at 37 degrees C for 1.5 hours, measuring the absorbance at 450 nm wavelength on microplate reader, and comparing the results; and
   (2) detecting the effect of liposome suspension on skin damage repair after ultraviolet irradiation comprising applying 1%, 2%, 5%, and 10% liposome suspension on the skin of experimental mice irradiated by ultraviolet A continuously for 14 days, and staining the skin tissue sections with hematoxylin and eosin for determining the content of hydroxyproline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The mesenchymal stem cell culture supernatant is specifically prepared by taking human mesenchymal stem cells cryopreserved in liquid nitrogen from the third to 20th generation, placing the cryopreservation tube containing human mesenchymal stem cells in 37 degrees C water bath, rapidly thawing within 1 minute, then placing in a biological safety cabinet for culturing, taking the resuscitated human mesenchymal stem cells, using flow cytometry for identifying the antigen expression on the cell surface for determining the cell type, transferring the thawed human mesenchymal stem cells in a centrifuge tube containing pre-heated complete growth medium, centrifuging at a rate of 1000 revolutions/minute for 3-5 minutes, discarding the supernatant under aseptic conditions, resuspending the cells in complete growth medium in a culture vessel for culturing, discarding the culture medium when the cell confluence reaches 80%, washing with phosphate buffer, adding serum-free human mesenchymal stem cell culture medium for continuing culturing for 48-72 hours, and taking the serum-free supernatant. The blank liposome suspension is specifically prepared by using egg yolk lecithin, cholesterol, cultured phosphatidylethanolamine and distearoylphosphatidylcholine in a mass ratio of 0.5-1.5:1-2:0.5-2, then dissolving in diethyl ether-chloroform solvent (2-6 times), using a rotary evaporator in 35-45 degrees C water bath for heating, reducing the pressure, and spin drying for removing the organic solvent to form a film, and vacuum drying at room temperature for 12 hours to prepare blank liposomes, rotating and washing the membrane with phosphate buffer containing 0.1-2% tocopherol acetate and 0.1-0.6% caprylic/capric triglyceride, stirring for hydration, ultrasonically treating in a water bath for 5 minutes, then transferring to an ultrasonic cell crusher for further sonication for 10 minutes in a cycle sequence of 10-30 seconds at 35 degrees C to prepare a blank liposome suspension with a mass ratio of 1-20%. The method comprises using Beckman Coulter particle size analyzer for detecting the particle size of liposomes and using centrifugal ultrafiltration method for detecting the encapsulation efficiency of liposomes. The method comprises performing liposome particle size detection by using 1 ml newly prepared liposome suspension and diluting with phosphate buffer. The liposome encapsulation efficiency is detected by using liposome suspension for determining the total protein concentration, placing the liposome suspension in an ultrafiltration tube, centrifuging at a rate of 6000 revolutions/minute at 4 degrees C for 30 minutes, collecting the centrifuged bottom solution to obtain free unencapsulated components, detecting the protein concentration, then calculating the encapsulation rate according to the standard formula as given in the formula of the specification, where the W total and W not indicates that the total protein concentration and the protein concentration of free uncoated components, respectively.

TI Perovskite-type composite metal oxide oxygen            carrier useful in chemical chain methane reforming            reaction, has perovskite structure containing lanthanum            cerium iron oxide compound.
AU GONG J
   WANG Z
   ZHANG X
   SU Y
   LIU R
   XIAO H
   WANG L
   WANG Y

AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   CHINA NAT PETROLEUM CO LTD PETROCHEMICAL (CNPC-C)
GA 202022273N
AB    NOVELTY - A perovskite-type composite metal oxide oxygen                carrier has a perovskite structure containing                lanthanum cerium iron oxide compound (I).
   USE - The perovskite-type composite metal oxide                oxygen carrier is useful in a chemical chain                methane reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The perovskite-type composite metal oxide                oxygen carrier has high activity and high                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A perovskite-type composite metal oxide oxygen                carrier has a perovskite structure containing                lanthanum cerium iron oxide compound of formula:                La1-xCexFeO3 (I).
   x = 0-1.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing the perovskite-type                composite metal oxide oxygen carrier, which                involves (1) weighing 4.3300(1-x) parts mass                lanthanum(III) nitrate hexahydrate, 4.3412x parts                mass of cerium(III) nitrate hexahydrate and 4.0399                parts mass iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate and a                complexing agent according to the stoichiometric                ratio of the compound (I), dissolving in deionized                water to configure a precursor solution, where the                molar ratio of the complexing agent to the total                amount of metal ions in the precursor solution is                1:1-4:1, and (2) evaporating the precursor solution                obtained in step (1) to obtain a gel state under                stirring conditions, then putting into a constant                temperature drying oven at 100-200 degrees C for                5-24 hours, and calcining the obtained precursor                powder in a carbon dioxide atmosphere at 800-1000                degrees C for 2-6 hours; and
   (2) use of the perovskite-type composite metal                oxide oxygen carrier in a chemical chain methane                reforming reaction, which involves reacting the                perovskite-type composite metal oxide oxygen                carrier with methane under anaerobic conditions in                a reduction stage, partially oxidizing lattice                oxygen in the perovskite-type composite metal oxide                oxygen carrier methane to syngas, and reducing the                perovskite-type composite metal oxide oxygen                carrier, and reacting the reduced perovskite-type                composite metal oxide oxygen carrier with carbon                dioxide or water vapor to realize the recycling of                the catalyst in an oxidation stage, and restoring                the structure before reaction with methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lattice structures                  of the perovskite-type composite metal oxide                  oxygen carriers are all orthogonal lattices.                  Preferred Method: In the reduction stage, a mixed                  gas of methane and nitrogen is introduced, where                  the volume percentage of methane is 5-50%                  calculated based on methane, and controlling the                  volumetric space velocity at 60-6000 hour-1.                  Preferred Conditions: The stirring rate in step                  (2) is 300-600 rpm, and the evaporation                  temperature is 50-90 degrees C. The reaction                  temperature of the reduction stage and the                  oxidation stage is 500-1200 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The complexing agent                  is citric acid, ethylene glycol, glycine and/or                  urea.
   Preferred Definitions:
   x = 0.3-0.7.

PN CN110757112-A
TI Method for processing large welding gear, involves heat-treating and polishing gears after cooling, and placing polishing gears in spacious field without covering flaw detection surface, so as to easily reach detected components.
AU DONG L
AE HUBEI FUYANG MACHINERY MFG CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020140819
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves clamping a blank of gear on a vertical turning machine, installing an auxiliary stabilization device on side of main shaft, and initially processing the accuracy of internal hole and end face of gear blank by mechanical processing equipment. Multiple gear blanks are mounted on same central axis, for cutting hob into gear blank when hobbing is provided as radial cutting. The workpiece is placed in activated carburizing medium and is heated to a single-phase austenite zone at predetermined degrees. The activated carbon atoms are decomposed from carburizing medium penetrated into the surface of steel, after holding for a sufficient time, so that the high carbon is obtained on the surface, for retaining the original composition by core portion. The gears are heat-treated and polished after cooling, and are placed in a spacious field without covering the flaw detection surface, so that any components that is detected is easily reached.
   USE - Method for processing large welding gear.
   ADVANTAGE - The solder joints can be fully heated and fused during welding. The carbon dioxide can be used for welding protection during welding, so that air can be effectively prevented from oxidizing the welding point. Thus the reliability of the welding point during welding can be improved, and the qualification rate of the product after welding can be improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon steel and alloy steel are annealed with carbon content, and the metal is slowly heated to the specified temperature, and the metal is cooled with the furnace. The inner hole and end face of the heat treated gear are machined. The tooth blank is fixed with CNC machine tool, for processing the inner hole and the end face of the tooth blank with the CNC machine tool. The gear components can be aligned with the welding points, so that the carbon dioxide gas can be used to protect the welding points, when the heating temperature reaches predetermined degrees.

PT P
PN CN110642683-A
TI Preparing grinding aid component of cement grinding aid using dichloroisopropyl ether as raw material, comprises e.g. adding solid base, water and catalyst into reactor, heating and reacting with dichloroisopropyl ether and hydrolyzing.
AU ZHANG R
   JIANG Y
   PAN Y
   WANG S
   ZOU J
   SUO X
AE HUBEI XINTONGLING WANXIANG TECHNOLOGY CO (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202004057C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing grinding aid component of cement grinding aid using dichloroisopropyl ether as raw material, comprises (i) adding solid base, water and catalyst into reactor, heating at 150-250 degrees C and adding dichloroisopropyl ether (I) at a rate of 10g/minute and increasing pressure to 1.0-6.5 MPa to generating carbon dioxide and then hydrolyzing to obtain fully converted dichloroisopropyl to 1,1'-oxydipropan-2-ol (II) in presence solid base, (ii) controlling vacuum degree of reactor at -0.07 MPa, and temperature in between 100-110 degrees C and distilling off water under reduced pressure to obtain organic liquid and residual liquid of salt, and (iii) removing the salt produced during the reaction by filter press device or centrifugal device to obtain water-soluble organic liquid containing dipropylene glycol as main active ingredient.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing grinding aid component of cement grinding aid using dichloroisopropyl ether as raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The grinding aid component: reduces cost of cement grinding aid along with diethanol monoisopropanolamine or triisopropanolamine as main component.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing grinding aid component of cement grinding aid using dichloroisopropyl ether as raw material, comprises (i) adding solid base, water and catalyst into reactor, heating at 150-250 degrees C and adding dichloroisopropyl ether of formula (I) at a rate of 10g/minute and increasing pressure to 1.0-6.5 MPa to generating carbon dioxide and then hydrolyzing to obtain fully converted dichloroisopropyl to 1,1'-oxydipropan-2-ol of formula (II) in presence solid base, (ii) controlling vacuum degree of reactor at -0.07 MPa, and temperature in between 100-110 degrees C and distilling off water under reduced pressure to obtain organic liquid and residual liquid of salt, and (iii) removing the salt produced during the reaction by filter press device or centrifugal device to obtain water-soluble organic liquid containing dipropylene glycol as main active ingredient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dichloroisopropyl ether is derived from by-product of the propylene oxide industry of chlorohydrin process. The reactor is high-pressure reactor or slurry-bed three-phase reactor suitable for solid-liquid-gas three-phase reactor and the material is obtained by 316 stainless steel reactor or Hastelloy reactor. The solid base is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate or potassium carbonate. The catalyst is hexadecyl tributylphosphine bromide or tetrabutylammonium bromide quaternary ammonium salt phase transfer catalyst. The filter press device in the filtering and desalting process is intermittent pressure filter and tube filter, and centrifugal device is tube centrifuge. The water-soluble organic liquid contains more than 93% dipropylene glycol, and small amount of 2-chloropropyl-allyl ether and 2-hydroxypropyl-allyl ether are by-produced and the organic liquid belongs to polyol strong polar compounds which are used as grinding aid component of cement grinding aid. The molar ratio between dichloroisopropyl ether and solid base is 2:1-2.2:1. The addition amount of catalyst is 0.5-2%. Preferred Method: The molar ratio between dichloroisopropyl ether and water is 1:2.1-1:10; It is necessary to ensure that water is excessively present in the reaction system so that the dichloroisopropyl ether is completely converted.

TI Transport refrigeration system for regulating environment of transport container has heat exchanger in fluid communication with first and second refrigerant system section to receive first and second refrigerant, respectively.
AU PERKOVICH M J
AE CARRIER CORP (CARG-C)
   CARRIER CORP (CARG-C)
   CARRIER CORP (CARG-C)
GA 2019A8474L
AB    NOVELTY - The transport refrigeration system (200) has a container (12) with an outer wall defining at least one compartment into the container, a first refrigerant system section (202) set at the exterior location of outer wall of container, and a second refrigerant system section (204) set at least partially within the interior location of outer wall of container. The first refrigerant system section has a first refrigerant e.g. hydrofluorocarbon (HFC), hydrofluoro-olefin (HFO), and A2L routed therethrough for cooling of first refrigerant. The second refrigerant system section has a second refrigerant e.g. carbon dioxide that is different from first refrigerant routed therethrough for cooling of second refrigerant. A heat exchanger (240) is in fluid communication with the first and second refrigerant system section to receive first and second refrigerant, respectively. The first refrigerant cools the second refrigerant within the heat exchanger.
   USE - Transport refrigeration system for regulating environment of transport container.
   ADVANTAGE - Eliminates any safety risk of leakage by ensuring that the primary refrigerant system is kept outside of the compartment of container. Minimizes primary refrigerant charge and allows liquid carbon dioxide to be distributed to the evaporators since separation of the primary refrigerant from the interior of container is achieved by utilizing more desirable refrigerant directly within the container. No oil is required and simple control scheme to each evaporator with solenoids or the like is implemented. Only temperature sensors are required for each evaporator and single sensor is required for the low pressure receiver.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of regulating an environment of a transport container.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a refrigeration system for a transport refrigeration unit of the transport refrigeration system.
   Container (12)
   Transport refrigeration system (200)
   First refrigerant system section (202)
   Second refrigerant system section (204)
   Heat exchanger (240)

TI Electrodialysis water treatment device with            intermediate electrode, has anion-cation exchange            membrane that is provided between intermediate            electrode plate, positive electrode plate, and negative            electrode plate.
AU CHEN D
AE WENZHOU JIEPU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (WENZ-Non-standard)
GA 2019A51929
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a preparation device (1), a                sodium hydroxide feed pipe (2), a sulfuric acid                feed pipe (3), and a phenol discharge port (4). A                carbon dioxide delivery pipe (5) is connected to                the middle of the lower end of the preparation                device. A cooling device (7) is connected to the                lower end of a distillation tube (6). A filter                collection device (8) is provided directly below                the cooling device. A filter drain pipe (9) is                connected to the lower end of the filter collection                device. A return pipe (10) is connected between the                preparation device and the filter drain pipe. The                lower end of the return pipe is fixedly connected                with a recovery device (11). Positive and negative                electrode plates are fixedly installed on the left                and right sides of the recovery device,                respectively. An anion-cation exchange membrane is                provided between an intermediate electrode plate,                positive electrode plate, and negative electrode                plate.
   USE - Electrodialysis water treatment device with                intermediate electrode.
   ADVANTAGE - The electric field force generated by the                middle electrode plate prevents positive and                negative ions from mixing with each other, and                moves to the positive and negative electrodes                respectively under the action of the electric field                force. The anion-cation exchange membrane separates                anions and cations, and an electrolytic reaction                occurs, which not only generates deionized water,                but also forms alkaline water and acidic water. The                structure solves the secondary pollution that                wastes too much acid and salt in the existing                preparation of ethylene sodium salicylate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                overall structure of the electrodialysis water                treatment device.

   Carbon dioxide delivery pipe (5)
   Distillation tube (6)
   Cooling device (7)
   Filter collection device (8)
   Filter drain pipe (9)
   Return pipe (10)
   Recovery device (11)

PN CN110346572-A
TI Measuring homocysteine by reacting pyruvic acid, oxygen, phosphate radical and acetyl phosphate for cleaning pyruvic acid in sample, generating chromogen red quinone imine using cystathionine beta-synthetase and detecting absorbance.
AU LI F
   NI J
   CHE T
AE ZHEJIANG LITACK TRANSMISSION TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201989068G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for measuring homocysteine involves (a) reacting pyruvic acid, oxygen, phosphate radical and acetyl phosphate to obtain carbon dioxide and hydrogen peroxide and then reacting hydrogen peroxide with hydrogen peroxidase to obtain oxygen and water for cleaning the original pyruvic acid in the sample, (b) reacting serine and homocysteine using cystathionine beta -synthetase to obtain L-cystathionine and water, reacting L-cystathionine and water with cystathionine beta -lyase to obtain homocysteine, pyruvic acid and amine and reacting the pyruvic acid, oxygen, phosphate radical and acetyl phosphate to obtain carbon dioxide and hydrogen peroxide and reacting hydrogen peroxide and 4-amino-antipyrine to generate chromogen red quinone imine, detecting the absorbance at a specific wavelength, and calculating the content of homocysteine and (c) detecting the absorbance of the final reactant at the wavelength of 500-600 nm and calculating the content of homocysteine.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring homocysteine. The kit is useful for measuring homocysteine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for kit, which comprises reagent (I) and reagent (II). The reagent (I) comprises 20-120 mmol/l buffer solution, 2-20 mmol/l preservative, 0.5-10 ml/l surfactant, 1-80 g/l stabilizer, 0.05-10 mmol/l enzyme activator, 0.1-2 KU/l pyruvic oxidase, 1.0-5 KU/l peroxidase, 0.5-4 KU/l vitamin C oxidase, 0.05-2.0 mmol/l chromogen and 0.1-1 mmol/l reducing agent. The reagent (II) comprises 20-120 mmol/l buffer solution, 2-20 mmol/l preservative, 1-100 g/l stabilizer, 0.5-10 mmol/l 4-amino-antipyrine, 2-40 mmol/l serine, 5-40 KU/l cystathionine beta -synthetase and 2-30 KU/l cystathionine beta -lyase. The ratio of reagent (I) and reagent (II) is 1/1-8/1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The ratio of tested sample to reagent is controlled at 1/50-1/100. The buffer solution comprises glycine, phosphate, trimethylolaminomethane, 2-morpholineethanesulfonic acid, 3-(N-morpholinyl)-2-hydroxypropanesulfonic acid, 4-hydroxyethylpiperazine ethanesulfonic acid and 4-hydroxyethylpiperazinepropanesulfonic acid and has pH of 6.5-8.5. The preservative comprises sodium azide, ProClin-300 (RTM: Methylisothiazolinone), p-hydroxybenzoic acid, esters or their combinations. The stabilizer comprises calf serum albumin, amino acids, sugar derivatives, polyhydric alcohols, flavin adenine dinucleotide or their combinations. The surfactant comprises Tween (RTM: Polysorbate) type polyethylene glycol ether type or polyoxyethylene ether type nonionic surfactant. The enzyme activator is calcium ions or magnesium ions. The color developer is 4-aminoantipyrine, phenols and anilines. The reducing agent comprises tris (2-carboxyethyl) phosphate hydrochloride, dithiothreitol, dithioerythritol, beta -mercaptoethanol or their combinations.

   CHENG F
AE HUBEI AEROSPACE CHEM TECHNOLOGY INST (CAER-C)
GA 201934141E
AB    NOVELTY - A triaminoguanidine nitrate purity determining method involves transferring 5.00 ml triaminoguanidine nitrate sample to the conical flask, adding an excess of potassium bromate solution, oxidizing and decomposing trisaminonitrate to form nitrogen, carbon dioxide and solution A. An excess potassium iodide is added to solution A to carry out the reaction to obtain solution B. The same amount of potassium bromate solution and potassium iodide are reacted under certain acidity conditions to obtain solution C. The solution B and solution C are respectively titrated with sodium thiosulfate standard solution, added starch indicator, and the solution changes from blue to colorless and transparent at the end of the titration, recording the volume V1 of the sodium thiosulfate standard solution consumed by the titration solution B and the volume V0 of the sodium thiosulfate standard solution consumed by the titration solution C, and determining purity of triaminoguanidine nitrate is ?.
   USE - Method for determining purity of triaminoguanidine nitrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A triaminoguanidine nitrate purity determining method involves weighing triaminoguanidine nitrate sample, dissolving to a volume of 250 ml and quantitatively transferring 5.00 ml sample to the conical flask, adding an excess of potassium bromate solution, reacting at a certain acidity, oxidizing and decomposing trisaminonitrate to form nitrogen, carbon dioxide and solution A. An excess potassium iodide is added to solution A to carry out the reaction to obtain solution B. The same amount of potassium bromate solution and the same amount of potassium iodide are reacted under certain acidity conditions to obtain solution C. The solution B and solution C are respectively titrated with sodium thiosulfate standard solution, added starch indicator, and the solution changes from blue to colorless and transparent at the end of the titration, recording the volume V1 of the sodium thiosulfate standard solution consumed by the titration solution B and the volume V0 of the sodium thiosulfate standard solution consumed by the titration solution C, and determining purity of triaminoguanidine nitrate is ?.
