TI Blending essential oil comprises Jasmine essential oil, olive oil, tea oil, preservatives, cinnamon oil, black sesame oil, rose essential oil, honey and lavender essential oil.
AU 
AE WANG X (WANG-Individual)
GA 201923941Q
AB    NOVELTY - Blending essential oil comprises 50-60 pts. wt. Jasmine essential oil, 10-15 pts. wt. olive oil, 15-20 pts. wt. tea oil, 1-2 pts. wt. preservatives, 5-8 pts. wt. cinnamon oil, 15-20 pts. wt. black sesame oil, 1-15 pts. wt. rose essential oil, 1-2 pts. wt. honey and 1-5 pts. wt. lavender essential oil.
   USE - Used as blending essential oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing blending essential oil, comprising (i) picking jasmine flower buds, cooling and allowing to stand for 3-10 hours, pacing in immersion machine when flower buds bloom and releases fragrance, adding No.70 solvent oil mass ratio of 1:8-10 according to weight ratio of jasmine flowers raw material, leaching in counter-current leaching extractor, recycling and discharging leachate from leaching machine after 5-6 hours; (ii) pumping high-temperature heat-conducting oil into immersion machine interlayer after discharging all leaching liquid from leaching machine, heating solvent remaining in slag by heat-conducting oil, cooling, separating and placing in storage tank for recycling to recover solvent oil at 70-85 degrees C for 90 minutes; (iii) filtering extract, placing in sealed interlayer boiler to heat and recover No.70 solvent oil to more 80% under normal pressure at 80-85 degrees C, transferring into vacuum decompression interlayer pot, concentrating under reduced pressure until solvent is completely recovered, heating at 60 degrees C to remove water, to make extract thick and condensed and cooling to obtain jasmine extract; (iv) pumping jasmine extract into supercritical carbon dioxide fluid while slightly heating to enter extraction, extracting aromatic oil at 25-30 degrees C and 9-13 MPa, shifting into first and second separator after 4-6 hours, heating second separator to release carbon dioxide gas, where loop enters cold box circulation application, fractionating separated crude oil to obtain water, residual solvent and impurities through rectification column to obtain jasmine essential oil; and (v) mixing raw materials according to proportion of essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The blending essential oil comprises 50 pts. wt. Jasmine essential oil, 10 pts. wt. olive oil, 15 pts. wt. tea oil, 1 pts. wt. preservatives, 5 pts. wt. cinnamon oil, 15 pts. wt. black sesame oil, 1 pts. wt. rose essential oil, 1 pts. wt. honey and 1 pts. wt. lavender essential oil.

PN CN109248568-A
TI Method for preparing polyvinylamine for capturing carbon dioxide separation membrane material, involves dissolving N-vinylformamide in aqueous solution, followed by arranging reaction concentration of azobisisobutylphosphonium hydrochloride.
AU WANG Z
   XU R
   YUAN Y
   DONG S
   WANG J
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019104972
AB    NOVELTY - A polyvinylamine preparing method involves dissolving N-vinylformamide having a purity of 99% in 20-50 wt.% aqueous solution, arranging the reaction concentration of azobisisobutylphosphonium hydrochloride to 0.038-0.6 mg/L and stirring the resultant product uniformly, adding the resultant product to the reaction device, protecting the polymerization reaction by nitrogen and adding an excess of hydrochloric acid solution to fully carry out the hydrolysis reaction. The hydrolyzed polymer solution is added to excess ethanol and ethanol precipitation is carried out to obtain polyvinyl amine-hydrochloric acid. The precipitate is dissolved in deionized water to prepare 2 wt.% aqueous solution, an excess of anion exchange resin is added and the mixture is stirred to obtain 2 wt.% polyvinyl amine aqueous solution. The prepared polyvinyl amine aqueous solution is applied to the surface of polysulfone ultrafiltration membrane to obtain the finished product.
   USE - Method for preparing polyvinylamine for capturing carbon dioxide separation membrane material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing polyvinylamine with better performance, in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyvinylamine preparing method involves dissolving N-vinylformamide having a purity of 99% in 20-50 wt.% aqueous solution, arranging the reaction concentration of azobisisobutylphosphonium hydrochloride is 0.038-0.6 mg/L and stirring the resultant product uniformly, adding the resultant product to the reaction device, protecting the polymerization reaction by nitrogen and adding an excess of hydrochloric acid solution to fully carry out the hydrolysis reaction. The hydrolyzed polymer solution is added dropwise to excess ethanol and ethanol precipitation is carried out to obtain polyvinyl amine-hydrochloric acid. The precipitate is dissolved in deionized water to prepare 2 wt.% aqueous solution, an excess of anion exchange resin is added and the mixture is stirred uniformly. The solution is alkaline and suction filtered with a glass core funnel to obtain 2 wt.% polyvinyl amine aqueous solution. The prepared polyvinyl amine aqueous solution is applied to the surface of polysulfone ultrafiltration membrane to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The anion exchange resin is strongly basic styrene type anion exchange resin or strongly basic polyethylene type pyridine resin.

PN KR1937828-B1
TI Apparatus for culturing microorganism, has control unit that is controlled, so that only oxygen is supplied, when amount of oxygen contained in supplied gas does not satisfy amount of oxygen necessary for biological reaction.
AU RHU D H
   JU D J
   JUNG M K
   PARK H K
   KIM D W
AE BKT CO LTD (BKTB-Non-standard)
GA 201907160N
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) comprises a fiber yarn (110) for allowing a microorganism to adhere to fiber yarn. An inlet supplies gas (120) to one side of fiber yarn. An amount of gas required for mixing liquid phase in the bioreactor is provided with influent wastewater, organic matter or ammonia nitrogen. A determination is made as to whether or not the amount of oxygen contained in supplied gas satisfies the amount of oxygen (122) required for biological reaction. The supply of gas is controlled to be increased or the supply amount of carbon dioxide (123) is increased according to whether the amount of gas other than oxygen in supplied gas is larger than the amount of gas required for mixing, when the amount of oxygen contained in supplied gas satisfies the amount of oxygen required for biological reaction. A control unit is controlled, so that only oxygen is supplied, when the amount of oxygen contained in supplied gas does not satisfy the amount of oxygen required for biological reaction.
   USE - Apparatus for culturing microorganism.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus rapidly and massively cultivates microorganisms cultured in an anaerobic environment by optimally maintaining an anaerobic environment essential for microbial growth by using a fiber scattering organ and an anagen module. The efficiency of oxygen delivery to the microorganisms is increased to reduce the amount of air blown to supply oxygen. The reaction tank is mixed at the same time while performing the single-shaft nitrogen removal process, and the anaerobic environment required for single-shaft nitrogen removal process using microorganisms is stably secured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for culturing microorganism.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for culturing microorganism. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Apparatus for culturing microorganism (100)
   Fiber yarn (110)
   Gas (120)
   Oxygen (122)
   Carbon dioxide (123)

PN US2018362435-A1; WO2018231623-A1; EP3634932-A1; CN111032609-A
TI Forming alpha, beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid, comprises applying reaction conditions to reaction mixture comprising transition metal precursor compound comprising first ligand, second ligand, olefin, carbon dioxide, diluent and promoter.
AU IACONO P
   HLAVINKA M L
AE CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
   CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
   IACONO P (IACO-Individual)
   HLAVINKA M L (HLAV-Individual)
   CHEVRON PHILLIPS CHEM CO LP (CPCH-C)
GA 2018A1271F
AB    NOVELTY - Forming alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt, comprises: contacting transition metal precursor compound comprising at least one first ligand, optionally, at least one second ligand, olefin, carbon dioxide, diluent and promoter comprising a porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprising associated metal cations in any order to provide a reaction mixture; and applying reaction conditions to the reaction mixture to form the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt.
   USE - The process is useful for forming alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The process forms alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid at high yields, provides more facile means of separation the acrylate product from the catalyst system regenerates polyphenoxide in a reactor setting, and retains its robustness and structural integrity such that it does not degrade under the reaction conditions or regeneration conditions using sodium treatment and allows for high sodium deposition density and facile sodium site access by incoming catalyst intermediates as well as ease of regeneration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) forming alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt, comprising contacting metalalactone compound, diluent and promoter comprising porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprising associated metal cations to provide reaction mixture and applying reaction conditions to the reaction mixture to induce metalalactone elimination reaction to form the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt; and
   (2) forming porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin, comprising contacting at least one phenol compound, formaldehyde, and an aqueous base under polymerization conditions in the presence of a basic particulate template to form a templated crosslinked polyphenol resin comprising a crosslinked polyphenol resin in contact with the basic particulate template, contacting the templated crosslinked polyphenol resin with an aqueous acid under pore forming conditions to remove the basic particulate template and form a porous crosslinked polyphenol resin and contacting the porous crosslinked polyphenol resin with a metal-containing base to form a promoter comprising a porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprising associated metal cations.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal precursor compound comprises a group 8-11 transition metal. The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin of the contacting step comprises a fixed bed. The basic particulate template has a solubility in water of less than 0.25 g/l at 25 degrees C. The basic particulate template has an average particle size of 2-200 mu m. The basic particulate template comprises an alkaline earth metal carbonate, phosphate, monohydrogen phosphate, or dihydrogen phosphate. The basic particulate template comprises magnesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, strontium carbonate, tribasic calcium phosphate, calcium monohydrogen phosphate, or calcium dihydrogen phosphate. The aqueous base is an alkaline metal hydroxide, and where the aqueous acid is hydrochloric acid or hydrogen bromide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The olefin comprises ethylene (preferred), propylene, butene, pentene, hexene, heptene, octene, or styrene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin is prepared by contacting at least one phenol compound, formaldehyde, and an aqueous base under polymerization conditions in the presence of a basic particulate template to form a templated crosslinked polyphenol resin comprising a crosslinked polyphenol resin in contact with the basic particulate template, contacting the templated crosslinked polyphenol resin with an aqueous acid under pore forming conditions to remove the basic particulate template and form a porous crosslinked polyphenol resin and contacting the porous crosslinked polyphenol resin with a metal-containing base to form a promoter comprising a porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprising associated metal cations. The reaction conditions comprise contacting the reaction mixture with a metal-containing base. The metal-containing base is alkali metal or an alkaline earth metal oxide, hydroxide, alkoxide, aryloxide, amide, alkyl amide, arylamide, or carbonate. The step of contacting the transition metal precursor with the olefin and carbon dioxide is conducted using 10-1000 psig of ethylene partial pressure and/or 20-2000 psig of carbon dioxide partial pressure or the ethylene and carbon dioxide are added in a constant or a variable ethylene: carbon dioxide molar ratio of 10:1-1:10, to provide the reaction mixture. The contacting step and/or the applying step is conducted at a weight hourly space velocity (WHSV) of 0.05-50 hour-1, based on the amount of the porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin, and at 0-250 degrees C. The process further comprises isolating the alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid, or its salt, regenerating the porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin by contacting a porous crosslinked polyphenol resin that is generated from the process with a base comprising a metal cation, or by contacting a porous crosslinked polyphenol resin that is generated from the process with a metal-containing salt. Preferred Components: The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin comprises a phenoxide-formaldehyde resin, a polyhydroxidearene-formaldehyde resin, a polyhydroxidearene- and/or fluorophenoxide-formaldehyde resin. The associated metal cations are group 1, 2, 12, or 13 metal. The porous crosslinked polyphenoxide resin has an average pore diameter of 2-250 nm. The diluent comprises an aromatic hydrocarbon solvent, an ether solvent, a carbonyl-containing solvent, a halogenated aromatic hydrocarbon solvent, carbon dioxide, or an alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid or its salt. The reaction mixture comprises a metalalactone compound.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107983412-A
TI Preparation of benzyl alcohol oxidation catalyst for preparing benzaldehyde involves mixing lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework, propylene oxide, hydrochloric acid, water, and anhydrous ethanol, and adding aqueous ammonia.
AU CAI T
   HAN L
   MENG J
AE UNIV JIANGSU NORMAL (UJSN-C)
GA 201836705X
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of the catalyst involves mixing lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework, propylene oxide, hydrochloric acid, deionized water, and anhydrous ethanol to obtain clear transparent solution, adding aqueous ammonia to transparent solution, adjusting the pH value, and stirring to obtain colorless transparent solution, pouring into a mold, placing in oven, and reacting to obtain milky white lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework wet gel, adding ethanol solution to sample in mold for aging treatment, replacing solvent, adding to reactor, and drying the sample.
   USE - The catalyst is used for the preparation of benzaldehyde by catalytic oxidation of benzyl alcohol.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent catalytic performance, and is obtained by a simple process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of benzaldehyde catalyst involves (1) mixing and uniformly stirring 2 mol lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework, 20 mol propylene oxide, 1 mol hydrochloric acid, 100 mol deionized water, and 20 mol anhydrous ethanol at 45 degrees C for 60 minutes to obtain clear transparent solution, (2) adding aqueous ammonia to the clear transparent solution, adjusting the pH value of the mixed solution to 8.5, and continuously stirring at 50 degrees C to obtain colorless transparent solution, pouring the obtained lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework sol solution into a mold, placing in oven at 50 degrees C for 3 hours, and reacting at constant temperature for 3 hours to obtain milky white lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework wet gel, (3) adding ethanol solution to sample in mold for aging treatment, replacing solvent 3 times for 24 hours each time to obtain milky white lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework alcohol gel, and (4) adding milky white lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework alcohol gel to a high-pressure reactor, drying the sample using supercritical carbon dioxide drying method at 50 degrees C for 18 hours, and controlling the carbon dioxide pressure at 10 MPa to obtain lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework aerogel particle catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The lanthanum oxide-covalent organic framework nanomaterial is prepared by (1) adding 16mg lanthanum oxide, 16 mg benzidine and 1 ml anhydrous tetrahydrofuran to reactor, heating in oil bath at 50 degrees C for 30 minutes, dripping 16 mg trishydroxytribenzaldehyde into 4 ml solution at a rate of 0.4 ml/hour into the reactor, continuously reacting for 12 hours, centrifuging and separating to obtain product lanthanum oxide-polyimine, and (2) adding the obtained lanthanum oxide-polyimine and 1.5 ml mixed solvent of n-butanol and o-dichlorobenzene with a volume ratio of 1:9 to a reaction tube, passing 77k liquid nitrogen to cool, freeze-thawing three times, sealing the tube, reacting in oven at 120 degrees C for 3 days, and separating.

TI Melt-spun multifilaments used for producing            technical textiles, are obtained by reacting organic            diisocyanate(s), polyol(s) comprising polyether            carbonate polyol(s) and polyols different from            polyether carbonate polyol, and chain extenders.
AU WAMPRECHT C
   NORWIG J
   GURTLER C
   MANVI P
   SEIDE G
   GRIES T
   GUERTLER C
   MANVI P K
   WAMPRECHT CH
   NORVIG Y
   GURTLER CH
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO GERMANY AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2018216462
AB    NOVELTY - Melt-spun multifilaments based on                thermoplastic polyurethane are obtained by reacting                (a) organic diisocyanate(s), (b) polyol(s) having                at least two isocyanate reactive groups, (c) chain                extender(s) having two isocyanate reactive groups,                (d) a monofunctional chain terminator, (e)                catalysts and (f) auxiliaries and/or additives. The                component (b) comprises at least one polyether                carbonate polyol (p1) obtained via addition                reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide onto                hydrogen-functional (H-functional) starter                substances, and 0-70 wt.% polyol(s) (p2) which are                different from the polyol (p1).
   USE - Melt-spun multifilaments are used for                producing technical textiles and clothing e.g.                socks, stockings, compression textiles such as                medical bandage, surgical hose and orthopedic                elastic bandage, sport textiles and underwear (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The multifilaments have excellent tensile                strength, elongation at break and elasticity at low                temperatures, and is produced by a simple and an                efficient method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Melt-spun multifilaments based on                thermoplastic polyurethane are obtained by reacting                (a) organic diisocyanate(s), (b) polyol(s) having a                number average molar mass of 500-5000 g/mol and at                least two isocyanate reactive groups, (c) chain                extender(s) having a molar mass of 60-490 g/mol and                two isocyanate reactive groups, and optionally (d)                a monofunctional chain terminator having an                isocyanate reactive group, (e) catalysts and (f)                auxiliaries and/or additives. The component (b)                comprises at least one polyether carbonate polyol                (p1) obtained via addition reaction of carbon                dioxide and alkylene oxide onto H-functional                starter substances, and 0-70 wt.% polyol(s) (p2)                which are different from the polyol (p1). The molar                ratio of the isocyanate groups of the component (a)                to the entirety of the isocyanate reactive groups                of the components (b), (c) and optionally (d) is                0.9:1-1.2:1. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of melt-spun multifilaments.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a melt                spinning machine.
   Hopper (1)
   Extruder (2)
   Spinning head (4)
   Quenching unit (5)
   Winder (9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic                  diisocyanate is chosen from                  4,4'-methylenediphenyl diisocyanate,                  1,6-hexamethylene diisocyanate and                  4,4'-diisocyanato dicyclohexylmethane. Preferred                  Properties: The component (b) has a number                  average molar mass of preferably 1000-3000 g/mol,                  and a number average functionality of 1.9-2.1.                  The component (c) has a molar mass of preferably                  60-300 g/mol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polyether                  carbonate polyol (p1) is obtained via addition                  reaction of carbon dioxide and propylene oxide or                  mixture of propylene oxide with up to 30 wt.%                  ethylene oxide, based on the alkylene oxide                  mixture onto H-functional starter                  substances.

PN CN107629250-A; CN107629250-B
TI Preparation of stable flame retardant, involves adding water, formaldehyde and acrylamide, adding acetone, dimethyldimethoxysilane, triethoxysilane and added particles, adding sodium nitrite, adding polyglutamic acid and catalyst.
AU XIAOLIANG M
   FANG L
   MA X
   LIU F
AE XIAOLIANG M (XIAO-Individual)
   JINAN YADA NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (JINA-Non-standard)
GA 2018101586
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of stable flame retardant, involves adding water, formaldehyde and acrylamide, adjusting pH with hydrochloric acid, adding acetone, dimethyldimethoxysilane, triethoxysilane and added particles, adding sodium nitrite, adding polyglutamic acid, 10-chloro-9,10-dihydro-9-oxa-10-phosphaphenanthrene and catalyst, replacing carbon dioxide in the reactor, freeze-drying the warming material, washing the dried material with water and anhydrous ethanol, drying, smashing and collecting crushed material as stable flame retardant.
   USE - Method for preparing stable flame retardant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of stable flame retardant with high stability, improved flame retardance effect and excellent heat resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing stable flame retardant, involves (1) weighing 70-80 pts. wt. water, 45-50 pts. wt. acetone, 35-40 pts. wt. formaldehyde, 20-30 pts. wt. dimethyldimethoxysilane, 18-22 pts. wt. triethoxysilane, 12-15 pts. wt. acrylamide, 10-13 pts. wt. polyglutamic acid, 9-16 pts. wt. added particles, 6-9 pts. wt. 10-chloro-9,10-dihydro-9-oxa-10-phosphaphenanthrene, 5-11 pts. wt. calcium nitrite and 3-6 pts. wt. catalyst, (2) adding water, formaldehyde and acrylamide to reactor, adjusting pH to 4-5 with hydrochloric acid, discharging ammonia gas, maintaining the reaction at 1.2-1.6 MPa and 60-70 degrees C, preheating, (3) adding acetone, dimethyldimethoxysilane, triethoxysilane and added particles, heating at 88-96 degrees C, stirring for 2-4 hours, adding sodium nitrite, stirring, cooling to 3-5 degrees C, leaving still, (4) adding polyglutamic acid, 10-chloro-9,10-dihydro-9-oxa-10-phosphaphenanthrene and catalyst, heating at 76-92 degrees C, replacing carbon dioxide in the reactor, stirring for 5-8 hours, (5) stirring, cooling to room temperature, discharging, distilling, recycling water and acetone, collecting the residue, freeze-drying the warming material, washing the dried material with water and anhydrous ethanol, drying, smashing and collecting crushed material as stable flame retardant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The added particles are obtained by mixing kyanite and vermiculite in mass ratio of 3:2, and ball milling the obtained product. The catalyst is selected from cobalt oxide and ferric chloride.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN107603952-A
TI Isolating and culturing rat olfactory cells, by providing pentobarbital anesthesia to male Sprague Dawley rats, cutting, adding neutral enzyme and trypsin containing disodium EDTA, hydrolyzing and measuring cell count and survival rate.
AU HE G
   ZHANG Y
   JIANG M
   QI L
AE JIANGSU CHI SCI CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201808576S
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating and culturing rat olfactory cells, by soaking tissue treatment device in aqueous ethanol, selecting male Sprague Dawley rats, providing pentobarbital anesthesia, cutting portion of inner canthus skin, exposing nasal septum mucosa, adding tissue into pre-cooled phosphate buffered saline containing double antibody, cutting, adding neutral enzyme and trypsin containing disodium EDTA, hydrolyzing, centrifuging, culturing, adding the mixed medium, incubating, observing cell morphology and growth, measuring cell count and survival rate, and identifying the cells.
   USE - The method is used for isolating and culturing rat olfactory cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for isolating and culturing rat olfactory cells, involves soaking tissue treatment device in a 75% aqueous ethanol, placing in UV sterile station for 30 minutes, drying the device by placing in aseptic bench, from February to March, selecting male Sprague Dawley (SD) rats, providing pentobarbital anesthesia, soaking in 75% ethanol for 2 minutes, fixing on board, disinfecting head with ethanol, under aseptic conditions, cutting portion of inner canthus skin, exposing nasal septum mucosa, adding tissue into pre-cooled phosphate buffered saline (PBS) containing double antibody, washing with ice water to remove blood stains, cutting the tissue into 1x 1x 1 mm pieces with scissors under sterile conditions, pre-warming at 37 degrees C, adding neutral enzyme and trypsin containing disodium EDTA, respectively, hydrolyzing, blowing wind once every 10 minutes, mixing rat serum, double antibody and insulin to obtain mixed medium, stopping hydrolysis, centrifuging the filtrate at 250-350 g for 10 minutes, removing supernatant, recovering precipitate, adding into polylysine-coated flasks by artificial attachment method, culturing in incubator at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide for 1-2 hours, slowly adding the mixed medium, incubating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, changing the medium once every 2-3 days, purifying cells by differential adherent method with enzyme digestion, observing cell morphology and growth, subculturing, measuring cell count and survival rate, and identifying the cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The pre-cooled PBS has concentration of 0.01 M and pH of 7.2-7.4. Amount of the neutral enzyme is 0.15%, where the hydrolysis is performed for 1.5 hours. Amount of the trypsin is 0.15%, where the hydrolysis is performed for 35 minutes. The trypsin containing disodium EDTA comprises 0.01% disodium EDTA. The mixed medium is Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM)/F12 (1:1) comprising 10% rat serum, double antibodies containing 100 u/ml penicillin and 100 u/ml streptomycin, and 4-6 mu g/ml insulin and has pH of 7.4. The cell purification method involves performing differential adherence method and digestion purification using 0.25% trypsin for 24 hours.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN113666399-A
TI Preparing and separating calcium magnesium carbonate by capturing carbon dioxide by using water of nanofiltration, involves taking seawater into the clarifying tank, naturally settling mud in sea water in clarifying tank.
AU LIU Y
   XIONG L
   WANG R
AE HUBEI FORBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021D74332
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium magnesium carbonate by capturing carbon dioxide (CO2) by using water of nanofiltration, involves taking seawater of salinity 35 plus minus 2 % into the clarifying tank, portion of the mud in the sea water naturally is settled in the clarifying tank, the seawater after clarifying through the water pump into the first-stage flue gas absorbing device, namely carbonizing tower, carbon dioxide flue gas is injected from the bottom of the primary carbonizing tower, the flue gas is controlled to control the temperature of the seawater at 20-30degrees Celsius at the same time adding calcium (CaO) or calcium hydroxide (CaOH) solution to control the pH value of the seawater to 8- 8.5, under weak alkaline condition the CO2 in the flue gas is reacted with the calcium ion in the sea water, generating calcium carbonate precipitate.
   USE - Method for preparing and separating calcium magnesium carbonate by capturing carbon dioxide by using water of nanofiltration (claimed) providing calcium carbonate used in tooth powder, toothpaste and other cosmetic raw material, the magnesium salt, magnesium oxide in fireproof coating, printing ink, glass, toothbrush, rubber filler, industrial waste water, gastric and duodenal ulcer disease of acid generator, acid poisoning antidote, SO2 removing agent containing SO2 in waste gas, dairy cattle feed additive and oil felt of anti-sticking agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the emission of carbon dioxide in the flue gas and utilizes the carbon dioxide to prepare the calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium magnesium carbonate by capturing carbon dioxide (CO2) by using water of nanofiltration, involves taking seawater of salinity 35 plus minus 2 % into the clarifying tank, portion of the mud in the sea water naturally is settled in the clarifying tank, the seawater after clarifying through the water pump into the first-stage flue gas absorbing device, namely carbonizing tower, carbon dioxide flue gas is injected from the bottom of the primary carbonizing tower, the flue gas is controlled to control the temperature of the seawater at 20-30degrees Celsius at the same time adding calcium (CaO) or calcium hydroxide (CaOH) solution to control the pH value of the seawater to 8- 8.5, under weak alkaline condition the CO2 in the flue gas is reacted with the calcium ion in the sea water, generating calcium carbonate precipitate, plate-and-frame filter press or centrifuge is used to dehydrate the sea water containing calcium carbonate to obtain calcium carbonate and decalcic seawater with purity of 90-98 % 4, using conventional nanofiltration technology to concentrate the decalcic seawater, the seawater salinity is increased to 52.5 -105 %, the concentrated seawater is pumped into the secondary flue gas absorption device, namely the carbonization tower, then injecting carbon dioxide flue gas from the bottom of the secondary carbonization tower, controlling the amount of the flue gas to control the seawater temperature at 30-45degrees Celsius, at the same time adding CaO or CaOH solution to control the pH value of the seawater is 8-8.5, under weak alkaline condition the CO2 in the flue gas is reacted with the magnesium ion in the sea water, generating magnesium carbonate precipitate, using plate-and- frame filter press or centrifuge is used to dehydrate the sea water containing magnesium carbonate to obtain magnesium carbonate with purity of 80-90 % so as to realize the separation of calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate.
DC A60 (Additives and Compounding Agents If the usage is very restricted it may be classified under the individual polymer or process involved.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); ; E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).)

PN CN113603201-A
TI Method for processing circulating cooling water, involves adding carbon dioxide adsorbent to circulating cooling water to control pH value.
AU CHEN C
   ZHANG G
   JI J
   SUN Z
   HU F
   WANG C
AE TIANJIN WOCHUAN WATER TREATMENT ENG TECH (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0589Y

   USE - The method is useful for sintering electronic ceramic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of sintering electronic ceramic with excellent solubility and stability.

The heating furnace is provided with an air inlet and an air outlet. The air inlet is connected to the carbon dioxide storage device through a pipeline. The air inlet fan is provided with the pipeline. The air outlet is connected to a cooling device through the pipeline.

PN CN113789033-A; CN113789033-B
TI Preparation of three-dimensional graphene composite material involves fixing polyimide paper on substrate, laser inducing to produce laser-induced graphene substrates, paving polymer powder on laser-induced graphene substrate, laser-selective carbonizing, soaking in filling material, and curing.
AU WANG G
   LIU F
   LUO S
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 202200050T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of three-dimensional graphene composite material involves (1) fixing polyimide paper on substrate, laser inducing to produce laser-induced graphene (LIG) substrates, (2) paving polymer powder on the surface of the substrate LIG, (3) using carbon dioxide laser to laser-selective carbonizing the paved polymer powder substrate, forming a pre-designed single-layer graphene film, (4) paving a layer of polymer powder on the surface of the formed graphene film, (5) using carbon dioxide laser to laser-selective carbonizing the paved polymer powder substrate, forming a pre-designed single-layer graphene film, (6) repeating steps (4) and (5), obtaining three-dimensional graphene structure, (7) soaking the three-dimensional graphene structure in filling material and curing. The thickness of powder layer is calculated by using correlation coefficient and laser power. In step (2) and (4), the thickness of the powder layer is 60-80% of the growth height of graphene.
   USE - Preparation of three-dimensional graphene composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, and produces product with high efficiency and simple steps. The method does not require the use of chemical reagents, additional processing technology assistance or processing in a special atmosphere, and achieves rapid prototyping. In this method, the structure of the prepared three-dimensional graphene composite material is controllable, and the printing of multi-scale complex three-dimensional graphene structure can be realized, and the obtained composite material has excellent comprehensive properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of three-dimensional graphene composite material involves (1) fixing polyimide paper on substrate, laser inducing to produce LIG substrates, (2) paving polymer powder on the surface of the substrate LIG, (3) using carbon dioxide laser to laser-selective carbonizing the paved polymer powder substrate, forming a pre-designed single-layer graphene film, (4) paving a layer of polymer powder on the surface of the formed graphene film, (5) using carbon dioxide laser to laser-selective carbonizing the paved polymer powder substrate, forming a pre-designed single-layer graphene film, (6) repeating steps (4) and (5) until printing is completed, obtaining three-dimensional graphene structure, (7) soaking the three-dimensional graphene structure in filling material, and curing. The thickness of powder layer (L) is calculated by using formula: a(93.5x-15.5), where a is correlation coefficient which is 60-80%, x is laser power (W), and L is thickness of the powder layer (mum). In step (2) and (4), the thickness of the powder layer is 60-80% of the growth height of graphene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The filling material is thermosetting resin, thermoplastic resin or rubber. The thermosetting resin is epoxy resin. The rubber is Ecoflex (RTM: poly(butylene adipate-co-terephthalate)) or polydimethylsiloxane. The polymer powder is thermoplastic powder, preferably polyimide, polyphenylene sulfide, polyetherimide or polyetheretherketone powder. Preferred Property: The particle size of the polymer powder is 10 mum or less.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The filling material further comprises diluent comprising acetone, isopropanol or ethyl acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The substrate is graphene film produced by laser-induced polyimide paper. Preferred Conditions: In the laser-selective carbonization, the laser focal length is 33.1-43.1 mm, scanning speed is 2.54-203.2 mm/second, printing resolution of 10-1000 ppi/inch, and laser power is 0.25-25 W. The laser selective carbonization uses single induction under ambient atmospheric conditions at room temperature. The soaking process is performed at room temperature for 30 minutes-6 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Demister (110) comprises laminates each                including a first layer in which linear structures                having the axial direction aligned with a first                direction are arranged in parallel to a second                direction orthogonal to the first direction, and a                second layer in which linear structures having the                axial direction aligned with a direction different                from the first direction are arranged in parallel                in a direction orthogonal to the axial direction,                where the laminates are stacked in a direction                orthogonal to both the first and second                directions.

   ADVANTAGE - The demister and the absorbing tower enable                efficient collection of mist. The demister has a                pressure loss which is close to the design value,                and mist removal rate which satisfies the target.                The demister ensures ease of design and ease of                manufacture, suppresses fluctuations in pressure                loss during use, and performs stable operation. The                demister can reduce its thickness by increasing the                pressure loss per unit thickness, and provides                carbon dioxide recovery device to realize the                required mist removal rate while reducing the                pressure loss in the demister. The demister                realizes the mist removal rate that is the target                at the time of design can be designed and                manufactured with high accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an absorption liquid absorbing tower                comprising an absorption tower body to which the                gas containing carbon dioxide is supplied, an                absorption liquid supply unit that supplies the                absorption liquid to the absorption tower body, and                the above-mentioned demister arranged on the                downstream side in the gas flow direction from the                absorption liquid supply position of the absorption                liquid supply portion of the absorption tower main                body; and
   (2) a demister production method, involving                (a) setting the mist removal rate, pressure loss,                thickness, and discharge of collected mist, (b)                based on the set mist removal rate, pressure loss,                thickness, and discharge of collected mist,                determining the number of stacked grid structures,                wire diameter, wire spacing, and amount of                misalignment between layers, and (c) laminating                structures and manufacturing a demister based on a                set number of stacks, a wire diameter, a line                spacing, and an amount of deviation between                layers.

AB    NOVELTY - Transplantation method of sugar-free and                strong seedling Rehmannia glutinosa, comprises (i)                performing Rehmannia Glutinosa detoxification                tissue culture in test tubes, multiplication of                virus-free vaccines, selecting virus-free                seedlings, cutting old roots, transplanting into a                sugar-free culture box containing vermiculite,                administering nutrient solution, covering lid, (ii)                introducing it on culture rack in a closed room,                accessing carbon dioxide into culture box, opening                vent of incubator, increasing carbon dioxide amount                gradually on dependence of incubation period, (iii)                illuminating intense light, maintaining light                intensity and illuminating light, (iv) replenishing                nutrient solution according to growth status of                virus-free vaccines, (v) spraying water according                to the wetness and dryness of vermiculite on third                day and, and (vi) taking out tissue culture                seedlings from culture box and transplanting into                soil in greenhouse.
   USE - Used as transplantation method of sugar-free                and strong seedling Rehmannia glutinosa.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and                environmentally friendly, dos not requires                sterilization, has 97.1% survival rate and utilizes                nutrient solution satisfying the nutrients needed                for tissue culture seedling growth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Transplantation method of sugar-free and                strong seedling Rehmannia glutinosa comprises (i)                performing Rehmannia Glutinosa detoxification                tissue culture in test tubes, performing                multiplication of virus-free vaccines, selecting                virus-free seedlings that grow for 35 days, cutting                old roots into 3cm stem segments, transplanting                into a sugar-free culture box containing                vermiculite, each box comprising 670 g vermiculite,                pouring 800 ml nutrient solution, 80                transplants/box, covering lid and closing the gap                between lid and box body with a sealing film, where                nutrient solution comprises 2 g/l calcium ammonium                nitrate and 170 mg/l monopotassium phosphate and                deionized water(remaining amount), and (ii)                introducing it on culture rack in a closed room,                accessing carbon dioxide into culture box, opening                vent of incubator, increasing carbon dioxide amount                gradually on dependence of incubation period, (iii)                maintaining darkness for the first 2 days of                culture period, illuminating the 80 mu mol/m2/                intensed light for 3 days, maintaining light                intensity to 160 mu mol/m2/seconds on the 8th day                and illuminating light for 12 hours/day, (iv)                replenishing nutrient solution according to growth                status of virus-free vaccines, (v) opening the lid                after 20 days in culture box, under natural                conditions with a light intensity of 80 mu                mol/m2/seconds, spraying water according to the                wetness and dryness of vermiculite on the third day                and moving day 5 to greenhouse, and (vi) taking out                the tissue culture seedlings from culture box and                transplanting into soil in greenhouse
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In step(ii), the highest                  value of carbon dioxide concentration in closed                  room is 1300 PPm, and lowest value is 800 PPmIn                  step (ii), the carbon dioxide gas supply is                  stopped for first 4 days of culture period, the                  carbon dioxide gas supply from 5th day is                  200-500ml/minutes, the carbon dioxide gas supply                  from 10 th day is 1800 ml/minutes and the time of                  supplying carbon dioxide is same as time of                  giving light. In step ( vi), the soil humidity in                  the greenhouse is 13-14%, and environmental                  humidity is more than 75%. In step (vi), the                  tissue culture seedlings are transplanted to                  greenhouse soil, the tissue culture seedlings are                  transplanted to the soil of the greenhouse and                  building small plastic arch shed. the temperature                  of greenhouse is more than 32 degrees C and hence                  covered with 50% of the shading nets.The                  perforation of small plastic arch shed is carried                  out 5-7 days after transplanting to greenhouse                  soil, the average 3 round holes with a diameter                  of 1 cm/square meter and gradually increasing the                  round hole.
   ACTIVITY - Fertlizer.

AB    NOVELTY - A refrigerating cycle apparatus (1) comprises a refrigerant circuit (10) comprising a compressor (21), a condenser (23), a pressure-reduction unit (24) and an evaporator (31), and a refrigerant comprising 1,2-difluoroethylene sealed in the refrigerant circuit. The refrigerant is a refrigerant (r1), a refrigerant (r2), a refrigerant (r3), a refrigerant (r4), a refrigerant (r5), a refrigerant (r6), or a refrigerant (r7). The refrigerant (r1) is carbon dioxide, trans-1,2-difluoroethylene (HFO-1132 (E)), difluoromethane (R32) and 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoro-1-propene (R1234yf). The refrigerant (r2) is cis-1,2-difluoroethylene (HFO-1132 (Z)) and 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (HFO-1234yf). The refrigerant (r3) is trans-1,2-difluoroethylene (HFO-1132 (E)), difluoromethane (HFC-32) and 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (HFO-1234yf). The refrigerant (r4) is HFO-1132 (E), HFO-1123 and HFO-1234yf. The refrigerant (r5) is HFO-1132 (E) and HFO-1234yf.
   USE - Refrigerating cycle apparatus is used for air-conditioner of motor vehicle. Uses include but are not limited to gasoline-powered car, hybrid vehicle, electric vehicle, and hydrogen-fueled car.
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerating cycle apparatus has improved efficiency. The refrigerant used in the apparatus has low global warming potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A refrigerating cycle apparatus (1) comprises a refrigerant circuit (10) comprising a compressor (21), a condenser (23), a pressure-reduction unit (24) and an evaporator (31), and a refrigerant comprising 1,2-difluoroethylene sealed in the refrigerant circuit. The refrigerant is a refrigerant (r1), a refrigerant (r2), a refrigerant (r3), a refrigerant (r4), a refrigerant (r5), a refrigerant (r6), or a refrigerant (r7). The refrigerant (r1) is carbon dioxide, trans-1,2-difluoroethylene (HFO-1132 (E)), difluoromethane (R32) and 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoro-1-propene (R1234yf). The refrigerant (r2) is cis-1,2-difluoroethylene (HFO-1132 (Z)) and 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (HFO-1234yf). The refrigerant (r3) is trans-1,2-difluoroethylene (HFO-1132 (E)), difluoromethane (HFC-32) and 2,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene (HFO-1234yf). The refrigerant (r4) is HFO-1132 (E), HFO-1123 and HFO-1234yf. The refrigerant (r5) is HFO-1132 (E) and HFO-1234yf. The refrigerant (r6) is at least one of HFC-32, HFO-1234yf, and 1,1-difluoroethylene (HFO-1132a) and tetrafluoroethylene (FO-1114). The refrigerant (r7) is difluoromethane (R32), carbon dioxide (CO2), pentafluoroethane (R125), 1,1,1,2-tetrafluoroethane (R134a), and 2,3,3,3- tetrafluoropropene (R1234yf).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of the refrigerant circuit.

PT P
PN CN110639358-A; CN110639358-B
TI Chemical coupling biological synchronous removal process of volatile organic compounds (VOCs), sulfides and ammonia sulfur in waste gas includes absorbing hydrogen sulfide and ammonia and converting into carbon dioxide and nitrogen.
AU WANG X
   ZHANG T
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202007565X
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical coupling biological synchronous removal process of volatile organic compounds (VOCs), sulfides and ammonia sulfur in waste gas comprises absorbing hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and soluble VOCs in waste gas by absorption tower, absorbing to produce sodium sulfide, ammonium sulfide, ammonia water and organic matters, pumping to micro oxygen bioreactor, oxidizing sulfide to elemental sulfur, converting organic matter and ammonia into carbon dioxide and nitrogen, sending gas to biofilter reactor, adsorbing by filler, adsorbing pollutants, dissolving in liquid phase aqueous solution, adsorbing pollutants, diffusing to biofilm, capturing, absorbing by microorganisms in biofilm, using pollutants entering microbial cells by microorganisms as nutrients, oxidizing, decomposing, treating mixed solution containing elemental sulfur produced in the bioreactor by sulfur recovery system, returning alkaline solution produced after sulfur separation to absorption tower for recycling.
   USE - The method is for chemical coupling biological synchronous removal process of volatile organic compounds (VOCs), sulfides and ammonia sulfur in waste gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has reasonable process and no secondary pollution, reduces electronic donor needed in denitrification process and saves operation cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical coupling biological synchronous removal process of volatile organic compounds (VOCs), sulfides and ammonia sulfur in waste gas comprises absorbing hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and soluble VOCs in waste gas by absorption tower, absorbing hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and soluble VOCs in waste gas by absorption solution containing micro alkaline solution in the absorption tower to produce sodium sulfide, ammonium sulfide, ammonia water and organic matters, pumping absorption liquid containing sodium sulfide, ammonium sulfide, ammonia water and organic matters to micro oxygen bioreactor, oxidizing sulfide to elemental sulfur by the joint action of sulfur oxidizing bacteria, ammonia oxidizing bacteria, denitrifying bacteria and sulfur autotrophic denitrifying bacteria, converting organic matter and ammonia into carbon dioxide and nitrogen for discharge, sending gas to biofilter reactor, adsorbing residual hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and VOCs in waste gas by filler containing functional microorganisms in biofilter, adsorbing pollutants, dissolving in liquid phase aqueous solution, adsorbing pollutants, diffusing to biofilm, capturing, absorbing by microorganisms in biofilm, using pollutants entering microbial cells by microorganisms as nutrients, oxidizing, decomposing into harmless products e.g. carbon dioxide, water and minerals to remove hydrogen sulfide, ammonia and VOCs, treating mixed solution containing elemental sulfur produced in the bioreactor by sulfur recovery system to obtain sulfur with high content for recycling, returning alkaline solution produced after sulfur separation to absorption tower for recycling and adjusting pH, temperature, residence time and dissolved oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred components: The alkaline absorption solution is sodium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate and/or sodium carbonate. The biofilter was filled with mixture of pine bark, peanut shell, dead leaves, sawdust and volcanic rock. The nitrogen source is ammonia absorption to produce ammonia salt. The phosphorus source is dihydrogen phosphate or dihydrogen phosphate. The carbon source of microorganism in biofilter reactor is VOCs in biogas to produce organic matter.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN109954507-A; CN109954507-B
TI Composition catalyst useful in a carbon dioxide            hydrogenation to methane reaction, comprises transition            a metal nickel and noble metal rhodium, which are            co-supported to alpha-beta-MoXC.
AU MA B
   YANG X
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
   UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 201959919P
AB    NOVELTY - Composition catalyst comprises transition a                metal nickel and noble metal rhodium, which are                co-supported to alpha - beta -MoXC (X = 1-2), where                the carrier alpha - beta -MoXC is a coexisting                phase of alpha -phase molybdenum carbide and beta                -phase molybdenum carbide, the added amount of Ni                auxiliary agent in the composition catalyst is 1-10                wt.% (preferably 3 wt.%) and the added amount of                rhodium auxiliary agent is 0.1-5% wt.% (preferably                1 wt.%).
   USE - The composition catalyst is useful in a carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to methane reaction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition catalyst: has high carbon                dioxide hydrogenation methane activite at the                optimum loading, has a methane selectivity of 86%,                which is 16% higher than the pure coexisting phase                alpha - beta MoXC; exhibits excellent catalytic                performance; has cheap and simple preparation and                modification method, has uniform controllable                morphology, and good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the composition catalyst, comprising (i)                providing the carrier coexisting phase alpha - beta                -MoXC (X=1-2) is ammonium molybdate, ammonium                metamolybdate is a source of molybdenum, aniline,                ethylenediamine, hexadecylamines are carbon                sources, and hydrochloric acid is a precipitant,                where alpha - beta -MoXC nanowires are prepared by                coprecipitation (length 100-250 nm, diameter                2-10nm), (ii) pretreating the surface of alpha -                beta MoXC (X=1-2) nanowire, the specific                pre-processing steps are: dispersing alpha beta                -MoXC in a solvent, performing ultrasonic treatment                for 1-3 hours, washing with water, washing with                absolute ethanol, then drying in an oven at 100-150                degrees C for 6-24 hours and take out the grinding                material, (iii) pretreating the surface of alpha -                beta -MoXC (X=1-2) and uniformly loading with Ni                and Rh auxiliary by immersion precipitation method,                where the specific process for preparation are: a                mixed solution of a nickel precursor and a                ruthenium precursor is disposed according to a                required Ni-Rh loading amount, dispersing the                surface-pretreated alpha - beta -MoXC in a nickel                ruthenium precursor mixed solution, after repeated                ultrasonic-stirring-stationary process, uniformly                dispersing Ni and Rh ions on the outer surface of                the alpha - beta -MoXC nanowire, where the                ultrasonic frequency is 40-60 KHz, the stirring                speed is 600-1200 rpm, and the obtained dispersion                is dried in a 50-100 degrees C water bath,                grinding, and carrying out hydrogen reduction; and                #use of composition catalyst in a carbon dioxide                hydrogenation to methane reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of                  alpha -phase molybdenum carbide and beta -phase                  molybdenum carbide in the alpha - beta -MoXC                  (X=1-2) and carrier is 6:1.5-1:5.8, the carrier                  alpha - beta -MoXC is a nanowire having a length                  of 100-250 nm and a diameter of 2-10 nm. The                  precursor of Ni is nickel sulfate, nickel                  nitrate, nickel chloride, nickel hydroxide, the                  precursor of Rh is rhodium chloride, rhodium                  chloride acid ammonium, carbonyl rhodium, and                  rhodium iodide. Preferred Method: The preparation                  method comprises (i) dissolving ammonium                  molybdate in distilled water and stirring to add                  aniline, ethylenediamine, hexadecylamine, adding                  1-2 mol/l hydrogen chloride solution to above                  solution to pH is 4-5, then moving to 60-100                  degrees C water bath, stirring for 5-12 hours,                  washing with water, washing with ethanol, suction                  filtration, drying, then preparing alpha - beta                  -MoXC precursor; (ii) grinding the sample after                  drying in step (i) into a quartz boat and                  transferring to a tube furnace at a volume                  concentration of 5-10% hydrogen/nitrogen                  atmosphere, and performing calcination at the                  target temperature, where the calcination time is                  3-6 hours, temperature is 740-850 degrees C, and                  finally passivation is 1-5 hours in volume                  concentration at 1-5% oxygen/nitrogen obtaining                  alpha beta -MoXC (X = 1-2) catalyst; (iii)                  simultaneously supporting the transition metal Ni                  and the noble metal Rh on the noble metal                  catalyst alpha - beta -MoXC, the surface of the                  alpha beta -MoXC after pretreatment is loaded                  with different contents of Ni and Rh additives by                  immersion precipitation method, as needed, the Ni                  and Rh loadings are mixed with a nickel precursor                  and a ruthenium precursor, the alpha - beta -MoXC                  nanowire obtained in step (ii) is                  surface-pretreated and dispersed in a nickel                  ruthenium precursor mixed solution, after                  repeated ultrasonic-stirring the process of                  slowing to allows the Ni-Rh ions to be uniformly                  dispersed on the outer surface of the alpha beta                  -MoXC, and the obtained dispersion is dried in a                  50-100 degrees C water bath, and grinding, and                  carrying out hydrogen-reduction (350-500 degrees                  C). The molar ratio of alpha -phase molybdenum                  carbide to the beta -phase molybdenum carbide in                  alpha beta -MoXC (X = 1-2) carrier is                  6:1.5-1:5.8. In the method, the repeated                  ultrasonic-stirring-stationary process includes                  ultrasound after 5-30 minutes, the ultrasonic                  frequency is 40-60 KHz, stirring the dispersion                  for 5-30 minutes with mechanical stirring, the                  stirring speed is 600-1200 rpm, then allowing to                  stand for 20-60 minutes, then proceed to the next                  cycle of ultrasound, and repeating for 3-5 times.                  The reaction of hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                  to methane is carried out on a pressurized fixed                  bed continuous flow reactor, the condition for                  hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methane is:                  the reaction pressure is 0.5-2 Mpa, the reaction                  temperature is 400-600 degrees C, the space                  velocity is 6000-8000 mg g1 h1, and the                  n(CO2):n(H2) molar ratio is 1-4. The catalyst is                  tableted and screened for 20-40 meshes. The                  catalyst is used after reduction, and the                  reduction condition of the catalyst is: reduction                  with pure hydrogen at 300-400 degrees C for 1-3                  hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN109863996-A
TI Performing a sugar-free tissue culture method for increasing the rooting rate of plants involves preparing a culture box and a bottle seedling, where the culture box is provided with one or more venting holes.
AU WANG Z
   GUO Y
   JIN H
   ZHENG Y
   SUN B
   SUN W
   GAO T
   CHENG N
AE SHAANXI HAITANG ECOLOGICAL AGRIC & FORESTRY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 201955457Q
AB    NOVELTY - Performing a sugar-free tissue culture method for increasing the rooting rate of plants involves preparing a culture box and a bottle seedling, where the culture box is provided with one or more venting holes. Then add water to the vermiculite and mix well, then bag, and autoclave to form a matrix. The add a special bacteriostatic agent for plant tissue culture in 1/2 MS medium containing no organic matter, and dispense into a bottle, and autoclave to prepare a culture solution. Then dispense the matrix into the culture box, pour the culture solution, and require the culture solution to not submerge the matrix. The seedlings are cut into pieces and inoculated into the culture box, place the culture box in a sugar-free culture chamber and incubate on the first day, by sealing the venting holes with plastic wrap and culturing in the dark.
   USE - Method for performing a sugar-free tissue culture method for increasing the rooting rate of plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Then from the second day, the light intensity is 3800-4200lux, the temperature is 25 plus minus 2 degrees C, the light period is 14 plus minus 2hours in the light culture stage, and the rest is dark. From the 3rd day, remove the plastic wrap and leave the rest of the conditions unchanged. From the 5th day, the light intensity is 5100-5400lux, the temperature is constant, and the illumination period is unchanged. From the 10th day, the light intensity is 6400-6500lux, the temperature is constant, and the illumination period is unchanged and began to pass carbon dioxide gas into the sugar-free culture chamber, maintain the indoor carbon dioxide concentration of 800-1500 parts per million and insert a trachea into the culture box, and introduce carbon dioxide gas into each culture box at a gas flow rate of 300 plus minus 100 ml/min in the light culture stage, and ventilating for 15 min plus minus 5 min per hour. From the 25th day, in the light culture stage, carbon dioxide gas is introduced into each culture box at a gas flow rate of 1000 plus minus 100 ml/min, and the air is ventilated for 30 min plus minus 5 min per hour, and the rest of the conditions are unchanged. The tissue culture seedlings in the culture box are successively transferred to the refinery greenhouse. In the refining greenhouse, the tissue culture seedlings are transplanted into the tray or the degradation bag for refining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Compositions: It is also included to add IAA to the culture medium at a standard of 0.5-1.0 mg/L. The plant tissue culture-specific bacteriostatic agent is added in a standard of 0.3-1.0 g/L. The amount of the culture solution added to the culture cartridge is converted in the ratio of 900-1100 ml of the culture solution is added per 400-450 g of vermiculite. The autoclaving is specifically carried out in a high-pressure steam sterilization pot at 121 plus minus 3 degrees C for 20 plus minus 5 min.Preferred Conditions: The particle size of the vermiculite is 3-8 mm.
DC C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); T04 (Computer Peripheral Equipment)
MC C04-F08; C11-A04A; D05-H08B; A11-C07; A12-W04; P13-B01; T04-K03D

PT P
PN US10213730-B1; US2019060824-A1; WO2019040332-A1; KR2020041364-A; CN111032193-A; EP3648870-A1; SG11202000963-A1; JP2020531262-W; CN111032193-B; JP6913822-B2; EP3648870-B1; SG11202000963-B
TI System for treating acid gas from sour gas stream has acid gas treatment unit to treat acid gas stream having hydrogen sulfide, oxidizer reactor to form calcium sulfate and oxygen-lean air stream, and calciner reactor to form calcium oxide.
AU YOUNES M
   HARALE A
   MUSAWI M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2019187796
AB    NOVELTY - A system (200) comprises acid gas treatment unit (202) which is operable to treat acid gas stream (228) comprising hydrogen sulfide and comprises fuel reactor (204) operable to receive acid gas stream and calcium oxide, where fuel reactor is further operable to react hydrogen sulfide with calcium oxide to produce calcium sulfide (232) and 1st gas stream comprising water vapor and carbon dioxide; oxidizer reactor (206) operable to receive calcium sulfide and 1st air stream (242) and to produce calcium sulfate (246) and oxygen-lean 2nd air stream (244), where oxygen-lean air stream comprises not greater than 15 wt.% oxygen; and calciner reactor (208) operable to receive oxygen-lean 2nd air stream and calcium carbonate (248) and to produce calcium oxide and 2nd gas stream comprising carbon dioxide.
   USE - A system for treating acid gas from sour gas stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system and method provide for efficient and economical recovery of sulfur from acid gas without the use of thermal oxidizer and a fuel-to-thermal energy conversion process for acid gas that does not require sweetening the gas before combustion. Moreover, the system and method have an improved operating flexibility as compared to exiting and alternative processes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following
   1) a method for treating acid gas from sour gas stream (220) which involves receiving acid gas stream comprising hydrogen sulfide and calcium oxide at a fuel reactor of acid gas treatment unit, operating fuel reactor to react hydrogen sulfide with calcium oxide to produce calcium sulfide and 1st gas stream comprising water vapor and carbon dioxide, operating oxidizer reactor of acid gas treatment unit to receive calcium sulfide and 1st air stream to produce calcium sulfate and oxygen-lean 2nd air stream, where oxygen-lean air stream comprises not greater than 15 wt.% oxygen, and operating calciner reactor to receive oxygen-lean air stream and calcium carbonate and produce calcium oxide and 2nd gas stream comprising carbon dioxide.
   2) a system comprising an acid gas treatment unit operable to treat an acid gas stream comprising hydrogen sulfide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a system for processing acid gas in a sour gas stream.
   System (200)
   Acid gas treatment unit (202)
   Fuel reactor (204)
   Oxidizer reactor (206)
   Calciner reactor (208)
   Sour gas stream (220)
   Acid gas stream (228)
   Calcium sulfide stream (232)
   Air stream (242)
   Oxygen-lean air stream (244)
   Calcium sulfate stream (246)
   Calcium carbonate stream (248)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Devices: The system comprises greater than or equal to 1 heat exchangers operable to receive 1st gas stream, 2nd gas stream, and/or oxygen-lean 2nd air stream, and to produce steam using heat from 1st gas stream, 2nd gas stream, and/or oxygen-lean 2nd air stream. Heat exchangers are further operable to heat 1st air stream using heat from 1st gas stream and/or 2nd gas stream. Heat exchangers are further operable to heat acid gas stream before fuel reactor receives acid gas stream. The system comprises power generation unit operable to receive steam from greater than or equal to 1 heat exchangers and produce power and reduced pressure steam. Reduced pressure steam is provided to regeneration column of acid gas removal unit. The system comprises acid gas removal unit operable to receive sour gas stream and produce acid gas stream, where acid gas removal unit comprises absorption column and regeneration column. The system comprises greater than or equal to 1 heat exchangers operable to transfer heat from oxidizer reactor to regeneration column. The system comprises selective membrane module disposed between sour gas stream and acid gas removal unit operable to produce acid gas stream, where selective membrane is operable to produce permeate stream having increased concentration of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide as compared to sour gas stream and retentate stream having reduced concentration of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide as compared to sour gas stream. Oxidizer reactor comprises fast riser reactor. Calciner reactor comprises fluidized bed reactor. Preferred Processes: Permeate stream is mixed with the acid gas stream before being received by the fuel reactor and the acid gas removal unit is operable to receive the retentate stream. The method comprises operating greater than or equal to 1 heat exchangers to receive 1st gas stream, 2nd gas stream, and/or oxygen-lean 2nd air stream to produce steam using heat from 1st gas stream, 2nd gas stream, and/or oxygen-lean 2nd air stream. The method comprises operating greater than or equal to 1 heat exchangers to heat acid gas stream before receiving acid gas stream and calcium oxide at the fuel reactor of the acid gas treatment unit. The method includes operating power generation unit to receive the steam from heat exchangers and produce power and reduced pressure steam. The method comprises providing reduced pressure steam to a regeneration column of an acid gas removal unit. Acid gas stream is received from an acid gas removal unit. The method further includes operating selective membrane module disposed between sour gas stream and acid gas removal unit to produce permeate stream having increased concentration of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide as compared to sour gas stream and retentate stream having reduced concentration of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide as compared to the sour gas stream. The method includes mixing the permeate stream with the acid gas stream before being received by the fuel reactor. The method comprises removing the water vapor from the 1st gas stream to produce carbon dioxide stream when the acid gas stream does not include hydrocarbons. The method comprises operating the oxidizer reactor of the acid gas treatment unit to receive the 1st gas stream when the 1st gas stream comprises an amount of carbon monoxide below a 1st threshold amount and an amount of hydrogen below a 2nd threshold amount. Preferred Conditions: Operating the fuel reactor comprises operating the fuel reactor at a temperature of 600-900 degrees C, preferably 800-900 degrees C. Operating the oxidizer reactor comprises operating the oxidizer reactor at a temperature of 800-1100 degrees C, preferably 850-1100 degrees C. Operating the calciner reactor comprises operating the oxidizer reactor at a temperature of 800-1000 degrees C.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
