PT P
PN US2019016603-A1; US10829384-B2
TI Oxycombustion with thermally integrated ammonia synthesis involves combining nitrogen stream and hydrogen feed stream, condensing crude ammonia stream, separating ammonia, combining fuel with oxygen stream, and combusting fuel mixture.
AU YOUNES M
   NIASS T
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201907107T
AB    NOVELTY - Oxycombustion with thermally integrated ammonia synthesis is performed by separating air feed stream with air separation unit into oxygen stream and nitrogen stream; flowing oxygen stream to oxycombustion reactor; flowing nitrogen stream to ammonia synthesis unit; combining nitrogen stream with hydrogen feed stream to synthesize ammonia and form crude ammonia stream containing ammonia and, optionally, unreacted nitrogen or hydrogen; condensing a portion of crude ammonia stream; separating ammonia in crude ammonia stream from any unreacted nitrogen or hydrogen to form purified ammonia stream; combining fuel from fuel stream with oxygen stream to form fuel mixture; and combusting fuel mixture to generate combustion heat; and transferring thermal energy through thermal integrations while fuel mixture is being combusted and ammonia is being synthesized.
   USE - A method for oxycombustion with thermally integrated ammonia synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods make efficient use not only of nitrogen and oxygen components of air to simultaneously feed ammonia synthesis, oxycombustion, and, optionally, urea synthesis, but also use the ammonia and/or urea to clean flue gasses that result from the oxycombustion. When ammonia and/or urea are generated in an integrated system, it is not necessary to procure the ammonia and/or urea from external sources when they are required or desired for use in scrubbing flue gas and removing pollutants from the flue gas. Additionally, the methods make efficient use of thermal energy through greater than or equal to 1 thermal integration in the oxycombustion systems, such as reactor thermal linkage of ammonia synthesis unit with oxycombustion reactor and/or separator thermal linkage of air separation unit with ammonia separation unit. Thermal integrations in this manner can eliminate needs in the oxycombustion systems for additional components, such as refrigeration systems, to condense ammonia or heaters to heat hydrogen and nitrogen in for ammonia synthesis, potentially lowering an overall carbon footprint of the oxycombustion systems, as compared to separate combustion, ammonia synthesis, and urea synthesis systems without any thermal integration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Thermal integrations comprise reactor thermal linkage of ammonia synthesis unit with oxycombustion reactor and/or separator thermal linkage of air separation unit with ammonia separation unit; air-separator capture-unit thermal linkage of air separation unit and carbon dioxide capture unit directing heat from air separation unit to carbon dioxide capture unit; and ammonia-separator capture-unit thermal linkage of the ammonia separation unit and the carbon dioxide capture unit directing heat from ammonia separation unit to carbon dioxide capture unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The set of thermal integrations comprises reactor thermal linkage of ammonia synthesis unit with oxycombustion reactor; reactor thermal linkage of ammonia synthesis unit with oxycombustion reactor comprises boiler loop that transfers combustion heat to ammonia synthesis unit from oxycombustion reactor, from flue gas formed in oxycombustion reactor, and/or from a portion of hot water or steam formed in oxycombustion reactor, where transferring thermal energy comprises circulating a heat transfer fluid through the boiler loop while the fuel mixture is being combusted and the ammonia is being synthesized. The set of thermal integrations comprises a separator thermal linkage of the air separation unit with the ammonia separation unit; the separator thermal linkage of the air separation unit with the ammonia separation unit comprises a separator loop that transfers heat to the air separation unit from the ammonia separation unit; and transferring the thermal energy comprises circulating a heat transfer fluid through the separator loop while the fuel mixture is being combusted and the ammonia is being synthesized. The set of thermal integrations comprises both a reactor thermal linkage of the ammonia synthesis unit with the oxycombustion reactor and a separator thermal linkage of the air separation unit with the ammonia separation unit; the reactor thermal linkage of the ammonia synthesis unit with the oxycombustion reactor comprises a boiler loop that transfers combustion heat to the ammonia synthesis unit from flue gas formed in the oxycombustion reactor and/or from hot water or steam that has been heated by the combustion heat; and separator thermal linkage of air separation unit with ammonia separation unit comprises a separator loop that transfers heat to the air separation unit from the ammonia separation unit; and transferring the thermal energy comprises circulating a 1st heat transfer fluid through the boiler loop and circulating a 2nd heat transfer fluid through the separator loop while the fuel mixture is being combusted and the ammonia is being synthesized. Preferred Processes: The method further includes treating, in a flue-gas treatment system, a flue gas formed by combustion of fuel in oxycombustion reactor to remove pollutants from flue gas, where treating the flue gas comprises using a portion of ammonia in the purified ammonia stream to remove the pollutants from the flue gas. The method further includes treating, in a flue-gas treatment system, a flue gas formed by the combustion of the fuel in the oxycombustion reactor to remove pollutants from the flue gas, the treating of the flue gas outputting carbon dioxide; capturing the carbon dioxide in a carbon dioxide capture unit; combining a portion of the carbon dioxide captured in the carbon dioxide capture unit with the ammonia in the ammonia stream to form a urea reactant mixture; and synthesizing urea from the urea reactant mixture in a urea synthesis unit.
DC E35; H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23))
MC E32-A01; H06-X; J04-X; Q73-T02

PT P
PN CN108181300-A
TI Determining the phosphorus content in a soil sample comprises e.g. preparing phosphorus standard solution, adding sodium-bicarbonate, adjusting the pH value, filtering, detecting the radiation intensity using spectrometer.
AU LIU S
   DU Y
   WU R
   GUO X
   BAI Y
   SUN H
   TIAN Y
AE BEIJING ACAD AGRIC & FORESTRY SCI (BJAF-C)
GA 201850298H
AB    NOVELTY - Determining the phosphorus content in a soil sample comprises (i) preparing 3 different concentrations of phosphorus standard solution, adding sodium-bicarbonate aqueous solution to the phosphorus standard solution respectively, and adding hydrochloric solution to adjust the pH value of the system, empting carbon-dioxide and using the inductively coupled plasma constant volume, (ii) adding the sodium-bicarbonate aqueous solution to the soil sample for leaching, and filtering to obtain a filtrate, (iii) sucking the filtrate and adding the hydrochloric solution to adjust the pH value, empting carbon-dioxide, determining the volume, detecting the radiation intensity using the inductively coupled plasma emission spectrometer.
   USE - The inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry is useful in the determination of phosphorus content in soil samples (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is quick and accurately.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining the phosphorus content in a soil sample comprises (i) preparing 3 different concentrations of phosphorus standard solution, adding sodium-bicarbonate aqueous solution to the phosphorus standard solution respectively, and adding hydrochloric solution to adjust the pH value of the system, empting carbon-dioxide and using the inductively coupled plasma constant volume, where the body emission spectrometer performs detection to obtain a radiation intensity, the concentration of phosphorus in the phosphorus standard solution is plotted on the abscissa, and the radiation intensity is plotted on the ordinate, and a working curve is prepared, (ii) adding the sodium-bicarbonate aqueous solution to the soil sample for leaching, and filtering to obtain a filtrate, (iii) sucking the filtrate and adding the hydrochloric solution to adjust the pH value, empting carbon-dioxide, determining the volume, detecting the radiation intensity using the inductively coupled plasma emission spectrometer, according to the working curve, obtaining a concentration of phosphorus element in the filtrate, denoted as rho 0, and the unit is mg/ml, according to formula (A), the concentration of available phosphorus in the soil sample is obtained as omega , and the unit is mg/kg, where rho 2 represents the concentration of phosphorus in the blank liquid obtained according to the standard curve, and the unit is mg/ml, where the blank liquid refers to the filtrate obtained when the soil sample is not added in the step (ii), V represents the volume of the filtrate, and the unit is ml, m represents the mass of the soil sample, the unit is g, the D represents the fractional multiple, i.e. the ratio of the volume of the aqueous sodium-bicarbonate solution to the fractionation volume in the step (ii), and the fractionation volume refers to volume of the filtrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The phosphorus standard solution is an aqueous solution of potassium dihydrogen phosphate, and the phosphorus standard solution has a volume concentration of 0-20 mu g/ml. The pH of the aqueous solution of sodium-bicarbonate is 8.5, which is regulated by an aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide and the concentration of the aqueous solution of sodium-bicarbonate is 0.5 mol/l. The hydrochloric solution is prepared from water and hydrochloric acid in a volume ratio of 1:1. In the step (ii), the ratio of the soil sample to the aqueous sodium-bicarbonate solution is: 1 g: 20 ml. In the step (2), the soil sample is a sample after being sieved to 1-2 mm. In the step (i), the volume ratio of the hydrochloric solution to the aqueous sodium-bicarbonate solution is 1:5-8. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the leaching step is performed on a shaker, and the mixture is shaken for 25-35 minutes under the condition of a rotation speed of 180-200 revolutions/minute.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); V05 (Valves, Discharge Tubes and CRTs)
MC S03-E04A; S03-E04D; V05-F04H; V05-F05C; V05-F05E3

PN CN115257185-A
TI High-viscosity solution electric spray nozzle            device, has base body formed with second-containing            cavity, piston is set in second-containing cavity, and            high-voltage power supply negative electrode connected            to substrate to form electric field to electrically            spray expansion solution.
AU YE D
   GUAN Y
   HUANG Y
   HUANG L
   TIAN Y
   DI L
   LAI W
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2022E0999V
AB    NOVELTY - The high-viscosity solution electric spray                nozzle device has a shell that is set in the                heating component, where two ends of the shell are                connected with an air inlet plug and a needle plug.                A needle head first end is set in the needle plug,                and the second end is protruded out of the needle                plug. A piston is movably set in the shell that                comprises a cylindrical base body and an air inlet                joint and a liquid inlet joint symmetrically                connected to a base body. The base body is formed                with a second-containing cavity, and the piston is                movably set in the second-containing cavity. The                air inlet joint, the liquid inlet joint, the air                inlet plug, and a needle head plug are connected                with a second containing cavity. The carbon dioxide                and solution are introduced into a                second-containing cavity through the air inlet                joint and the liquid inlet joint. The                pressure-stabilizing gas is introduced into the                second containing cavity through the air inlet                plug.
   USE - High-viscosity solution electric spray nozzle                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-viscosity solution electric spray                nozzle device realizes the combined application of                electro-fluid jet printing technology and                supercritical fluid technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The pressure stabilizing gas applies pressure                to the piston that moves along the                second-containing cavity to exert pressure on the                carbon dioxide, the pressure of the first end of                the second-containing cavity close is made to the                needle plug reaches the critical pressure of carbon                dioxide. The shell is heated through the heating                component so that the temperature in the shell                reaches the critical temperature of carbon dioxide                that reaches the supercritical state which is mixed                with the solution to form an expansion solution. A                negative electrode of the high-voltage power supply                is connected to the substrate to form an electric                field to electrically spray the expansion                solution.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                high-viscosity solution electric spray nozzle                device.

TI System for capturing carbon-dioxide from flue gas based on magnetic activated carbon immobilized carbonic anhydrate, has first-level magnetic separator whose first slurry outlet is connected with second-level magnetic separator.
AU GUO Z
   YANG S
   CAI M
   YAO M
   CHENG G
   LI Y
   ZHAO H
   YANG C
AE XIAN THERMAL POWER RES INST CO LTD (CHHN-C)
GA 202211455E
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a catalytic decarburizing tower (2) whose inner lower portion is provided with a slurry supplementing layer (5). A first material outlet (12) of a first-level magnetic separator (11) is connected with a slurry tank (18). A first slurry outlet (13) of the first-level magnetic separator is connected with a second-level magnetic separator (15). A second material outlet (21) of the second-level magnetic separator is connected with the slurry tank. A second slurry outlet (22) of the second-level magnetic separator is connected with a mineralizing reaction device (30). An output pipeline of the slurry tank is mixed with ammonia water, and is connected to a slurry supplementing layer inlet (19) of the slurry supplementing layer.
   USE - System for capturing carbon-dioxide from flue gas based on magnetic activated carbon immobilized carbonic anhydrase.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses the activated carbon to magnetize, immobilizes carbonic anhydrase, and utilizes the characteristics of magnetic recyclability and separation to realize the reuse of catalyst carriers. The carbon-based carrier has better porous structure, large amount of carbonic anhydrase, and strong adsorption capacity to realize for the solid-liquid-gas three-phase reaction and accelerate the reaction, so that the immobilized carbonic anhydrase can improve the stability of the enzyme, and combined with the magnetic separation technology, which is convenient for the recovery and reuse of the enzyme, thus realizing simple process scheme and high catalytic efficiency for industrial applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for capturing carbon-dioxide from flue gas based on magnetic activated carbon immobilized carbonic anhydrate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system for capturing carbon-dioxide from flue gas based on magnetic activated carbon immobilized carbonic anhydrase. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN CN113520590-A
TI Carbon dioxide laser treatment machine is used for cutting human body tissue, has laser tube whose one side is respectively fixedly connected to one side of first mounting plate, second mounting plate and third mounting plate, and aerosol purifier is arranged on top of chassis.
AU HU J
   ZHANG Y
   TAO Y
AE WUHAN HI-TECH HENGDA OPTOELECTRONIC CO (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4403L
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide laser treatment machine has a nozzle whose one end is fixedly connected to one end of an exhaust pipe (8). A threading interface is fixedly connected to the side of the nozzle on the side of a treatment hand tool (10). The inside of the threading interface is connected to one end of a wire (9). A liquid crystal terminal (13) is provided on one side of an emergency stop button (5) on the top of a chassis (1). A first mounting plate, a second mounting plate and a third mounting plate are arranged inside the chassis. A laser tube is provided on one side of the first mounting plate. A condenser tube is sleeved on the outer periphery of the laser tube. One side of the laser tube is respectively fixedly connected to one side of the first mounting plate, the second mounting plate and the third mounting plate. An aerosol purifier (31) is arranged on the top of the chassis.
   USE - Carbon dioxide laser treatment machine is used for cutting human body tissue, vaporizing, carbonizing, solidifying and irradiating, mainly treating vascular skin disease, pigment skin disease, malignant tumour, benign tumour or cyst, keratinization, hyperplasia and other skin diseases and so on.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine adopts the air pump arranged inside the cabinet and the exhaust pipe fixedly connected with the air pump, and is realized that the large amount of smoke and virus particles produced when cutting, vaporizing and carbonizing the skin is directly blown away and diffused during the treatment. The distance between the nozzle and the cut, vaporized, and carbonized skin area is shortened, by fixing the nozzle directly on the side of the treatment hand tool. The air flow from the nozzle directly blow away the smoke and virus particles produced by cutting, vaporizing, and carbonizing the skin. The nozzle angle is adjusted to increase the accuracy of airflow blowing. The condensed water is driven by the water pump to cool down in circulation, through the condenser tube set inside the chassis, which increases the cooling rate and improves the working efficiency of the laser treatment machine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of carbon dioxide laser treatment machine.
   Chassis (1)
   Emergency stop button (5)
   Exhaust pipe (8)
   Wire (9)
   Treatment hand tool (10)
   Liquid crystal terminal (13)
   Aerosol purifier (31)

PN CN113046290-A; CN113046290-B
TI Culture method for reducing accumulation level of heavy metal copper in marine diatom cells involves performing long-term acidification and adaptive cultivation of bait diatoms and controlling nutrient level of nitrogen and phosphorus in cultivation base.
AU XU D
   YE N
   WANG W
   SUN K
   ZHANG X
   FAN X
   WANG Y
AE CAFS YELLOW SEA FISHERIES RES INST (CAFS-C)
GA 202178644G
AB    NOVELTY - Culture method for reducing accumulation level of heavy metal copper in marine diatom cells involves performing long-term acidification and adaptive cultivation of bait diatoms by inoculating the bait diatoms in the exponential growth phase into sterile f/2 medium with an initial inoculation density of 10000 cell/ml, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for aerated culture, setting the incubator carbon dioxide concentration to 1000 mu atm, controlling pH of the control medium at 7.9 plus minus 0.2, controlling the density of diatom cells to be less than 500000 cell/ml, after 160 days of aerated culture, calculating the specific growth rate of diatom cells in mu /day, where when the specific growth rate of diatoms is inoculated for two consecutive times, the specific growth rate of algae cells is not statistically different, and obtaining long-term acidification adaptive bait microalgae, and controlling the nutrient level of nitrogen and phosphorus in the cultivation base.
   USE - The culture method is useful for reducing accumulation level of heavy metal copper in marine diatom cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the accumulation efficiency of bait diatom copper, improves the growth rate of bait diatoms, shortens the period of bait diatom cultivation and saves labor costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culture method for reducing accumulation level of heavy metal copper in marine diatom cells involves performing long-term acidification and adaptive cultivation of bait diatoms by inoculating the bait diatoms in the exponential growth phase into sterile f/2 medium with an initial inoculation density of 10000 cell/ml, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for aerated culture, setting the incubator carbon dioxide concentration to 1000 mu atm, controlling pH of the control medium at 7.9 plus minus 0.2, controlling the density of diatom cells to be less than 500000 cell/ml, after 160 days of aerated culture, calculating the specific growth rate of diatom cells in mu /day, where when the specific growth rate of diatoms is inoculated for two consecutive times, the specific growth rate of algae cells is not statistically different, and obtaining long-term acidification adaptive bait microalgae, and controlling the nutrient level of nitrogen and phosphorus in the cultivation base. The nutrient level of nitrogen and phosphorus in the medium is controlled by during the acidification adaptive culture process, measuring the changes in the concentration of nitrate and phosphate in the culture medium and controlling the removal efficiency of nitrogen and phosphorus during the 4-day culture period to be less than 40%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The calculation method of the specific growth rate of the diatom cells is mu = (lnN-4day-lnN-0day)/4, where N-4day is the algal cell density on the 4th day of culture and N-0day is cell density of the initial inoculation algae. The calculation method of the nutrient salt removal efficiency is NRE=100%x (C-0day-C-4day )/C0day, where C-4day is the concentration of nitrate or phosphate on the fourth day of culture and N-0day is concentration of nitrate or phosphate at the time of initial inoculation.

PT P
PN CN113024338-A
TI Preparing ethylbenzene involves feeding refinery dry gas raw material into acid gas removal device, removing acid gas and obtaining purified dry gas, and then pressurizing and cooling purified dry gas.
AU LIU J
   GONG W
   YANG W
   HOU N
   WANG H
   LV J
   YAN B
   GAO Y
AE WISON ENG CHINA CO LTD (WISO-Non-standard)
   CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2021769205
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethylbenzene involves feeding refinery dry gas raw material into an acid gas removal device, removing acid gas and obtaining purified dry gas, and then pressurizing and cooling the purified dry gas. The purified dry gas is placed into a carbon dioxide absorption tower to obtain a carbon-rich secondary absorbent at the bottom of the tower. The obtained carbon-rich absorbent is fed into a carbon-rich desorption tower, and desorbed to obtain carbon-rich gas and the carbon-rich absorbent, where the carbon-rich absorbent returns to the carbon-rich absorption tower. The carbon-enriched gas is cooled, and placed into a propylene absorption tower, and contacted with a propylene absorbent to remove propylene to obtain propylene-removed carbon-enriched gas and the propylene-enriched absorbent.
   USE - Method for preparing ethylbenzene by fully utilizing refinery dry gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the ethylbenzene by fully utilizing refinery dry gas in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with high utilization rate of the raw material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing ethylbenzene involves feeding refinery dry gas raw material into an acid gas removal device, removing acid gas and obtaining purified dry gas, and then pressurizing and cooling the purified dry gas. The purified dry gas is placed into a carbon dioxide absorption tower to obtain a carbon-rich secondary absorbent at the bottom of the tower. The obtained carbon-rich absorbent is fed into a carbon-rich desorption tower, and desorbed to obtain carbon-rich gas and the carbon-rich absorbent, where the carbon-rich absorbent returns to the carbon-rich absorption tower. The carbon-enriched gas is cooled, and placed into a propylene absorption tower, and contacted with a propylene absorbent to remove propylene to obtain propylene-removed carbon-enriched gas and the propylene-enriched absorbent. The obtained depropylene carbon dioxide gas is placed into an ethylbenzene synthesis and product separation device, reacted ethylene in depropylene carbon dioxide gas with raw material benzene to generate ethylbenzene under action of a catalyst, and then separated and purified to obtain the ethane-rich gas and an ethylbenzene product. The obtained ethane-rich gas is mixed and heated with an oxidant and a diluent gas, then placed the mixture into an ethane oxidative dehydrogenation reactor to generate an oxidative dehydrogenation product rich in ethylene. The oxidative dehydrogenation product is cooled and placed to the acid gas removal device to get the ethylbenzene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidant is air or oxygen. The diluent gas is a mixture of nitrogen, water vapor or carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorbent is ethylbenzene, polyethylbenzene or benzene. The propylene absorbent used in the propylene absorption tower is benzene, ethylbenzene or polyethylbenzene.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)

PN CN112811449-A
TI Preparing magnesia-aluminum hydrotalcite by mixing            magnesium compound and aluminum compound, composite            catalyst, and water, wet-grinding, transferring            hydrotalcite slurry to autoclave, and (d) subjecting to            solid-liquid separation.
AU HU S
   CHEN T
   SONG F
AE JIANGSU ATK FLAME RETARDANT MATERIALS CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202158683V
AB    NOVELTY - New method for preparing magnesia-aluminum                hydrotalcite involves (a) mixing the magnesium                compound and aluminum compound of a certain                particle size, composite catalyst, and water to                obtain a mixed solution, (b) wet-grinding the mixed                liquid obtained in step (a), when grinding, the                mixed liquid is input into the wet grinding                equipment, converting the magnesium compound, and                the aluminum compounds and carbon dioxide into                magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite, obtaining a                hydrotalcite slurry containing both                magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite and a very small                amount of unconverted reactants, and (c)                transferring the hydrotalcite slurry obtained in                step (b) to an autoclave, converting into                magnesia-aluminum hydrotalcite to obtain a                magnesia-aluminum hydrotalcite slurry, and (d)                subjecting the magnesia-aluminum hydrotalcite                slurry to solid-liquid separation and drying to                obtain the product.
   USE - The new method used for preparing                magnesia-aluminum hydrotalcite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy to industrialized                production, has low requirements for various                production equipment, and reduces equipment                investment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - New method for preparing magnesia-aluminum                hydrotalcite involves (a) mixing the magnesium                compound and aluminum compound of a certain                particle size, composite catalyst, and water to                obtain a mixed solution, where the molar ratio of                the magnesium compound and aluminum compound is                (0.5-6.0)/1, (b) wet-grinding the mixed liquid                obtained in step (a), when grinding, the mixed                liquid is input into the wet grinding equipment,                and at the same time, passing the carbon dioxide                into the circulating grinding, during this process,                magnesium compounds, aluminum compounds and carbon                dioxide gradually produce magnesium aluminum                hydrotalcite, during the grinding process, taking                regular samples to detect the particle size of the                solids in the mixed liquid, when the particle size                D50 and D90 meet the target value, stop the                circular grinding, converting the magnesium                compound, and the aluminum compounds and carbon                dioxide into magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite,                obtaining a hydrotalcite slurry containing both                magnesium-aluminum hydrotalcite and a very small                amount of unconverted reactants, and (c)                transferring the hydrotalcite slurry obtained in                step (b) to an autoclave, controlling the                temperature at 120-260 degrees C for 1.5-6 hours,                converting into magnesia-aluminum hydrotalcite to                obtain a magnesia-aluminum hydrotalcite slurry, and                (d) subjecting the magnesia-aluminum hydrotalcite                slurry obtained in step (c) to solid-liquid                separation and drying to obtain the product, where                the filtrate produced by solid-liquid separation                contains a composite catalyst, which can be                returned to the front end for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves                  first transferring the mixed liquid into a closed                  container, and connecting the lower outlet of the                  closed container to the feed inlet of the wet                  grinding equipment through a pump, and connecting                  the discharge port of the wet grinding equipment                  with the upper inlet of the closed container to                  form a closed loop. Preferred Conditions: The                  temperature of the autoclave in step (c) is                  controlled at 220 degrees C for 4 hours.                  Preferred Components: In step (a), the molar                  ratio of the magnesium compound and the aluminum                  compound is 3/1. In step (a), the magnesium                  compound comprises magnesium hydroxide or                  magnesium oxide. The aluminum compound comprises                  aluminum hydroxide or aluminum oxide. In step                  (a), the solid content of the magnesium compound                  and the aluminum compound in the mixed solution                  is preferably 10-30%. In step (a), the composite                  catalyst is: diethanolamine and sodium phosphate                  with a mass ratio of 1:1, where the composite                  catalyst is calculated at 0.5-1% of the magnesium                  compound added amount. In step (b), a connecting                  pipe between the discharge port of the wet                  grinding equipment and the upper inlet of the                  airtight container is installed vertically, and a                  vertical pipe greater than or equal to 10 m is                  provided on the connecting pipe. The step (b)                  involves using the pump is a diaphragm pump or a                  hose pump. Preferred Properties: In step (b),                  when the particle diameters D50 and D90 meet the                  target values, stop circular grinding, the target                  particle diameter is controlled at D50 greater                  than or equal to 2.5 mu m, D90 greater than or                  equal to 4 mu m, and the priority particle size                  is controlled at D50 less than or equal to 1.5 mu                  m, D90 less than or equal to 3 mu m.

PN CN111100752-A
TI Extracting fatty acids rich in EPA from garlic            algae comprises crushing algae raw materials, adding            different acid and alkali salts, extracting, performing            ion exchange treatment and secondary purification,            deodorizing and filtering.
AU CHEN G
   LIU J
   LI X
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV TIANJIN QINGDAO OCEAN TECHNOLOGY (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020403613
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting fatty acids rich in EPA from garlic                algae comprises (a) crushing the algae raw                materials, adding different acid and alkali salts                for ultrasonic wall breaking treatment, filtering,                (b) extracting the filtrate by supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction method, taking the extract,                separating to obtain crude oil, (c) passing the                crude oil through an anion exchange resin column                and a cation exchange resin column in order to                perform ion exchange treatment to obtain a                first-level purified oil, (d) performing secondary                purification and dehydration of primary purified                oil to obtain secondary purified oil, (e) adding 1                wt.% activated carbon to the secondary purified oil                to decolorize, heating under nitrogen protection,                stirring and cooling to room temperature, (f)                removing activated carbon, performing steam                degassing and deodorization, cooling to room                temperature and (g) circulating and filtering the                deodorized secondary purified oil with a filter to                obtain a finished product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting fatty                acids rich in EPA from garlic algae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes supercritical carbon                dioxide for extraction, which has no chemical                reaction occurs during the extraction process,                tasteless, odorless, non-toxic, can be reused in                production, save energy and prevent ingredients                after being destroyed; extracts the solvent-free                residue, which prevents the generation of harmful                substances again and adopts secondary purification                treatment to remove impurities e.g. trace elements,                sugar and protein to obtain relatively pure EPA                fatty acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting fatty acids rich in EPA from garlic                algae comprises (a) crushing the algae raw                materials, adding different acid and alkali salts                for ultrasonic wall breaking treatment, filtering                to obtain the filtrate, (b) extracting the filtrate                by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method                at 30-50 degrees C for 10-80 minutes, where the                extraction pressure is 25-40Mpa, the carbon dioxide                flow rate is 50-300 ml/min, taking the extract,                separating to obtain crude oil, (c) passing the                crude oil through an anion exchange resin column                and a cation exchange resin column in order to                perform ion exchange treatment to obtain a                first-level purified oil, (d) performing secondary                purification and dehydration of primary purified                oil to obtain secondary purified oil, (e) adding 1                wt.% activated carbon to the secondary purified oil                to decolorize, heating under nitrogen protection,                stirring and cooling to room temperature, (f)                removing activated carbon, performing steam                degassing and deodorization, cooling to room                temperature and (g) circulating and filtering the                deodorized secondary purified oil with a filter to                obtain a finished product.

PN CN110591245-A
TI PVC foam material used for e.g. home decoration, is prepared by mixing material comprising PVC resin powder and zinc stearate, preparing film, cutting, performing heat preservation and pressure maintaining reaction, foaming and forming.
AU LIU C
AE HUAIAN BINGQING CONSTR ENG MANAGEMENT CO (HUAI-Non-standard)
GA 2019A7579B
AB    NOVELTY - PVC foam material is prepared by (i) adding 100 pts. wt. PVC resin powder, 40-48 pts. wt. plasticizer, 3-5 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 3-5 pts. wt. barium stearate, 6-8 pts. wt. polyester-type thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer rubber, 7-9 pts. wt. trioctyl trimellitate, 12-16 pts. wt. nitrile rubber, 5-6 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide and 10-13 pts. wt. magnesium hydroxide into a high and low speed mixer for mixing and discharging to obtain a mixed PVC material, (ii) putting the mixed PVC material into a double-roller plastic mill for mixing and preparing a PVC film, (iii) cutting the PVC film into square sheets, putting the square sheets into a supercritical carbon dioxide pressurizing reaction device, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction device and carrying out heat preservation and pressure maintaining reaction and (iv) relieving pressure, foaming the square sheets in a water bath heater and finally putting the square sheets into cold water for cooling and forming.
   USE - The foam material is useful for home decoration and packaging products such as wallpaper, soft bags and handbags.
   ADVANTAGE - The foam material is environmentally-friendly and has excellent indexes such as hardness, foaming rate and flame retardance and closely arranged foam holes with uniform size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - PVC foam material is prepared by (i) adding 100 pts. wt. PVC resin powder, 40-48 pts. wt. plasticizer, 3-5 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 3-5 pts. wt. barium stearate, 6-8 pts. wt. polyester-type thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer rubber, 7-9 pts. wt. trioctyl trimellitate, 12-16 pts. wt. nitrile rubber, 5-6 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide and 10-13 pts. wt. magnesium hydroxide into a high and low speed mixer for mixing for 10 minutes and discharging to obtain a mixed PVC material, (ii) putting the mixed PVC material into a double-roller plastic mill for mixing at 175-180 degrees C for 15-20 minutes and preparing a PVC film with a thickness of 0.5 mm, (iii) cutting the PVC film into square sheets, putting the square sheets into a supercritical carbon dioxide pressurizing reaction device, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction device and carrying out heat preservation and pressure maintaining reaction for 2 hours and (iv) relieving pressure, foaming the square sheets in a water bath heater for 6-8 seconds and finally putting the square sheets into cold water for cooling and forming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The plasticizer is dioctyl phthalate. Preferred Method: The step (i) involves adding 100 pts. wt. PVC resin powder, 44 pts. wt. plasticizer, 4 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 4 pts. wt. barium stearate, 7 pts. wt. polyester-type thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer rubber, 8 pts. wt. trioctyl trimellitate, 14 pts. wt. nitrile rubber, 6 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide and 12 pts. wt. magnesium hydroxide into the high and low speed mixer for mixing. The step (iii) involves carrying out the heat preservation and the pressure maintaining reaction at 30 degrees C and 15 MPa for 2 hours. The step (iv) involves foaming in the water bath heater at 80-90 degrees C.

PN CN110317622-A
TI Preparing high softening point asphalt useful in anode materials comprises e.g. pumping heated molten substrate asphalt to stirring device, stirring to heat, adding epoxy resin, organic solvent, heating, stirring to obtain refined asphalt.
AU NIE K
   WANG Z
AE PINGDINGSHAN DONGSHENG HI-TECH IND CO (PING-Non-standard)
GA 201987701U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high softening point asphalt comprises adding heated molten substrate asphalt to the stirring device by pumping, stirring to heat, uniformly stirring, adding epoxy resin and organic solvent, heating, continuously stirring to obtain refined asphalt, adding refined asphalt to the reaction kettle, electrically heating, continuously stirring until the refined asphalt is in fully molten state, adding reinforcing agent and stabilizer, uniformly stirring, adding acid solution to acidify the reaction, adding alkaline solution to perform neutralization reaction, adding molecular sieve catalyst to the reaction kettle, heating to carry out polymerization reaction, introducing inert gas, carbon dioxide, or steam into the reaction kettle, discharging the light component in reaction kettle to outside, finishing adding inert gas, carbon dioxide or steam, cooling, orderly adding foaming agent and modifier, carrying out modification, and discharging to obtain high softening point asphalt.
   USE - The high softening point asphalt is useful in anode materials (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, and has low preparation requirement; improves the softening point of the asphalt, and does not produce the intermediate phase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high softening point asphalt comprises (i) adding heated molten substrate asphalt to the stirring device by pumping, stirring to heat at 80-110 degrees C, uniformly stirring, adding 5-10% epoxy resin and 3.5-5.6% organic solvent, heating at 110-130 degrees C, and continuously stirring for 20-30 minutes to obtain refined asphalt, (ii) adding refined asphalt to the reaction kettle, electrically heating at 140-150 degrees C, raising the temperature to 180-220 degrees C at a rising rate of 10-15 degrees C/minute, continuously stirring until the refined asphalt is in fully molten state, adding reinforcing agent and stabilizer, uniformly stirring, adding acid solution to acidify the reaction, and adding alkaline solution to perform neutralization reaction, (iii) adding 1.5-4% molecular sieve catalyst to the reaction kettle, heating at 220-320 degrees C to carry out polymerization reaction, introducing inert gas, carbon dioxide, or steam into the reaction kettle, and discharging the light component in the reaction kettle to the outside, and (iv) finishing adding inert gas, carbon dioxide or steam, cooling to below 250 degrees C, orderly adding 2.5-3.2% foaming agent and 5.2-6.4% modifier, carrying out modification at 180- 220 degrees C, and discharging to obtain high softening point asphalt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (i), the initial stirring speed is 120-150 revolutions/minute, and the stirring speed of continuous stirring is 160-180 revolutions/minute. In the step (iii), the gas flow rate of the inert gas, carbon dioxide, and/or steam is 15-25 l/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the organic solvent is butyl acetate, tributyl methyl ether, isopropyl acetate, and/or methyl ethyl ketone. In the step (ii), the reinforcing agent is asphalt-based carbon fiber, and/or adhesive-based carbon fiber; the stabilizer is isopropanol, 4-chloro-1-butanol, and/or 4-bromobenzyl alcohol. In the step (iv), the foaming agent is sodium bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, silicon nitride, and/or isobutane; the modifier is styrene-butadiene-rubber (SBR) modifier, and/or modifier latex; the softening point of the obtained high softening point asphalt is 210-300 degrees C.

PN CN110002933-A; CN110002933-B
TI Preparation of methanol and olefins using polygeneration involves drying coal, exhaust gas, obtaining coal powder, obtaining upgraded gas, coal and feed gas, preparing gas using conversion gas, reformed gases, and preparing crude methanol.
AU LI J
   JIN F
   CHEN F
   LI Z
   PAN J
   ZHANG H
AE ZHEJIANG TIANLU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019632270
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of methanol and olefins using polygeneration in low-order coal partition involves drying coal, and exhaust gas, obtaining coal powder, subjecting to gasification reduction involves heating coal powder under, obtaining reforming conversion feed gas, preparing gas using one or more of reforming conversion feed gas, conversion gas (A), conversion gas (B), and reformed gases by adjusting ratio of hydrogen to carbon having R (gas constant) value of 2.05-2.1, processing reforming conversion feed gas by reforming conversion process, preparing reformed gas (X) by treating coal water slurry with oxygen or air by gasification to obtain carbon monoxide, treating water gas comprising carbon dioxide, and hydrogen, obtaining reformed gas (Y) using upgraded coal, preparing water gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen by reacting upgraded coal with water and oxygen, and obtaining reformed gas (Z), synthesizing crude methanol by methanol rectification process.
   USE - Preparation of methanol and olefins.
   ADVANTAGE - The process effectively recovers water and coal powder from low rank coal, utilizing and saving resources, recycling wastewater, and provides environmental protection by reusing pulverized coal. The process using less methanol impurities with high quality which highly improves the utilization rate of low-rank coal, and has efficient cleaning and utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of methanol and olefins using polygeneration in low-order coal partition involves drying coal, and exhaust gas, obtaining coal powder by dust removal using dry exhaust gas, subjecting to gasification reduction process involves heating coal powder under oxygen-free or micro oxygen-free conditions to obtain upgraded gas, and coal, carrying out purification, desulfurization, obtaining reforming conversion feed gas, preparing methanol synthesis gas using one or more of reforming conversion feed gas, conversion gas (A), conversion gas (B), and reformed gases by adjusting ratio of hydrogen to carbon having R (gas constant) value of 2.05-2.1, processing reforming conversion feed gas by reforming conversion process, preparing reformed gas (X) by treating coal water slurry with oxygen or air by gasification to obtain carbon monoxide, treating water gas comprising carbon dioxide, and hydrogen by purification process, obtaining reformed gas (Y) using upgraded coal by upgrading coal-to-gas process, preparing water gas comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and hydrogen by reacting upgraded coal with water and oxygen by purification process, and obtaining reformed gas (Z), synthesizing crude methanol by methanol rectification process to obtain refined methanol and methanol gas, converting methanol to olefins by passing crude methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The methanol synthesis process using 0.1 ppm sulfur. The coal water slurry is chosen from one or more of low rank coal powder, and upgraded coal. The crude methanol comprises 5 wt.% water. The hydrocarbon mixture comprises low olefins, low alkanes, and alkynes. Preferred Process: The reformed gas (Y) or reformed gas (Z) converted to methanol synthesis gas by shift conversion process. The preparation of reforming conversion feed gas by passing purification process through compression process and desulfurization process. The conversion of methanol to olefin involves low carbon olefin recovery process, and preparing coal water slurry using wastewater produced by methanol to olefin conversion, and treated by wastewater treatment process. The methanol to olefin conversion further involves refining crude methanol, converting to hydrocarbon mixture using catalyst, and fractionating obtained mixture to obtain low olefin.

PN CN109930043-A; CN109930043-B
TI Preparing as-cast high-strength fast-etching magnesium alloy comprises pretreating magnesium ingot, aluminum ingot and germanium particles, heating, cooling molten material, casting steel mold for preheating, and naturally cooling ingot.
AU WEI Y
   LIU X
   HOU L
   YIN M
   DU H
   LIU B
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201959028Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing as-cast high-strength fast-etching magnesium alloy comprises removing oxides and oil stains on the surface of magnesium ingots and aluminum ingots, cleaning with absolute alcohol, drying to obtain pretreated magnesium ingot and aluminum ingot, removing the oxides and oil stains on the surface of germanium, washing with absolute alcohol, drying to obtain pre-treated germanium particles, placing the pretreated magnesium ingot and aluminum ingot in a furnace under the protection of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride mixed gas to be heated until to melt, heating adding pre-treated germanium, stirring under the protection of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride mixed gas to obtain molten material, preserving, cooling the molten material, allowing to stand, casting steel mold for pre-heating, and naturally cooling the ingot to room temperature to obtain alloy.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing as-cast high-strength fast-etching magnesium alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully utilizes aluminum and germanium elements to increase the strength and corrosion rate of magnesium alloy, adds less species and less added amount by less alloying elements, and significantly improves mechanical properties and corrosion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing as-cast high-strength fast-etching magnesium alloy comprises (i) removing oxides and oil stains on the surface of magnesium ingots and aluminum ingots, cleaning with absolute alcohol, drying at 90 degrees C to obtain pretreated magnesium ingot and aluminum ingot, (ii) removing the oxides and oil stains on the surface of germanium, washing with absolute alcohol, and drying at 90 degrees C to obtain pre-treated germanium particles, (iii) placing the pretreated magnesium ingot and aluminum ingot in a furnace under the protection of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride mixed gas to be heated to 700-760 degrees C until to melt, (iv) after melting, placing at 700-760 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, heating to 770-800 degrees C, adding pre-treated germaniumparticles, stirring under the protection of carbon dioxide and sulfur hexafluoride mixed gas to obtain molten material, and preserving for 35-60 minutes, and (v) cooling the molten material to 650-700 degrees C, allowing to stand for 10-20 minutes, casting steel mold for pre-heating to 200-300 degrees C, and naturally cooling the ingot to room temperature to obtain alloy, where the alloy comprises 2-8 wt.% aluminum, 0.5-7 wt.% germanium and remaining amount of magnesium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium ingots and aluminum ingots are cut into small pieces of 100 mmx 30 mmx 20 mm before removing the oxides and oil stains on the surface of magnesium ingots and aluminum ingots. The germanium comprises industrial grade purity germanium.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly crosslinked imidazolium ionic liquid porous organic polymer (III) comprises dissolving 1,3-dibenzylimidazolium chloride salt (I) and 1,4-p-dichlorobenzyl chloride (II) in 1,2-dichloroethane, carrying out alkylation reaction at 60-90 degrees C for 12-30 hours under the catalyst ferric chloride, filtering, washing filtrate with methanol, purifying, and vacuum drying to obtain highly crosslinked imidazolium ionic liquid porous organic polymer, where molar-volume ratio between 1,3-dibenzylimidazolium chloride salt, 1,4-p-dichlorobenzyl chloride, ferric chloride and 1,2-dichloroethane is 1 mmol:1-20 mmol:1-50 mmol:10-300 ml.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing highly crosslinked imidazolium ionic liquid porous organic polymer of formula (III) comprises dissolving 1,3-dibenzylimidazolium chloride salt of formula (I) and 1,4-p-dichlorobenzyl chloride of formula (II) in 1,2-dichloroethane, carrying out alkylation reaction at 60-90 degrees C for 12-30 hours under the catalyst ferric chloride, filtering, washing filtrate with methanol, purifying, and vacuum drying to obtain highly crosslinked imidazolium ionic liquid porous organic polymer, where molar-volume ratio between 1,3-dibenzylimidazolium chloride salt, 1,4-p-dichlorobenzyl chloride, ferric chloride and 1,2-dichloroethane is 1 mmol:1-20 mmol:1-50 mmol:10-300 ml.
   R = alkyl or aryl.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of highly crosslinked imidazolium ionic liquid porous organic polymer as catalyst for synthesizing cyclic carbonate compound in the reaction of carbon dioxide with ethylene oxide or its derivatives comprising reacting oxirane compound of formula (IV) with carbon dioxide under the action of catalyst to obtain 1,3-dioxolan-2-one compound of formula (V), then highly crosslinking ethylene oxide or its derivative with catalyst, where mass ratio between (ethylene oxide or its derivative with catalyst) and imidazole ionic liquid porous organic polymer is 15-50 mmol:0.05-0.5 g, then passing 0.1-2 MPa carbon dioxide at 70-150 degrees C for 1-10 hours, filtering the catalyst to obtain product cyclic carbonate compound, then washing filtered catalyst, and drying, where ethylene oxide or its derivative is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epoxy bromopropane, 1,2-epoxyhexane, 1,2-epoxycyclohexane, allyl glycidyl ether or styrene oxide.

PN CN108423719-A
TI Lead-zinc flotation residue wastewater treatment agent comprises calcium carbonate, diesel oil, calcium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, nonionic polyacrylamide and zinc sulfate.
AU SUN G
   LI B
   SUN S
AE ZHONGSHAN LVLANG AUXILIARIES CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2018667406
AB    NOVELTY - Lead-zinc flotation residue wastewater treatment agent comprises calcium carbonate, diesel oil, calcium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, nonionic polyacrylamide and zinc sulfate.
   USE - Lead-zinc flotation residue wastewater treatment agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The lead-zinc flotation residue wastewater treatment agent ensures easy metal wastewater treatment, low treatment cost, easy actual operation. It has multiple functions like rapid calcium removal, suspended matter removal and precipitation of heavy metal ions. The treated wastewater meets the GB8978-1996 standard discharge standard.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for preparing the calcium carbonate, which involves calcining the limestone raw material to form lime and carbon dioxide, treating with water to form lime milk, adding then carbon dioxide, precipitating the carbonized lime milk to form calcium carbonate, and finally dehydrating, drying and crushing to obtain calcium carbonate;
   (2) a method for preparing the sodium carbonate, which involves reacting ammonia with water and carbon dioxide to form one molecule of ammonium hydrogencarbonate, and then reacting ammonium hydrogencarbonate with sodium chloride to form sodium bicarbonate precipitate and ammonium chloride, filtering to obtain sodium hydrogencarbonate solid, and heating and decomposing to produce sodium carbonate;
   (3) a method for preparing the zinc carbonate, which involves reacting the zinc or zinc oxide raw material with sulfuric acid to obtain a crude zinc sulfate solution, oxidizing by potassium permanganate to remove impurities, then adding zinc powder, removing nickel, copper and cadmium impurities by stirring, using potassium permanganate for the second Sub-oxidation to remove manganese and iron, obtaining refined zinc sulfate solution, and adding pure alkali liquor to form basic zinc carbonate, controlling reaction at 46 degrees C temperature and 6.8 pH, 0.4-0.5% free base, metathesizing the crushed material, pressure-filtering the slurry material, drying the filter cake at 100 degrees C temperature until 2.5% or less moisture content, and finely crushing and sieving to obtain zinc carbonate;
   (4) a method for preparing the calcium hydroxide, which involves calcining limestone into calcium oxide, adding with water in 1:3-3.5 ratio to form a calcium hydroxide solution, purifying and separating, then dehydrating by centrifugation, drying and sieving at 150-300 degrees C temperature to obtain the calcium hydroxide; and
   (5) a method for preparing the potassium carbonate, which involves obtaining the potassium hydroxide solution by electrolyzing potassium chloride, carbonizing by carbon dioxide in a carbonization tower, evaporating by a multi-effect evaporator, filtering to obtain potassium hydrogencarbonate, and calcining to obtain potassium carbonate.

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a pair of screws (28)                received in the cylindrical barrel (24) and                arranged with their axes rotation of parallel to                each other. A driving unit (30,32,34) is provivded                for driving the screws into rotation at the same                speed, but in opposite directions, around their                respective axes of rotation. A tank containing a                foaming gas. An injection means is provided for                injecting the foaming gas coming from the gas tank                into the cylindrical barrel. A head (36) is mounted                at a second end of the cylindrical barrel opposite                to the first one. The heating means (38) is                arranged around the cylindrical barrel to transfer                heat. The gas tank contains carbon dioxide (CO2) as                a foaming gas.
   USE - Apparatus for producing foamed polymeric                material e.g. polyethylene foam and polypropylene                foam, used in packaging field and acoustic                insulation field.
   ADVANTAGE - The foamed polymeric material is produced at                lower costs and with lower risks. The degassing                time of the material is reduced to a few hours by                using carbon dioxide as a foaming gas, so that the                foamed material thus produced can be used after                only few hours from its production. Since carbon                dioxide is a much less expensive gas than                isobutane, a considerable saving is obtained in                terms of costs for producing the material. CO2 is                not flammable, so there is no risk of fire or                explosion throughout the production method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for production of foamed polymeric material.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                apparatus.

AB    NOVELTY - Calcining raw material (3) to obtain cementitious material by: providing a flow of raw material containing a metal carbonate to a calcination system; introducing the flow into a first thermal reactor (1) and partially decarbonating the raw material by subjecting to a first heat treatment at greater than or equal to 650 degrees C; optionally introducing the partly decarbonated material into a second thermal reactor; and subjecting the decarbonate material to a second heat treatment above the first temperature. The first reactor is heated by electrical energy (2). The cementitious is obtained as a result of the heat treatments.
   USE - The method is useful for calcining a raw material to obtain a cementitious material e.g. hydraulic cement and supplementary cementitious materials such as granulated blast-furnace slag, fly ash, natural pozzolans, burnt oil shale and calcined clay.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables an efficient use of electrical energy for the production of cementitious materials, and reduces the use of fossil fuels and other alternative fuels which are traditionally used in the cement process. This enables the reduction of the carbon dioxide footprint of the production cementitious material, as electricity has a lower carbon-di-oxide footprint than fossil or alternative fuels. Since the heat that is required to decarbonize raw material is generated by using electrical energy in the calcination device, the combustion of fuel, such as fossil fuel, in the calcination device can be eliminated. The exhaust gas from the thermal reactor, where no fuel is burnt, is pure carbon dioxide, so that the process operates without the requirement to separate carbon dioxide from flue gases.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the calcination system.

Ammonium salt aqueous solution discharged from the absorption unit is reacted with divalent metal hydroxide aqueous solution. An absorption liquid regenerating unit (160) circulates and supplies the absorbed solution to the absorbent unit. A washing unit (120) receives fresh water from a washing water supply module (121). A cooling module (123) cools the exhaust gas through cooling water. A neutralizing agent adjusts pH of the washing water supplied to a washing module (122). The aqueous solution of divalent metal hydroxide stored in the storage tank is calcium hydroxide or magnesium hydroxide produced by reacting fresh water with calcium oxide or magnesium oxide.
   USE - Exhaust gas recirculation (EGR)-coupled greenhouse gas emission reduction device for ship (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device maintains absorption rate, regenerates and reuses the absorbent liquid, and adjusts concentration of the absorbent liquid to prevent deterioration of absorption performance of the absorption liquid. A propellant inlet (13) is formed for introducing the propellant (T) into the interior of the pressure vessel. An outlet (14) of the pressure vessel is formed for removing plastic granulate impregnated with the propellant from the interior of the pressure vessel. The pressure vessel is designed for the continuous impregnation of plastic granulate in the interior of the pressure vessel and for the continuous removal of impregnated plastic granulate from the pressure vessel via the outlet. The outlet is set with a metering valve (Vd2) so that a volume flow of the impregnated plastic granulate removed from the interior of the pressure vessel per unit of time set.

   ADVANTAGE - The device enables the batch process described at the outset, which has a high carbon dioxide loss, a continuous process in which the impregnation and removal of the plastic granulate take place continuously, i.e. without complete pressure reduction in the interior of the pressure vessel, in contrast to the discontinuous batch process.

GA 202141973T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining basic data of a target in an in-service coal-fired power plant without carbon capture utilization and storage (CCUS) improvement and CCUS improvement and free carbon quota and carbon emission right price related to carbon restriction. Cash flow of each time period in a residual life cycle of the target in in-service coal-fired power plant is calculated according to the basic data of the target. Remaining life cycle of the cash flow of the target in the service coal power plant is calculated in each time period. Reconstruction period and carbon dioxide capture amount are combined according to basic data of the target active coal-fired power plant with CCUS reconstruction. The reconstruction period and the carbon dioxide capture amount are combined. The target in the service coal power plant with CCUS reconstruction is calculated when the cash flow of each time period is from the reconstruction period to the retirement period.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing important reference for evaluating CCUS improvement potential of the target in-service coal-fired power plant under the carbon constraint condition.

PT P
PN US2020129960-A1; US10710056-B2
TI Ceric oxide supported palladium catalyst used for producing dimethyl ether from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises promoter comprising calcium, and catalytic material with lead and ceric oxide and has low operating pressure.
AU ZAMAN S F
   MALIK A S
   ALZAHRANI A A
   DAOUS M A
   PETROV L A
AE UNIV KING ABDULAZIZ (UYKI-Non-standard)
GA 202035527B
AB    NOVELTY - Ceric oxide (CeO2) supported palladium catalyst comprises 0.05-2 wt.% promoter comprising calcium (Ca), 1-8 wt.% catalytic material comprising lead (Pd) and 90-98 wt.% CeO2, where CeO2 supported palladium catalyst has an atomic ratio of palladium to calcium ranging from 2:1 to 6:1; and the promoter and the catalytic material are deposited on the CeO2.
   USE - Ceric oxide supported palladium catalyst used for producing dimethyl ether from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The ceric oxide supported palladium catalyst shows high CO2 conversions even at low pressure, high production yields of dimethoxyethane (DME), low carbon-monoxide (CO) selectivities, low methane (CH4) selectivities, and low operating pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method of preparing the CeO2 supported palladium catalyst, which involves heating an aqueous solution of a Ce(III) salt and citric acid to form a first mixture; mixing the first mixture with an aqueous solution of a Pd(II) salt and an aqueous solution of a Ca(II) salt to form a second mixture; heating the second mixture to form a gel; drying the gel to form a dried powder; calcining the dried powder in air to form a pre-catalyst; and subjecting the pre-catalyst to hydrogen (H2) gas for forming the CeO2 supported palladium catalyst; and
   (2) method of producing dimethyl ether from carbon-dioxide (CO2) and H2, involves contacting a stream comprising CO2 and H2 with the CeO2 supported palladium catalyst of claim 1 in a reactor, for producing dimethyl ether and at least one side product selected from the group consisting of methanol, carbon monoxide, and methane, where a molar ratio of the H2 to the CO2 ranges from 1:1 to 6:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The promoter and the catalytic material are in the form of particles. The CeO2 supported palladium catalyst has a surface area of 50-100 m2/g. The atomic ratio of cerium to palladium ranges from 7:1 to 50:1. The molar ratio of the citric acid to a total content of the Ce(III) salt and Pd(II) salt ranges from 2:1 to 10:1, molar ratio of the Pd(II) salt to Ca(II) salt ranges from 1:50 to 75:1, and molar ratio of the Ce(III) salt to the Pd(II) salt ranges from 5:1 to 500:1. The stream has a gas hourly space velocity of 1,800-12,000 mL/g/hour. The stream is contacted with the CeO2 supported palladium catalyst at a temperature of 250-400 degrees C. The stream is contacted with the CeO2 supported palladium catalyst at a pressure of 1-30 bar. The stream further comprises an inert carrier gas, and 7-80 mol.% CO2 present in the stream is converted to dimethyl ether and one side product. The selectivity for dimethyl ether converted from the CO2 ranges from 18-80%. The selectivity for carbon monoxide converted from the CO2 is less than 22%, selectivity for methane converted from the CO2 is less than 20%, and selectivity for methanol converted from the CO2 is less than 30%. The conversion of CO2 and a selectivity for dimethyl ether are maintained over a reaction period of 90 or more hours. A selectivity for dimethyl ether converted from CO2 ranges from 18-80%, selectivity for carbon monoxide converted from the CO2 is less than 22%, selectivity for methane converted from the CO2 is less than 20%, and selectivity for methanol converted from the CO2 is less than 30%. Preferred Components: The Ce(III) salt is cerium(III) nitrate; Pd(II) salt is palladium(II) nitrate, and Ca(II) salt is calcium(II) nitrate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Device for comprehensive utilization of hot steel            slag, comprises steel slag processing unit, carbonation            and iron recovery unit, and heat energy comprehensive            unit, where steel slag processing unit is connected to            carbonation unit.
AU ZHU T
   LI Y

   XIONG J

AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 202008421V
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a steel slag processing unit,                a carbonation unit, an iron recovery unit, a                tailings collection unit, and a heat energy                comprehensive utilization unit, where the outlet of                the steel slag processing unit is connected to the                inlet of the carbonation unit. The air outlet of                the steel slag processing unit is connected to the                air inlet of the thermal energy comprehensive                utilization unit. The outlet of the carbonation                unit is connected to the inlet of the iron recovery                unit. The slag outlet of the iron recovery unit is                connected to the feed inlet of the tailings                collection unit.
   USE - Device for comprehensive utilization of hot                steel slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes the efficient recycling of                resource and heat energy in the steel slag.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for comprehensive utilization of hot steel slag,                which involves:
   (A) cooling the hot steel slag at 1600-1700                degrees C to 500-800 degrees C, using carbon                dioxide gas, and then spraying and pulverizing with                intermittent water spray to obtain powdered steel                slag;
   (B) carbonizing the steel powder slag at a                temperature of 300-400 degrees C in a carbon                dioxide atmosphere to obtain a carbonized steel                slag;
   (C) magnetically selecting the steel slag                having a carbonation temperature of 50-100 degrees                C to obtain iron-containing substances and                tailings, using iron-containing substances for                sintering or steelmaking; and
   (D) using tailings for raw materials for                cement or roadbed materials, collecting unused                carbon dioxide during the cooling process and the                carbonation process and then returning for                reuse.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of the                device. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

PT P
PN CN110479283-A; CN110479283-B
TI Preparing catalyst with nickel supported on surface of copper ferrite spinel for producing low-carbon olefins, by dissolving iron salt, copper salt and chelating dispersant, evaporating water and supporting nickel salt on powder surface.
AU LIU J
   ZHANG J
   YANG Y
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019A1279N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing catalyst with nickel supported on surface of copper ferrite spinel, involves (i) dissolving iron salt, copper salt and chelating dispersant in deionized water, and adding organic alcohol to form complex ions of metal cations, (ii) fully mixing the solution obtained in step (i), evaporating water in the solution to obtain a colloid, drying colloid and calcining at 600-800 degrees C for 3-5 hours, so that the chelating dispersant generates gas and is released, while the complex ion reacts to generate spinel phase copper ferrite, and grinding copper ferrite into powder, (iii) dissolving nickel salt in deionized water, adding the powder of step (ii), so that the nickel salt is supported on the surface of the powder, evaporating water, and calcining at 300-500 degrees C for 3-5 hours, so that the nickel salt generates an oxide load on the surface of the powder, and (iv) grinding the product obtained in step (iii) to obtain the target product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing catalyst with nickel supported on surface of copper ferrite spinel for a catalyst of carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction to produce low-carbon olefins (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts simple preparation method, and has excellent catalyst structure performance, high carbon dioxide conversion efficiency, and excellent target product selection performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a catalyst prepared by the method; and
   (2) a use of the catalyst for a catalyst of carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction to produce low-carbon olefins, where the number of carbon atoms of the low-carbon olefins is 2-5.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron salt comprises iron nitrate and/or iron sulfate. The copper salt comprises copper nitrate and/or copper sulfate. The chelating dispersant comprises citric acid, oxalic acid and/or urea. Preferred Method: The mixing step (ii) involves uniformly stirring at 50-80 degrees C for 1-4 hours and evenly mixing the mixture. The step (iii) involves supporting the nickel salt on the surface of the powder by ultrasonically stirring at 30-50 degrees C for 10-12 hours. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of the amount of the metal cation to the substance of the chelating dispersant in step (i) is 1-1.2. The ratio of the amount of copper ions and iron ions is (0.2-1):(2-2.8). The ratio of the amount of substances of nickel ions and copper ions is (0.2-1):(1-1.2). The temperature for preparing the low-carbon olefin by carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction is 400-500 degrees C, and the mass fraction of 1-pentene in the low-carbon olefin is greater than 90%. Preferred Properties: The particle size of the powder obtained in step (ii) is less than 75 mu m. The particle diameter of the catalyst obtained in step (iv) is less than 75 mu m.

PT P
PN RU2698858-C1
TI Method of endovideosurgical access to thyroid lobe has one point is located in projection of crest of greater tubercle of humerus.
AU VERTYANKIN S V
   TURLYKOVA I A
   MESHCHERYAKOV V L
   GREKOV V V
   BORISOV A K
AE UNIV SARAT STATE MEDICAL (UYSA-Non-standard)
GA 2019832011
AB    NOVELTY - Invention refers to medicine, namely to endoscopic endocrine surgery, thyroid surgery and can be used for endovideosurgical access to the thyroid lobe. Three points for introduction of trocars are determined. One point is located in projection of crest of greater tubercle of humerus. Second point is along the upper medial edge of the periareolary area on the ipsilateral breast. Third point is located along the external edge of the greater pectoral muscle at the intersection with the bisector of the surgical action angle formed by the lines connecting the first and second points to the projection point of the involved thyroid gland, respectively. Primary cavity above the clavicle and behind the lateral edge of the pectoral-clavicular-mastoid muscle is created from the third point using an endoscope to connect all trocars. That is followed by dissection, enlarging the primary cavity, and creating an operating cavity for visualizing a portion of the thyroid. Initially, prior to the introduction of the trocar, a Veress needle is inserted into the third point under the subcutaneous fascia in the direction of the thyroid lobe through which carbon dioxide is injected. Carbon dioxide is injected at 16-20 mm Hg. Primary cavity is created by a tube of an endoscope introduced into the trocar, with simultaneous palpation of the pectoris-clavicle-mastoid muscle and insufflation of carbon dioxide. Prior to introduction of tools into trocars from two points, probe-palpator is used.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Method enables performing endovideosurgical access to the thyroid lobe and improving the surgical outcome of the patients suffering a thyroid pathology, improving the safety of the surgical intervention by the technique of determining the points and introducing three trocars to improve visualization of the operating cavity, and eliminating the conflict of the instruments.

PN CN109880715-A
TI Wine comprises kiwi fruit, glutinous rice or rice, water, citric acid, food flavor and carbon dioxide.
AU WANG P
AE SICHUAN FENGHE YELLOW WINE CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201954927L
AB    NOVELTY - Wine comprises 5-43% kiwi fruit, 6-22% glutinous rice or rice, 38-71% water, 0.01-0.36% citric acid, 0.01-0.17% food flavor and 0.01-0.34% carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine is rich in nutrition and has good mouth feel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for processing wine, comprising: (i) placing glutinous rice or rice into the water, washing 2-3 times, placing in water with a temperature of 20-25 degrees C, soaking for 10-14 hours, removing and draining the water; (ii) placing the glutinous rice or drained rice into the steamer, steaming to 100 degrees C at high flame, boiling at low flame for 20-25 minutes, boiling the water, cooking for 5 minutes, removing the laying and cooling to 28-31 degrees C, placing steamed glutinous rice or rice into a fermenting tank that is cleaned and disinfected; (iii) mixing uniformly saccharified yeast and yeast in the glutinous rice or rice of the fermenting tank, controlling the fermentation temperature at 28-32 degrees C, carrying out the incubation for 28-36 hours to complete the separation of glutinous rice or rice and fermenting, rinsing the fresh kiwifruit simultaneously with the fermentation time of glutinous rice or rice, placing in another tank, mixing uniformly with saccharified yeast and yeast, controlling the fermentation temperature at 28-32 degrees C, covering the fermentation for 28-36 hours to complete the separate fermentation of kiwifruit; (iv) mixing the separately fermented glutinous rice or rice with kiwi fermented mash in a fermentation tank and mixing uniformly, maintaining at 28-30 degrees C, carrying out combined fermentation, dropping 2 degrees C every day until normal temperature and completing fermentation process after 25-30 day; and (v) pressing, sterilizing, storing, mixing citric acid and edible fragrance, , filtering, mixing carbon dioxide and filling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The wine comprises 24% kiwi fruit, 17% glutinous rice or rice, 58.6% water, 0.09% citric acid, 0.15% food flavor and 0.16% carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The boiling water in the pot is 5-10% of the weight of the glutinous rice or rice and the water temperature is 80-100 degrees C.
