PN WO2019034648-A1; KR2020038938-A; CN111032639-A; EP3668849-A1; US2020369640-A1; JP2020535116-W; EP3668849-B1; US11292780-B2
TI Preparing cyclic carbonates used as reactive            diluents involves reacting propargylic alcohol with            carbon dioxide in presence of transition metal catalyst            comprising metals of specific groups and bulky ligand            e.g. organic ligand.
AU BAYARMAGNAI B
   SCHAUB T
   MORMUL V
   RUDOLF P
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 201917974Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonates (la) or (lb) or                their mixtures, comprises (a) reacting a                propargylic alcohol (II) with carbon dioxide in the                presence of at least one transition metal catalyst                TMC1 , which comprises a transition metal                comprising metals of groups 10, 11 and 12 elements                and at least one bulky ligand comprising group of                ligands consisting organic group containing                compound (III) and nitrogen containing compound                (IV).
   USE - The process is useful for preparing (la)                and/or (lb) which is used as reactive diluent in                the manufacture of epoxy resins, as electrolyte                additive in electrochemical storage systems or as                monomer in polymerization reactions (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides (Ia) and/or (Ib) with                high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyclic carbonates of formula (la) or                (lb) or their mixtures, comprises (a) reacting a                propargylic alcohol of formula (II) with carbon                dioxide in the presence of at least one transition                metal catalyst TMC1 , which comprises a transition                metal comprising metals of groups 10, 11 and 12                elements and at least one bulky ligand comprising                group of ligands consisting organic group                containing compound of formula (III) and nitrogen                containing compound of formula (IV).
   R1 = H or 1-40C organic group;
   R2-R4 = 1-40C organic group;
   R5 = 1-40C organic group or R2;
   Z1 = divalent bridging group comprising                -CR7=CR8-, -CR7=N-, -CR7R9-CR8R10- or                -CR7R9-CR8R10-CR11R12-; either
   R7-R12 = H or R5; or
   R7R8, R10R11 = monocyclic or polycyclic,                optionally substituted, aliphatic or aromatic ring                system which has 4-40C atoms and can also comprise                at least one Si, Ge, N, P, O or S; and
   D1 = P, As or Sb.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                cyclic carbonate of formula (laa) or (lbb).
   R1a = -CH2OR13; and
   R13 = H, SiH3 or 1-40C organic group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The transition metal                  catalyst TMC1 is prepared in situ by using a                  transition metal compound, which does not                  comprise any bulky ligand, (III) or (IV) as bulky                  ligand or the protonated form of (IV) is nitrogen                  containing compound of formula (V) together with                  a base. The process step (a) is performed at a                  pressure of 1-50 bar. The process step (a) is                  performed at a temperature of 0-100 degrees C.                  The process step (a) is carried out in the                  presence of a solvent comprising aliphatic                  hydrocarbons, aromatic hydrocarbons, halogenated                  hydrocarbons, amides, ureas, nitriles,                  sulfoxides, sulfones, esters, carbonates, ethers                  and/or alcohols. (la) and/or (lb) are separated                  from the transition metal catalyst TMC1 after                  process step (a) via distillation. The transition                  metal catalyst TMC1 is recycled to the reaction                  step (a) after (la) and/or (lb) were removed via                  distillation.X1- = anion equivalent.Preferred Components: The transition metal                  of transition metal catalyst TMC1 is silver. The                  transition metal compound comprises silver                  acetate, silver(I) fluoride, silver(I) oxide or                  silver(I) carbonate. The bulky ligand is (III).                  The bulky ligand comprises e.g.                  (2'-dicyclohexylphosphanyl-biphenyl-2-yl)-dimethyl-amine,                  dicyclohexyl-(2'-methyl-biphenyl-2-yl)-phosphane,                  dicyclohexyl-(2',4',6'-triisopropyl-biphenyl-2-yl)-phosphane,                  (2'-(di-t-butyl-phosphanyl)-biphenyl-2-yl)-dimethyl-amine,                  2'-diphenylphosphanyl-Nasterisk2asterisk,Nasterisk2asterisk,Nasterisk6asterisk,Nasterisk6asterisk-tetramethyl-biphenyl-2,6-diamine                  or biphenyl-2-yl-di-t-butyl-phosphane,                  (1,1')binaphthalenyl-2-yl-di-tert-butyl-phosphane                  and their mixtures. The molar ratio of the bulky                  ligand to the transition metal of transition                  metal catalyst TMC1 is 0.4-1.2. The amount of                  transition metal catalyst TMC1 used in process                  step (a) based on the amount of (II) is 0.005-5                  mole.%.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1 = H, hydroxymethyl, acetoxy-methylene,                formyloxy-methylene or -CH2-O-C(O)OCH3.
   EXAMPLE - In a chemspeed accelerator slt 106                high-throughput robot system. 1 mmol                4-Hydroxybut-2-yn-1-y acetate, 0.05 mmol silver                acetate and 0.05 mmol                (2'-dicyclohexylphosphanyl-biphenyl-2-yl)-dimethyl-amine                in 2 ml acetonitrile were pressurized with carbon                dioxide (20 bar) and stirred at room temperature                for 12 hours. After releasing the carbon dioxide                overpressure, 1 mmol anisole and 1 ml deuterated                chloroform were added to the reaction mixture and                stirred for 5 minutes to give                (E)-2-(2-oxo-1,3-dioxolan-4-ylidene)ethyl                acetate.
DC A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. PN WO2018175637-A1; US2018271826-A1; AU2018237160-A1; CA3056768-A1; EP3600246-A1
TI Dry powder useful for forming wafer, and for            delivering cannabinoid into lung, comprises            cannabinoid, a polymer binding agent, a dispersing            agent, and a bulking agent, and optionally an            antioxidant.
AU SIEVERS R
   REBITS L
   SIEVERS R E
AE COLORADO CAN LLC (COLO-Non-standard)
   COLORADO CAN LLC (COLO-Non-standard)
   SIEVERS R E (SIEV-Individual)
   REBITS L (REBI-Individual)
   COLORADO CAN LLC (COLO-Non-standard)
   COLORADO CAN LLC (COLO-Non-standard)
GA 201875488D
AB    NOVELTY - Dry powder comprises at least one cannabinoid,                a polymer binding agent, a dispersing agent, and a                bulking agent, and optionally an antioxidant. The                dry powder is formed by carbon dioxide-assisted                nebulization and drying in a flowing stream of gas.                The dry powder has an aerodynamic particle                distribution effective for delivery of the dry                powder by respiration into a lung of a                patient.
   USE - The dry powder is useful for forming wafer,                and for delivering cannabinoid into a lung of a                patient (all claimed). No biological data                given.
   ADVANTAGE - The dry powder exhibits increased                bioavailability and/or storage stability of a                cannabinoid; is safer, more accurate and more                reliable than those administered by combustion; and                can provide rapid delivery of cannabinoids to the                bloodstream for expeditious relief.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a wafer formed by compressing the dry                powder;
   (2) a method (M1) for preparing the dry powder                comprising a cannabinoid, comprising subjecting a                solution of a volatile component, a cannabinoid, a                polymer binding agent, a dispersing agent and a                bulking agent, and optionally an antioxidant, to                carbon dioxide-assisted nebulization, and drying                droplets formed by the nebulization in a flowing                dry gas stream to form a dry powder, where the dry                powder has an aerodynamic particle distribution                effective for delivery of the dry powder by                respiration into a lung of a patient; and
   (3) a method (M2) for increasing the                bioavailability and/or storage stability of a                cannabinoid, comprising subjecting a solution of a                volatile component, a cannabinoid, a polymer                binding agent, a dispersing agent, and a bulking                agent, and optionally an antioxidant, to carbon                dioxide-assisted nebulization, and drying droplets                formed by the nebulization in a flowing dry gas                stream to form a dry powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flowing gas stream                  comprises gaseous nitrogen or gaseous carbon                  dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The dry powder is                  formed by carbon dioxide-assisted nebulization of                  a solution comprising a volatile component, a                  cannabinoid, a polymer binding agent, a                  dispersing agent, and a bulking agent, and                  optionally an antioxidant. The dry powder                  comprises at least two cannabinoids. The dry                  powder comprises at least 30% of particles having                  a size of less than 5.8 mu m as modeled by an                  Andersen Cascade Impactor according to US                  Pharmacopeia-601. Preferred Components: The                  volatile component comprises at least one of                  ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate (both                  preferred), triethylammonium bicarbonate,                  trimethylammonium carbonate, trimethylammonium                  bicarbonate, ammonium acetate, triethylammonium                  acetate, trimethylammonium acetate, ammonium                  formate, trimethylammonium formate, and                  triethylammonium formate. The dispersing agent                  comprises at least one of methionine (preferred),                  alanine, arginine, asparagine, aspartic acid,                  cysteine, glutamic acid, glutamine, glycine,                  histidine, isoleucine, leucine, lysine,                  phenylalanine, proline, serine, threonine,                  tryptophan, tyrosine, valine, vitamin A, vitamin                  C, vitamin E, alpha -carotene, astaxanthin, beta                  -carotene, canthaxanthin, lutein, lycopene,                  zeaxanthin, flavonoid, cinnamic acid, chicoric                  acid, chlorogenic acid, rosmarinic acid,                  curcumin, xanthone, eugenol, citric acid, oxalic                  acid, and lipoic acid. The bulking agent                  comprises at least one of mannitol (preferred),                  glucose, galactose, fructose, mannose, allose,                  altrose, fucose, gulose, sorbose, tagatose,                  arabinose, lyxose, rhamnose, ribose, xylose,                  erythrose, threose, lactose, maltose, sucrose,                  trehalose, lactulose, cellobiose, chitobiose,                  allolactose, sucralose, mannobiose, maltitol,                  sorbitol, xylitol, erythritol, isomalt, arabitol,                  ribitol, galactitol, fucitol, iditol,                  myo-inositol, volemitol, lactitol, maltotriitol,                  maltotetraitol, maltodextrin, and polyglycitol.                  The cannabinoid comprises cannabidiol. The                  cannabinoid is delta -9-tetrahydrocannabinol. The                  volatile component comprises a volatile salt. The                  wafer comprises a terpene. The terpene is infused                  in the wafer.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer binding                  agent comprises at least one of                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyethylene glycol,                  poly(lactic-co-glycolic) acid (all preferred),                  polyvinyl alcohol, polyacrylic acid,                  N-(2-hydroxypropyl) methacrylamide,                  polyoxazoline, polyphosphazene, xanthan gum, gum                  Arabic, pectin, chitosan derivative, dextran,                  carrageenan, guar gum, cellulose ether,                  hyaluronic acid, albumin, and starch. The polymer                  binding agent comprises polyvinylpyrrolidone,                  polyethylene glycol and/or                  poly(lactic-co-glycolic) acid.
   ACTIVITY - Respiratory-Gen.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Tetrahydrocannabinol was decarboxylated by                placing 200 mg into a glass vial and heating in an                oven at 110-115 degrees C for 110 minutes. The                resulting material was resuspended in 1 ml of                methanol by sonication for 15 minutes. A                methanol/water solution (7:3 methanol:water)                comprising 3.2 wt.% total dissolved solids was                made, half of which was ammonium bicarbonate. The                remaining dissolved solids were composed of 40 wt.%                of the described decarboxylated material, 10%                methionine, 10% polyvinylpyrrolidone, and 40%                mannitol. The solution was dried to obtain the dry                powder.

A second gas introduction port introduces an exhaust gas into the main portion. A piston controls the internal pressure of the main portion and is provided in a top portion of the main portion. A high voltage discharge apparatus is provided in a bottom side surface of the piston.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide fixation apparatus.

The greenhouse effect by the influence on the sea ecology by brine and emission of exhaust gas is prevented. Since the sub products are not required for an industrial production process, the installation in a middle-small sized factory is eased. Since the apparatus is lower-cost than waste processing of the solid and the liquid, and a thin film, the environmental pollution is dropped effectively. The sulfide in the exhaust gas is removed by using the desulfurizing-apparatus for the second gas introduction port, the efficiency of reaction is improved, and is avoided from the harm to the corrosive installation and environment by occurrence of an oxidized substance.

PN CN107794164-A
TI Wine recipe comprises purple sweet potato, sticky rice or rice, water, citric acid, flavor composition and carbon dioxide, and prepared by washing sticky rice or rice with water, elutriating sticky rice or rice in water, and soaking.
AU WANG P
   WANG H
AE SICHUAN FENGHE YELLOW WINE CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2018221585
AB    NOVELTY - Wine recipe comprises 15-60 wt.% purple sweet potato, 5-15 wt.% sticky rice or rice, 35-70 wt.% water, 0.03-0.3 wt.% citric acid, 0.02-0.23 wt.% flavor composition and 0.05-0.33 wt.% of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Wine recipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine recipe has unique taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing wine, which involves:
   (A) washing sticky rice or rice with water for 2-3 times, putting at 20-23 degrees C for elutriating sticky rice or rice in water, soaking for 10-14 hours, fishing out and draining moisture;
   (B) putting sticky rice or rice into steamer, boiling to 100 degrees C by big fire, boiling on small fire 20-25 minutes, then boiling for 5 minutes, taking out, cooling to 28-31 degrees C, putting steamed sticky rice or rice into clean and disinfected fermenting crock;
   (C) uniformly mixing saccharified yeast and yeast in fermentation jar, fermenting at 28-32 degrees C, preserving temperature and fermenting for 30-36 hours to obtain fermented sticky rice or rice;
   (D) cleaning fresh purple sweet potato, cutting 1.5-2.5 cm pieces, steaming, cooling to 28-31 degrees C, putting into another crock, uniformly mixing saccharified yeast and yeast, fermenting at 28-32 degrees C for 30-36 hours to obtain fermented purple sweet potato; and
   (E) fermenting separated sticky rice or rice and sweet potato, mixing in fermentation cylinder, combining at 28 degrees C to ferment, reducing to 2 degrees C every day for 25-30 days, finishing fermentation, squeezing, sterilizing, storing, mixing citric acid and edible essence, filtering, mixing carbon dioxide, and filling to obtain wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The wine recipe comprises 50 wt.% purple sweet potato, 10 wt.% sticky rice or rice, 39.77 wt.% water, 0.06 wt.% citric acid, 0.01 wt.% flavor composition and 0.23 wt.% of carbon dioxide. Preferred Condition: The temperature of water is 80-100 degrees C.

TI Preparation of nickel-based catalyst for reducing carbon deposition during reforming methane and carbon dioxide to produce synthesis gas, involves mixing silicon source, structure-directing agent, mineralizer and water.
AU HE D
   WANG J
   LIN L
   JIN C
AE NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 201820082E
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing nickel-based catalyst, involves dissolving nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate in deionized water to obtain nickel salt precursor solution, mixing silicon source, structure-directing agent, mineralizer and water to obtain molecular sieve mother liquor, adding nickel salt precursor solution, crystallizing, washing the resulting hydrothermal solid product, drying to obtain nickel-based molecular sieve solid powder, calcining the solid powder in calciner and naturally cooling to obtain nickel-based molecular sieve catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing nickel-based catalyst for reducing carbon deposition during reforming methane and carbon dioxide to produce synthesis gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of nickel-based catalyst with high activity, developed pore structure and large specific surface area, and effectively reduces the deposition of carbon deposits, during reaction of methane and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing nickel-based catalyst, involves (a) dissolving nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate in deionized water to obtain nickel salt precursor solution, (b) mixing silicon source, structure-directing agent, mineralizer and water to obtain molecular sieve mother liquor, (c) adding nickel salt precursor solution to molecular sieve mother liquor, mixing, transferring to stainless steel reaction vessel with PTFE liner, crystallizing at 150-200 degrees C for 7 days, washing the resulting hydrothermal solid product for multiple times and drying to obtain nickel-based molecular sieve solid powder, (d) calcining the solid powder in calciner at 800 degrees C for 2 hours, naturally cooling to obtain nickel-based molecular sieve catalyst. The concentration of nickel salt precursor solution in nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate is 0.1-0.4 mol/L. The molar ratio of hydroxide ion, silicon dioxide, structure-directing agent, mineralizer and water is (0.1-3):1:(0.10-0.35):(0.1-3):(5-50).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The quality of nickel salt precursor in molecular sieve mother liquor is 1-5%. The silicon source is selected from tetraethyl orthosilicate, white carbon and silica sol. The mineralizer is selected from sodium hydroxide and sodium chloride. Preferred Conditions: The crystallization process is carried out at 170 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The structure-directing agent is selected from tetrapropylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium hydroxide and triethylamine.

PN CN107676062-A
TI Method for exploiting natural gas hydrate, involves carrying out pressure reduction exploitation on wells, and performing displacement exploration by injecting carbon dioxide and chemical agents into natural gas hydrate storage section.
AU YUAN P
   ZHANG X
AE DAQING DONGYOU RUIJIA PETROLEUM TECHNOLO (DAQI-Non-standard)
GA 2018144688
AB    NOVELTY - Method for exploiting natural gas hydrate, involves arranging row-type horizontal well pattern, combining geological data, arranging row-type horizontal well pattern for target storage, dividing horizontal well into injection well and production well, carrying out matched well completion operation, arranging production well matched ground system, carrying out pressure reduction exploitation on injection well and production well, carrying out displacement exploration by injecting carbon dioxide and chemical agents into natural gas hydrate storage section.
   USE - Method for exploiting natural gas hydrate by virtue of mixed-phase flooding of row-type horizontal well.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves gas production efficiency, improves final recovery rate and increases economic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for exploiting natural gas hydrate by virtue of mixed-phase flooding of row-type horizontal well, involves arranging row-type horizontal well pattern, combining geological data, arranging row-type horizontal well pattern for target storage, dividing horizontal well into injection well and production well, improving storage capacity of natural gas hydrate by arranging horizontal well in row-type manner, (ii) carrying out matched well completion operation after the arrangement of row-type horizontal well pattern is completed, (iii) arranging production well matched ground system after the well completion operation is carried out, then carrying out pressure reduction exploitation on the injection well and the production well until economic benefit is achieved, (iv) arranging an injection system which comprises a ground supply system and an underground injection system, (v) carrying out displacement exploration by injecting carbon dioxide and chemical agents into the natural gas hydrate storage section through the injection well, and collecting natural gas and formation water from the production well.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In displacement exploration step, 0.3 times the pore volume of gaseous carbon dioxide is injected into the gas hydrate storage, then 0.1 times the pore volume of chemical agents are injected, and carbon dioxide and chemical agents are injected alternately at the mentioned rates of injection, and natural gas and formation water are collected from production well. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is gaseous carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The chemical agents include 5 %mass methanol, 5 %mass ethanol, 3 %mass dodecyltrimethylammonium chloride, and remaining source water.

PT P
PN CN111296465-A
TI High-efficiency herbicidal effervescent tablet            used for preventing e.g. Cyperus sedge, comprises            mefenacet, penoxsulam, mesotrione, wetting and            dispersing agent, solid acid, carbon dioxide source,            desiccant, binder, glidant and filler.
AU ZHAO X
   WANG Y
   ZHU L
   XU L
   DONG J
   JIANG W
   ZHOU Y
AE SHANDONG GREENLAND CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202059129B
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficiency herbicidal effervescent tablet                comprises mefenacet, penoxsulam and mesotrione as                active ingredients and wetting and dispersing                agent, solid acid, carbon dioxide source,                desiccant, binder, glidant and filler as additive                agent.
   USE - The effervescent tablet is useful for                preventing barnyard grass, Cyperus sedge,                Monochoria korsakowii, Sagittaria trifolia, Alisma                plantago-aquatica, Leptochloa chinensis and                Ammannia baccifera (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The effervescent tablet exhibits                high-efficiency and broad-spectrum weeding                performance and has excellent effects on removing                malignant weeds barnyard grass, Cyperus sedge,                M.korsakowii, S.trifolia, A.plantago-aquatica,                L.chinensis and A.baccifera in rice fields and no                adverse effect on rice.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the effervescent tablet involving (i)                weighing mefenacet, penoxsulam, mesotrione, the                wetting and dispersing agent, the solid acid and                the filler, adding to a vertical mixer, stirring                and mixing for 30 minutes, turning on an airflow                crusher, controlling an airflow pressure to 0.6-0.8                MPa and performing airflow crushing to obtain an                acid powder, (ii) adding the binder to prepare a                wet acid particle, (iii) drying the wet acid                particles by a boiling dryer to obtain dried acid                particles, where the temperature is controlled to                be 60-80 degrees C and the moisture content is less                than 1%, (iv) putting the dried acid particles, the                carbon dioxide source, the glidant and the                desiccant into a mixer, mixing for 30 minutes,                putting into a tablet press to obtain the tablet                and controlling the weight of the effervescent                tablet to be 1-5 g and (v) weighing the                effervescent tablet according to production                requirements to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The wetting and                  dispersing agent is one or mixture of naphthalene                  sulfonate, sodium lauryl sulfate, nekal powder,                  sodium lignosulfonate, alkylphenol                  polyoxyethylene ether, succinic acid sulfonate,                  dodecylbenzene sulfonate, polycarboxylate and                  fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether. The solid                  acid is one or a mixture of tartaric acid, citric                  acid, succinic acid and adipic acid. The carbon                  dioxide source is one or a mixture of sodium                  bicarbonate, sodium carbonate, calcium carbonate,                  potassium carbonate and potassium bicarbonate.                  The desiccant is one or a mixture of anhydrous                  calcium oxide, anhydrous calcium chloride,                  anhydrous calcium sulfate, and anhydrous sodium                  sulfate. The binder is one or a mixture of                  polyethylene glycol, starch, polyvinyl alcohol,                  dextrin, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, guar gum and xanthan gum.                  The glidant is one or a mixture of magnesium                  stearate, sodium benzoate and magnesium dodecyl                  sulfate. The filler is one or a mixture of                  kaolin, bentonite, attapulgite, corn starch,                  ammonium sulfate, microcrystalline cellulose,                  talc powder, white carbon black and lactose.                  Preferred Composition: The effervescent tablet                  comprises 10-30 %mass mefenacet, 0.5-5 %mass                  penoxsulam, 1-10 %mass mesotrione, 1-10 %mass                  wetting and dispersing agent, 10-30 %mass solid                  acid, 10-30 %mass carbon dioxide source, 0-5                  %mass desiccant, 1-5 %mass binder, 0-5 %mass                  glidant and 0-50 %mass filler. The effervescent                  tablet comprises 18 %mass mefenacet, 1 %mass                  penoxsulam, 3 %mass mesotrione, 5 %mass sodium                  lignosulfonate, 6 %mass sodium lauryl sulfate, 3                  %mass polyethylene glycol, 20 %mass tartaric                  acid, 20 %mass sodium bicarbonate, 1 %mass                  magnesium stearate and 23 %mass kaolin.
   ACTIVITY - Herbicide.

PN US2020179865-A1; US10730005-B2
TI Selective adsorption system, comprises adsorbent bed having adsorbents having porous material having carbon, and byproduct stream which is substantially comprised of remaining components from mixed gas stream.
AU HO J G S
   COOPER J C
AE W-N APACHE CORP (WNAP-Non-standard)
GA 2020506745
AB    NOVELTY - Selective adsorption system (S1) comprises: at least one adsorbent bed, where the adsorbent bed comprising adsorbents including porous material comprising carbon, the selective adsorption system is operable to continuously and simultaneously separate components of a mixed gas stream comprising carbon dioxide into a substantially pure carbon dioxide stream by selective adsorption of carbon dioxide at pressures of greater than 1 bar versus other gases in the mixed gas stream, and a byproduct stream being substantially comprised of remaining components from the mixed gas stream, where the porous material exhibits pore diameters of 1-2 nm creating at least 0.3 cm3/g of pore volume.
   USE - Used as selective adsorption systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The systems utilize porous material that exhibits an oxygen content of more than 4 wt.% as measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, exhibits a nitrogen content of 1-12 (preferably at least 1) wt.% as measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy and enhances selectivity of the porous material to adsorb gases heavier than ethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an adsorption system (S2), comprising at least one adsorbent bed comprising an adsorbent porous material, where the adsorbent bed is operable at pressures at greater than or equal to 1 bar, and the adsorption system is operable to separate components of a feed gas stream into a substantially pure target hydrocarbon stream and a product stream being substantially comprised of components with a greater molecular weight than the target hydrocarbon stream, the adsorbent bed comprises at least one material comprising carbon-based adsorbents, silica gels, activated aluminas, zeolite imidazole frameworks (ZIFs), metal organic frameworks (MOFs), molecular sieves and/or other zeolites, and the material exhibits pore diameters of 1-2 nm crating at least 0.3 cm3/g of pore volume.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous material exhibits a surface area of at least 1200 m2/g and a total pore volume of at least 0.8 cm3/g. At least 40% of pores in the porous material exhibit diameters of 1-2 nm. The porous material comprises a heteroatom comprising oxygen, nitrogen and/or sulfur. The more than 40% of pores in the porous material exhibit a diameter of less than 2 nm, where the mixed gas stream comprises a hydrocarbon power production mixed exhaust gas. The porous material comprises carbon is a porous carbon material with a carbon content of 75-95 wt.% as measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy. The porous material selectively adsorbs carbon dioxide at pressures of greater than 5 bar, preferably greater than 1 bar.

   USE - Used as a heat insulated graphite polystyrene foam extruded sheet.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing heat insulated graphite polystyrene foam extruded sheet (a) taking the raw materials in the bin, (b) preliminary melting by mixing polystyrene, filler, plasticizer, and antioxidant in the bin, adding into the storage barrel of the extruder, heating to melt, pouring the raw materials in the molten state into the stirring device and continuously heating, (c) adding the expanded graphite, aluminum hydroxide, phosphorus compound, silicon flame retardant and zinc borate to the stirring device, stirring to mix, heating, adding carbon dioxide foaming agent under high pressure for mixing, extruding using an extruder and cooling, (d) pulling into a trimming machine, cutting the edge of the formed sheet, secondary processing of grooving, embossing, brushing and peeling based on the requirement and lateral cutting based on the length of the formed sheet, (e) drawning into the product roll stand by the tractor to perform secondary cooling, automatic palletizing based on the predetermined quantity, conveying into a packaging machine for packaging and transferring to warehouse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The graphite polystyrene foam extruded sheet comprises 79 pts. wt. polystyrene, 4 pts. wt. carbon dioxide foaming agent, 2 pts. wt. filler, 0.7 pts. wt. plasticizer, 0.8 pts. wt. antioxidant, 3 pts. wt. expanded graphite, 2 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide, 2 pts. wt. phosphorus compound, 2 pts. wt. silicon flame retardant and 4.5 pts. wt. zinc borate. Preferred Components: The filler is wood flour. The plasticizer is phthalate ester. The antioxidant is polyolefin.

PN CN110577553-A; CN110577553-B
TI Terbium complex used as heterogeneous catalyst for chemical fixation of carbon dioxide to cyclic carbonate, has three-dimensional porous structure.
AU HE H
   LI C
   DU M
AE UNIV TIANJIN NORMAL (UTJN-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN NORMAL (UTJN-C)
GA 202000099U
AB    NOVELTY - Terbium complex has three-dimensional porous structure.
   USE - Terbium complex used as heterogeneous catalyst for chemical fixation of carbon dioxide to cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The terbium complex has a catalytic site and a rich pore structure, and is a good catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Terbium complex of linear formula (Tb6L2.(2-FBA)2.(H2O)6.( mu -OH)8)n has three-dimensional porous structure, where ( mu -OH) is OH-, which has bridging function, 2-FBA is 2-fluorobenzoic acid, and L is a deprotonated 3,3',5,5 -tetra (4-carboxyphenyl) -4,4 -biphenyldiamine anionic ligand of formula (I).. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing terbium complex, which involves adding 3,3',5,5 -tetra(4-carboxyphenyl)-4,4 -biphenyldiamine anionic ligand of formula (I) and N,N-dimethylformamide to 2-fluorobenzoic acid and terbium (III) metal salt, stirring to make it uniformly dispersed, heating to 100-120 degrees C for 3-6 days, cooling down to room temperature of 20-25 degrees C, filtering to obtain brown massive single crystal of terbium complex of linear formula (Tb6L2.(2-FBA)2.(H2O)6.( mu -OH)8)n; and
   (2) a method for an application of terbium complex, which involves dispersing epichlorohydrin and tetrabutylammonium bromide in acetonitrile at a ratio of 1:(0.15-0:20), adding the terbium complex as a heterogeneous catalyst, performing reaction at 70-85 degrees C for 24-36 hours under an atmospheric pressure in a carbon dioxide environment at which epichlorohydrin is converted into a cyclic carbonate with a conversion rate of up to 100%, where terbium complex and epichlorohydrin are reacted in weight ratio of 50:(0.5-1.5), epichlorohydrin and acetonitrile are reacted in weight ratio of 1:(2-3) and terbium complex is used for 3-6 times in circulation as a heterogeneouscatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The terbium complex begins to decompose when heated above 450-500 degrees C and the remaining residue is a metal oxide terbium(III) oxide. The ratio of the amount of the material of the terbium (III) metal salt to the volume of the N,N-dimethylformamide is 1:(140-155). The temperature maintained for 3-6 days is 100-115 degrees C. The brown massive single crystals obtained after filtering are washed with N,N-dimethylformamide and dried at 20-25 degrees C. The method for uniformly dispersing the 3,3',5,5 -tetra(4-carboxyphenyl)-4,4 -biphenyldiamine, 2-fluorobenzoic acid, and terbium (III) metal salt in N,N-dimethylformamide is stirring at room temperature at 20-25 degrees C for at least 30 minutes.

TI Stabilization method of existing management type waste disposal site e.g. sea surface waste disposal site, involves embedding feed pipe, and supplying neutralizing gas which contains carbon dioxide from feed pipe.
AU UKAI K
   KASHIMA F
   TANAKA Y
AE GOYO KENSETSU KK (GOYK-C)
   PENTA OCEAN CONSTR CO LTD (PENT-Non-standard)
GA 201983480C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves accumulating the waste material deposit (3) in the waste accumulation layer. A feed pipe (8) is embedded so that an exhaust port (7) is located near the lower limit of a retained water level (5) of the waste accumulation layer. The neutralizing gas (9) containing carbon dioxide is supplied from the feed pipe. The waste accumulation layer includes a drainage pipe that collects leachate and drains the leachate into an internal water pond formed on the waste accumulation layer. The feed pipe is connected to the drainage pipe. The neutralizing gas is supplied from the feed pipe to the drainage pipe.
   USE - Stabilization method of existing management type waste disposal site e.g. sea surface waste disposal site.
   ADVANTAGE - The stabilization is achieved in a short period of time by supplying neutralizing gas containing carbon dioxide to the waste layer of an existing managed waste disposal site. The clogging due to the scale of the drainage pipe and the feed pipe is prevented. The stabilization of the high pH and chemical oxygen demand (COD) range is emphasized, and the disposal site is stabilized inexpensively and efficiently. The acceleration of stabilization of the range with high pH or the high range of COD is performed preponderantly. The handling and management is facilitated since no deleterious substances such as hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid are used, and adverse effects on the surrounding environment is suppressed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the stabilization method of the existing management type waste disposal site.
   Waste material deposit (3)
   Retained water level (5)
   Exhaust port (7)
   Feed pipe (8)
   Neutralizing gas (9)

PT P
PN CN109022533-A
TI Treating expired daily chemical washing products involves mixing liquid raw materials of expired daily cleaning products with urea, iron, magnesium, manganese, carbon dioxide, nickel, vitamin B1 and water, and mixture comprises urea.
AU ZHANG Z
   CHEN Y
   BAI J
   ZHENG X
   QI L
   LI J
   LIN X
AE SHANXI LONGPAN MICROBIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOG (SHAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU HUANLV ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5144D
AB    NOVELTY - Treating expired daily chemical washing products involves mixing liquid raw materials of expired daily cleaning products with urea, iron (Fe2+), magnesium (Mg2+), manganese (Mn2+), carbon dioxide (Co2+), nickel (Ni2+), vitamins B1 and water, where mixture is made so that the concentration of each component in the mixture is 20-30g/l Expired daily cleaning product, 5-15g/l urea , 5-12mg/l iron, 1-5mg/l magnesium, 3-8mg/l manganese, 40-60mg/l carbon dioxide, 10-20mg /l nickel, 1-5mg / l vitamin B1. The composite flora is added to inoculate the mixture, so that the concentration of the composite bacteria in the mixture is 60-200 g / l, using a baffled fermentation process, 30-37 degrees C temperature is controlled, and fermentated for 5-10 days. The composite flora comprises 35-50% acclimated activated sludge, 5-10% photosynthetic bacteria, 20-30% of methanogens, 5-15% Bacillus subtilis, and 5-15% Trichoderma.
   USE - Method for treating expired daily chemical washing products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat expired daily chemical washing product utilizes microbial degradation technology to degrade harmful components in expired daily-use detergents, meets national emission standards, and realizes harmless treatment of expired daily-use detergent products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The biogas slurry produced by fermentation enters the sedimentation tank for sedimentation and separation, and the supernatant liquid is directly discharged. The biogas residue is dried and used as a biological bacterial fertilizer, and the waste liquid of the purification tower is recycled and used. The biogas product produced by fermentation is subjected to purification through a purification tower, and then enters a gas storage tower, compressed, transported or directly used.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The solid raw material is dissolved and reconstituted when the raw material of the daily washing product is solid, and then mixed with urea, Fe2+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Co2+, Ni2+, B vitamins and water to form a mixture. The Fe2+, Mg2+, Mn2+, Co2+, and Ni2+ are added in the form of Fe, Mg, Mn, Co, and a chloride or sulfate of the Ni element. The fermentation process requires the addition of an antifoaming agent. The purification tower is provided with a unit for deamination, decarbonation, desulfurization and dehydration. Preferred Process: The activated sludge is domesticated by taking 100-300ml activated sludge from the sewage treatment port of the manufacturer of the washing product, adding 90-150ml of enrichment medium, and 4-8g of detergent product to make a culture solution, and sealed. The culture solution is is cultured and acclimated at 35-37 degrees C, the domestication is divided into 4-6 stages, and the domestication cycle is 4-6 weeks, the influence of the raw materials of the daily washing products on the activated sludge is gradually increased. The amount of the enriched medium is gradually reduced by half, and the amount of the detergent product is increased sequentially, each time by 0.5-2 g. The last time only detergent is added, no enrichment medium is added, until the amount of daily chemical washing product in the system is finally increased to 30 plus minus 2g/l, and the culture is continued for 7-10 days until the total organic carbon content in the culture solution is lower than 20mg/l, and domestication is completed, centrifuged the culture solution, discarded the supernatant, and the lower layer is the activated sludge after acclimation. Preferred Composition; The enriched medium comprises 0.5-3 g/l Beef cream, 0.5-2 g/l peptone , 0.2-1 g/l Monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4), 0.01-1.5 g/l Sodium Dihydrogen Phosphate Dodecahydrate (Na2HPO4 12H2O), 10-20 mg/l Fe2+, 50-100 mg/l Mg2+, 30-80 mg/l Mn2+, 80-200 mg/l Co2+, 31-10 mg/l Sodium selenite (Na2SeO), and balance is water.

A vacuum pump (105) is connected to the vacuum test cabinet and the controller. A molecule generator (103) is connected to the vacuum test cabinet and the controller is configured to control the vacuum pump to extract air from the vacuum test cabinet so as to create a predetermined vacuum level. PN US2018216010-A1
TI Production of paraffin-rich hydrocarbon fuel from            renewable feedstock used for producing diesel fuel,            involves producing hydrogen mixture, supplying mixture            to reaction zone, treating feedstock, separating            product stream, and recycling.
AU HONG J
AE HONG J (HONG-Individual)
GA 2018603119
AB    NOVELTY - Production of paraffin-rich hydrocarbon fuel                from renewable feedstock involves producing                hydrogen-rich mixture (18) in steam fuel reformer,                supplying the mixture without hydrogen purification                as a source of hydrogen to reaction zone, treating                the feedstock in the reaction zone by hydrogenating                and deoxygenating the feedstock using at least one                catalyst at reaction conditions in the presence of                hydrogen to provide a reaction zone product stream                comprising hydrogen, carbon dioxide, water, and a                hydrocarbon fraction comprising paraffins,                separating the reaction zone product stream into at                least gaseous component comprising at least                hydrogen and carbon dioxide, hydrocarbon component                comprising paraffins, and recycling the stream                containing at least hydrogen and depleted in carbon                dioxide to the reaction zone.
   USE - Production of paraffin-rich hydrocarbon fuel                used for producing diesel boiling range fuel and                jet fuel from renewable feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process minimizes the use of hydrogen and                reduces the cost. By optimizing the isomerization                requirement due to climate, cost savings can be                achieved. Saving the utilities of pumping and                reheating reduce the cost of overall process. The                process can be operated at pressure of 1379 kPa                absolute (200 psia)-4826 kPa absolute (700 psia)                which is lower than other operations. By utilizing                large hydrocarbon recycle, the solubility of                hydrogen in the liquid phase in reaction zone is                greatly increased and higher pressures are not                needed to increase the amount of hydrogen in                solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of paraffin-rich diesel boiling range                product from renewable feedstock.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                producing paraffin-rich hydrocarbon fuel from                renewable feedstock.

   Heat exchanger (17)
   Hydrogen-rich mixture (18)
   Air cooler (232)
   Vapor stream (236)

TI Method and system useful recovering carbonate from steelmaking slag, comprises e.g. mixing steelmaking slag and acidic solvent, passing via first filter, adding ligand, separating solution by electrochemical method, and adding buffer.
AU YOU J Y
   KOO J H
   SHIN K H
   KIM Y S
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 201842500F
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering carbonate from steelmaking slag, comprises (a) mixing steelmaking slag and acidic solvent to prepare first mixture containing metal ions, (b) passing the first mixture through a first filter to separate into a second mixture comprising metal ions and a residue, (c) adding ligand to the second mixture and mixing the mixture to prepare third mixture comprising complex prepared by reacting metal ion with ligand, (d) separating the solution containing the complex compound by electrochemical method, (e) adding a buffer solution to solution containing the complex compound, and reacting with carbon dioxide to produce carbonate, and (f) separating the produced carbonate.
   USE - The method and system are useful for recovering carbonate from steelmaking slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system recover steelmaking slag without emitting carbon dioxide into atmosphere, and can reuse costly ligands after the entire process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recovery system comprising a metal ion extracting unit for mixing steelmaking slag and acidic solvent to produce first mixture containing metal ions, a first separator for separating the first mixture into a second mixture containing metal ions and residue, a complex compound production unit for adding ligand to the second mixture separated from the first separation unit and mixing the metal ions and the ligand to produce complex, a complex-compound separating unit for separating only the solution containing the complex compound prepared in the complex compound production unit, a carbonation unit for producing carbonate by reacting the solution containing the complex separated in the complex separation unit with carbon dioxide, and a carbonate separator for separating the carbonates produced in the carbonation unit, where the carbonation unit comprises a complex compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (f), the mixed solution having undergone the carbonate formation reaction in step (e) is passed through a second filter including micro-sized pores, and recovering the carbonate which is not passed through the second filter. The method comprises recovering the mixed solution having passed through the second filter and supplying the mixed solution to the reaction vessel for producing the third mixture in the step (c). Preferred Components: In the step (d), the solution containing the complex compound has pH of 1-3, and it is changed into pH of 8-10 by adding the buffer solution in the step (e). The buffer solution is slag buffer solution containing slag. The pore has diameter of 30-40 mu m. The metal ion is Ca (II). The carbonate is calcium carbonate. The first filter has diameter of 30-50 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbonate separator is configured such that the carbonic acid-generating mixed solution is passed through a filter including micro-sized pores, and recovering the carbonate which is not passed through the filter. The carbonate separator further comprises a recovery unit for recovering the carbonate and transferring the remaining mixed solution to the complex compound supply unit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic solvent includes nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrobromic acid, hydroiodic acid, perchloric acid and/or phosphoric acid. The ligand includes acetic acid, nitrilotriacetic acid, nitrobenzene, iminodiacetic acid, citrate (3+), glutamate(+) and/or oxalic acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114249300-A
TI Preparing synthesis gas by dry reforming of methane, comprises e.g. taking methane and carbon dioxide as raw materials, and nickel/aluminium oxide as a catalyst, and then reacting in plasma reactor.
AU ZHANG X
   DIAO Y
   SHI C
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202250239C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing synthesis gas by dry reforming of methane, comprises taking methane and carbon dioxide as raw materials, and nickel/aluminium oxide (Ni/Al2O3) as a catalyst, and then reacting in a plasma reactor to obtain synthesis gas, the aluminium oxide is nano-flaky aluminium oxide, feather-type aluminium oxide, bulb-type aluminium oxide and/or rod-type aluminium oxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthesis gas by dry reforming of methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the dispersibility of metallic nickel, simultaneously improves the energy efficiency on the catalyst under the plasma discharge condition and the discharge stability of the catalyst, and realizes methane and carbon dioxide under mild conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the catalyst, the mass fraction of nickel is 2.5-20%, the average particle size of nickel is 3-20 nm, the specific surface area of the catalyst is 50-200 m2/g, the pore volume is 0.2-0.8 cm3/g, and the pore diameter is 5-50 nm. In step (i), the mass ratio of aluminum nitrate and urea is 0.6-0.9, in step (ii), the mass ratio of aluminum nitrate and urea is 0.3-0.55, in step (iii), the mass ratio of aluminum nitrate and glucose is 1.0-2.2, and in step (iv), the mass ratio of aluminum nitrate and urea is 0.3-0.5.Preferred Method: The plasma discharge form is dielectric barrier discharge, the input power is 30-200 W, the discharge power is 30-200 W, the center frequency is 5-30 kHz, and the input voltage is 10-200 V. The reaction process is heated without additional heat source. The reaction is carried out at normal pressure, and the reaction gas is a mixture of methane, carbon dioxide and inert gas, the volume ratio of methane/carbon dioxide is 4/1-1/4, and the mass space velocity of the reaction gas is 50,000-2,500,000 ml/g/h. The freeze-drying conditions are: freeze-drying at minus 30-60degrees Celsius for 24-72 hours. The preparation of aluminium oxide comprises (i) dissolving aluminum nitrate and urea in deionized water, stirring evenly, then pouring the mixed solution into a hydrothermal reaction kettle, and placing it in an oven at 100-110degrees Celsius for 36-48 hours, reacting, naturally cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, filtering, washing with water, then freeze-drying the filter cake, and finally calcining at 500-600degrees Celsius for 2-5 hours in an air atmosphere to obtain nano-flaky aluminium oxide i.e. NS-Al2O3, (ii) dissolving aluminum nitrate and urea in deionized water, stirring evenly, then pouring the mixed solution into a hydrothermal reaction kettle, and placing it in an oven at 120-150degrees Celsius for 12-16 hours, reacting, cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature naturally, filtering, washing with water, then freeze-drying the filter cake, and finally calcining in an air atmosphere at 500-600degrees Celsius for 2-5 hours to obtain feathery alumina i.e. F-Al2O3, (iii) dissolving aluminum nitrate and glucose in deionized water, stirring evenly, then pouring the mixed solution into a hydrothermal reaction kettle, and reacting at 150-180degrees Celsius for 15-30 hours, naturally cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, filtering, and washing with water, then freeze-drying the filter cake, and finally calcining at 500-600degrees Celsius for 2-5 hours in an air atmosphere to obtain spherical alumina i.e. S-Al 2 O 3, and (iv) dissolving aluminum nitrate and urea in deionized water, stirring evenly, then pouring the mixed solution into a hydrothermal reaction kettle, and reacting at 180-200degrees Celsius for 20-32 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing with water, then freeze-drying the filter cake, and finally calcining at 500-600degrees Celsius for 2-5 hours to obtain rod-shaped aluminium oxide i.e. rod-Al2O3. In any of the steps (i)-(iv): the stirring conditions include carrying out vigorous stirring at a stirring speed of 300-800 revolutions/minute for 30-60 minutes, and the freeze-drying conditions are: freeze-drying at minus 35-60degrees Celsius for 24-72 hours. The preparation of catalyst, comprising firstly measuring the water absorption of alumina with different morphologies, then taking soluble nickel salt and dissolving it in deionized water to obtain nickel salt solution, immersing it in an equal volume of aluminium oxide carrier, placing at room temperature for 24-36 hours, freeze-drying, calcining the dried precursors at 500-600degrees Celsius for 4-6 hours in air atmosphere to obtain nickel/aluminium oxide catalysts with different morphologies, which were denoted as Ni/NS-Al2O3, Ni/F-Al2O3, Ni/rod-Al2O3 and Ni/S-Al2O3.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

The carbon dioxide reduction device that generates methane by reducing the carbon dioxide with hydrogen.

The powdered catalyst is heated to a temperature below 300 degrees C with the internal space of the reaction vessel kept at a temperature of 100-230 degrees C under normal pressure. The carbon dioxide and the electrolytic hydrogen are fed into the reaction vessel. The carbon dioxide reduction device sends the carbon dioxide diluted with an inert gas into the reaction vessel. The carbon dioxide reduction device is installed in the reaction vessel in a state where the powdery catalyst is supported on a porous structure made of silicon carbide.

TI Preparation of basic magnesium carbonate by adding magnesium hydroxide and deionized water to reactor, sonicating, heating, passing through carbon dioxide gas, measuring pH, stopping reaction, pyrolyzing, cooling, filtering, and oven drying.
AU WEI Q
AE SHANGLI COUNTY SHANGLI TOWN CENT PRIMARY (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202025919N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of basic magnesium carbonate comprises adding magnesium hydroxide and deionized water to reactor, sonicating to obtain magnesium hydroxide slurry, heating to 40 degrees C, passing 99.9% carbon dioxide gas through the metal tube to porous distributor at the bottom of reactor, measuring pH value every 30 minutes until it is close to constant value, and continuously feeding carbon dioxide gas for 1 hour until the reaction is over; and high temperature pyrolyzing product for 2 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, and oven drying to 50 degrees C for 24 hours.
   USE - The method is for preparation of basic magnesium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The basic magnesium carbonate has sheet structure surface.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing polyol, comprises (a1) adding                alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide onto H-functional                starter substance in the presence of a catalyst to                obtain polyether carbonate polyol and cyclic                carbonate, (a2) separating the cyclic carbonate                from the resulting reaction mixture, (a3)                performing hydrolytic cleavage of the separated                cyclic carbonate to obtain carbon dioxide and diol                and (a5) optionally purifying the diol by                distillation.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                polyol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly; and                maximizes the yield with respect to the alkylene                oxide used in the preparation of polyether                carbonate polyols, i.e. to recycle the cyclic                carbonate obtained as a by-product for recycling in                a polyol application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a3): at                  least one compound including alkali metal                  hydroxides (preferred), alkaline earth metal                  hydroxides or hydrolases is used as the                  hydrolysis catalyst. In the method, 0.05-1 w.%                  hydrolysis catalyst is used, based on the cyclic                  carbonate used in the step (a3).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises (b1) adding alkylene oxide and                  optionally carbon dioxide, cyclic carboxylic acid                  anhydride and/or cyclic esters to the diol and                  optionally further H-functional starter substance                  or (b2) reacting carboxylic acid, cyclic                  carboxylic anhydride, non-cyclic ester and/or                  cyclic ester with the diol and optionally further                  alcohols. The addition in the step (a1) takes                  place in the presence of a double metal cyanide                  (DMC) catalyst or a metal complex catalyst based                  on the metals zinc and/or cobalt. The step (a1)                  comprises (a11) placing a partial amount of                  H-functional starter substance and/or a                  suspending agent which does not have any                  H-functional groups in a reactor, optionally                  together with a catalyst, (a12) optionally adding                  a partial amount (based on the total amount of                  the amount of alkylene oxide used in the                  activation and copolymerization) of alkylene                  oxide to the mixture resulting from the step                  (a11) to activate DMC catalyst, where the                  addition of a partial amount of alkylene oxide                  can optionally take place in the presence of                  carbon dioxide, and the temperature peak                  (hotspot) occurring due to the following                  exothermic chemical reaction and/or a pressure                  drop in the reactor is then respectively waited                  for, and the step (a12) for activation can also                  be carried out several times, (a13) adding                  H-functional starter substance, alkylene oxide                  and optionally a suspending agent which has no                  H-functional groups and/or carbon dioxide into                  the reactor during the reaction, and (a14)                  optionally transferring the reaction mixture                  continuously removed in the step (a13) to a                  post-reactor in which the free alkylene oxide                  content in the reaction mixture is reduced by a                  post-reaction. In the step (a2): the cyclic                  carbonate is separated by thermal processes; and                  the cyclic carbonate is purified by distillation                  before the step (a3). The step (a3) is carried                  out at a temperature of at least 40 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: A thin film evaporator or a                  falling film evaporator is used in combination                  with a stripping column. The cyclic carbonate in                  the step (a2) is cyclic propylene carbonate                  and/or cyclic ethylene carbonate. The molar ratio                  of cyclic carbonate to water in the step (a3) is                  1:1-1:10. The polyol is at least one compound                  made of polyether polyol, polyester polyol and                  polyether carbonate polyol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110527074-A
TI Preparing crosslinked polypropylene carbonate useful in the fields of plastic and rubber comprises taking propylene oxide and carbon dioxide, using maleic anhydride homopolymer, polymerizing and performing one pot synthesis.
AU GAO L
   HUANG M
   FENG J
   LIANG X
   XIAO Z
AE UNIV LINGNAN NORMAL (ULNN-C)
GA 2019A2975P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing crosslinked polypropylene carbonate comprises taking propylene oxide and carbon dioxide as first and second monomers, using maleic anhydride homopolymer as third monomer, carrying out polymerization using zinc glutarate as catalyst and performing one pot synthesis to obtain the product.
   USE - The polypropylene carbonate is useful in the fields of plastics and rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and suitable for industrial production; comprises maleic anhydride homopolymer which increases catalytic efficiency; reduces carbon dioxide pressure; reduces polymerization time; having less than or equal to 5 wt.% polyolefins (PO) enhances thermal stability, mechanical strength and dimensional stability; solves the problems of easy decomposition and easy deformation of products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the zinc glutarate to propylene oxide is 1:100-500. The mass ratio of maleic anhydride homopolymer to propylene oxide is 1-5:100. The initial pressure of carbon dioxide is 4.0 MPa. The molecular weight of maleic anhydride homopolymer is 300-1000. Preferred Method: The preparation of crosslinked polypropylene carbonate comprises adding zinc glutarate and maleic anhydride homopolymer in a high-pressure reaction kettle, vacuum drying, performing cooling by replacing air with nitrogen thrice, evacuating, injecting propylene oxide, filling carbon dioxide, maintaining the polymerization reaction temperature, stirring and reacting, allowing the polymer to dissolve in dichloromethane, adding 5% hydrochloric acid to dissolve zinc glutarate catalyst, washing with distilled water until neutral pH, precipitating the polymer in dichloromethane solution using ethanol and vacuum drying to obtain the polymer.The vacuum drying is carried out at 80 degrees C for 24 hours. The polymerization reaction is carried out at 50-70?Oc for 10-20 hours.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)

PN CN109832597-A; CN109832597-B
TI Extraction method for improving sanshoamides recovery rate of Chinese prickly ash by pretreating Chinese prickly ash sheet, adding into extracting tank, introducing entrainer, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and separating.
AU DENG D
AE ZHENGZHOU XUEMAILONG FOOD SPICES CO LTD (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201952501X
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction method for improving sanshoamides recovery rate of Chinese prickly ash comprises pretreating Chinese prickly ash sheet with thickness of 0.1-0.5 mm as raw material, adding into extracting tank at 30-35 MPa at 45-55 degrees C, introducing entrainer, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid for 2-3 hours to obtain extract liquid, and separating extract liquid in separating tank at 6-8 MPa at 50-55 degrees C to obtain Chinese prickly ash oil and gaseous carbon dioxide.

   ADVANTAGE - The method has high recovery rate and simple technique.

PN CN109534959-A; CN109534959-B
TI Decomposing phenol sodium salt by subjecting phenol sodium salt aqueous solution and gas containing carbon dioxide to counter current contact reaction, separating to obtain primary aqueous phase containing sodium bicarbonate.
AU XIONG J
   HAN S
   HUANG L
   GUO L
AE BEIJING SJ ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION & NE (SJEP-C)
   BEIJING INST PETROCHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY (SNPC-C)
   BEIJING SANJU ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION M (SJEP-C)
GA 2019310417
AB    NOVELTY - Method for decomposing phenol sodium salt involves subjecting phenol sodium salt aqueous solution and gas containing carbon dioxide to counter current contact reaction, separating to obtain primary crude phenol and primary aqueous phase containing sodium bicarbonate, where pH value of the primary aqueous phase is 7-10, performing secondary decomposition to the mixed solution obtained by mixing the primary crude phenol with water by counter current reaction with gas containing carbon dioxide, separating to obtain crude phenol product and secondary aqueous phase containing sodium bicarbonate with the pH of 7-9.
   USE - Method for decomposing phenol sodium salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves higher sodium phenol salt decomposition rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of primary crude phenol and water is 1:(0.3-1.5). The content of carbon dioxide gas is not less than 15 vol.%. Preferred Method: The method involves heating primary aqueous phase and secondary aqueous phase to separate sodium bicarbonate solution, water and carbon dioxide gas, causticizing sodium bicarbonate solution, recovering sodium hydroxide solution and recycling carbon dioxide gas and water back to primary and secondary decomposition steps. The separated water satisfies the requirement of secondary decomposition process, the primary crude phenol mixed water and calcium carbonate filter cake flushing water in the causticization process ensures the water remaining in the system.

PT P
PN CN108772091-A
TI Heterojunction photocatalyst useful for catalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises graphitic carbon            nitride body and bismuth(III) oxide doped in graphitic            carbon nitride.
AU GUO R
   LIU X
   QIN H
   WANG Z
   SHI X
   TANG J
   HUANG C
   LIU J
   SUN X
   LIU S
   WANG G
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ELECTRIC POWER (UYSI-C)
GA 2018900643
AB    NOVELTY - Heterojunction photocatalyst comprises                graphitic carbon nitride body and bismuth(III)                oxide doped in graphitic carbon nitride, where the                mass percentage of bismuth(III) oxide is                20-80%.
   USE - The heterojunction photocatalyst is useful for                catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, and low cost;                is suitable for large-scale industrial production;                reduces the electron-hole pair recombination rate;                improves carbon dioxide reduction ability of the                photocatalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing heterojunction photocatalyst comprising                (i) grinding urea, sintering, cooling and grinding                to obtain graphitic carbon nitride, and (ii)                placing obtained graphitic carbon nitride in                ethylene glycol and sonicating, then adding                bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate and urea, and                stirring to obtain homogeneous solution, carrying                out heat treatment, washing, drying, and calcining                to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sintering process is                  as follows: heating to 380-420 degrees C at a                  rate of 12-18 degrees C/minutes, then reacting                  for 2-3 hours, increasing to the same heating                  rate to 550-580 degrees C, and then reacting for                  2-3 hours. The heat treatment is hydrothermal                  treatment in an autoclave at a temperature of                  180-200 degrees C for 12-18 hours. The washing is                  carrying out with deionized water, the drying                  temperature is 80-110 degrees C, and the drying                  time is 12-24 hours. The calcination temperature                  is 360-400 degrees C, and the calcination time is                  2-3 hours. Preferred Components: The urea is                  grinding to a powdery urea having a particle                  diameter of greater than or equal to 100 mu m.                  The mass ratio between graphitic carbon nitride,                  bismuth(III) nitrate pentahydrate and urea is                  1:0.2-0.8:2.

PN KR2018000604-A; KR2022152-B1
TI Producing adsorbent useful for e.g. absorbing carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. preparing squid ink particles including nitrogen, oxygen and carbon, and carbonizing squid ink particles to produce carbon structure doped with nitrogen and oxygen.
AU CHEOL Y W
   KIM H
   WONCHEOLYOO
   SU K
AE UNIV HANYANG IND COOP FOUND (UHYG-C)
GA 201803100G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing adsorbent, comprises (a) preparing squid ink particles including nitrogen, oxygen and carbon, (b) carbonizing the squid ink particles to produce carbon structure doped with nitrogen and oxygen, and (c) activating the carbon structure doped with nitrogen and oxygen by providing activation gas to obtain activated carbon with micropore development.
   USE - The adsorbent is useful for absorbing carbon dioxide in thermal power plant, aerospace industry and seabed industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has improved physical adsorption ability and excellent selective adsorption ability of carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for adsorbent comprising activated carbon having carbonized structure in which the carbonized squid ink particles are activated and micropores.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) is carried out by performing acid treatment process on the carbon structure to remove impurities produced by the carbonization process, and forming mesopores on carbon structure, and removing impurities. The method is carried out by increasing temperature of the carbonization process for increasing volume and surface area of the micropores formed in the carbon structure, increasing carbon weight percentage contained in the carbon structure, and decreasing nitrogen and oxygen weight percentage contained in the carbon structure and size of the carbon structure. The method comprises increasing volume and specific surface area of the micropores formed in the activated carbon. Preferred Components: The activation gas is carbon dioxide gas. The weight percent of nitrogen and oxygen contained in the activated carbon is increased, when selectivity of carbon dioxide gas increases. The activated carbon further comprises mesopores having size larger than the micropores size. The adsorbent has selective adsorptivity of carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas. The volume of the micropores is increased, when adsorption capacity for carbon dioxide gas increases, and adsorption capacity of nitrogen gas decreases.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114702205-A
TI Treating high-phosphorus food industrial            wastewater comprises discharging waste water in            coagulation flotation tank, neutralizing colloidal            substances, adhering, decomposing organic            macromolecules, converting organic pollutant to carbon            dioxide, monitoring phosphorus and using for advanced            treatment.
AU ZHENG L
   CHEN J
   CHEN Y
AE ZHANGZHOU TECHNOLOGY INST (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202291044Q
AB    NOVELTY - Treating high-phosphorus food industrial                wastewater comprises discharging waste water into a                coagulation flotation tank, adjusting the pH value                of the solution in the coagulation flotation tank                to weakly alkaline, neutralizing the colloidal                substances in wastewater by coagulants and                flocculating agent, adhering, bridging to form                large alum, discharging wastewater pretreated by                the coagulation-air flotation tank into the                hydrolysis and acidification tank, so that the                wastewater and the sludge particles are fully                contacted for hydrolysis and acidification                reaction, decomposing organic macromolecules into                small molecules and discharging into a contact                oxidation tank, converting organic pollutants in                the wastewater into carbon dioxide, nitrogen and                water through the biofilm, discharging into a                biochemical secondary sedimentation tank for                mud-water separation, monitoring the total                phosphorus in the wastewater and using the                phosphorus removal chemicals for advanced treatment                of wastewater.
   USE - The method is useful for treating                high-phosphorus food industrial wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats the wastewater based on the                total phosphorus in the wastewater at the inflow                end of the wastewater; ensures that the secondary                biochemical phosphorus removal system composed of                hydrolysis acidification tank and contact oxidation                tank can ensure that the effluent meets the                discharge standard; avoids blindly injecting                chemicals; enables that the three-stage combined                phosphorus removal process ensures that the                effluent water quality is stable and up to standard                with low investment, stable operation and high                treatment efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                high-phosphorus food industry wastewater treatment                system comprising a coagulation air flotation tank                (2), a hydrolysis acidification tank (3), a contact                oxidation tank (4), a biochemical secondary                sedimentation tank (5) and a phosphorus removal                sedimentation tank (6).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                high-phosphorus food industry wastewater treatment                system.
   2Coagulation air flotation tank
   3Hydrolysis acidification tank
   4Contact oxidation tank
   5Biochemical secondary sedimentation                tank
   6Phosphorus removal sedimentation tank

TI Treating volatile organic compounds by wet method            comprises e.g. capturing and oxidizing organic matter            and electrode oxidizing water, providing iron carbon            filter layer on top of absorption tower, generating            Fenton reaction between hydrogen dioxide and iron            dioxide, oxidizing and degrading.
AU WANG X
AE QINGDAO RUNHUASHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2022931706
AB    NOVELTY - Treating volatile organic compounds (VOC) by                wet method comprises (i) sequentially capturing and                oxidizing organic matter and electrode oxidizing                the water, (ii) adding a capture agent to the water                and spraying and washing, so as to change the                affinity between water and oil, (iii) usually                trapping the generated waste water containing                volatile organic matter as dangerous waste or after                being treated after passing through the water                treated step of the wet capture and the electrode                oxidation step of electrode oxidation, (iv)                providing an iron carbon filter layer on the top of                an absorption tower, (v) generating a Fenton                reaction between hydrogen dioxide and iron dioxide                to generate hydroxy free radical, (vi) oxidizing                carbon dioxide and water, and (vii) degrading the                ozone and preventing the ozone from escaping.
   USE - The method for treating volatile organic                matter (VOC) by wet method and uses include                headache, nausea, vomiting and hypodynamia.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures the volatile organic matter                trapping efficiency, reduces the discharge of                excess organic matter, and prevents the ozone                escaping. The system can be used without open fire                and high temperature, filling the blank of VOC                treatment technology normal temperature wet treated                by adding capture agent the water, changing the                affinity property of water and oil, so as to obtain                the organic capture when using electrode                electrolysis, generating ozone, hydrogen peroxide,                ozone, hydroxy free radical and other oxidized                particles, decomposing and oxidizing the organic                matter and finally decomposing into carbon dioxide                and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                wet method VOC treatment system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                main body structure of the wet method VOC treatment                system.
   Tower body (1)
   Air inlet pipeline (2)
   Air outlet pipeline (3)
   Oxidation unit (5)
   Upper sequentially washing unit (6)
   Washing unit (7)

TI Pipeline pressure tapping device for carbon            dioxide air conditioning unit, has limiter for            controlling big ball valve and small ball valve to            rotate at same time, and upper end cover for fixing and            sealing ball valve.
