   CHEN C
   DONG B
   QIN Y
   CHENG X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202274859P
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a pressure flow passage whose                one end is connected with the pressure pipe. A                limiting slide way is fan shaped annular groove.                The circle centre position on a shell is provided                with a limiter. One end of the limiter slides in                the limiting slide way. A valve rod (9), a limiter,                an upper end cover (10), an upper valve seat (11),                a valve seat 12), a big ball valve (13) and a small                ball valve (14) are orderly assembled. The big ball                valve is installed on the pressure flow passage.                The small ball valve is installed on the pressure                release flow passage. The opening directions of the                big ball valve and the small ball valve are                staggered for 90 ?o. The limiter controls the big                ball valve and the small ball valve to rotate at                the same time. The upper end cover, the upper valve                seat and the valve seat play the roles of fixing                and sealing the ball valve respectively.
   USE - Pipeline pressure tapping device for carbon                dioxide air conditioning unit with ball valve                switch for monitoring high and low pressure                condition of cooling medium in pipeline.
   ADVANTAGE - The ball valve in the pressure taking device                is double-acting type, which can realize the                pressure-taking cavity separating from the main                pipeline and pressure relief balance at the same                time. It not only saves the space, but also reduces                the unnecessary welding port. The inlet and the                outlet are the most suitable conical design, which                furthest eliminates the turbulence phenomenon in                pressure device, reduces the pressure drop and                improves the precision of the pressure data                collection.

TI Air conditioning system for building energy efficiency for indoor heating and cooling, has air conditioning unit for sucking indoor air through predetermined cylindrical fan and discharging indoor air to discharge port.
AU KANG H
AE E GEN ENG CO LTD (EGEE-Non-standard)
GA 202274031V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an air conditioning unit for sucking indoor air through a predetermined cylindrical fan (11) and discharging the indoor air to a discharge port (14). An exchanging unit (600) selectively cross-mixes the indoor and outdoor air by selectively opening and closing a path of an inhaling unit. An air control unit (700) is provided in the path of the in-hailing unit and an x-hailing unit to selectively open and close the path. A barrier part (610) opens and closes a cross pipe according to a temperature difference between the incoming outdoor air and indoor air.
   USE - Air conditioning system for building energy efficiency for indoor heating and cooling by adjusting a degree of selective opening and closing of air passages.
   ADVANTAGE - The system establishes energy efficiency of indoor heating and cooling through adjustment of selective opening and closing degree of an air passage so as to minimize temperature deviation between outer and inner circumferences of an indoor space by uniform distribution of discharged air, so that carbon dioxide concentration of the indoor air can be quickly stabilized by selectively sucking the outside air according to the carbon dioxide of indoor air, and dew condensation caused by temperature difference of the air flowing in from the outdoor space can be prevented. The system selectively introduces the outdoor air into the room through difference in temperature and humidity between outdoor air and indoor air to increase energy efficiency and reduce cooling and heating energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the air conditioning system for building energy efficiency for indoor heating and cooling by adjusting a degree of selective opening and closing of air passages (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   Discharge port (14)
   Exchanging unit (600)
   Barrier part (610)
   Air control unit (700)

PN CN114425437-A
TI Catalyst containing silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve useful for catalytic conversion of diesel oil, comprises natural mineral, inorganic oxide, metal and molecular sieve including silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve and faujasite structure molecular sieve.
AU ZHOU X
   LIN W
   FENG M
   ZHAO L
   HAN L
   SONG H
   WANG P
   WANG L
AE SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM PROCESSING (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202265910L
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst containing silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve comprises 10-70 wt.% natural mineral, 10-60 wt.% inorganic oxide, 0.01-10 wt.% metal and 10-70 wt.% molecular sieve. The molecular sieve includes silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve and faujasite structure molecular sieve. The metal includes tungsten, copper, iron, cobalt, magnesium, molybdenum, zirconium and/or calcium.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalytic conversion of diesel oil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can improve the yield of light olefins.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a method for preparing catalyst, comprising (i) impregnating Y and silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve with an aqueous solution of metal salt, drying, and roasting to make metal-supported mixed molecular sieve, (ii) mixing and beating natural mineral, inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor to the prepare metal-supported mixed molecular sieve and spray-drying to prepare catalyst particle, (iii) drying and roasting catalyst particles to obtain roasted catalyst particles, (iv) washing calcined catalyst particles with sodium and drying, or, (a) impregnating Y and silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve with an aqueous solution of metal salt, drying and calcining to make a metal-supported mixed molecular sieve, (b) mixing and beating natural mineral, inorganic oxide and/or inorganic oxide precursor to prepare the metal-supported mixed molecular sieve, and spray-drying to prepare catalyst particles, (c) carrying out catalyzer particle to wash sodium exchange to obtain the catalyst particle, and (d) drying and calcining the catalyst particles after washing and sodium exchange; and
   (2) a method for catalytic conversion of diesel oil, comprising: contacting and reacting diesel oil, carbon dioxide with a catalyst and optionally a diluent gas at 400-800degrees Celsius under the pressure of 0.1-2 MPa, where the weight ratio of carbon dioxide and diesel oil is 0.2-3:1, and the mass space velocity of diesel oil is 0.3-10 hour-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst containing silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve comprises 25-50 wt.% natural mineral, 10-44 wt.% inorganic oxide, 0.7-3.5 wt.%, preferably 0.8-3.5 wt.% metal and 28-48 wt.% molecular sieve.Preferred Components: The metal includes copper, cobalt, and magnesium. The silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve is SAPO-11 molecular sieve, SAPO-31 molecular sieve, SAPO-34 molecular sieve and/or SAPO-47 molecular sieve. The weight ratio of faujasite molecular sieve and silicoaluminophosphate molecular sieve is 0.05-1:1, preferably 2.1-3.5:1. The faujasite structure molecular sieve is preferably Y-type molecular sieve. The natural mineral is kaolin, montmorillonite, diatomite, attapulgite, sepiolite, halloysite, hydrotalcite, bentonite and/or rectorite. The inorganic oxide is silica, alumina, alumina-silica, zirconia, titania, boron oxide, amorphous silica-alumina, aluminum phosphate, tungsten oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide-zirconia, molybdenum oxide titanium oxide, tungsten oxide-titanium oxide, zinc oxide and/or niobium oxide.Preferred Method: In the step (i) and (iii), the calcination condition respectively includes: the calcination temperature is 400-800degrees Celsius, and the calcination time is 0.5-8 hours. The conditions of the contact reaction includes: the temperature is 480-650degrees Celsius, the pressure is 0.1-0.3 MPa, the weight ratio of carbon dioxide and diesel oil is 0.25-2:1, and the mass air velocity of diesel oil is 0.5-5 hour-1.

PN US2022119720-A1
TI Forming renewable fuel involves exposing feedstock comprising biomass to gasification conditions to form gasification output gas stream comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, performing electrolysis and forming hydrocarbonaceous compounds from portion of enriched synthesis gas.
AU SUMMERS Z M
   ONEILL B J
   BIELENBERG J R
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 202252313E
AB    NOVELTY - Forming renewable fuel involves exposing a feedstock comprising biomass to gasification conditions in a gasification reaction system to form a gasification output gas stream comprising hydrogen (H2), carbon monoxide (CO), and carbon dioxide (CO2). The gasification output gas stream comprises H2 and CO in a molar ratio of 1.5 or less. The gasification reaction system comprises a reaction environment oxygen (O2) concentration. The electrolysis is performed to form a first molar quantity of H2 and a second molar quantity of O2, and at least a portion of the gasification output gas stream is combined with at least a portion of the first molar quantity of H2 to form an enriched synthesis gas having H2 and CO in a molar ratio of 1.8-2.2, and a portion of second molar quantity of O2 is passed into the gasification reaction system to maintain reaction environment O2 concentration, and hydrocarbonaceous compounds are formed from at least a portion of the enriched synthesis gas.
   USE - Method for forming renewable fuel
   ADVANTAGE - The method forms renewable fuel, which reduces or minimizes generation of excess CO2 while forming hydrocarbon-like products that retain a substantial amount of renewable character, reduces or minimizes amount of additional CO2 production that is required to process biomass and convert the synthesis gas into hydrocarbon-like products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gasification reaction system comprises at least one gasification reactor comprising one or more gasifier inputs and a gasifier output, an electrolyzer comprising an electrolyzer O2 output in fluid communication with at least one gasifier input of the one or more gasifier inputs and an electrolyzer H2 output, multiple batteries for providing power to the electrolyzer, and a synthesis stage for formation of hydrocarbonaceous compounds, where synthesis stage is in fluid communication with the gasifier output and in fluid communication with the electrolyzer H2 output.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The portion of first molar quantity of H2 comprises 80% or more of the first molar quantity of H2, or at least a portion of the second molar quantity of O2 comprises 80% or more of second molar quantity of O2 or its combination. The biomass comprises a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon of 2.1:1 to 0.7:1. The biomass comprises hydrogen and carbon in a molar ratio of 2.4:1 to 0.7:1 and carbon to oxygen in a molar ratio 1.3:1 to 9.0:1. The electrolysis is performed by using electricity derived from a renewable source. The power of electricity derived from a renewable source varies by 50% or more relative to a peak power of electricity derived from the renewable source during a 12 hour period, or at least a portion of the electricity derived from renewable source is stored in one or more batteries before being used for performing electrolysis, or its combination. The hydrocarbonaceous compounds are formed by fermentation of at least a portion of the enriched synthesis gas. The fermentation further comprises formation of additional biomass, and feedstock comprising biomass comprises at least a portion of the additional biomass. The hydrocarbonaceous compounds are formed by using at least a portion of enriched synthesis gas as a feed for Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, methanol synthesis, or its combination. The portion of the second molar quantity of O2 is passed into gasification reaction system as an oxygen-containing gas flow comprising 99.6 wt.% O2 or more, or gasification is performed at a temperature of 500-1500degreesC. The method further involves exposing feedstock comprising biomass to second gasification conditions in gasification reaction system to form at least a second gasification output gas stream comprising H2, CO, and CO2, and second gasification output gas stream comprising a second molar ratio of H2 to CO that is lower than the molar ratio of H2 to CO for the gasification output stream, and second gasification conditions comprises a reaction environment O2 concentration that is at least partially maintained based on providing an oxygen-containing input stream with an oxygen content of 70 vol.% or less, and forming hydrocarbonaceous compounds from at least a portion of the second gasification output stream. The method further involves exposing a portion of the second gasification output stream to water gas shift conditions to form a shifted portion of second gasification output stream, where hydrocarbonaceous compounds are formed from a portion of second gasification output stream and hydrocarbonaceous compounds are formed from the shifted portion of the second gasification output stream. The method further involves exposing the feedstock comprising biomass to second gasification conditions in gasification reaction system to form at least a second gasification output gas stream comprising H2, CO, and CO2, where second gasification output gas stream comprising a second molar ratio of H2 to CO that is lower than the molar ratio of H2 to CO for the gasification output stream, and second gasification conditions comprises a reaction environment O2 concentration that is at least partially maintained based on providing an oxygen-containing input stream with an oxygen content of 70 vol.% or less, and performing electrolysis to form a third molar quantity of H2 that is lower than the first molar quantity of H2, combining at least a portion of the second gasification output gas stream with at least a portion of the third molar quantity of H2 to form a second enriched synthesis gas having a molar ratio of H2 to CO of less than 1.8, and forming hydrocarbonaceous compounds from at least a portion of the second enriched synthesis gas. The method further involves exposing a portion of the second enriched synthesis gas to water gas shift conditions to form a shifted portion of the second enriched synthesis gas, where hydrocarbonaceous compounds are formed from at least a portion of the second enriched synthesis gas and hydrocarbonaceous compounds are formed from the shifted portion of the second enriched synthesis gas, where a portion of gasification output gas stream is combined with a portion of first molar quantity of H2 without exposing the at least a portion of the gasification output gas stream to a catalyst having water gas shift activity. The gasification reaction system comprises an indirect gasification system, and indirect gasification system comprises at least a first reactor comprising the gasification conditions and at least a second reactor comprising the reaction environment O2 concentration. The synthesis stage comprises a fermentation stage and system further comprise a biomass growth stage in fluid communication with fermentation stage and in solids flow communication with at least one gasifier input of the one or more gasifier inputs. The synthesis stage is in fluid communication with the gasifier output by a first fluid communication flow path, where first fluid communication flow path is free of catalyst with water gas shift activity. The system further comprises a renewable power source for providing power to the plurality of batteries to the electrolyzer, or its combination.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. TI New self-contained microporous ladder polymer i.e.            1,1,1-trifluoroethane compound useful in preparing flat            membrane and/or hollow fiber membrane for carbon            dioxide capture and separation.
AU CAI Z
   WU L
AE CHINESE ACAD METEOROLOGY SCI (CHME-Non-standard)
GA 202243959P
AB    NOVELTY - Self-contained microporous ladder polymer i.e.                1,1,1-trifluoroethane compound (I) is new.
   USE - The self-contained microporous ladder polymer                is useful in preparing flat membrane and/or hollow                fiber membrane for carbon dioxide capture and                separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The self-contained microporous ladder polymer                has excellent solubility in organic solvents,                excellent processability and simple film-forming                process, and is easier to use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Self-contained microporous ladder polymer i.e.                1,1,1-trifluoroethane compound of formula (I) is                new.
   R1=                pentacyclo[6.6.6.0{2,7}.0{9,14}.0{15,20}]icosa-2,4,6,9(14),10,12,15(20),16,18-nonaene-11,12,17,18-tetrayl,                9,9'-spirobi[fluorene]-2,2',3,3'-tetrayl,                nonacyclo[14.6.6.6{5,12}.0{2,15}.0{4,13}.0{6,11}.0{17,22}.0{23,28}.0{29,34}]tetratriaconta-2,4(13),6(11),7,9,14,17(22),18,20,23(28),24,26,29(34),30,32-pentadecaene-8,9,19,20,25,26,31,32-tetrayl,                or                tetracyclo[6.6.2.0{2,7}.0{9,14}]hexadeca-2(7),3,5,9(14),10,12-hexaene-4,5,11,12-tetrayl;                and

   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. preparation of (I).

TI Liquefied natural gas (LNG) production facility,            has liquefaction unit that condenses vapor product from            heavies removal unit into LNG, and LNG loading facility            that receives LNG from LNG storage tank and transfers            LNG to gas turbine.

The                heavies removal unit to receive dry purified                natural gas stream from dehydration unit and                produce liquid condensate product and vapor                product. The                liquefaction unit (110) is provided to condense                vapor product into LNG storage tank. The                post-combustion capture unit is provided to                generate first carbon dioxide-rich stream and                sequestration compression unit (130).
   USE - LNG production facility for producing                liquefied natural gas (LNG) using hydrocarbon                emissions conversion technologies, such as thermal                oxidizers and flares.
   ADVANTAGE - The facility improves the overall efficiency                of the facility and reduces greenhouse gas                emissions. The facility reduces the amount of                carbon dioxide emissions from the gas turbine, and                reduces or eliminates the need for thermal                oxidizers, flares, and marine vent systems in the                facility, thus reducing the overall cost and                complexity of the facilities, and hence improving                the efficiency and reducing the greenhouse gas                emission from the facility. The hydrogen generation                unit is operated in the hydrogen generation mode,                so that the hydrogen is combusted along with the                hydrocarbons, thus improving the overall                performance of the hydrogen production facility and                increasing the production of liquefied natural gas                (LNG) from the liquefaction unit, and reducing or                eliminating the use of thermal oxidizer, flare,                and/or marine vent system, and improving the                facility's overall efficiency and efficiency.

PN US2022056156-A1
TI Making hydroxyethyl cellulose for treating brine in enhanced oil recovery operation, involves extracting biomass comprising plant waste biomass and date palm waste, and acid leaching, followed by hydroxyethylating bleached cellulose.
AU MUSTAFA J
   LWISA E G
   GALIWANGO E
   AL-MARZOUQI A H
   MOHAMMAD A F
AE UNIV UNITED ARAB EMIRATES (UYUN-Non-standard)
GA 202227795J
AB    NOVELTY - Making hydroxyethyl cellulose involves providing a biomass comprising plant waste biomass and date palm waste, extracting the biomass to produce an extractive-free biomass, and acid leaching the extractive-free biomass to obtain a cellulose or lignin-rich residue. The cellulose or lignin-rich residue is alkali treated with sodium hydroxide to produce a cellulose-rich residue. The cellulose-rich residue is bleached at 75degreesC with 15 wt.% acetic acid, 20 wt.% hydrogen peroxide, and 10 wt.% sulfuric acid to obtain bleached cellulose, followed by hydroxyethylating the bleached cellulose with 7.5 wt.% sodium hydroxide and 11 wt.% aqueous urea to produce alkaline hydroxyethyl cellulose with a pH of 8-9.50.
   USE - Method for making hydroxyethyl cellulose for treating brine (claimed) including flue gas, flare gas, exhaust gas and natural gas in enhanced oil recovery operations.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides hydroxyethyl cellulose to treat brine with reduced salinity and reduced interfacial tension value of 4.95 mN/m, thus the treated brine has high potential for enhancing oil recovery from reservoir rocks, and increased ability to form water-oil emulsions, which assist in driving oil in a more spontaneous way out from rock pores.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a method for treating brine, which involves:
   a. mixing concentrated brine with the hydroxyethyl cellulose to produce an aqueous feed stream;
   b. feeding the aqueous feed stream through a bubble column reactor; and
   c. introducing a gas stream into the bubble column reactor while feeding the aqueous feed stream through the bubble reactor, where the gas stream comprises 0.15 wt.% carbon dioxide and 10 wt.% carbon dioxide in order to produce treated brine; and
   2. a method for enhancing oil recovery, which involves administering the treated brine to an oil well during an oil recovery procedure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The biomass is extracted with benzene or ethanol (2:1) solvent in a Soxhlet extractor for 5 hours. The extractive-free biomass is acid leached with 0.1 M hydrochloric acid at 100degreesC for 2 hours. The cellulose or lignin-rich residue is alkali treated with 0.1 M sodium hydroxide at 100degreesC for 2 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aqueous feed stream has a starting sodium chloride concentration of more than 50 g/l. The concentrated brine in the aqueous feed has a concentration of more than 50 g/l sodium chloride and the treated brine has less than 50 g/l sodium chloride.
DC A11 (Polysaccharides; natural rubber; other natural polymers (only a restricted range of (modified) natural polymers are included. Thus starch would be excluded, but chemically modified starch included).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

AB    NOVELTY - The heat exchanger (100) has a shell (310) to                which a secondary heat medium is configured to be                supplied. A plate fin coil case (320) is provided                in the shell and comprises a set of plate fins                (324). A coil (322) passes through the set of the                plate fins and through which a primary heat medium                flows. The coil includes heat medium passages each                of which includes a passage inlet and outlet via                which the primary medium flows into and out from                the passages, respectively. A distributor and a                merging manifold are connected to the passage                inlets and outlet of the passages.
   USE - Heat exchanger for condensing or reliquefying                low-boiling-point refrigerant or carbon dioxide                gas. Uses include but are not limited to plate heat                exchanger, shell-and-plate heat exchangers, and                shell and tube heat exchages.

The high-temperature gas and the low-boiling-point                refrigerant can be cooled efficiently. The heat                transfer efficiency of the plate fin can be                increased. The size of the shell can be reduced.                The number of the plates can be decreased. The                manufacturing cost of the heat exchangers can be                lowered. The efficiency of heat exchange between                the primary heat medium and the secondary heat                medium can be enhanced. The generation of heat loss                can be suppressed. The production cost can be                minimized. The durability and reliability of the                exchanger are improved.

TI Composite for separating acid gas from acid gas absorbent and regenerating absorbent, comprises inorganic layered compound and aluminum-containing oxide.
AU SUZUKI A
   IMADA T
   SANO K
   SAITO H
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
GA 2021A3555S
AB    NOVELTY - Composite comprises inorganic layered compound and aluminum-containing oxide. The composite is capable of separating acid gas from acid gas absorbent, and regenerating absorbent.
   USE - Composite used for separating acid gas from acid gas absorbent and regenerating the absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite separates and removes acid gases such as carbon dioxide from the acid gas absorbent which absorbed acid gases, and regenerates the absorbent at low temperature, is suitable for carbon dioxide capture and storage (CCS) plants, which is desired continuous operation, efficiently released even when the heating temperature is set to a lower temperature, the amount of energy consumption can be reduced, which potentially facilitates the wide use of the technology as a means of preventing global warming.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   1. a regenerator of regenerating acid gas absorbent, which comprises composite, where acid gas absorbent that has absorbed acid gas is brought into contact with composite to separate acid gas from acid gas absorbent;
   2. an acid gas removal apparatus, which comprises acid gas absorbent, absorber that brings subject gas mixture containing acid gas into contact with acid gas absorbent to make acid gas absorbent absorb acid gas and remove acid gas from the subject gas mixture, and the regenerator, where acid gas absorbent regenerated by the regenerator is reused in the absorber;
   3. a method for performing acid gas removal, which involves:
   a. bringing subject gas mixture containing acid gas into contact with an acid gas absorbent to make the acid gas absorbent absorb the acid gas and thereby to remove the acid gas from the subject gas mixture;
   b. regenerating acid gas absorbent that has absorbed the acid gas by allowing the acid gas absorbent to release the acid gas, reusing regenerated acid gas absorbent in acid gas absorption step, where acid gas absorbent that has absorbed the acid gas is brought into contact with a composite comprising an inorganic layered compound and an aluminum-containing oxide to regenerate the acid gas absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic layered compound is hydrotalcite-like compound. The univalent metal ion (M1+) is selected from lithium ions, sodium ions, potassium ions, rubidium ions and cesium ion. The divalent metal ion (M22+) is selected from magnesium ions, calsium ions, zinc ions, iron ions, copper ions, manganese ions and nickel ions. The trivalent metal ion (M32+) is selected from aluminum ions, chromium ions, iron ions, cobalt ions, gallium ions, vanadium ions and indium ions. The aluminum-containing oxide further comprises metal selected from titanium, silicon and cerium.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Preparation of Dendrobium nobile capsule by obtaining Dendrobium nobile alkaloid, adding pseudo-ginseng saponin, shizandra extract and red yeast powder, uniformly mixing, mixing with auxiliary materials, and filling capsule.
AU GONG D
   YAO Y
AE GONG D (GONG-Individual)
GA 202185076U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of Dendrobium nobile capsule comprises taking Dendrobium nobile stem, drying and crushing; sieving; using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method to extract; dissolving chloroform extract, filtering, and recycling chloroform to obtain crude extract; taking crude extract into reflux extractor; adding mixed solution of chloroform-50% ethanol aqueous solution-water as solvent system to extract to obtain extracting solution; separating extracting solution by ultrafiltration to obtain purified extracting solution; adding ammonium salt substance into purified extracting solution, refluxing and stirring, carrying out reverse osmosis, and vacuum drying to obtain Dendrobium nobile alkaloid; adding pseudo-ginseng saponin in Dendrobium nobile alkaloid, shizandra extract and red yeast powder, uniformly mixing to obtain mixture; and taking mixture, mixing with auxiliary materials, using capsule filling machine for filling capsule.
   USE - The method is used for preparing Dendrobium nobile capsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is green and non-toxic, and has no side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of Dendrobium nobile capsule comprises taking Dendrobium nobile stem, drying and crushing; sieving by 20-80 mesh sieve of 20-80 meshes; using carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method to extract; dissolving chloroform extract, filtering, and recycling chloroform to obtain crude extract; taking crude extract into reflux extractor provided with ultrasonic oscillator; adding mixed solution of chloroform-50% ethanol aqueous solution-water as solvent system to extract to obtain extracting solution; separating extracting solution by ultrafiltration to obtain purified extracting solution; adding ammonium salt substance into purified extracting solution, refluxing and stirring at 65 degrees C for 90 minutes, carrying out reverse osmosis, vacuum drying for 12-18 hours to obtain Dendrobium nobile alkaloid; adding pseudo-ginseng saponin in Dendrobium nobile alkaloid, shizandra extract and red yeast powder, uniformly mixing to obtain mixture; and taking mixture, mixing with auxiliary materials, using capsule filling machine for filling capsule. The aqueous solution of ammonium salt substance is not thermally decomposed into ammonia gas at 65 degrees C or below 65 degrees C.

TI Preparing lycopene particle based on subcritical extraction and supercritical granulation combined process involves pre-treating tomato and placing pre-treated tomato into extracting tank, adding pure water, heating, and stopping heating.
AU HUANG D
   ZHAO K
   ZOU J
   QIAN H
   LI B
   CHEN W
AE UNIV CHINA PHARM (UYCP-C)
GA 202178956N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lycopene particle based on subcritical extraction and supercritical granulation combined process involves pre-treating tomato and placing the pre-treated tomato into an extracting tank, adding pure water, heating, stopping heating after reaching a certain temperature, pressurizing to a certain pressure to extract for a certain time, after finishing extracting, cooling the steam discharged from the extracting tank, further cooling the steam through the low-temperature cooling tank, the final extracting solution enters the receiving tank, and collecting the extracting solution. The water is fully removed from the extracting solution, used organic solvent to dissolve to obtain the sample solution, and introduced the carbon dioxide (CO2) into the crystallization kettle.
   USE - Method for preparing lycopene particle based on subcritical extraction and supercritical granulation combined process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that the extracting rate of the lycopene is low and the utilization rate of the extracted lycopene is low.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing lycopene particle based on subcritical extraction and supercritical granulation combined process involves pre-treating tomato and placing the pre-treated tomato into an extracting tank, adding pure water, heating, stopping heating after reaching a certain temperature, pressurizing to a certain pressure to extract for a certain time, after finishing extracting, cooling the steam discharged from the extracting tank, further cooling the steam through the low-temperature cooling tank, the final extracting solution enters the receiving tank, and collecting the extracting solution. The water is fully removed from the extracting solution, used organic solvent to dissolve to obtain the sample solution, introduced the carbon dioxide (CO2) into the crystallization kettle, set a certain pressure and a certain temperature range, introduced the sample solution into the crystallization kettle, introduced CO2, ended the sample solution, maintained the CO2 flow for a certain time, removed the residual solvent, finally relieved pressure, and gradually reduced pressure to the atmospheric pressure in the crystallizing kettle. The crystallizing kettle is opened and collected the lycopene superfine particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is acetone, ethyl acetate or dichloromethane. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of liquid to material is 15:1-24:1(mL/g). The extraction temperature is 110-140 degrees C, the extracting pressure is 0-4 megapascal (MPa), and the extracting time is 20-50 minutes. The sample solution is in the range of 0-0.15mg/mL, without adding any auxiliary materials. The granulating time is 20-50 minutes. Preferred Process: The pre-treatment of tomato involves making a certain amount of fresh tomato into paste or using a granulator to cut into not more than 10 cm3 of tomato dice.

PN US2021085899-A1
TI Medical device e.g. cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) device for negative pressure ventilation, has controller retracts piston generates negative pressure ventilation and returns piston from expansion position to reference position.
AU TAYLOR T
   DANZIGER B
   BOWMAN R
   CLARK N
AE PHYSIO-CONTROL INC (STYC-C)
GA 202142221T
AB    NOVELTY - The medical device (100) has a retention structure (108) which is provided for partially encircling a patient's body, and includes a central element and a support portion (112) placed underneath a patient. A piston (106) is extended from the central element. A driver (118) is coupled to the piston configured to retract and extend the piston. A patient contact element is attached to the piston and is configured to adhere to the patient's body. A controller (104) is configured to cause the driver during a session to perform two cycles of negative pressure ventilation, retract the piston from a reference position to an expansion position to expand a chest of the patient to generate negative pressure ventilation, and return the piston from the expansion position to the reference position. A user interface (136) is configured to transmit the at least one input to the controller, where the input includes blood oxygen saturation of the patient and end-tidal carbon-dioxide of the patient.
   USE - Medical device such as cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) device, defibrillator, monitor, monitor-defibrillator, ventilator and capnography device used for negative pressure ventilation, configured for use with children.
   ADVANTAGE - The medical device automatically changes the performance of the negative pressure ventilation cycles or the high frequency ventilation cycles based on sensed end-tidal carbon-dioxide, capnogram, ECG, invasive blood pressure, oxygen saturation, or plethysmograph values without operator input. The pediatric versions can have smaller target tidal volumes and higher ventilation rate that can be achieved by moving the plunger less distance and faster or more frequently. The inclusion of valves on the ventilation bag can reduce the risk of barotrauma. The operator can select the appropriate container size based on the size of the ventilation bag and attach the container to patient support portion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the components of medical device.
   Medical device (100)
   Controller (104)
   Piston (106)
   Retention structure (108)
   Support portion (112)
   Driver (118)

TI Providing target aqueous urea composition with            target concentration urea involves reacting carbon            dioxide and ammonia in synthesis section to form crude            urea mixture, separating crude urea mixture in first            aqueous urea solution.
AU BYNES A
   AMIN S
   VAN BELZEN R
AE YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
GA 202119268S
AB    NOVELTY - Providing a target aqueous urea composition                with a target concentration urea involves reacting                carbon dioxide and ammonia in a synthesis section                to form a crude urea mixture, separating the crude                urea mixture in an first aqueous urea solution and                a process condensate using a separating section,                passing the process condensate through a first                desorption column to provide a first partially                purified flow, passing a unit of the first                partially purified flow through a hydrolyser to                provide a second partially purified flow, passing                the second partially purified flow through a second                desorption column to provide a purified water flow,                optionally concentrating the first aqueous urea                solution in a pre-evaporator to provide a                concentrated second aqueous urea solution, mixing                the first aqueous urea solution and/or the second                concentrated aqueous urea solution with the first                partially purified flow and a unit of the purified                water flow in a ratio to obtain the final                product.
   USE - Method for providing a target aqueous urea                composition with a target concentration urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides more flexibility in a urea                production process and/or production plant and an                energy efficient method of producing a urea                solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a process condensate treatment plant,                which comprises a first desorption column                comprising an inlet for a process condensate and an                outlet for a first partially purified flow outlet,                a hydrolyser, which comprises an inlet for the                first partially purified flow and an outlet for a                second partially purified flow, a second desorption                column comprising an inlet for the second partially                purified flow and an outlet for a purified water                flow, a tapping point for bypassing partially the                first partially purified flow from the hydrolyser                and the second desorption column; and
   (2) a urea production plant, which comprises                the process condensate treatment plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first contaminant                  is ammonia. The synthesis section comprises an                  urea reactor, a carbamate condenser, a                  ammonia-stripper, a carbon dioxide-stripper                  and/or a carbamate decomposer. Preferred                  Conditions: The target aqueous urea composition                  has a upper limit for a contaminant. The amount                  of first partially purified flow used is so that                  in the target aqueous urea composition 10-100% of                  the limit for the first contaminant is reached.                  The upper limit for the first contaminant is 0.20                  % by weight, compared to the total weight of the                  target aqueous urea composition. The first                  desorption column is placed on top of the second                  desorption column. The procedure is performed in                  a condenser. The ratio of the first partially                  purified flow over the purified water flow is                  0.5. The ratio of first aqueous urea solution or                  the second urea solution over the sum of the                  first partially purified flow and the purified                  water flow is 1.0. Preferred Process: The                  concentration of the urea and first contaminant                  in the first partially purified flow is                  determined, which is used to determine the ratio.                  The concentration of urea in the first aqueous                  urea solution and the second aqueous urea                  solution is determined, which determines the                  ratio.

TI Gas separation system for producing methane-enriched gas, has gas separation membrane units including gas inlet, permeated gas outlet and non-permeated gas outlet, compression unit interposed in stock gas feed line, and connection lines.
AU NAKAMURA T
   FUKUDA N
AE UBE IND LTD (UBEI-C)
   UBE IND LTD (UBEI-C)
GA 2020980734
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has: a first gas separation membrane unit (11) and a second gas separation membrane unit (12), where each gas separation membrane unit includes at least a gas inlet, a permeated gas outlet and a non-permeated gas outlet; a stock gas feed line (16) connected to a gas inlet (11a) of the first gas separation membrane unit; a first compression unit (21) interposed in the feed line; a first connection line (14) for connecting a permeated gas discharge port (11b) of the first gas separation membrane unit, and a gas inlet port (12a) of the second gas separation membrane unit; and a second connection line (18) for connecting a non-permeated gas discharge port (12c) of the second gas separation membrane unit and the feed line. The separation membrane units have a gas separation selectivity (P' carbon dioxide/P' methane) of at least 30 and a methane recovery rate of at least 98%.
   USE - The system is used for producing methane-enriched gas from a stock gas containing carbon dioxide and methane, where the stock gas is biogas (all claimed). The system is used for methane separation and methane enrichment.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces the membrane area while suppressing the required compression power as compared with the conventional gas separation system while achieving high recovery rate and high purity of methane. The system reduces number of membrane modules without significantly impairing the overall efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing methane-enriched gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the configuration of a gas separation system for methane separation.

   First connection line (14)
   Stock gas feed line (16)
   Second connection line (18)
   First compression unit (21)

TI Preparing calcium carbonate filler useful for            paper making, comprises e.g. crushing limestone,            sieving, completely calcining, removing impurity,            introducing calcined lime to digestion tank for            digestion reaction, adding water, and heating.
AU WEI M
AE GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI XIAYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202084795W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate filler comprises                e.g. (i) crushing limestone, sieving, completely                calcining at 1000-1200 degrees C, removing                impurity, introducing the calcined lime obtained by                calcining to digestion tank for digestion reaction,                and adding water to obtain calcium hydroxide                slurry, and (ii) conveying the calcium hydroxide                slurry to the carbonation reaction tower, heating                to 50-70 degrees C, adding 1-2 wt.% urea, 0.3-0.8                wt.% melamine and 0.5-1.5 wt.% pod galactomannan,                stirring uniformly, introducing kiln gas containing                carbon dioxide for carbonation reaction, the                carbonation reaction to pH is 8, adding fatty acid                salt, sodium lanolin sulfosuccinate and isopropyl                triisostearoyl titanate, continuously carbonating                reaction until the pH is 6.5-7, closing the kiln                gas, stopping carbonation reaction, and obtaining                calcium carbonate slurry.
   USE - The calcium carbonate filler is useful for                paper making.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate has uniform particle                size distribution, good stability, good                dispersibility, good bonding force with fibers and                good flame retardant and antibacterial                properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium carbonate filler comprises                (i) crushing limestone, sieving, completely                calcining at 1000-1200 degrees C, removing                impurity, introducing the calcined lime obtained by                calcining to digestion tank for digestion reaction,                and adding water to obtain calcium hydroxide                slurry, (ii) conveying the calcium hydroxide slurry                to the carbonation reaction tower, heating to 50-70                degrees C, adding 1-2 wt.% urea, 0.3-0.8 wt.%                melamine and 0.5-1.5 wt.% pod galactomannan,                stirring uniformly, introducing kiln gas containing                carbon dioxide for carbonation reaction, the                carbonation reaction to pH is 8, adding fatty acid                salt, sodium lanolin sulfosuccinate and isopropyl                triisostearoyl titanate, continuously carbonating                reaction until the pH is 6.5-7, closing the kiln                gas, stopping carbonation reaction, and obtaining                calcium carbonate slurry, (iii) filtering the                calcium carbonate slurry by filter press, adding                2-sulfonic acid formamide, bi-cyclic cage phosphate                for secondary surface modification, drying,                crushing, and sieving to obtain calcium carbonate                filler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the method, the adding                  amount of the first surface modifying agent is                  1-3% of the dry weight of the calcium hydroxide.                  In the method, the mass ratio of the fatty acid                  salt in the first surface modifying agent, sodium                  lanolin sulfosuccinate and isopropyl                  triisostearoyl titanate is 3-5:1-3:1-3. In the                  method, the adding amount of the 2-sulfonic acid                  formamide and bicyclic cage phosphate is 0.8-1.5%                  of the dry weight of calcium hydroxide. In the                  method, the digestion reaction is digestion                  reaction at gray-water ratio of 1:5-7, and water                  temperature of 70-80 degrees C. In the method,                  performing carbonation reaction at 25-32 degrees                  C, and rotation speed of 60-120                  revolutions/minute. In the method, the filter                  pressing is filter pressing until the solid                  content is 70-80%. In the method, the drying is                  drying using pulse airflow drier. In the method,                  the feeding speed of the pulse airflow drier is                  200-300 kg/hour, the drying temperature is 80-110                  degrees C, and the wind speed is 10-15                  m/seconds.

AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
   CHINA ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY GROUP CORP N (CETC-C)
   CHINA ELECTRONICS SCI & TECH NO 18 RES I (CETC-C)
GA 2020819093
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen by isooctane adsorption                enhancement reforming comprises (1) filling calcium                oxide-aluminum complex bifunctional catalyst in the                fixed bed reactor, where the calcium oxide-aluminum                complex bifunctional catalyst contains 0.05-2 wt.%                of precious metal promoters, (ii) injecting                isooctane and water into fixed-bed reactor under                the condition of inert gas as the carrier gas,                where the volume flow rate of the isooctane is                0.3-6 ml/hour, the volume space velocity of the                water is 2.4-13.2 ml/hour, andthe reaction                temperature is 400-700 degrees C, (iii) when the                adsorption of carbon dioxide reaches saturation and                then performing catalyst regeneration in the                nitrogen atmosphere, where the regeneration                temperature is 500-1000 degrees C and the                regeneration time is 0.1-6 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrogen by                isooctane adsorption enhancement reforming.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces hydrogen from isooctane                has concentration of 98%; does not change the                concentration of hydrogen for 10 cycles; and there                is no carbon deposit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen by isooctane adsorption                enhancement reforming comprises (1) filling calcium                oxide-aluminum complex (M-CaOCa12Al14O33)                bifunctional catalyst in the fixed bed reactor,                where the calcium oxide-aluminum complex                (M-CaOCa12Al14O33) bifunctional catalyst contains                0.05-2 wt.% of precious metal promoters, (ii)                injecting isooctane and water into fixed-bed                reactor under the condition of inert gas as the                carrier gas, where the volume flow rate of the                isooctane is 0.3-6 ml/hour, the volume space                velocity of the water is 2.4-13.2 ml/hour, andthe                reaction temperature is 400-700 degrees C, (iii)                when the adsorption of carbon dioxide reaches                saturation and then performing catalyst                regeneration in the nitrogen atmosphere, where the                regeneration temperature is 500-1000 degrees C and                the regeneration time is 0.1-6 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i): the                  precious metal promoter in the calcium                  oxide-aluminum complex (M-CaOCa12Al14O33)                  bifunctional catalyst is rhodium, ruthenium,                  palladium, platinum and/or iridium, the mass                  content of M in the M-CaOCa12Al14O33bifunctional                  catalyst is 5-15 wt.%, and the mass content of                  the precious metal promoter in the                  M-CaOCa12Al14O33bifunctional catalyst is 0.5-2                  wt.%. The molar ratio between calcium in the                  calcium salt and aluminum in the aluminum salt is                  2-4, the mass ratio between metal M in the metal                  M salt and precious metal in the precious metal                  solution is 5-15:0.05-2; the molar ratio between                  aluminum salt and sodium hydroxide is                  0.25-0.45:1, and the molar ratio of sodium                  hydroxide and sodium carbonate is 2-1:1.                  Preferred Method: In step (i): the regeneration                  temperature is 750-850 degrees C, and the                  regeneration time is 0.5-1 hour; and in step                  (ii): the volumetric space velocity of the inert                  gas is 100-200 ml/minute, the volumetric space                  velocity of the isooctane is 0.6-3.6 ml/hour, and                  the volumetric space velocity of water is                  6.6-10.8 ml/hour and the reaction temperature is                  550-600 degrees C. In step (i): the calcium                  oxide-aluminum complex (M-CaOCa12Al14O33)                  bifunctional catalyst is prepared by dissolving                  metal M salt, calcium salt and aluminum salt in                  water, adding precious metal aqueous solution,                  and then adding sodium hydroxide and sodium                  carbonate for precipitation reaction, adjusting                  pH value, allowing to stand, then carrying out                  aging, filtering, drying and calcining to obtain                  calcium oxide-aluminum complex (M-CaOCa12Al14O33)                  bifunctional catalyst containing precious metal                  promoter. The temperature of the precipitation                  reaction is room temperature, and the                  precipitation reaction time is 0.2-2 hours, the                  pH value is 9-11; the allowing to stand and aging                  temperature is 50-90 degrees C, and the allowing                  to stand and aging time is 12-24 hours, the                  calcination temperature is 700-800 degrees C, and                  the calcination time is 2-4 hours, and the                  concentration of the aluminum salt in water is                  0.3-0.7 mmol/ml.M = Ni, Co, Cu or Fe.

PT P
PN CN111349245-A; CN111349245-B
TI Nanosheet material used for e.g. electrocatalytic reactions, comprises composite sheet structure composed of metal organic framework nanosheets and graphene-based material sheets, which are laminated or alternately laminated.
AU WANG S
   XIA Z
   SUN G
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202063025Q
AB    NOVELTY - A nanosheet material comprises a composite sheet structure composed of metal organic framework nanosheets and graphene-based material sheets, which are laminated or alternately laminated. The thickness of each layer of metal organic framework nanosheets is 1-5 nm. The thickness of each layer of graphene-based material is 1-10 nm. The thickness of the composite sheet is 2-100 nm.
   USE - Nanosheet material used in electrocatalytic reactions such as oxygen reduction electrocatalysis, oxygen evolution electrocatalysis, methanol oxidation or carbon dioxide electroreduction, and gas adsorption materials, drug release materials, and material separation materials (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the nanosheet material, which involves preparing graphene oxide by Hummer method with or without the use of graphene oxide to prepare doped graphene and with or without loading graphene with metal nanoparticles, dispersing graphene-based material in solutions of dimethylformamide, dimethylacetamide or dimethyl sulfoxide at a concentration of 0.5-5 mg/ml to obtain a colloidal, adding transition metal salt to the obtained colloidal solution at a concentration of 0.01-0.5 mmol/ml, and uniformly ultrasonically dispersing, adding same amount of ligand as the transition metal salt, and ultrasonically dispersing again to obtain a uniform mixture, adding mixed solvent of ethanol and water in a volume ratio of (1:5)-(5:1) to the above mixture so that the mixed solvent accounts for 5-20% of the total volume of the mixture, and then dispersing again, adding 1-4% of the total volume of the mixture of triethylamine precipitant under inert gas protection, and quickly sealing the container, and placing under ultrasonic conditions for reaction, separating and washing, freeze-drying the precipitate to obtain the composite structural material sample; and
   (2) use of the nanosheet material used in electrocatalytic reactions such as oxygen reduction electrocatalysis, oxygen evolution electrocatalysis, methanol oxidation or carbon dioxide electroreduction, or gas adsorption materials, drug release materials, or material separation materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves preparing graphene-based materials using graphite oxide as precursor by Hummer method, ultrasonically dispersing prepared graphite oxide in deionized water at a concentration of 0.1-10 mg/ml, and continuously stirring for 2-8 hours, condensing to solid state with liquid nitrogen, placing it in a freeze dryer for 24-48 hours, freeze-drying until the solvent is completely removed to obtain graphene oxide, adding doping element precursors, and preparing graphene oxide containing doped elements or graphene oxide loaded with metal nanoparticle precursors by chemical pyrolysis or hydrothermal synthesis to obtain intermediate products, dispersing intermediate product in deionized water at a concentration of 10-50 mg/ml, condensing to solid state with liquid nitrogen, placing it in a freeze dryer for 24-48 hours, freeze-drying until the solvent is completely removed, and placing the dried sample in a tube furnace and chemically reducing in a hydrogen-argon mixture containing 3-5 vol.% hydrogen at 150-500 degrees C for 2-8 hours to obtain the metal nanoparticle-loaded graphene. If the graphene-based material is a graphene-supported metal nanoparticle sheet material, the metal precursor salt is added to the ethylene glycol dispersion solution of graphene oxide according to the ratio of metal to graphene mass of 1-40% by hydrothermal synthesis, adjusting the pH to 11-14 with sodium hydroxide, reacting in a water bath at 120-150 degrees C for 1-6 hours, filtering and washing after full reduction. Preferred Components: The metal organic framework nanosheet layer comprises a metal complex. The metal is chosen from iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc, and zirconium. The ligand molecule is terephthalic acid, trimellitic acid, benzoic acid and bipyridine. The graphene-based material comprises graphene oxide, doped graphene, and graphene loaded with metal nanoparticles. The doping element in the doped graphene comprises nitrogen, boron, sulfur, or phosphorus. The graphene-supported metal nanoparticles are alloy materials chosen from gold, platinum, silver, ruthenium, palladium, iron, cobalt, and nickel. The transition metal salt is chosen from nickel chloride, cobalt chloride, iron chloride, zinc chloride, copper chloride, and zirconium chloride. The ligand is chosen from terephthalic acid, trimellitic acid, pyridine, and 2-2'bipyridine. The inert gas is chosen from nitrogen, argon and helium. The washing solvent comprises ethanol. The graphene-based material is a graphene sheet layer doped with nitrogen, boron, sulfur, or phosphorus. The doping element precursor is chosen from melamine, boric acid, sodium thiosulfate, and phosphorus pentoxide. The prepared graphene materials are doped with nitrogen, boron, sulfur, and phosphorus. The metal precursor salt is chosen from chloroplatinic acid, chloropalladic acid, ruthenium chloride, chloroauric acid, silver nitrate, nickel chloride, cobalt chloride, and iron chloride. Preferred Composition: The loading amount of metal nanoparticles in graphene-supported metal nanoparticles is 0.5-60 %mass. The doping amount of the doping element is 0.1-10 %mass. The volume ratio of the mixed solvent of ethanol and water is (1:2)-(2:1). The total mass percentage concentration of metal precursor salt and graphene oxide is 1-10%. Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonic dispersion is carried out at a temperature of (-10 degrees C)-(10 degrees C) with a power of 50-500 W, and is reacted for 6-24 hours. The separation is carried out by centrifugal separation at 3000-10000 rpm, and is repeated 4-6 times.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN111087378-A; CN111087378-B
TI Method of preparing ethylene carbonate, involves            feeding carbon dioxide gas from gas feed unit to            tubular fixed bed reactor, and feeding ethylene oxide            from liquid feeding unit to tubular fixed bed reactor,            and obtaining ethylene carbonate.
AU ZHU J
   LIU Z
   DONG Z
   GU X
   TIAN W
   YU H
AE JIANGSU OXIRAN CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020388287
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out in a tubular                fixed bed reactor. The tubular fixed bed reactor is                provided with a reactor box (20), and several tubes                (8) that are arranged at intervals in the reactor                box. Several column tubes are filled with a                solid-supported catalyst. Several baffles are                provided between a heat exchange medium inlet (10)                and a heat exchange medium outlet (7). A shell side                is formed between the heat exchange medium inlet                and the heat exchange medium outlet. A liquid feed                unit is separated from the gas feed unit, by a gas                distributor (3). The gas distributor is provided                with axial through holes, such that the liquid                feeding unit and the gas feeding unit are in fluid                communication. The carbon dioxide gas is fed from                the gas feed unit to the tubular fixed bed reactor,                and ethylene oxide is fed from the liquid feeding                unit to the tubular fixed bed reactor, and ethylene                carbonate is obtained.
   USE - Method of preparing ethylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out in a column-tube                reactor, which realizes the uniform distribution of                gas and liquid into each tube, eliminates the dead                zone and retention layer in the liquid chamber, and                avoids the hidden danger of self-polymerization                explosion. The engineering design problems of                carbonylation reaction gas-liquid-solid three-phase                reaction gas-liquid distribution unevenness,                partial flow stagnation, slow heat transfer and low                reaction efficiency are solved during the                preparation of ethylene carbonate. The use of a                solid-supported catalyst prevents the catalyst from                being carried out by the reaction product, avoids                the process of separating the traditional                homogeneous catalyst from the product ethylene                carbonate, reduces process energy consumption and                simplifies the process flow.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural                diagram of a tubular fixed bed reactor.
   Gas distributor (3)
   Heat exchange medium outlet (7)
   Tube (8)
   Heat exchange medium inlet (10)
   Reactor box (20)

AU LIU T
   WANG J
   CHEN L
   CHENG W
   ZHOU W
   WANG H
   GAO L
   ZHANG W
   LIU W
AE QINGDAO INST BIOENERGY & BIOPROCESS TECH (CAQD-C)
GA 202038004N
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a first liquid storage tank (1) which is provided with a first liquid discharging pipe (8) and a first liquid inlet pipe (10), and a second liquid storage tank (2) is provided with a second liquid discharging pipe and second liquid inlet pipe (9). The first row of liquid pipe and second drainage pipe are connected with a first port adapter (11). The first inlet pipe and second inlet pipe are connected with a second port adapter (12). The first port adapter and second port adapter connect closed main portion reactor. The first liquid discharging pipe is provided with a first drain valve, and the second liquid discharging pipe is provided with a second drain valve.    USE - Circulation device used for circulating microalgae culture liquid.

The setting mode of the liquid storage tank is more flexible, and the microalgae culture liquid circulation device achieves good application prospect. The cutting force suffered by the cell is greatly reduced to avoid cell damage. The microalgae culture liquid circulation device realizes the flow mode, flow speed and flow rate, reactor liquid level height of convenient and reliable control, by changing the pressure.

   ROLLS-ROYCE PLC (RORO-C)
GA 2020172180
AB    NOVELTY - The turbomachine (105) has an inlet (201). An inducerless radial impeller (202) has multiple backswept blades. A fully vaneless diffuser (203) is provided. A volute (204) comprises a tongue and has a flow area at the tongue equal to that of the diffuser. The inlet is radially flared to induce a radial component in flow prior to an entry to the impeller. A hub hade angle of the impeller at the entry is from 50 to 70 degrees. The backswept blade has a blade exit angle of from -50 to -70 degrees. The backswept blade comprises a set of main blades (211) and a set of splitter blades. A meridional chord length of the splitter blades (212) is 70 percent of a meridional chord length of the main blades. The impeller comprises one splitter blade for each main blade.
   USE - Turbomachine for closed indirect-heated brayton cycle (claimed) e.g. air-breathing gas turbine for compressing supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The radial impeller continue to operate stably with little or no pressure drop. The diffusion and static pressure distortion at the exit of the diffuser is avoided. The turbomachine provides the widest possible operating range due to increased stability margin. The hub hade angle reduces risk of separation. The risk of condensation is reduced. The inlet is large relative to the size of the impeller to facilitate sufficient margin in inlet velocity for a given mass flow to the velocity. The absolute Mach number at the impeller exit is reduced, thus reducing losses in the diffuser and improving efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating turbomachine.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has direct contact steam generation system (1) that converts a gaseous, liquid or solid fuel (4) in presence of oxidant (5) and introduces fuel, oxygen and water into direct contact steam generation system through inlets, to produce mixed vapor stream (7) using moderator water (6). The mixed vapor stream is then led into pressurized heat recovery system (2) to produce a partially condensed product (10). The partially condensed product is led into carbon di-oxide (CO2) separation system (3) through connecting portion between the pressurized heat recovery system and CO2 separation system. The CO2separation system is configured to reduce the carbon di-oxide content from partially condensed product to produce a CO2-lean liquid product (11). The pressurized heat recovery system is configured to utilize a latent heat of the mixed vapor stream to produce a lower pressure vapor stream (12) from the CO2-lean liquid product exiting the CO2 separation system.
   USE - System for removing carbon dioxide (CO2) from steam product from direct contact steam generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The pressure differential between outlet of the direct contact steam generation and outlet of the CO2 separation system is such that the minimum approach temperature of the heat exchangers is 30 degrees C. The CO2 content in the CO2-lean liquid product is reduced to between 0 to 5% by mass before being sent back to the pressurized heat recovery system to be re-evaporated.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare titanium-based laser cladding coating that does not generate cracks and pores, and has good cladding effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing titanium-based laser cladding coating involves polishing surface of the metal substrate, mixing prepared powder coating powder according to certain ratio, and then preheating mixed raw material powder, applying the preheated powder uniformly on the surface of the metal substrate, laser cladding of the preset powder, so that the preset powder and the surface of the metal substrate melt at the same time, forming laser cladding coating on the surface of the substrate, and laser cladding steps, using carbon dioxide transverse laser beam for cladding and inert gas protection on the surface of the metal substrate. The laser power is selected from 3.2-3.8 kilowatt, scanning speed is 450-650mm/min, spot diameter is 4-6mm, and thickness of the cladding layer is 0.5-0.9mm, after plasma cladding, laser power is 1500 watt and speed of light is 10mm/s. The laser cladding process uses side blowing 12-18L /min inert gas to protect the cladding area and repeat, so that the thickness of the cladding layer is 3-4mm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The inert gas is argon. Preferred Process: The method involves performing rough sanding the surface of the metal substrate with coarse sandpaper, after sanding, finely sanding surface of the metal substrate with fine sandpaper until surface of the metal substrate is smooth, preheating the metal substrate to soften the surface of the metal substrate, grinding the surface of the metal substrate with a grinding wheel, using cleaning solution to clean polished metal substrate, and blow drying after cleaning. The mixed raw materials are placed on a hot plate, put it in preheating box, and start the preheating box, adjust preheating temperature to 80-120 degrees C, and preheating time is 16-20 minutes, increase the preheating temperature to 160-200 degrees C, and preheating time is 20-24 minutes.

PN CN110157008-A
TI Biporous covalent organic skeleton material linked            by imine bond useful in preparing photocatalyst for            catalyzing the reduction of carbon dioxide to carbon            monoxide.
AU ZHANG F
   LU Y
   YANG Y
   DING W
   ZHANG H
AE UNIV HARBIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHS-C)
GA 201977127W
AB    NOVELTY - Biporous covalent organic skeleton material                linked by imine bond (I) is claimed. (I) is                prepared by reacting                3,3',5,5'-tetraformyl-4,4'-biphenol and                p-phenylenediamine by solvothermal method and (I)                belongs to hexagonal PM space group having unit                cell parameter a is 30.7 Angstrom , b is 3.5                Angstrom , c is 30.7 Angstrom , alpha is 90?, beta                is 90?, and delta is 120? with regular hexagonal                and triangular staggered holes, and aperture sizes                of 21 Angstrom and 9 Angstrom respectively.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing photocatalyst for                catalyzing the reduction of carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biporous covalent organic skeleton material                linked by imine bond of formula (I) is claimed. (I)                is prepared by reacting                3,3',5,5'-tetraformyl-4,4'-biphenol and                p-phenylenediamine by solvothermal method and (I)                belongs to hexagonal PM space group having unit                cell parameter a is 30.7 Angstrom , b is 3.5                Angstrom , c is 30.7 Angstrom , alpha is 90?, beta                is 90?, and delta is 120? with regular hexagonal                and triangular staggered holes, and aperture sizes                of 21 Angstrom and 9 Angstrom respectively. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing                biporous covalent organic skeleton materials linked                by imine bond comprising (i) adding                3,3',5,5'-tetraformyl-4,4'-biphenol,                p-phenylenediamine, 1,4-dioxane and mesitylene                sequentially into vacuum tube, ultrasonically                treating at room temperature for 1 hour, adding                acetic acid, and performing three liquid nitrogen                freezing and degassing operations to realize vacuum                oxygen-free environment, (ii) transferring                processed vacuum tube into oven, reacting at 120                degrees C for 72 hours, cooling to room temperature                and filtering to obtain crude product, and (iii)                washing obtained crude product with tetrahydrofuran                until the filtrate is colorless, performing solvent                exchange with acetone, and vacuum drying at 100                degrees C for 24 hours to obtain final                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), molar                  ratio between 3,3',5,5'-tetraformyl-4,4'-biphenol                  and p-phenylenediamine is 1:2 based on 1/10000                  scale; and adding 0.15 mmol                  3,3',5,5'-tetraformyl-4,4'-biphenol and 0.3 mmol                  p-phenylenediamine into vacuum tube. In step (i),                  amount of acetic acid is 1 M, and volume of                  acetic acid added is 0.5 ml. Preferred Method:                  The step (i) comprises removing 1,4-dioxane and                  mesitylene in a molar ratio of 1:1 accurately                  using pipette, then pipetting 1.5 ml 1,4-dioxane                  and 1.5 ml mesitylene into vacuum tube.

PT P
PN CN110078390-A
TI Preparing high-purity high-quality calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide powder comprises e.g. crushing calcined quicklime and then digesting, adding ammonium chloride and filtering calcium chloride solution.
AU DENG J
   DENG C
   YANG L
AE DENG J (DENG-Individual)
GA 2019692341
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity high-quality calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide powder comprises (a) crushing calcined quicklime and then digesting, adding ammonium chloride and mixing digested slaked lime with ammonium chloride to form a calcium chloride solution; (b) filtering calcium chloride solution, filtering out insoluble impurities, removing iron and secondary filtration, filtering calcium chloride solution, adding a small amount of hydrogen peroxide and a small amount of ammonium carbonate, and then filtering to remove impurities of ferrous iron; (c) adding calcium chloride solution after secondary filtration, passing through purified carbon dioxide to form a high-purity calcium carbonate precipitate; (d) subjecting high-purity calcium carbonate to liquid-solid separation and drying, and subjecting to secondary calcination; (e) crushing, digesting, and then drying the second-calcined high-purity and high-quality calcium oxide; and (f) drying, crushing and classifying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high-purity high-quality calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide powder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-purity high-quality calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide powder comprises (a) crushing calcined quicklime and then digesting, adding ammonium chloride and mixing digested slaked lime with ammonium chloride to form a calcium chloride solution; (b) filtering calcium chloride solution, filtering out insoluble impurities, removing iron and secondary filtration, filtering calcium chloride solution, adding a small amount of hydrogen peroxide and a small amount of ammonium carbonate, and then filtering to remove impurities of ferrous iron; (c) adding calcium chloride solution after secondary filtration, passing through purified carbon dioxide to form a high-purity calcium carbonate precipitate; (d) subjecting high-purity calcium carbonate to liquid-solid separation and drying, and subjecting to secondary calcination to form high-purity and high-quality calcium oxide; (e) crushing, digesting, and then drying the second-calcined high-purity and high-quality calcium oxide to form high-purity and high-quality calcium hydroxide; and (f) drying high-purity and high-quality calcium oxide and calcium hydroxide, crushing and classifying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b) digestion process, the water-to-feed ratio is 12-13:1, the digester-level stirring speed is 700-800 revolutions/minute and the digester outlet temperature is less than 70 degrees C. In the step (c) ammonium chloride addition process, the molar ratio of ammonium chloride and calcium oxide with water is 0.98-1:1. In the step (c) ammonium chloride addition process, the purity of ammonium chloride added to the water is analytically pure. In the step (e) in the iron removal process, the amount of hydrogen peroxide and ammonium carbonate added to the water is the mass of the calcium oxide and hydrogen peroxide 0.01-0.02% and ammonium carbonate is 0.05-0.08%. In the step (e) in the iron removal process, the hydrogen peroxide is analyzed as pure hydrogen peroxide. In the step (e) in the iron removal process , ammonium carbonate is a by-product of the ammonia gas formed by adding ammonium chloride and the carbon dioxide by-product formed in the calcination and is purified in a small amount of water to obtain the reaction. In the step (f) purified carbon dioxide is introduced and the carbon dioxide by-product formed in the calcination is obtained after purification. In second digestion process, the water-to-feed ratio during digestion is 0.55-0.70, the digester-level stirring speed is 700-800 revolutions/minute and the digester outlet temperature is about 70 degrees C. In the classification process the classifier rotates at 1000-1200 revolutions/minute.
