PT P
PN RU2691912-C1
TI METHOD FOR PRODUCTION OF MICROBALLOONS FROM MELT OF CEMENT CLINKER.
AU KRYSANOV N O
   GRIGORYAN A F
   GURDZHINYAN S KH
AE KRYSANOV N O (KRYS-Individual)
   GRIGORYAN A F (GRIG-Individual)
   GURDZHINYAN S KH (GURD-Individual)
GA 201951923C
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to cement industry, in particular, to a method for production of cement clinker in form of microspheres. In gas generator 2 there supplied is mixture containing lime, coke, clay (mixture of quartz oxide and other admixtures) and inert, and from below oxygen is fed into gas generator 2. Heating of oxygen rising from bottom of gas generator 2, coming from air short-cycle adsorption unit 1, is performed due to heat release of heated and lowering inert. Oxygen reacts with coke carbon to release a large amount of heat to form carbon dioxide and water. Superheated CO2 gases and H2O (form of steam) reacts with carbon of coke and form synthesis gas, which is a mixture of carbon monoxide CO2 and hydrogen H. Lime, after passing through combustion zone of gas generator 2, is decarbonised. Further, synthesis gas is supplied to scrubber 9, where it is purified from water and resins, and then supplied to an amine purification plant 10, thereafter, part of synthesis gas is fed to steam conversion unit 11 where carbon monoxide is converted to hydrogen and carbon dioxide. Further, converted synthesis gas is supplied to an amine purification plant 12, where hydrogen is released, supplied to the unit of microwave plasmatrons with surface discharge devices 30 with oxygen supply nozzles 6 in reactor 5, where hydrogen dissociates with conversion of diatomic hydrogen into atomic hydrogen, with subsequent recombination into diatomic hydrogen on the surface of clinker, with release of large amount of heat and temperature of about 3,600C, leading to its melt and its transfer into a liquid, fluid state. Increase in temperature of clinker and concomitant decrease in viscosity of clinker increases rate of dissolution of CaO formed from coke in clinker, which contributes to sharp increase in clinker formation rate. Obtained melt of clinker is exposed to a spherical agent, in which under action of surface tension forces microballoons are formed, which get into the cyclone are cooled.
   USE - Manufacturing technology.
   ADVANTAGE - High rate of clinker formation.
1 cl, 5 dwg

PT P
PN US2019144895-A1; US10894968-B2
TI Converting feedstock to fermentation product comprises e.g. treating feedstock to obtain fermentable carbohydrate, conducting first fermentation to produce fermentation product and obtaining biogas produced from second fermentation.
AU FOODY P J
   FOODY B
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 201942209S
AB    NOVELTY - Converting (P1) feedstock to fermentation product, comprises treating the feedstock to obtain a fermentable carbohydrate, conducting a first fermentation to produce fermentation product, where the first fermentation is conducted on the fermentable carbohydrate, obtaining biogas produced from a second fermentation, where the second fermentation comprises an anaerobic digestion, conducting a third fermentation to produce fermentation product, where the third fermentation is conducted on a gas substrate comprising at least one component obtained or derived from the biogas and the components comprise carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide or hydrogen, using an aqueous stream comprising fermentation product produced in the third fermentation within the process so that fermentation product produced in the third fermentation is copresent with fermentable carbohydrate in a carbohydrate fermentation and recovering fermentation product produced in the first and third fermentations.
   USE - The processes are useful for converting feedstock to fermentation product and converting cellulosic feedstock to ethanol (claimed) for forming a fuel or a fuel intermediate.
   ADVANTAGE - The processes: improve product yield from non-fossil organic material and/or maintain a beneficial greenhouse gas (GHG) emission impact; utilize synergistic components; are efficient; and have a reduced cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for converting (P2) cellulosic feedstock to ethanol, comprising (a) treating the cellulosic feedstock to produce a sugar, (b) conducting a first fermentation to produce ethanol, where the first fermentation is conducted using the sugar as a substrate, (c) feeding a portion of the treated feedstock not converted to ethanol to an anaerobic digester to produce biogas, (d) collecting biogas from the anaerobic digester, where the biogas comprises carbon dioxide and methane, (e) fermenting a gas to produce ethanol, where the gas comprises at least one component of the collected biogas and/or at least one component derived from the collected biogas and (f) recovering the ethanol produced in steps (b) and (e).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The fermentable carbohydrate is derived from cellulose and the biogas is obtained from a landfill or anaerobic digestion of cellulosic material. The process (P1) comprises: subjecting a gas to a reforming process to produce syngas, where the gas comprises methane from the biogas; subjecting a gas to a hydrogen production process to produce hydrogen, where the gas comprises methane from the biogas; using heat produced during the reforming process as an energy source for producing the ethanol; using heat produced during the hydrogen production process as an energy source for producing the ethanol; conducting the second fermentation, where the second fermentation is conducted in an anaerobic digester and the anaerobic digester is fed at least one secondary stream produced during the process of converting feedstock to fermentation product; and separating carbon dioxide in the biogas from methane in the biogas, where the gas substrate comprises the separated carbon dioxide and hydrogen produced from fossil fuel. Recovering the ethanol produced in the first and third fermentations comprises distilling an effluent of the first fermentation comprising the ethanol produced in the third fermentation. The gas fermented in step (e) comprises hydrogen produced by converting fossil methane to carbon monoxide and hydrogen by reforming, converting at least a portion of the carbon monoxide by a water-gas shift reaction to carbon dioxide, thus producing a stream comprising fossil carbon dioxide and hydrogen and separating at least a portion of the hydrogen from the stream of previous step from non-hydrogen components to produce a stream enriched in hydrogen and a stream comprising fossil carbon dioxide. The process (P2) comprises using an aqueous stream comprising ethanol produced in step (e) within the process so that ethanol produced in a gas fermentation is copresent with sugar being fermented. The portion of the treated feedstock fed to the anaerobic digester comprises still bottoms produced by recovering ethanol produced within the process. Preferred Components: The fermentation product produced in the first and third fermentations is ethanol. The gas subject to the reforming process comprises raw biogas, partially purified biogas or renewable natural gas. The gas substrate comprises the syngas or hydrogen produced from natural gas. The gas substrate comprises carbon dioxide produced and collected from the first fermentation. The gas subject to the hydrogen production process comprises raw biogas, partially purified biogas or renewable natural gas. The gas substrate comprises the hydrogen produced by the hydrogen production process or carbon dioxide produced and collected from the first fermentation. The ethanol produced in the first and third fermentations qualifies as cellulosic ethanol.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PN WO2019080090-A1; EP3677299-A1; US2020238033-A1; CN111565780-A; EP3677299-A4
TI Method for monitoring anesthetic output            concentration, involves calculating concentration of            anesthetic output by anesthetic output device by using            fresh gas flow, inhaled gas flow, inhaled gas            anesthetic concentration.
AU WAN C
   ZHOU X
   CHEN P
AE SHENZHEN MINDRAY BIO MEDICAL ELECTRONICS (MRAY-C)
   SHENZHEN MINDRAY SCI CO LTD (MRAY-C)
   SHENZHEN MINDRAY BIO MEDICAL ELECTRONICS (MRAY-C)
   SHENZHEN MINDRAY SCI CO LTD (MRAY-C)
   SHENZHEN MINDRAY BIO MEDICAL ELECTRONICS (MRAY-C)
   SHENZHEN MINDRAY SCI CO LTD (MRAY-C)
   SHENZHEN MINDRAY BIO MEDICAL ELECTRONICS (MRAY-C)
   SHENZHEN MINDRAY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MRAY-C)
GA 201937590X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves monitoring flows of fresh                gas flowing into a breathing circuit and inhaled                gas of an anesthetized object (S11). The                concentration of anesthetic in the inhaled gas and                exhaled gas of the anesthetized object is monitored                (S12) when an inhaled gas flow is greater than a                fresh gas flow. The concentration of anesthetic in                gas reflowing into a breathing branch in the                exhaled gas by using an exhaled gas anesthetic                concentration and a concentration of carbon dioxide                in the exhaled gas are calculated for obtaining a                re-inhaled gas anesthetic concentration (S13). The                concentration of anesthetic output is calculated                (S14) by an anesthetic output device by using the                fresh gas flow, the inhaled gas flow, an inhaled                gas anesthetic concentration, and the re-inhaled                gas anesthetic concentration for obtaining an                anesthetic output concentration.
   USE - Method for monitoring anesthetic output                concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables calculating concentration                of the anesthetic output by the anesthetic output                device by using the fresh gas flow, the inhaled gas                flow, the inhaled gas anesthetic concentration and                the re-inhaled gas anesthetic concentration for                obtaining the anesthetic output concentration, thus                monitoring the anesthetic output concentration in                an efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   an anesthetic output concentration monitoring                system
   an anesthetic output concentration monitoring                device
   a computer readable storage medium comprising                a set of instructions for monitoring anesthetic                output concentration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for monitoring anesthetic output                concentration. '(Drawing includes non-English                language text)'
   S11Step for monitoring flows of fresh gas                flowing into breathing circuit and inhaled gas of                anesthetized object
   S12Step for monitoring concentration of                anesthetic in inhaled gas and exhaled gas of                anesthetized object
   S13Step for calculating concentration of                anesthetic in gas reflowing into breathing branch                in exhaled gas by using exhaled gas anesthetic                concentration and concentration of carbon dioxide                in exhaled gas calculated for obtaining re-inhaled                gas anesthetic concentration
   S14Step for calculating concentration of                anesthetic output

AB    NOVELTY - Ethylene-vinyl alcohol (EVOH) copolymer having excellent barrier against oxygen, nitrogen, carbon dioxide and helium and extending the shelf life of food products and protects their nutrients against bacteria is claimed.
   USE - The copolymer is useful for extending the shelf life of food products and protects their nutrients against bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The EVOH: increases the visual appeal of products for consumers and taste, scent, color and freshness of all packaged products remain intact; reduces the need for preservatives or even avoids them entirely and packaged products stay fresh all the way through the supply chain and even when stored for extended periods of time; paper bag is a new addition in keeping the freshness and aroma of spices; is recyclable and environmentally safe; provides proper fusion of the paper to the EVOH film, developed through several trials over a period of 1 year; paper bag forms fused sheet for holding bulk products as well as to meet the food safety needs of the food industry having sterilization of the products comprising ethylene oxide treatment (ETO), steam sterilization and gamma ray irradiation; fused bags have high barrier property that is not suitable where the user wants to use ETO method of treatment, thus these bags would be rendered unsuitable when the ETO method of sterilization is to be used; fused bags can be used very effectively only when the user wants to use the steam sterilization, irradiation or just no treatment; film can be replaced by a 5-layer film to accommodate users who want to use ETO method of sterilization, and the five layer film does not have EVOH and has better permeability and allows the ETO gas to penetrate and has been proven suitable for use in ETO through our trials and the EVOH automatically includes the five layer film as an alternative; and is flexible, crystal clear, and glossy thermoplastic copolymer with excellent flex-crack resistance, and very high resistance to hydrocarbons, oils and organic solvents.

The phosphorus-rich molecule is phosphoric acid, phosphate, polyphosphoric acid, polyphosphate, polyphosphoric acid derivative and/or phytic acid. The clay particles are sepiolite, halloysite, kaolin, talc, montmorillonite, saponite, nontronite, beidellite, diatomaceous earth, layered double hydroxide and/or graphene oxide. The crosslinking agent is divinyl sulfone, epoxy chloropropane, citric acid, boric acid, borate, formaldehyde, glyoxal, glutaraldehyde, 1-(3-dimethylamino propyl)-1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide and/or 1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylamino propyl)carbodiimide. TI Buccal tablet useful for relieving discomfort of mouth and throat, comprises radix stemonae, mint, microcrystalline cellulose, sweetening agent, sodium carboxymethyl starch, starch, cooling additive and magnesium stearate.
AU LUO R
   LUO Y
   CAO Y
   LAN Z
   LUO J
AE GUANGDONG EFONG PHARM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201927298P
AB    NOVELTY - Buccal tablet for relieving discomfort of mouth and throat, comprises 10-20 pts. wt. radix stemonae, 10-40 pts. wt. mint, 10-40 pts. wt. microcrystalline cellulose, 30-95 pts. wt. sweetening agent, 2-8 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl starch, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. starch, 0.2-0.6 pts. wt. cooling additive and 0.1-1 pts. wt. magnesium stearate.
   USE - The tablet is useful for relieving discomfort of mouth and throat.
   ADVANTAGE - The tablet has a cool and chilly taste, unique flavor, and has better efficacy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the tablet comprising (i) washing mint, drying at 50 degrees C below low temperature, pulverizing into 40-60 mesh fine powder, extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain peppermint oil, (ii) taking the peppermint extracted by supercritical carbon dioxide, adding radix stemonae, adding water twice, 7-8 times the amount of water for the first time, decocting for 20-30 minutes, adding 6-7 times for the second time, decocting for 20-30 minutes, filtering, combining the filtrate, concentrating under vacuum at 60-80 degrees C to a relative density of 1.06-1.10, and spray drying to obtain a mixed extract, (iii) taking menthol, smashing and passing through 80-100 mesh sieve, (iv) taking sucralose, sucrose powder, microcrystalline cellulose, mannitol, sodium carboxymethyl starch and (ii) mixed extract, and uniformly mixing to obtain a mixed powder, which is reserved, (v) taking starch, adding water to make starch slurry, evenly pouring into the mixed powder in (iv), mixing well to make soft material, and use sway granulator to prepare granules, (vi) adding magnesium stearate, mint flavor, jasmine flavor, and menthol powder, uniformly mixing, and evenly spraying the mint oil to obtain a mixture of granules, and (vii) taking the mixture of particles and preparing the lozenge by a tableting machine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The tablet comprises 12.5 pts. wt. radix stemonae, 10 pts. wt. mint, 20.5 pts. wt. microcrystalline cellulose, 63 pts. wt. sweetening agent, 4 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl starch, 0.8 pts. wt. starch, 0.25 pts. wt. cooling additive, 0.5 pts. wt. magnesium stearate and 0.1 pts. wt. methanol. Preferred Components: The sweetener is a mixture of sucrose powder, sucralose and mannitol in the form of 25-45:9.8-48:0.19-0.22, preferably sucrose powder, sucralose and mannitol are formulated as 35:27.6:0.2. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction conditions are the pressure of the extraction kettle is 20-25 MPa, and the separation I pressure is 5-10 MPa, the separation II pressure 4-8 MPa, extraction temperature is 35-45 degrees C, separation I temperature is 45-55 degrees C, separation II temperature is 30-35 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-30 L/h and the extraction time is 1.5-3 hours.

GA 2019231567
AB    NOVELTY - Nano-liposomes comprises lecithin, surfactant, vitamin E and cell penetrating peptides in weight ratio of 10-60:10-30:0.1-10:0.2-20, where the cell penetrating peptide comprises biological source cell penetrating peptide, chimeric cell penetrating peptide or synthetic cell penetrating peptide.
   USE - The nano-liposomes is useful as an agricultural additive in the field of agrochemical and chemical fertilizer or a component of agricultural additives (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-liposomes: is environmentally-friendly and insensitive to changes in pH and ionic strength; has high stability, excellent compatibility with pesticides and/or fertilizers; and can be stored for a long time at room temperature, rapidly reaches the minimum medicine concentration, prolongs the effect time, improves the control effect and delays the development of medicine resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing nano-liposomes, comprising (i) taking lecithin, surfactant, vitamin E and cell penetrating peptide, (ii) adding surfactant and cell penetrating peptide into the reaction vessel, adding hot water with a total weight of surfactant and cell penetrating peptide 50-200 times and temperature of 40-80 degrees C and stirring and dissolving to obtain solution I, and (iii) introducing pure carbon dioxide replaced from the bottom of the reaction kettle and removing the air in the reaction kettle, adding lecithin and vitamin E into solution I, continuously introducing pure carbon dioxide at the bottom of the reaction kettle to make the final pressure in the reaction kettle 0.3 MPa, rapidly heating to 125 degrees C and homogenizing for 20 minutes until the reaction solution is clear, stirring and cooling to room temperature and atmospheric pressure; and
   (2) use of nano-liposome as an agricultural additive in the field of agrochemical and chemical fertilizer or a component of agricultural additives and use method comprising mixing the nano-liposome and water at a volume ratio of 1:100-8000, adding pesticides and/or fertilizers, stirring and applying.

The lecithin comprises soybean lecithin, egg yolk lecithin or synthetic phospholipids. The synthetic lecithin comprises distearoylphosphatidylethanolamine-polyethylene glycol, dipalmitoyl phosphatidyl ethanolamine-polyethylene glycol, dioleoyl phosphatidyl ethanolamine-polyethylene glycol or dilauroyl phosphatidyl ethanolamine-polyethylene glycol and the linear and/or branched polyethylene glycol contained in the synthetic lecithin has a molecular weight of 200-100000. PN CN109393158-A
TI Producing zinc oxide for feed, involves preparing raw materials required in production process, includes zinc salt, water, soda ash, absolute ethanol, and radix isatidis, preparation of equipment needed in production process.
AU LU Y
AE XINGHUA JINKONGQUE IND DEV CO LTD (XING-Non-standard)
GA 201922851X
AB    NOVELTY - Producing zinc oxide for feed, involves preparing raw materials required in production process, includes zinc salt, water, soda ash, absolute ethanol, and radix isatidis, preparation of equipment needed in production process, includes stirring reactor, zinc oxide calciner, drying furnace, steaming furnace. The basic zinc carbonate is prepared and weighed zinc salt, water, soda ash and put it into reaction kettle for stirred. The additional 0.4-0.6 wt.% soda ash is added, and stirring speed is 200-360 revolution/minute. The stirring time is 40-60 minutes. The stirring temperature is 42-46 degrees C. The zinc salt, water and soda ash are reacted to form basic zinc carbonate. The zinc carbonate with zinc hydroxide dihydrate are reacted to form zinc oxide, carbon dioxide and trihydrate.
   USE - Method for producing zinc oxide for feed (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce zinc oxide has good sterilizing effect and improves immunity and disease resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The zinc oxide is prepared to obtain basic zinc carbonate by water and absolute ethyl alcohol to wash two times to dehydrate, dehydrated, dried in drying oven, after dry basic zinc carbonate into zinc oxide, calcined furnace for calcine, calcining temperature control at 450-550 degrees C, calcine time is 90-120 minutes, after calcined to obtain porous nano-pure zinc powder.The reaction equation of calcining process by reating zinc carbonate, zinc hydroixde to form zinc oxide, carbon dioxide, trihydrate. The zinc oxide prepared into stove upper end of steamer, radix isatidis powder is mixed with water and then put into steamer of steam end, open steamer, fumigated time is 20-30 minutes, fumigated temperature is 85-100 degrees C, fumigated after stopped steaming furnace, cooled zinc oxide after softened, dried. The zinc oxide is placed into heated drying process in drying furnace. The drying temperature is 120-150 degrees C, drying duration is 40-60 minutes, dry to obtain light yellow zinc oxide powder product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc salt is zinc sulfate, zinc nitrate and zinc chloride. Preferred Conditions: The calcining temperature is 550 degrees C, and calcining time is 90 minutes.

TI New polymeric biologically active compounds comprising aliphatic, preferably ethyl, isopropyl or butyl substituent, and having specified degree of functionalization and polymer chains in specified mole ratios, used in anti-cancer therapy.
AU JASZCZ K
   MROWKA M
AE POLITECHNIKA SLASKA PSTROWSKI (PLSK-C)
   POLITECHNIKA SLASKA (PLSK-C)
GA 2019138906
AB    NOVELTY - Polymeric biologically active compounds with antitumor activity, comprising R substituent i.e. aliphatic, preferably ethyl, isopropyl or butyl substituent, and having specified degree of functionalization and polymer chains in specified mole ratios, are new. Method of preparing the biologically active compounds with antitumor activity, comprises e.g. obtaining polyester or polycarbonate by polyaddition reaction of succinic anhydride or carbon dioxide and glycidyl ethers, preferably allyl glycidyl ether (AGE) at 20-100 mole% glycidyl esters, preparing a solution in toluene at 1-10 (preferably 4) g/100 ml, adding solution of N-acetyl-L-cysteine in alcohol to the obtained solution, adding 5-20 (preferably 10) mole% radical thermal initiator having allyl groups, preferably azobisisobutyronitrile (AIBN) to resulting mixture, performing the reaction at 50 degrees C to boiling temperature of solvent mixture, preferably at 70 degrees C for 1-3 (preferably 2) hours, cooling, and evaporating the solvents.
   USE - The compounds are useful in anti-cancer therapy. No biological data given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polymeric biologically active compounds with antitumor activity, comprising R substituent i.e. aliphatic, preferably ethyl, isopropyl or butyl substituent, and having specified degree of functionalization and polymer chains in specified mole ratios, are new. Method of preparing the biologically active compounds with antitumor activity, comprises obtaining polyester or polycarbonate by polyaddition reaction of succinic anhydride or carbon dioxide and glycidyl ethers, preferably allyl glycidyl ether (AGE) at 20-100 mole% glycidyl esters, preparing a solution in toluene at 1-10 (preferably 4) g/100 ml, adding a solution of N-acetyl-L-cysteine in alcohol to the obtained solution, adding 5-20 (preferably 10) mole% radical thermal initiator having allyl groups, preferably azobisisobutyronitrile (AIBN) to the resulting reaction mixture, performing the reaction at 50 degrees C to the boiling temperature of the solvent mixture, preferably at 70 degrees C for 1-3 (preferably 2) hours, cooling, evaporating the solvents and washing with distilled water to remove the unreacted acetyl cysteine and drying again.
TF ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN CN109174096-A; CN109174096-B
TI Preparation of composite photocatalyst for conversion of carbon dioxide to methane under UV light, involves dissolving adenosine triphosphate in hydrochloric acid solution, stirring mixture in water bath, centrifuging, washing and drying.
AU GUAN J
   ZHOU J
   LI J
   WU D
   ZHOU Y
   WANG H
   HUO P
   YAN Y
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 201909497B
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of composite photocatalyst involves dissolving adenosine triphosphate in hydrochloric acid solution, stirring the mixture in a water bath, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain functionalized attapulgite, dissolving cerium nitrate hexahydrate and sodium hydroxide in deionized water, then adding attapulgite, placing the mixed solution in a vacuum reactor, heat treating in an oven and then naturally cooling to room temperature, washing with deionized water and ethanol and drying to obtain cerium oxide/attapulgite composite photocatalyst, dissolving tetrachloroauric acid in purified water to obtain chloroauric acid solution, dissolving cerium oxide/attapulgite composite photocatalyst in purified water, stirring, adding chloroauric acid solution to the cerium oxide/attapulgite solution, reducing gold nanoparticles with sodium citrate solution, pouring mixed solution into a high-pressure reaction vessel and heat treating in an oven, and naturally cooling.
   USE - Preparation of composite photocatalyst for conversion of carbon dioxide to methane under UV light (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of composite photocatalyst in an environmentally-friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of composite photocatalyst involves dissolving adenosine triphosphate in hydrochloric acid solution, stirring the mixture in a water bath, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain functionalized attapulgite, dissolving cerium nitrate hexahydrate and sodium hydroxide in deionized water, then adding attapulgite, placing the mixed solution in a vacuum reactor, heat treating in an oven and then naturally cooling to room temperature, washing with deionized water and ethanol and drying to obtain cerium oxide/attapulgite composite photocatalyst, dissolving tetrachloroauric acid in purified water to obtain chloroauric acid solution, dissolving cerium oxide/attapulgite composite photocatalyst in purified water, stirring, adding chloroauric acid solution to the cerium oxide/attapulgite solution, reducing gold nanoparticles with sodium citrate solution, pouring mixed solution into a high-pressure reaction vessel and heat treating in an oven, naturally cooling to room temperature, drying and centrifuging to obtain solid, and grinding obtained solid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The stirring step is carried out at temperature of 80 degrees C for 10-24 hours.

PN CN109161418-A; CN109161418-B
TI Processing coal-to-natural gas comprises e.g.            adding coal powder into coal gasification furnace,            carrying out catalytic gasification reaction, fine            desulfurizing reaction gas, performing reductive            methanation and separating.
AU ZHANG Y
   SHI L
   LI C
   CHANG J
   FENG L
   ZHANG J
AE XINDI ENERGY ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIND-Non-standard)
   XINDI ENERGY ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIND-Non-standard)
GA 201906439C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing coal-to-natural gas comprises e.g.                (i) adding coal powder into the coal gasification                furnace, carrying out catalytic gasification                reaction, and separating carbon residue, tar and                synthetic raw material gas rich in methane, (ii)                subjecting the synthetic feed gas into dust removal                and purification, entering at least one series of                sulfur-resistant methanation reactor, separating                into low-temperature methanol washing section, and                removing the inorganic sulfur and a large amount of                carbon dioxide in reaction gas, (iii) entering the                purified reaction gas into the fine desulfurization                reactor containing the copper-based desulfurization                catalyst to remove trace sulfur, and (iv) fine                desulfurizing the reaction gas, entering at least                one supplementary methanation reactor loaded with                the nickel-based methanation catalyst for reductive                methanation, and separating the reactor outlet gas                by condensation.
   USE - The method is useful for processing                coal-to-natural gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing coal-to-natural gas comprises (i)                adding coal powder into the coal gasification                furnace, carrying out catalytic gasification                reaction in the coal gasification furnace, and                separating carbon residue, tar and synthetic raw                material gas rich in methane, (ii) subjecting the                synthetic feed gas into dust removal and                purification, entering at least one series of                sulfur-resistant methanation reactor loaded with a                molybdenum-based catalyst for sulfur-resistant                methanation reaction, the outlet reaction gas                recovers heat by heat exchange, separating into a                low-temperature methanol washing section by                condensation, and removing the inorganic sulfur and                a large amount of carbon dioxide in the reaction                gas, (iii) entering the purified reaction gas into                the fine desulfurization reactor containing the                copper-based desulfurization catalyst to remove                trace sulfur, so that the sulfur content in the                reaction gas is less than 0.03 ppm, and (iv) fine                desulfurizing the reaction gas, entering at least                one supplementary methanation reactor loaded with                the nickel-based methanation catalyst for reductive                methanation, and separating the reactor outlet gas                by condensation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  catalyst for catalytic gasification of coal is                  alkali metal, an alkaline earth metal, and a                  transition metal, and the catalyst is supported                  on the carbonaceous raw material by a dipping                  method, a dry mixing method or an ion exchange                  method. In the step (ii), the carrier is oxide                  complex carriers of aluminum oxide, zirconium                  dioxide, cerium oxide, titanium oxide, silicon                  oxide and magnesium oxide, and the proportion of                  the carrier is 60-85%, and the auxiliary agent is                  potassium, boron, tungsten, nickel, cobalt, and                  lanthanum. The water vapor ratio of the inlet gas                  of the sulfur-tolerant methanation reactor is                  preferably 0.2-1. and/or sulfur-tolerant                  methanation outlet gas composition is 10-20%                  hydrogen, 10-20% cobalt, 35-50% carbon dioxide,                  20-30% methoane, 0-1% hydrogen sulfide, and less                  than 1% other impurity gases. The supplemental                  methanation reactor has a water to gas ratio of                  0.05-0.15:1, preferably 0.1-0.13:1. In the step                  (iv), the product composition of the natural gas                  product obtained is expressed by volume: 0.52.8%                  hydrogen, 0.1-1.5% carbon dioxide, 94-98%                  methoane, 0.3-1% nitrogen and 0.01-0.5% carbon                  2-5. Preferred Method: The coal powder enters the                  coal gasifier after the pretreatment process, and                  the pretreatment process includes drying and                  pulverization. The catalytic gasification                  conditions of the coal are a reaction pressure of                  1-5 MPa and a reaction temperature is 600-1000                  degrees C and/or coal gasifier outlet gas is                  separated to obtain methane synthesis methane raw                  material, i.e. 25-35% hydrogen, 12-20% cobalt,                  30-45% carbon dioxide, 10-24% methoane, 0-1%                  hydrogen sulfide, and less than 1% other impurity                  gases. In the step (ii), the methane synthesis                  raw material gas after dedusting and degreasing                  is exchanged to 200-400 degrees C and enters the                  sulfur-resistant methanation reactor, the                  sulfur-tolerant methanation reactor is at least                  one series of sulfur-tolerant methanation                  reactors, and the reactor type is one of a fixed                  bed and a slurry bed and/or the sulfur-tolerant                  methanation catalyst is a molybdenum-based                  multifunctional catalyst, and the active                  component of the catalyst is molybdenum                  disulfide, which accounts for 5-30% of the total                  mass of the catalyst. The sulfur-resistant                  methanation reaction conditions are: reaction                  pressure is 1 -5 MPa, reaction temperature is 260                  -650 degrees C, space velocity is                  1000-10000/hour. The sulfur-tolerant methanation                  outlet gas is desulfurized by low temperature                  methanol elution. The operating conditions are:                  temperature -20 to -55 degrees C, pressure 1-5                  MPa, sulfur content below 0.1 ppm, carbon dioxide                  content reduced to 0.1-5 V%. The reaction                  temperature of the fine desulfurization reactor                  is 150-240 degrees C, the reaction pressure is                  1-5 MPa, the space velocity is 4000-10000/hour,                  the outlet sulfur content is less than 0.05 ppm,                  the reaction temperature is preferably 170-210                  degrees C, and the space velocity is                  5000-10000/hour. The replenishing methanation                  reactor is an adiabatic fixed bed reactor having                  at least two stages connected in series and/or                  supplement methanation reaction conditions are                  temperature 260-600 degrees C, pressure 1-5 MPa,                  space velocity 5000-12000/hour.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. PN CN108795008-A; CN108795008-B
TI Producing a heat-resistant flame-retardant acrylonitrile butadiene styrene /polycarbonates alloy comprises e.g. mixing phosphogypsum, additive and modifier, adding calcium oxide heating, stirring and filtering, extruding and pelleting.
AU CHEN X
   WANG L
   ZHENG K
   LUO X
   ZHOU D
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201892713D
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a heat-resistant flame-retardant acrylonitrile butadiene styrene / polycarbonates alloy comprises (i) mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier and milling to obtain raw material, roasting to obtain clinker, and performing the solid-liquid separation, (ii) subjecting the precipitate to flotation, separating the sulfide, roasting, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid, (iii) adding calcium oxide to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation in step (i), heating, stirring and filtering to obtain a sodium metaaluminate solution, (iv) passing carbon dioxide into the sodium metaaluminate solution until no precipitation occurs, washing precipitate with distilled water, drying and grinding to obtain aluminum hydroxide, and (v) placing into a high speed mixer, adding triphenyl phosphate, ammonium polyphosphate, polycarbonates, acrylonitrile butadiene styrene, compatibilizer and antioxidant uniformly, extruding and pelleting.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a heat-resistant flame-retardant acrylonitrile butadiene styrene / polycarbonates alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The alloy is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a heat-resistant flame-retardant acrylonitrile butadiene styrene / polycarbonates alloy comprises (i) mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier and milling to obtain raw material, transferring to kiln for roasting to obtain clinker, subjecting the clinker to water-milling and dissolution and carrying out the solid-liquid separation, (ii) subjecting the precipitate obtained by solid-liquid separation in the step (i) to flotation, separating the sulfide, roasting, catalyzing the flue gas by vanadium pentoxide, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid, (iii) adding calcium oxide to the liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation in step (i), heating, stirring and filtering to obtain a sodium metaaluminate solution, (iv) passing Carbon dioxide into the sodium metaaluminate solution until no precipitation occurs, washing the precipitate with distilled water, drying and grinding the washed precipitate to obtain aluminum hydroxide, and (v) placing into a high speed mixer, adding triphenyl phosphate, ammonium polyphosphate, polycarbonates, acrylonitrile butadiene styrene, compatibilizer and antioxidant uniformly, extruding by a double- screw extruder and pelleting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The alloy comprises 20-30 pts. wt. aluminim hydroxide, 3-5 pts. wt. triphenyl phosphate, 4-6 pts. wt. ammonium polyphosphate, 40-50 pts. wt. polycarbonates, 30-40 pts. wt. acrylonitrile butadiene styrene, 5-8 pts. wt. compatibilizer and 0.5-2 pts. wt. antioxidant. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate and/or sodium hydroxide, the modified agent is anthracite, carbo and/or coal gangue, the kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. In the step (i), the mass ratio of phosphogypsum and fly ash is 1-1.3:1-1.6. The additive addition ratio is 1:1 according to the molecular ratio of the sum of sodium oxide, aluminim oxide and Iron(III) oxide contained in the raw material. The mixing ratio of the modifier is 8-22% of the total weight of the raw meal. The calcinations is carried out at 1280-1380 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The liquid-solid ratio at the time of elution of the water mill is 2-6:1. In the step (iii), the calcium oxide is analytically pure and the amount is 7-10 g/l. In the step (iv), the concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide gas is greater than 38%. In the step (v), the compatibilizer is a maleic anhydride graft copolymer. The antioxidant is an antioxidant 1010. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the calcinations is carried out at 1150-1250 degrees C for 3-5 hours. The condition is to place the sulfide in an oxygen-rich environment of 40 -50%. The heating is carried out at 75-95 degrees C, stirring is carried out at 1-2 hours. The drying is carried out at 95-105 degrees C. In the step (v), the extruding temperature of the double screw extruder is 210-230 degrees C, the rotation speed of the screw is 30-50 revolutions/minute. The extrusion temperature of twin-screw extruder is 220 degrees C and screw speed of 40 revolutions/minute.
DC A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN108578646-A
TI Antibacterial herbal care solution used for, e.g. detoxification includes Camellia seed cake, henbane, blueberry leaves, dandelion, small algae, duckweed, rhizoma zedoariae, fructus trichosanthis, cationic surfactant, and butanediol.
AU DUAN Y
AE HEFEI XIAKANG E-COMMERCE CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 2018799668
AB    NOVELTY - An antibacterial herbal care solution comprises Camellia seed cake, henbane, blueberry leaves, dandelion, small algae, duckweed, rhizoma zedoariae, motherwort, fructus trichosanthis, Biancaea sappan, cationic surfactant, and butanediol.
   USE - Antibacterial herbal care solution used for clearing away heat, detoxification, relieving itching, removing putrefaction, promoting tissue healing.
   ADVANTAGE - The herbal care solution has good antibacterial, anti-inflammation and anti-virus effect, colon Bacillus, Staphylococcus aureus and Candida albicans inhibiting rates of 95.5%, 95.7% and 95.1% respectively, and no stimulation to human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the solution comprising (A) crushing henbane, dandelion, duckweed and motherwort, sieving through 50 mesh sieve, adding 15 times its volume distilled water, soaking for 30 minutes under ultrasonic assisted condition, boiling in micro-pressure machine for 2 hours, filtering, adding residue with 10 times its volume distilled water, boiling in micro-pressure machine for 1.5 hours, filtering, adding residue with 5 times its volume distilled water, boiling in micro-pressure machine for 1 hour, filtering, combining filtrates, adjusting pH to 7.3-7.9 using sodium bicarbonate and acetic acid, and concentrating through evaporation until relative density is 1.20-1.50 g/cm3 to obtain Chinese herbal medicine water extract; (B) crushing Camellia seed cake, blueberry leaves, small algae, rhizoma zedoariae, fructus trichosanthis and Biancaea sappan, sieving through 40 mesh sieve, adding to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, extracting with carbon dioxide at flow rate of 15-25 L/hour, pressure of 15-25 MPa and temperature of 40-50 degrees Ci for 1 hour, and centrifuging to obtain supercritical extract; and (C) adding Chinese herbal medicine water extract with distilled water, adding supercritical extract and butanediol, filtering to remove residual residue, adding cationic surfactant, homogenizing, adjusting pH to 6.8-7.8 using sodium bicarbonate and acetic acid, and concentrating until relative density is 1.20-1.60 g/cm3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The antibacterial herbal care solution comprises 30-40 pts. wt. Camellia seed cake, 28-38 pts. wt. henbane, 25-35 pts. wt. blueberry leaves, 25-35 pts. wt. dandelion, 20-30 pts. wt. small algae, 19-29 pts. wt. duckweed, 19-29 pts. wt. rhizoma zedoariae, 17-27 pts. wt. motherwort, 15-25 pts. wt. fructus trichosanthis, 10-20 pts. wt. Biancaea sappan, 1-3 pts. wt. cationic surfactant, and 35-45 pts. wt. butanediol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cationic surfactant is dodecyldimethylbenzylammonium chloride.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial; Antiinflammatory; Virucide; Antidote; Antipruritic.

PN CN108557779-A
TI Production of acid and co-production of mullite refractory brick, involves reacting phosphogypsum, coal ash, additive, modifier, kaolinite, illite, montmorillonite, emery powder, quartz stone, talc powder and aluminum hydroxide.
AU CHEN X
   WANG L
   ZHENG K
   LUO X
   ZHOU D
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201878009Q
AB    NOVELTY - The production of acid and co-production of mullite refractory brick involves (A) mixing phosphogypsum, coal ash, additive and modifier and grinding to obtain mixture, roasting mixture to prepare clinker, carrying out water milling and solid-liquid separation, (B) carrying out solid-liquid separation of precipitate, separating out sulfide and roasting separated sulfide, carrying out catalytic reaction on flue gas generated by roasting through vanadium pentoxide catalytic reaction and absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid, (C) adding carbon dioxide to liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until precipitates are not generated, drying and crushing precipitate to prepare aluminum hydroxide, (D) crushing kaolinite, illite, montmorillonite, emery powder, quartz stone and talc powder, mixing aluminum hydroxide, saw dust, expandable polystyrene spheres, polyvinyl alcohol and water, molding, drying and sintering.
   USE - Production of acid and co-production of mullite refractory brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of acid and co-production of mullite refractory brick ensuring enhanced flame retardance by simple and economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The production of acid and co-producing mullite refractory brick involves (A) mixing phosphogypsum, coal ash, additive and modifier and grinding to obtain mixture, feeding mixture to be roasted into kiln to prepare clinker, carrying out water milling dissolution on clinker and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (B) carrying out floatation on precipitate obtained by solid-liquid separation, separating out sulfide and roasting separated sulfide, carrying out catalytic reaction on flue gas generated by roasting through vanadium pentoxide catalytic reaction and absorbing concentrated sulfuric acid, (C) adding carbon dioxide to liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation until precipitates are not generated, drying and crushing the precipitate to prepare aluminum hydroxide, (D) crushing kaolinite, illite, montmorillonite, emery powder, quartz stone and talc powder, adding mixture to mixer to obtain mixture, uniformly mixing mixture with aluminum hydroxide, adding saw dust, expandable polystyrene spheres and polyvinyl alcohol, uniformly mixing to obtain raw material powder, adding water, mixing to obtain slurry, injecting slurry into mold, drying and sintering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The refractory brick comprises 20-25 pts. wt. kaolinite, 6-8 pts. wt. illite, 7-9 pts. wt. montmorillonite, 12-15 pts. wt. corundum powder, 14-18 pts. wt. quartz stone, 3-5 pts. wt. talc powder, 40-50 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide, 5-10 pts. wt. sawdust, 6-12 pts. wt. expandable polystyrene balls and 3-5 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (A) specifically involves mixing phosphogypsum and fly ash at mass ratio of 1:(1-1.7), adding additive at mass ratio of 1:1 according to molecular ratio of sum of sodium oxide, aluminum oxide and ferric oxide contained in the raw material, and the mixing ratio of modifier is 12-23 %mass of total weight of raw material. Preferred Composition: In step (D), the mass ratio of raw material powder and water is 1:(1-1.2). In step (A), the water-solid ratio is (5-8):1. Preferred Components: The additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or caustic soda. The modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. Preferred Conditions: In step (A), the calcination temperature is controlled at 1130-1380 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The step (B) involves roasting at 1000-1200 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The step (C) involves drying at 100-120 degrees C and controlling water content less than or equal to 0.5%. The step (D) involves sintering at 1300-1400 degrees C for 5-10 hours, heat-preserving for 2-5 hours, reducing temperature to 700-800 degrees C for 10-15 hours and naturally cooling.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The step (D) involves crushing to 100-120 mesh. The grain diameter of expandable polystyrene sphere is 1-2 mm.

PN CN108163898-A; CN108163898-B
TI Self-regulating ferrite material useful e.g. for producing carbon monoxide, preparing hydrogen and in three-way catalytic reaction or partial methane oxidation reaction, is composite metal oxide based on variable valence iron ion.
AU ZHANG J
   HUANG J
   FU Y
   SUN Y
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 201849216K
AB    NOVELTY - Self-regulating ferrite material is a composite metal oxide based on a variable valence iron ion, where the chemical composition formula is MxFe3-xO4, x is 1.5-2.7, M is nickel, magnesium and/or cobalt, and the molar ratio of M/(M+Fe) is 0.5-0.9.
   USE - The material is useful for producing carbon monoxide by thermo-chemical direct decomposition of carbon dioxide, and preparing hydrogen by direct decomposition water; and in three-way catalytic reaction, chemical chain reaction or partial methane oxidation reaction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high decomposition activity and stability and long service life; adopts point defect transmission and ion diffusion; and reduces reaction temperature of direct decomposition water. The material has long service life; and adjusts redox potential and bulk phase.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for:
   (1) preparing the material, comprising either preparing mixed salt solution of iron soluble salt and M soluble salt according to a molar ratio of each element in the self-regulating ferrite material, adding precipitant for coprecipitation, aging, washing and filtering the precipitate, and drying and roasting or taking the metal oxides of iron and M according to the molar ratio of each element in the self-regulating ferrite material, thoroughly mixing metal oxide by a ball milling method or a mortar mixing method, and calcining or preparing mixed salt solution of iron soluble salt and M soluble salt according to a molar ratio of each element in the self-regulating ferrite material, adding complexing agent, uniformly dissolving, adding dispersant, stirring to obtain gel and drying, and roasting; and
   (2) preparing carbon monoxide or hydrogen, comprising heating the ferrite material and activating in an inert atmosphere, cooling under the protection of inert atmosphere, and infusing carbon dioxide or water for decomposition reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dynamic cycle of the material is based on a ferric ion/ferrous ion redox pair that vary with oxidation potential. Preferred Method: In the first preparation the process comprises taking the iron soluble salt from nitrate of iron, sulfate of iron, acetate of iron or chlorate of iron, taking M soluble salts from nitrates of M, sulfates of M, acetates of M or chlorates of M, taking the mixed salt solution of concentration 0.1-2.5 mol/l, and precipitant of concentration 0.5-2 mol/l, taking precipitating agent sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, aqueous ammonia or sodium carbonate, aging for 2-12 hours to a pH of 8-10, washing and filtering until the pH is neutral, drying at 80-120 degrees C, and roasting at 600-1000 degrees C for 2-10 hours. In the second preparation the process further comprises roasting at 800-1200 degrees C for 1-5 hours. In the third preparation the process comprises taking the iron soluble salt from nitrate of iron or chlorate of iron, taking M soluble salts from nitrates of M, sulfates of M or chlorates of M, taking the mixed salt solution of concentration 0.1-1 mol/l, taking complexing agent citric acid, propylene oxide, oxalic acid or ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, taking dispersing agent ethylene glycol, urea or ethanol, where the molar ratio of the complexing agent to the total molar number of iron and M is 1:1-10:1, and the mass ratio of the dispersing agent to the complexing agent is 1:3-4:1, adding a complexing agent at 70-90 degrees C, drying at 100-150 degrees C, and roasting 500-900 degrees C for 2-8 hours. The activation temperature is 1300-1500 degrees C, and the time is 10-60 minutes. The decomposition reaction temperature is 1100-1400 degrees C.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN107963923-A
TI Preparing slow-release anti-caking granular fertilizer, comprises e.g. ball milling the mica rock, sieving, filtering, fermenting, sterilizing, concentrating, ultrasonically dispersing, atomizing and coating.
AU ZHANG L
   XU H
   CHEN L
AE CHANGZHOU JIEXUAN TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201834462A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing slow-release anti-caking granular fertilizer, comprises (i) ball milling the mica rock, sieving, mixing the polyallyl-amine and organic solvent and stirring, adding urea and mica particles under stirring state, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas and filtering, (ii) stirring and mixing chicken, water and sucrose, adding chicken feather, stirring, fermenting, sterilizing, filtering, concentrating, (iii) ultrasonically dispersing phospholipids, chitosan, mucin and water, and (iv) stirring feather hydrolyzed liquid and dispersion liquid, and (v) atomizing and coating.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing slow-release anti-caking granular fertilizer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing slow-release anti-caking granular fertilizer, comprises (i) ball milling the mica rock for 4-8 hours, sieving to obtain mica powder, taking 40-50 pts. wt. particle urea, 8-10 pts. wt. mica powder, 10-12 pts. wt. polyallyl-amine and 120-150 pts. wt. organic solvent, mixing the polyallyl-amine and organic solvent and stirring for 10-40 minutes, adding urea and mica particles under stirring state, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas and filtering to obtain pre-grain urea, (ii) stirring and mixing 40-60 pts. wt. chicken, 40-50 pts. wt. water and 3-5 pts. wt. sucrose at constant temperature, adding 4-6% chicken feather of Bacillus licheniformis, stirring at constant temperature, fermenting for 7-10 days, sterilizing, filtering, concentrating to obtain hydrolyzed feather solution, (iii) ultrasonically dispersing 30-40 pts. wt. phospholipids, 2-4 pts. wt. chitosan, 10-15 pts. wt. mucin and 150-200 pts. wt. water to obtain dispersion liquid, and (iv) stirring 20-30 pts. wt. feather hydrolyzed liquid and 60-80 pts. wt. dispersion liquid at constant temperature to obtain film liquid, and (v) taking 30-50 pts. wt. film-forming liquid and 100-150 pts. wt. pre-treated urea particles, in the fluidized bed, atomizing and coating the film forming liquid on the surface of the pretreated urea granules in the fluidized bed and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the diameter of urea particle is 1-3 mm. In the step (i), the organic solvent is 8C alcohol and 1-methyl-2-pyrrolidone in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:3. In the step (iii), the phospholipid is soybean lecithin or yolk lecithin. In the step (iii), the mucin is porcine serum mucin or mucin in chicken egg.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN107873930-A
TI Processing blueberry preserved fruit by soaking blueberry into ethanol solution, draining water, standing, UV sterilizing, placing in sealed container, standing in ethylene, evacuating internal gas, introducing ozone and removing kernel.
AU SU W
AE BENGBU HUIJIXING AGRIC TECHNOLOGY FARMER (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201828147G
AB    NOVELTY - Processing blueberry preserved fruit involves soaking blueberry into 75% ethanol solution, draining water, standing, UV sterilizing, placing in sealed container, standing in ethylene, evacuating internal gas, introducing ozone, removing kernel, freezing, putting into ultrasonic wave oscillating groove, heating, oscillating, drying, heating, introducing carbon dioxide gas, heating water, adding sugar to saturated solution, adding dried blueberry, high pressure dipping, boosting, processing, depressurizing, secondary high pressure dipping, and drying.
   USE - Method for processing blueberry preserved fruit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves easy to become soft and spoilage problem at room temperature, low temperature storage and transportation that prolongs preserving time, high cost, difficult to control transportation process and service time problems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing blueberry preserved fruit involves soaking blueberry into 75% ethanol solution for 5-7 minutes, taking out, draining water, standing at 4-7 degrees C for 40-50 minutes, UV sterilizing for 16-18 minutes to obtain sterilized blueberry, placing in sealed container, standing in ethylene at 7-10 degrees C for 2-3 hours, evacuating internal gas, introducing ozone for 25-30 minutes, removing kernel to obtain processed blueberry, freezing at - 13 to - 11 degrees C for 65-70 minutes, putting into ultrasonic wave oscillating groove, heating to - 3-1 degrees C, oscillating for 20-23 minutes, drying in drying box, heating to 46-50 degrees C, introducing carbon dioxide gas for circulating drying at flow rate of 2-3 L/minute to prepare dried blueberry, heating water to 45-50 degrees C, adding sugar to saturated solution, adding dried blueberry, high pressure dipping into high pressure steamer at 42-46 degrees C, boosting to 1.2-1.5 MPa, processing for 24-28 minutes, depressurizing for 40-45 minutes at 0.7-0.9 MPa, secondary high pressure dipping to obtain sugar dipped blueberry, and drying at low temperature to water content of less than 5%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: UV sterilization is done at intensity of 290-310 lux. Oscillation is done at frequency of 17-20 kHz. Low temperature drying is done at 40-45 degrees C for 30-40 minutes.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN114308084-A
TI Preparing titanium dioxide/lead-free halogen perovskite composite photocatalytic material useful in e.g. water pollution treatment comprises dispersing titanium dioxide in solvent ultrasonically, adding lead-free halogen perovskite to titanium dioxide solution stirring, and centrifuging.
AU WEN L
   FAN Q
   WEI S
   MA J
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 2022562695
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing titanium dioxide/lead-free halogen perovskite composite photocatalytic material, comprises dispersing titanium dioxide in a solvent ultrasonically to obtain a titanium dioxide solution, adding the lead-free halogen perovskite to the titanium dioxide solution in proportion, stirring, and finally centrifuging and drying the mixture.
   USE - The photocatalytic material is useful in water pollution treatment, carbon dioxide reduction, and hydrogen production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases its specific surface area; broadens the photoresponse range to the visible light region; improves visible light catalytic performance of titanium dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) ultrasonically dispersing titanium dioxide powder in absolute ethanol, isopropanol or chloroform solvent to obtain a titanium dioxide solution; (b) adding lead-free halogen perovskite into the titanium dioxide solution, stirring at 10-35degrees Celsius for 30-60 minutes, and finally, centrifuging the mixture for 10-30 minutes at centrifugal speed 2000-8000 revolutions/minutes, taking bottom precipitate and drying in an oven at 30-60degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours to obtain the titanium dioxide/lead-free halogen perovskite composite photocatalytic material. The specific steps for preparing lead-free halogen perovskite by the ligand-assisted recrystallization method are adding cesium bromide, silver bromide and bismuth tri bromide into a glass bottle containing dimethyl sulfoxide, and ultrasonically treating for 180 minutes, to obtain transparent light yellow-green precursor solution, adding precursor solution dropwise to the isopropanol solvent under high-speed stirring at a rate of 1 drop/seconds, stirring for 5-30 minutes to obtain lead-free perovskite solution by centrifugation. In the step (ii), the mass-volume ratio of titanium dioxide to the solvent is 1-10 mg:50 ml, the ultrasonic power is 40-80 kHz, and the ultrasonic time is 30-90 minutes.Preferred Components: The mass volume ratio of described cesium bromide, silver bromide and bismuth tri bromide and dimethyl sulfoxide is 6-10 mg: 1-4 mg: 6-10 mg: 0.6-1.2 ml. The titanium dioxide is commercially available titanium dioxide or self-made titanium dioxide by a sol-gel method or a hydrothermal method. The size range of the lead-free halogen-based perovskite is 5-1000 nm, and the preparation method is a ligand-assisted recrystallization method or a thermal injection method.

TI Catalytic preparing cyclic carbonate by using multi-site ionic liquid as high-efficient catalyst, comprises carrying out reaction on epoxy compound in presence of multi-site ionic liquid as catalyst.
AU CHENG W
   LUO Z
   DONG L
   SU Q
   CHU J
   QIAN W
   GOU H
   CHANG J
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG S
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 202128548B
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic preparing cyclic carbonate by using multi-site ionic liquid as high-efficient catalyst, comprises taking epoxy compound as raw material and carrying out reaction in presence of multi-site ionic liquid as catalyst to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for catalytic preparing cyclic carbonate by using multi-site ionic liquid as high-efficient catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes multi-site ionic liquid catalyst, which realizes efficient conversion of carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic carbonate under mild condition, and has excellent activity; and has simple process, mild reaction conditions, and easy separation from liquid phase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The multi-site ionic liquid is double-cation liquid, where the ionic liquid is of formula (I) or formula (II). The epoxy compound is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, styrene oxide, epoxy cyclohexane and/or cyclopentane oxide. The molar ratio between catalyst ionic liquid and epoxy compound is 0.1-20%.A1, A2=aliphatic, alicyclic or aryl (all optionally substituted with 1-6C alkyl, 2-6C alkenyl, 2-6C alkynyl, 6-30C aryl, 3-30C heteroaryl or 1-6C alkoxy (all optionally substituted)), preferably organic alkali including 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene or 1,5-diazabicyclo(4.3.0)non-5-ene;R1, R3-R5, R11, R33, R44 and R55=H, hydroxy, amino, nitro, aldehyde, ester, carboxy, nitroso, amido or carbonyl;R2, R22=divalent fragments including alkylene, alkenylene, alkynylene (-CH2-carbocyclyl-CH2-)n or polysiloxane groups;heteroatoms in alkylene, alkenylene and alkynylene=O, S, N or Si;T=1-6C alkyl, 2-6C alkenyl, 2-6C alkynyl, 6-30C aryl, 3-30C heteroaryl or 1-6C alkoxy (all optionally substituted);n=1-20;B1-B4=organic anions (including ROSO3-, RSO3-, ROPO3-, (RO)2PO2-, RPO32-, RCOO-, RO-, (RCO)2N-, (RSO2)2N-, NCN-) or inorganic anions (including tetrafluoroborate, hexafluorophosphate, nitrate, sulfate, hydrogen sulfate, phosphate, hydrogen phosphate, dihydrogen phosphate, hydroxide, carbonate, ammonium carbonate, halide ion); andR=1-6C alkyl, 2-6C alkenyl, 2-6C alkynyl, 6-30C aryl, 3-30C heteroaryl or 1-6C alkoxy (all optionally substituted).Preferred Method: The method comprises reacting epoxy compound of formula (III) with carbon dioxide in presence of catalyst to obtain 1,3-dioxolan-2-one of formula (IV). The catalytic reaction is carried out at 30-80 degrees C under the pressure of 0.1-3 MPa for 1-6 hours. The method comprises placing (III) and catalyst powder in a sealed reaction kettle and mixing uniformly, maintaining the temperature of the reaction kettle within the range of 30-80 degrees C, continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction kettle and maintaining the pressure of the reaction system in the kettle within the range of 0.1-3 MPa, and carrying out addition reaction for 1-6 hours and magnetically stirring to obtain cyclic carbonate.

PN CN112499584-A
TI Preparing hydrogen nitrogen synthesis gas from desorbed gas comprises e.g. increasing pressure of the desorbed gas produced in hydrogen purification stage of raw gas to preset pressure value, and performing fine desulfurization.
AU SONG R
   LI Y
   GAO Y
   SONG T
   GAO H
AE SHAANXI DONGXINYUAN CHEM CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021284134
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen nitrogen synthesis gas from desorbed gas comprises increasing pressure of the desorbed gas produced in the hydrogen purification stage of raw gas to preset pressure value, performing fine desulfurization and fine deoxygenation treatment on the desorbed gas, applying pressure swing adsorption to the desorbed gas for removing carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to obtain product gas, and using the product gas for synthesizing ammonia.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrogen nitrogen synthesis gas from desorbed gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the investment and operating cost of air separation plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The adsorbent is used for pressure swing adsorption of the desorbed gas includes activated carbon, molecular sieve and cuprous chloride supported adsorbent. The pressure swing adsorption of the desorbed gas includes the following steps: absorbing, uniformly pressing and reducing pressure, reversely placing, vacuumizing and boosting. The carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide adsorbed in the pressure swing adsorption process in the desorption gas are transported to the fuel pipe network after vacuum desorption, and then transporting to the pyrolysis furnace or used for power generation. The method further comprises refining and decarbonizing the product gas before conveying the product gas into the ammonia synthesis device, and the refining process includes shifting and methanol washing processes. The preset pressure value is greater than 0.6 MPa. The method further comprises supplementing hydrogen from the outside until the ratio of hydrogen to nitrogen in the mixed gas is 3:1.

   USE - The hypoxic-treated stem cell exosome is useful in preparing medicine and scaffold materials for treating spinal cord injury (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The duration of the hypoxic stimulation treatment is 5 minutes to 72 hours. The hypoxia stimulation treatment time is 6-24 hours. The conditions of the preliminary treatment are before step (i): stem cells are cultured under normal conditions for 6-72 hours after passage, and replace with new medium before the hypoxic stimulation treatment, where the normal condition is: oxygen volume percentage is 19-21%, carbon dioxide volume percentage is 4.5-5.5%, temperature is 36-37 degrees C, and the medium is complete medium of mesenchymal stem cells. The complete medium of mesenchymal stem cells is supplemented with serum that has been exosomed, and the volume percentage of the serum and the complete medium of mesenchymal stem cells is less than or equal to 20%. The stem cells are cultured under normal conditions for 6-48 hours after passage. The freezing and centrifugal conditions are: centrifugal force is greater than or equal to 100000 g, centrifugation time is 3-4 hours, and temperature is 0-4 degrees C.

   YAN B
   CHEN D
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202011299F
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling waste aluminum involves pre-treating                waste aluminum, mixing pre-processed waste aluminum                with lithium carbonate molten salt, then putting it                into a crucible, placing crucible in reaction                furnace, and passing inert gas to exhaust the air                in the reaction furnace, heating to the reaction                temperature.    USE - Method for recycling waste aluminum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recycle waste aluminum                that reduces production cost and realizes carbon                emission reduction and recycling.

Preferred Conditions: The average size of                  the crushed waste aluminum is 2mmx 2mm. The                  partial pressure of carbon dioxide must not be                  higher than 20%. AU LI Y

AB    NOVELTY - Use of mesenchymal stem cell conditioned medium is claimed for improving hypoxia/reoxygenation-induced cell damage. PT P
PN CN108219959-A
TI Extracting essential oil from a peony petal comprises e.g. mixing fresh peony petals and water, carrying out pulverization and ultrasonic treatment, carrying out centrifugation to obtain the peony flower juice.
AU CHEN Z
AE ANHUI XINYUANKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018533457
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting essential oil from a peony petal comprises (1) mixing fresh peony petals and water, carrying out pulverization and ultrasonic treatment, carrying out centrifugation to obtain the peony flower juice, (2) adding a super absorbent resin to the peony juice obtained in (1), stirring for 10-20 minutes, and allowing to stand for 20-30 minutes to obtain a gelatinous peony extract, (3) using gelatinous peony extract obtained in step (2) and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain peony essential oil.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting essential oil from a peony petal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The super absorbent resin comprises 12-15 pts. wt. corn starch, 65-70 pts. wt. acrylic acid, 5-8 pts. wt. corn stover powder, 0.5-0.7 pts. wt. potassium persulfate, 0.06-0.08 pts. wt. N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide, 26-29% mass fraction 85-95 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide solution and 15-17 pts. wt. water. Preferred Method: The super absorbent resin is prepared by (1) adding corn starch to 70-80 degrees C water, stirring at a constant temperature for 40-50 minutes, to obtain a gelatinized starch solution A, (2) adding acrylic acid to the sodium hydroxide solution, stirring for 20-30 minutes, then adding corn stover powder, potassium persulfate, N, N'-methylenebisacrylamide, and stirring uniformly to obtain a mixed solution B, (3) mixing the starch solution A obtained in the (1) step and the mixed solution B obtained in the (2) step, and adding into the microwave reactor for microwave treatment for 3-5 minutes, the microwave power is 250-300 W, drying and pulverizing to obtain high water absorption resin. The ratio of fresh peony petals to water is 1:1-1.3. The superabsorbent resin in the step (2) is 0.02-0.04 times the weight of the peony juice. In the step (3) carbon dioxide extraction has an extraction pressure of 23-35 MPa, an extraction temperature is 35-45 degrees C for 50-90 minutes and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 15-18 l/h. The supercritical carbon dioxide extractor used in the step (3) has two separation kettles, and the first separation kettle The pressure is 8-10 MPa, the temperature is 40-45 degrees C, the pressure of the second separation vessel is 3-5 MPa, and the temperature is 30-35 degrees C. The super-low carbon dioxide extraction in the step (3) uses cyclohexane as cosolvent. The flow rate of the cosolvent cyclohexane is 6-8% of the carbon dioxide flow rate. The ultrasonic power of the ultrasonic treatment is 200-300w, the frequency is 28-32 Hz, and the ultrasonic time is 15-20 minutes.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A12 (Polymers of di-and higher olefins; acetylenics; nitroso compounds.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal-organic                  structure compound includes a metal cluster                  including metal ions and organic ligand. The                  metal cluster includes a tetravalent cation metal                  ion. The tetravalent cation metal ion includes                  zirconium cation precursor. The metal-organic                  structure compound has a three-dimensional                  structure in which the structural unit of                  zirconium oxide based compound of formulae                  ([Zr6O4(OH)4(PyDC)x(F1)z]n) (I), and                  ([Zr6O4(OH)4(BPyDC)x(F1)z]n) (II) are repeated.                  The ionic component is treated with alkyl halide                  of formula (RX) (III) to give pyridinium cation                  of halogen ion and cation. (III) is iodomethane,                  iodoethane, 1-iodopropane, 2-iodopropane,                  bromoethane, 1-bromopropane and/or 1-                  chloropropane. Preferred Method: The catalyst is                  formed by zirconium oxide based repeating                  structural units of formulae                  ([Zr6O4(OH)4(PyDC)x(PymDC-RX)y(F1)z]n) (IV) or                  ([Zr6O4(OH)4(BPyDC)x(BPymDC-RX)y(F1)z]n) (V) (is                  repeated to have a three-dimensional structure)                  of the ionic metal-organic structure                  compound.x=4-6;z=0-4;x+z=6-8;F1=formate;RX=(III);R=methyl, ethyl, propyl or iso-propyl;                  andX=Cl, Br or I.Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried                  out by performing solvothermal synthesis of metal                  clusters and organic ligand precursors. The step                  (b) is carried out by reacting unshared electron                  pair of the pyridine group included in the                  metal-organic structure compound with alkyl                  halide (III) to induce an ionic component in the                  form of pyridinium cation of halogen ion and                  cation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ligand                  includes pyridi-based organic ligand precursor in                  its backbone. The pyridine-based organic ligand                  precursor includes 2,5-pyridinedicarboxylic acid                  and/or 2,2'-bipyridine-5,5'-dicarboxylic acid.                  The epoxy compound is propylene oxide, butylene                  oxide, epichlorohydrin, allyl glycidyl ether,                  glycidol and/or styrene oxide.

AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202200091N
AB    NOVELTY - A photocatalyst comprises a bismuth-rare-earth-containing oxyhalide.
   USE - Photocatalyst is used for purifying pollutants, decomposition of water, and carbon dioxide reduction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst is doped with rare-earth element, which makes the photocatalyst generate oxygen vacancy and/or impurity energy level, enables capture of photo-generated electrons, and improves the separation efficiency of photo-generated carrier, generates wide spectrum response, enables absorption of UV light, visible light, and near-infrared light, and improves the light utilization rate, photocatalytic activity, and photocatalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A photocatalyst comprises a bismuth-rare-earth-containing oxyhalide of formula: MBi1-yREyO2X, where M is calcium, strontium, barium, lead and/or cadmium, RE is rare-earth element, X is halide, and y is 0.001-0.9.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the photocatalyst, which involves mixing a compound of element (M), a bismuth source, a rare-earth compound, and a halide to obtain a premix, and sintering the premix or treating the premix under supercritical condition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound of element (M) is an oxide, halide or carbonate. The bismuth source is bismuth oxide or bismuth oxyhalide. The rare-earth compound is an oxide or nitrate. The halide is ammonium halide or bismuth oxyhalide. When the premix is treated under supercritical conditions, the compound of element (M) is a nitrate or halide, the bismuth source is bismuth nitrate, the rare-earth compound is a nitrate, and the halide is an alkali metal halide, ammonium halide, a halide containing element (M), or an ionic liquid halide. The rare earth element contains erbium, terbium, cerium and thulium.Preferred Composition: The concentration of the premix is 0.063-0.125 mol/L.Preferred Method: The premix is preferably sintered at 300-1500degrees Celsius for 1-40 hours. When the premix comprises solvent, preferably water, alcohol and/or ester, the premix is treated under supercritical conditions at 110-250degrees Celsius for 1-40 hours. After being treated under supercritical conditions, the product is heated at 200-1200degrees Celsius for 0.5-6 hours.
