PN JP2021075774-A
TI Gas supply apparatus arranged in electrochemical reaction apparatus for supplying e.g. carbon dioxide to electrolytic solution, has supply unit that supply generated microbubble on surface of electrode along extension direction of electrode.
AU FUSE Y
   KAWAGUCHI M
   FUJII K
   KOIKE K
   WADA T
AE SHIMIZU CONSTR CO LTD (SHMC-C)
   RIKEN (RIKE-C)
GA 202153590Y
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a microbubble generation unit (26) generates a microbubble or ultra-fine bubble from gas. A supply unit (28) assigns and supplies the generated microbubble on surface of an electrode (16) along extension direction of electrode.
   USE - Gas supply apparatus arranged in electrochemical reaction apparatus for supplying gas such as carbon dioxide to electrolytic solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas solubility with respect to electrolytic solution is improved. The contact of an electrode is increased, and the ability to accelerate efficient electrochemical reaction is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an electrochemical reaction apparatus; and
   (2) a gas supply method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view of the arrangement of electrochemical reaction apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Electrochemical reaction apparatus (10)
   Electrode (16)
   Microbubble generation unit (26)
   Supply unit (28)
   Electrolytic solution (S)

PN CN112775627-A
TI Welding process of plate splicing welding channel steel, comprises e.g. opening V-shaped groove on wing plate and web plate front surface, adopting carbon dioxide gas shielded welding at V-shaped groove, performing full penetration welding.
AU DOU J
   DAI C
   LI B
AE SHANDONG LUNENG GUANGDA STEEL STRUCTURE CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021565296
AB    NOVELTY - Welding process of plate splicing welding channel steel, comprises opening a V-shaped groove on the contact front surface of the wing plate and the web plate, adopting carbon dioxide gas shielded welding at the V-shaped groove position, and performing full penetration welding on the contact back of the wing plate and the web plate by adopting submerged arc welding.
   USE - The welding process of plate splicing welding channel steel is useful in full penetration welding seam between web plate and wing plate in the plate splicing welding channel steel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the existing problem that the increased material and energy consumption, low efficiency, affecting the welding seam quality, unenvironmentally friendly and increasing process steps and high welding costs; and qualifies 100% UT flaw detection of welding seam without the carbon arc gouging and root cleaning process.
DC M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); P56 (Machine tools (B23P,Q).)
MC M23-D01A1; M23-D01A3; M23-D01A4; M23-D01C; P56-U40; P56-X
IP B23K-028/02; B23P-015/00

PN CN213177668-U
TI Carbon dioxide filling device, has platform scale provided with bracket and base, and static clamping block whose end is hinged with end of movable clamping block, where another end of static clamping block is connected with end of movable clamping block.
AU SUN Y
   ZHOU S
AE WUHAN TUTENG IND & TRADE DEV CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 202154568S
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide filling device with good safety performance, comprising a platform scale; the platform scale is composed of a bracket and a base; the bracket is fixedly connected behind the base; the bracket is fixedly connected with a first connecting rod; the other end of the first connecting rod is fixedly connected with a first lantern ring; the first lantern ring is composed of a first static clamping block and a first movable clamping block; one end of the first static clamping block is hinged with one end of the first movable clamping block; the other end of the first static clamping block is connected with the other end of the first movable clamping block through a first buckle; the device realizes the installation of protective measures on the platform scale; so as to eliminate safety hidden trouble.
DC Q69 (Storing/distributing gas/liquid (F16T, F17))

PT P
PN CN112786989-A
TI Separating active material layer of battery electrode sheet from current collector, involves dissolving carbon dioxide or/and sulfur dioxide gas in salt solution, mixing battery pole pieces with solution followed by recovering mixture.
AU WANG L
   LI J
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2021550798
AB    NOVELTY - Separating active material layer of a battery electrode sheet from a current collector involves dissolving carbon dioxide or/and sulfur dioxide gas in a weak acid salt solution by pressure to obtain solution A. The battery pole pieces are mixed with the solution A, using reducing pressure and/or increasing temperature to make the gas escape, to obtain mixture A after stirring and shaking. The mixture A is sieved and separated back to the collection fluid to obtain mixture B followed by filtering and recovering the mixture B to obtain an active material layer.
   USE - Method for separating active material layer of a battery electrode sheet from a current collector.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separating active material layer of battery electrode sheet at rate of 98% with high purity, in simple, short flow, environmentally friendly and cost-effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weak acid salt solution is potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, lithium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, lithium bicarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium acetate, sodium acetate, lithium acetate, sodium oxalate, potassium oxalate, and lithium oxalate solutions.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC L03-E01B3; L03-E06; L03-J01; X16-B01; X16-E01; X16-E02; X16-M
IP H01M-010/54
